acceptance:
1. A reusable delimiter helper exists for untrusted artifact bodies.
2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.

acceptance:
1. TestIntake_TooLargeDecomposed_CreatesChildrenAndBlocksParent verifies pre-claim child beads are created, dependencies are wired, and parent is not claimed.
2. TestIntake_DecompositionEventIncludesACMap verifies triage-decomposed event includes child_ids, rationale, and ac_map.
3. TestIntake_DecompositionACMapRejectsDroppedAC verifies lossy splits block for operator without filing children.
4. TestIntake_DepthCapOverflow_BlocksOperator verifies queue-level max_decomposition_depth overflow sets needs-human-decomposition and does not attempt implementation.
5. TestPostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges_AutoDecomposes verifies no_changes_needs_investigation with a too-large/depth-capped rationale runs orchestrator decomposition, files children, blocks the parent, and does not require operator intervention.
6. TestPostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges_UsesQueueDepthNotAttemptPromptDepth verifies an implementation prompt depth-cap message does not prevent orchestrator decomposition when the bead is below agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth.
7. TestPostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges_LossySplitBlocksHuman verifies operator action is required only when the splitter cannot produce a complete AC map or queue-level depth overflow occurs.
8. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go Step 0 prompt no longer contradicts docs/triage/decomposition.md max_decomposition_depth semantics.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestIntake_.*Decompos|TestIntake_.*DepthCap|TestPostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges" -count=1 passes.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Large beads must be decomposed without operator intervention before implementation, or immediately after a failed implementation attempt proves the pre-claim gate missed a too-large bead. The recent bounced workflow beads showed the gap: the implementer correctly reported that the bead was too large, but decomposition was prohibited inside the attempted worktree/depth context, so an operator had to split it manually.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1386 embeds decomposition instructions in the implementer prompt, which happens after dispatch rather than before claim.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1661 emits an implementation-attempt decomposition depth cap message; that prompt cap can prevent the worker from creating children even when the queue-level orchestrator could still split safely.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md requires ac_map and parent blocking semantics, but execute-loop does not yet file children from a pre-claim intake decision or from post-attempt no_changes_needs_investigation caused by oversized work.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:29 defines no_changes_needs_investigation, but the work loop does not yet convert a too-large rationale into orchestrator-level decomposition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Handle pre-claim too_large_decomposed by creating child beads with standalone descriptions/AC and dependency wiring.
- Handle post-attempt no_changes_needs_investigation whose rationale says the bead is too large, too broad, depth-capped inside the attempt, or requires decomposition by invoking the same orchestrator splitter on the original bead.
- Treat implementation-attempt depth as advisory only; queue-level max_decomposition_depth is the limit that decides whether DDx can split or must ask the operator.
- Parent receives triage-decomposed event body with child_ids, rationale, and ac_map.
- Every parent AC must map to at least one child AC or explicit needs_human/non_scope; lossy decomposition blocks for operator instead of creating children.
- Parent is blocked/dependency-blocked on children and not claimed again for implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Safe rewrite handling.
- Review-time decomposition beyond using the same splitter when review_too_large is already classified.
title: intake: auto-decompose too-large beads before or after failed attempts

acceptance:
1. `TestPreClaimIntakeHook_PreservesExplicitRoutingPins` proves a runtime/config with explicit `Harness=codex` and `Model=gpt-5.4-mini` dispatches the intake hook with those pins preserved instead of clearing routing pins.
2. `TestPreDispatchLintHook_PreservesExplicitRoutingPins` proves the lint hook preserves explicit `Harness=codex` and `Model=gpt-5.4-mini` instead of forcing unpinned cheapest-profile routing.
3. `TestLifecycleHooks_UnpinnedWorkersStillUseProfileSelection` proves unpinned workers keep the existing strongest/cheapest profile-selection behavior.
4. `TestDDxWork_PinnedLifecycleHooksDoNotEmitRouteUnavailableWarnings` or equivalent integration test covers `ddx work --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini` and asserts lifecycle hook dispatch does not fail with `ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate` when the pinned route is available.
5. No production execution path calls `ResolveRoute` directly; DDx continues to pass explicit constraints to Fizeau Execute.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Pinned `ddx work` workers are still producing repeated lifecycle-hook routing warnings before every attempt:

- `readiness route unavailable: runner error: ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate: 3 candidates rejected`
- `lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: policy "air-gapped" has no candidate satisfying local endpoint`

This is happening even when the worker is explicitly started with a viable implementation route, for example:

`ddx work --watch --idle-interval 30s --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini --review-harness codex --review-model gpt-5.5`

The main implementation attempt then routes correctly to `codex/gpt-5.4-mini`, but pre-claim intake and pre-dispatch lint ignore the operator's pins and spend 60-120 seconds failing through unrelated unpinned/profile routing. This slows every worker cycle and makes healthy pinned execution look system-unready.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:134-143` constructs `AgentRunRuntime` with `ProfileOverride: selectProfileForDispatch(... SelectStrongestProfile)` and sets `ClearRoutingPins: true`, `ClearProfile: true`, `ClearMinPower: true`, and `ClearMaxPower: true`. That strips explicit worker harness/model pins before dispatching the intake prompt.

`cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-137` does the same for pre-dispatch lint, selecting the cheapest profile and setting `ClearRoutingPins: true`, `ClearProfile: true`, `ClearMinPower: true`, and `ClearMaxPower: true`.

In projects where autorouting/local endpoint validation is not yet complete, those unpinned hook dispatches fail even though the pinned implementation route is usable.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update lifecycle hook dispatch so explicit operator route pins from `ResolvedConfig` are preserved for pre-claim intake and pre-dispatch lint when `ddx work`/`ddx try` was started with `--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`, or `--model-ref`.
- Keep the existing profile-selection behavior only for genuinely unpinned workers.
- Preserve the strong/cheap intent for unpinned hook dispatch without overriding explicit operator constraints.
- Ensure lifecycle hook failures caused by unavailable optional hook routes are not recorded as system-unready when the implementation route itself is explicitly pinned and executable.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement DDx-side model/provider/harness ranking, fuzzy matching, fallback, or catalog lookup.
- Do not change Fizeau routing policy.
- Do not remove lifecycle hooks.
- Do not change post-attempt review model routing.
- Do not change local provider validation policy beyond preserving explicit operator pins for these hook dispatches.

INTERSECTIONS
- This is a DDx-side workaround needed while Fizeau autorouter/local-provider validation work is in flight.
- Related to the Fizeau routing-boundary cleanup: DDx should pass explicit operator constraints through, not substitute a DDx-side route.
title: work: preserve explicit route pins for lifecycle hook dispatch

acceptance:
1. `ddx run --help` exits 0 and describes the layer-1 task invocation command, or shipped skills are updated to stop documenting `ddx run` and use an existing command that actually supports the documented flags.
2. A CLI test covers command registration for `ddx run`.
3. Tests cover the documented flag surface used by skills: prompt input, harness/provider/model/model-ref passthrough, min/max power passthrough, and persona behavior or its documented replacement.
4. `rg "ddx run" .agents/skills .claude/skills skills .ddx/plugins` either finds only valid examples backed by the command, or finds no stale references.
5. The fix preserves the routing-boundary invariant: DDx forwards hard pins/power bounds and does not select, rank, or fallback concrete harness/provider/model values itself.
6. Relevant CLI tests pass.
description:
The shipped DDx skills now document `ddx run` as the layer-1 task invocation primitive, and workflow skills such as adversarial-review, compare-prompts, benchmark-suite, effort-estimate, and bead-breakdown all instruct agents to run commands like `ddx run --harness codex --min-power 10 --prompt review-target.md`.

Actual behavior in the current ddx binary on 2026-05-07:
- `ddx version` reports Commit bf77ce841, built 2026-05-07T03:42:23Z.
- `ddx --help` lists `try` and `work`, but no top-level `run`.
- `ddx run --help` fails with: unknown command "run" for "ddx".
- `ddx try --help` says it executes a bead through production machinery and exposes min/max power passthrough.
- `ddx work --help` says work iterates `ddx try` and owns retry-power policy.

Impact: checked-in/project DDx skills can route agents to a non-existent command, breaking review, comparison, benchmark, effort-estimate, and bead-breakdown workflows. This is especially visible in downstream Fizeau, whose updated DDx skill bundle references `ddx run` repeatedly.

Likely regression: during the routing-boundary cleanup, docs/skills moved from `ddx agent run` to the intended layer-1 `ddx run`, but the actual CLI command target was not added or was dropped before release.

In scope:
- CLI command registration for top-level `ddx run` or an explicit compatibility alias to the correct existing layer-1 command.
- Help text and tests proving `ddx run --help` exists.
- Compatibility with documented flags used by shipped skills: `--prompt`, `--text` if supported, `--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--model-ref`, `--min-power`, `--max-power`, and persona support if the skills continue to document it.
- Update shipped skill docs only if the intended command is not `ddx run`.

Out of scope:
- Changing `ddx try`/`ddx work` queue semantics.
- Reintroducing DDx-side routing policy; passthrough constraints must remain opaque.
title: Restore ddx run layer-1 command

acceptance:

2. Each row has schema_version=1.
3. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or equivalent) appends a row on attempt finalization using O_APPEND.
4. ddx work metrics backfill (or equivalent CLI) reads existing events.jsonl files and produces .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl from existing data, idempotent.
5. TestMetrics_AppendRowOnFinalization, TestMetrics_BackfillFromEvents, TestMetrics_BackfillIdempotent, TestMetrics_SchemaVersion all pass.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Performance analysis across model / prompt / harness / spec
  dimensions today requires fan-out reads of every bead's
  events.jsonl. There is no global per-attempt table with
  denormalized fields ready for query. After sister ADR formalizes
  the storage architecture, this bead implements the cold-tier
  metrics extraction.

  Without this, optimization is blind: we cannot answer "which
  model has the best cost-per-success for our drain" or "which
  prompt template fails most on which spec class."

CURRENT STATE
  - .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl contains lifecycle
    events: routing, cost, review-pass, review-block,
    closed-merged, etc. These rows have the raw signals
    (model, harness, cost_usd, tokens, verdict) needed for
    aggregate analysis.
  - There is no global attempts.jsonl file aggregating across
    beads.
  - Sister beads B29 (retention) cannot safely prune local
    evidence until metrics have been captured.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Define schema for .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl (one row per
     bead-attempt; JSONL, append-only):
        - schema_version: 1 (integer; for future migration)
        - attempt_id: string (current "run-id" until rename
          bead lands, then attempt-id)
        - bead_id: string
        - session_id: string (agent harness session)
        - ts_start, ts_end: RFC3339 timestamps
        - model: string
        - harness: string
        - profile: string (optional)
        - provider: string
        - prompt_template_id: string (optional)
        - prompt_template_hash: string (hash of the rendered
          prompt; enables grouping by prompt variant)
        - spec_id: string (FEAT-N, SD-N, TD-N, ADR-N if known)
        - outcome: string (success | execution_failed |
          review_block | review_fixable_gap | etc.; see TD-031 §5)
        - exit_code: integer
        - cost_usd: float
        - duration_ms: integer
        - input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens: integer
        - review_verdict: string (APPROVE | BLOCK | etc.;
          optional)
        - review_finding_count_by_severity: map of
          severity -> count (optional)
        - ladder_min_power_initial, ladder_min_power_final:
          integer (per ADR-024 ladder semantics)
        - ladder_steps_taken: integer

  2. Forward-going hook:
     - On attempt finalization in
       cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or sibling),
       extract the fields from result.json + manifest.json + the
       attempt's events and append a row to
       .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
     - Use O_APPEND for atomic single-writer appends.
     - On multi-drain machines (future federation): partition
       to .ddx/metrics/<machine-id>/attempts.jsonl (sister bead
       deferred until federation arrives).

  3. One-shot backfill:
     - cli/cmd/work_metrics_backfill.go (new file) implements
       ddx work metrics backfill.
     - For each closed bead, read its events.jsonl (in
       .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl or inline in
       beads.jsonl) and emit one row per attempt found.
     - DO NOT read .ddx/executions/<dir>/ for the backfill;
       events.jsonl is sufficient.
     - Idempotent: skip rows already in attempts.jsonl
       (dedupe by attempt_id).

  4. Tests:
     - TestMetrics_AppendRowOnFinalization: assert a row is
       added per attempt with all expected fields populated.
     - TestMetrics_BackfillFromEvents: assert backfill emits
       correct rows from existing events.
     - TestMetrics_BackfillIdempotent: assert re-running
       backfill does not duplicate rows.
     - TestMetrics_SchemaVersion: assert schema_version field
       is present and equals 1.

NON-SCOPE
  - The analyze CLI (sister bead).
  - Multi-machine partitioning (deferred until federation).
  - Adding agent-log-derived fields (tool_call_count, etc.) —
    those can be added later via schema_version bump.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR): formalize 3-tier evidence storage"
    — this bead implements the cold-tier metrics layer the ADR
    documents.
  - Sister bead B29 (RetainDays prune) DEPENDS on this bead
    landing first; pruning local evidence is safe only after
    metrics are extracted.
  - Sister bead "feat(work): ddx work analyze CLI" reads
    .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
title: feat(metrics): extract per-attempt rows to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- All five new files (`operation.go`, `id.go`, `errors.go`, `context.go`, `backend.go` expansion) exist and compile.
- `Operation` interface declared with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter implements `Operation`. Test: `TestOperation_MutateFuncAdapter_RoundTrip`.
- ID generation: tests `TestValidateID_AcceptsValidIDs`, `TestValidateID_RejectsInvalidCharset`, `TestValidateID_RejectsLengthBounds`, `TestRandomHexIDGenerator_ProducesValidIDs` (100 generations all pass `ValidateID`), `TestSequentialIDGenerator_ProducesUniqueOrdered` (1000 generations all distinct + lexicographically increasing).

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.

description:
PROBLEM
  Story 14 requires federated query resolvers that aggregate beads, runs, and projects across registered spokes. Today the GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql defines FederatedBead, FederatedRun, and FederatedProject types and the corresponding query fields, but write-routing (hub forwards mutations to owning spoke) is not implemented, and the resolver_federation.go file has no mutation forwarding.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:61 defines FederatedBeads, :124 defines FederatedRuns, and :184 defines FederatedProjects — all read-only fan-out via FederationProvider.FanOut (cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:31). cli/internal/federation/fanout.go:208 provides the FanOutClient.Execute fan-out transport. No mutation resolver in resolver_mutation_*.go calls FanOut or routes writes to an owning spoke; mutations remain hub-local. The ownerSpoke routing metadata fields (nodeID, spokeURL) are populated by existing fan-out code but no write-routing contract enforces "hub forwards, never broadcasts."

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add write-routing helper in cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go: routeToOwner(ctx, ownerSpokeURL, mutationQuery, variables) error that forwards mutations via HTTP to the owning spoke.
  - Wire routeToOwner into the relevant mutation resolvers in resolver_mutation_beads.go or resolver_mutation_runs.go for any mutation that operates on a federated resource.
  - Add schema.graphql comment block documenting the write-routing contract ("hub forwards to ownerSpoke; never broadcasts").
  - Name the ownerSpoke field on FederatedBead/FederatedRun/FederatedProject as the routing discriminator.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery and registration (covered by ddx-fe63969f).
  - Federated mutations on FederatedProject (out of Story 14 scope; read-only for projects).

description:
PROBLEM
Preclaim intake, terminal review blocks, land-conflict recovery, and implementation-attempt validation still park beads by adding needs_human labels or emitting needs_human reasons. Those paths are high-leverage because they decide whether ddx work keeps moving or stops for an operator.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:195-204 instructs classification as ambiguous_needs_human.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:333-348 maps ambiguous_needs_human as a canonical readiness outcome.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:760-835 parks non-actionable preclaim outcomes with needs_human label behavior.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1961-1981 adds needs_human labels/events in parkBeadPostIntakeRejection.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2017-2036 treats outcome reason needs_human as invalid implementation attempt evidence.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_terminal.go:57-76 applies terminal review blocks by adding LabelNeedsHuman and cooldown metadata.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go defines ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman.

PROPOSED FIX
Route all agent parking and operator-required paths through the pure lifecycle transition to status=proposed. Rename canonical outcomes away from ambiguous_needs_human toward proposed/operator_required/human_review_required language in prompts, parsers, status enums, and events. Terminal review blocks, intake ambiguity, land-conflict operator-only outcomes, and validation failures that cannot be autonomously repaired should set status=proposed with reason/suggested action metadata. Cases that a smart agent can still investigate should stay status=open and remain eligible for ddx work.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change no-changes parser behavior in this bead; no_changes has its own bead. Do not change GraphQL/frontend naming in this bead.

DEPS

description:
PROBLEM
The pre-push/full-test gate can fail under `-race` with a data race in workerprobe JSONL mirroring. This blocks otherwise unrelated worker landings and pushes, and it undermines the reliability of the worker-status/liveness work that depends on the same event path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:465-484` defines `teeWriter` with a mutable `buf []byte`, but `Write` appends to and slices that buffer without synchronization.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2718-2736` calls `sink.Write(line)` and then `sink.Write(newline)`. Multiple execute-loop goroutines can write through the same sink during cleanup/progress emission.
- Observed race evidence from the 2026-05-13 pre-push/full-test gate: `TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue` in `cli/cmd/work_test.go:371-387` failed under `-race`; one goroutine wrote `workerprobe.(*teeWriter).Write` from `writeLoopEvent` during `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run`, while another wrote through the same tee from `runExecutionCleanupPass`.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the workerprobe JSONL tee concurrency-safe and preserve event parsing semantics. Add a mutex or equivalent serialization around `teeWriter.Write` buffer mutation and dispatch, and consider making `writeLoopEvent` emit the JSON line plus newline in a single `Write` to reduce interleaving. The fix must be local to event mirroring; structured logging must remain fail-open and must not block work if the probe/server is unavailable.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change worker scheduling, readiness classification, or server ingest contracts. Do not disable the race detector or loosen pre-push gates.
title: workerprobe: serialize JSONL tee writes under concurrent loop events

description:

The queue-drain loop still lacks a command-level regression that proves host resource exhaustion stops ddx work before it touches the next ready bead. Existing unit tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resource_exhausted_test.go:52-100 verify the loop stops after one executor call, but cli/cmd/work.go:99-105 only routes into the worker and there is no end-to-end test that exercises the command boundary with two ready beads and an injected resource_exhausted setup failure. That leaves a gap where a future change could accidentally claim the second bead after ENOSPC-like failure.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:451-556 owns startup cleanup, pre-claim cleanup, and the main loop exit path. cli/cmd/work.go:99-105 hands ddx work straight into that loop, but no test currently proves the observable command behavior: exit after the first resource_exhausted attempt, no second claim, and the failed bead remains reclaimable.

PROPOSED FIX
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_e2e_test.go or a new sibling test file: add a command-level test that seeds two ready beads in a temp project and injects a resource checker / cleanup runner failure that returns resource_exhausted before the second claim.
- Assert the first bead is touched once, the second bead is never claimed, and the loop emits the resource_exhausted stop message.
- Verify the first bead stays open and unowned so it can be reclaimed later.
- Keep the harness in temp dirs and fakes; do not consume real disk or inodes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new production behavior to the loop itself.
- Do not test unrelated provider/model routing, retry cooldowns, or no_changes paths.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim exists and runs ddx work against two ready beads with an injected resource_exhausted setup failure.
2. The test proves only the first bead is touched and the second bead is not claimed.

4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

description:
PROBLEM
The guidance change is risky in two directions: it must stop broad interactive sessions from opportunistically editing ready beads, and it must not suppress non-interactive ddx work execution. The project needs deterministic regression coverage for both sides.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go contains load-bearing prompt invariant tests, but it does not assert a DDX mode boundary. Existing work-loop tests exercise no-ready-work and execution behavior, but they do not prove the new interactive queue-steward guidance is read-only or that ddx work --once still executes a ready bead after the guidance lands. The combined interactive-steward skill bead owns the fixture-backed route table and reference guidance that separate broad interactive prompts from worker prompts.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a focused regression layer that tests representative prompt routes and the worker carve-out. Use deterministic fixtures/static assertions rather than requiring an LLM call: broad interactive prompts should produce queue-steward guidance with no code-edit instruction, while ddx work/try worker prompts should produce bead_execution guidance and remain implementation-capable.

NON-SCOPE
- Benchmarking model quality.
- Running external harnesses or networked agents in unit tests.
- Replacing existing execute-bead integration tests.

PARENT AND DEPS

2. `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/git-fixture-env-audit.md` is committed and lists every `rg` hit for raw Git fixture setup under `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, and fixture scripts, with disposition `converted`, `already-safe`, or `follow-up`.

4. `scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh` scrubs Git-local env vars before fixture Git commands, or has a regression-backed note explaining why supported invocations cannot inherit hook-local Git env.
5. `sh scripts/git-config-health.sh` passes after the new regression tests run, proving `core.bare=true` and `DDx Fixture <fixture@ddx.test>` did not leak into the primary checkout.

description:
PROBLEM
The local `.git/config` corruption was repaired and guarded at the hook boundary, but the exact raw Git fixture callsites that can corrupt the primary checkout have not been audited end-to-end. If tests or fixture scripts run `git init/config/commit` in temp directories while inheriting hook-local variables such as `GIT_DIR`, `GIT_WORK_TREE`, or `GIT_INDEX_FILE`, Git can ignore the intended temp directory and mutate the outer repository instead. This produced observed corruption modes including `core.bare=true` and leaked fixture identity in the primary checkout.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/git/exec.go:12-28 documents the failure mode: raw `exec.Command("git", ...)` with inherited Git-local environment can mutate the hook's outer repository, and `git.Command` / `git.CleanEnv` are the intended isolation primitives.
- scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh:71-73 runs fixture `git config user.email "fixture@ddx.test"` and `git config user.name "DDx Fixture"`; those exact values leaked into the primary checkout during the incident.
- lefthook.yml:557-562 now scrubs Git-local env vars before pre-push `full-tests`, but that is a boundary guard and does not prove all fixture callsites are safe outside that hook path.
- cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:769-771 is one representative raw fixture setup that shells through `git init` and `git config`; similar raw Git fixture setup exists across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, and cli/internal/server and needs a disposition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit raw Git fixture invocations under `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, and scripts used by tests/fixtures. Focus on `git init`, `git config`, `git commit`, `git worktree`, and helper wrappers around `exec.Command("git", ...)` or `runCmd(..., "git", ...)`.
- Convert reusable test helpers to call `gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...)` or to set `cmd.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()` consistently when raw `exec.Command` is still needed.
- Harden `scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh` by scrubbing Git-local env vars before fixture Git commands, or document with a regression why the script cannot inherit hook-local Git env in supported invocations.
- Add regression coverage that contaminates `GIT_DIR`, `GIT_WORK_TREE`, and `GIT_INDEX_FILE`, runs representative fixture setup, and proves the primary checkout config is unchanged while the temp fixture repo receives its intended config.
- Commit a small audit artifact under `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/git-fixture-env-audit.md` listing each raw Git fixture hit, its risk classification, and whether it was converted, proven safe, or deferred to a follow-up bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken or bypass `lefthook.yml` pre-push `merge-policy`, `git-config-health`, or `full-tests` gates.
- Do not bulk commit unrelated untracked `.ddx/executions/*` directories from prior failed attempts.
- Do not refactor production Git behavior except where a shared test helper must use the existing clean Git wrapper.

description:
PROBLEM
  After sister bead extracts per-attempt metrics to
  .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, operators need a query tool to
  read it. Without a CLI, optimization signals are unused: the
  data exists but answering "which model has the best
  cost-per-success?" requires manual jq scripting per query.

CURRENT STATE
  - No ddx subcommand reads .ddx/metrics/.
  - No documented query patterns for cross-attempt analysis.

PROPOSED FIX
  Implement ddx work analyze with the following query dimensions
  and aggregations:

  Dimensions (--by <dim> or composable):
    - model
    - harness
    - profile
    - prompt_template_hash
    - spec_id
    - outcome
    - bead_id
    - day / week / month (by ts_start bucket)

  Aggregations (printed as columns):
    - count(attempts)
    - success_rate (closed-merged / total)
    - avg cost_usd, sum cost_usd
    - p50/p95 duration_ms
    - avg input_tokens, output_tokens
    - review_block_rate
    - escalation_rate (ladder_steps_taken > 0)

  Output format:
    - Default: human-readable table to stdout.
    - --json: emit JSON rows for piping to other tools.
    - --csv: emit CSV for spreadsheet import.

  Examples:
    ddx work analyze --by model
        → rows: model, count, success_rate, avg_cost, ...
    ddx work analyze --by model,outcome
        → rows: model+outcome combos
    ddx work analyze --by spec_id --since 7d
        → recent week, by spec
    ddx work analyze --where model=claude-sonnet-4-6 --by prompt_template_hash
        → drill into a specific model

  Implementation:
    - cli/cmd/work_analyze.go (new file).
    - Reads .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl in full (or partition
      files if multi-machine arrives later).
    - In-memory aggregation (millions of rows fit; this is not
      a database).
    - Pure-Go, no external deps.

  Tests:
    - TestAnalyze_ByModel
    - TestAnalyze_BySpecId
    - TestAnalyze_ComposedDimensions
    - TestAnalyze_SinceFilter
    - TestAnalyze_WhereFilter
    - TestAnalyze_JSONOutput
    - TestAnalyze_CSVOutput

NON-SCOPE
  - SQL or declarative query language (too much for v1).
  - Live dashboards / web UI (separate concern).
  - Cross-machine aggregation (deferred until federation
    + partition).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "feat(metrics): extract per-attempt rows" —
    this bead reads the output of that one.
  - ADR bead documents this as the analytics entrypoint.
title: feat(work): ddx work analyze CLI for cross-attempt performance queries

description:
CRITICAL FINDING from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md: code added by recently-landed beads has tests but NO production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. The drain has been closing beads as success while the actual fix code sits unwired. This explains why operational pain (worker auto-exit, lock contention) kept recurring even after fixes 'landed.'

Affected functions (per audit):
- escalation.Ladder.Next (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go) — added by S10 ladder work, no caller
- EvaluateRetryPolicy (cli/internal/agent/retry_policy.go) — added by recent retry work, no caller
- RunWithLockBackoff (cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go or similar) — added by ddx-da11a34a lock contention, no caller
- DispatchPostAttempt — added by ddx-aede917d or sibling, no caller

For each: decide wire vs delete based on whether the BEAD that added it is still relevant (its acceptance criteria still describes desired behavior) OR whether subsequent design changes have made it obsolete.

Recommended decision: **WIRE all four**. The beads that added them are recently-landed and the acceptance criteria are still relevant. The unwired state is a process failure (the implementer wrote and tested the code but didn't replace the old code path). Wiring is the right fix; delete only if the underlying behavior is genuinely obsolete (none of these are).

After wiring, verify with: drain runs and observes the new code paths firing in execution evidence. Spot-check 3-5 beads' .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json for evidence of escalation/retry/triage events.
notes: superseded by REACH-BACKFILL (ddx-83440482); the 4 unwired functions (escalation.Ladder.Next, EvaluateRetryPolicy, RunWithLockBackoff, DispatchPostAttempt) surfaced as initial production-reachability violations and are absorbed into child bead ddx-83b8994f (internal/agent backfill).
title: audit + wire (or delete) the unwired escalation/retry/triage code from recent landed beads

4. The regression suite includes TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract, TestDDxSkillQueueStewardRouting, TestExecuteBeadInstructionsContainsBeadExecutionMode, TestAgentRunRuntimePropagatesDDXMode, TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue, and TestDDxWorkOnce_BeadExecutionIgnoresQueueStewardDefault.

description:
PROBLEM
Interactive agents opened inside a DDx project can treat broad DDx prompts like "what's next", "what's blocking the queue", or "do work" as permission to start code edits. That competes with ddx work, which is supposed to drain execution-ready beads, and leaves no crisp default for human-required queue intervention, planning, and worker-capacity decisions.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy immediately without declaring a default interactive queue-steward posture. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work execution layers but not the interactive-agent boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work" and "what's on the queue" through the ddx skill without distinguishing queue stewardship from bead execution. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" to ddx bead ready, which exposes worker-ready beads rather than human-required intervention.

PROPOSED FIX
Define explicit DDx agent modes and implement the guardrails around them: queue_steward for broad interactive sessions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead workers, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Add a focused operator command surface for intervention work and worker-capacity suggestions, while preserving ddx work as the owner of execution-ready implementation beads.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing ddx work's existing execution loop.
- Building a full TUI or chat UI.
- Rewriting bead tracker storage by hand.
- Changing merge-policy or tracker audit semantics.

CHILD QUEUE
- Spec guidance updates define the mode taxonomy and precedence.
- DDx skill/AGENTS guidance makes queue stewardship the broad interactive default.
- Agent execution plumbing marks ddx work/try prompts with bead_execution mode.
- A command surface exposes human-required intervention and worker-capacity advice.
- Regression tests and prompt evals prove interactive guidance does not suppress ddx work execution.
title: agent-modes: define queue-steward execution boundary

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:migration, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.

description:
PROBLEM
  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.
  - withHeartbeat starts the internal goroutine + WaitGroup, calls fn(), cancels the ticker goroutine on fn return, and returns fn's result.
  - Drain loop body (execute_bead_loop.go:732) replaces the inline goroutine block with a single withHeartbeat call.
  - Add TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn and TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates to cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go using a stub ticker channel injection.

NON-SCOPE
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Changes to heartbeatInterval policy or the Store.Heartbeat call itself.
  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: heartbeat timing and lifecycle belong in one helper, not inline with candidate execution.
- P4: one bead heartbeat bug must not leak into the rest of the drain loop.
- P5: extracted heartbeat behavior supports always-visible worker state.

description:
PROBLEM
Failure reasons such as too-large beads, missing tests, provider quota, ENOSPC, git locks, review transport, and no-op attempts are not consistently owned by a readiness, preflight, cleanup, retry, close-policy, or triage surface.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:50-112` defines lifecycle checkpoints and fail-open behavior but does not provide a shared failure taxonomy.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:535-575` describes quality hooks but does not map reason classes to owner surfaces.
- `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md` owns queue actions but does not yet distinguish bead readiness failures from system readiness failures using the new vocabulary.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a reason taxonomy to ADR-023 and reference it from FEAT-010 and TD-031. The taxonomy must separate bead-readiness reasons, system-readiness reasons, and post-attempt reasons, and must map each reason to the subsystem that should detect/respond.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement classifiers, cleanup, provider pause, or CLI output changes.
title: docs: define readiness failure taxonomy and owner surfaces

description:
PROBLEM
  The run/try/work dispatch paths must not set Profile, Harness, or Model directly on ResolvedConfig or Runtime structs — those are durable knobs that belong in config.LoadAndResolve. Without a mechanically-enforced lint rule, future edits can silently reintroduce the anti-pattern and bypass the fizeau routing contract.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go (package routinglint) currently forbids retired compensating-routing identifiers (ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, etc.) and flag literals (--escalate, profile_ladders). It does not yet forbid direct field assignment of Profile/Harness/Model on Runtime structs inside run/try/work command code paths. The SD-024 runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go:41) forbids durable-knob fields on *Runtime structs generically, but does not name a per-command allowlist or produce a test that verifies the run/work/try paths specifically. No TestRoutingLint* test exercises this invariant.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extend routinglint (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go) to flag direct Profile/Harness/Model field assignment on types matching *Runtime in cli/cmd/ run/work/try command files, or add a new rule in runtimelint covering this.
  - Alternatively, add a targeted integration test TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly that runs the analyzer against the live tree and asserts zero diagnostics for the assignment pattern.
  - Fix any existing violations: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1893-1897 passes overrides.Profile/Harness/Model through CLIOverrides to config.LoadAndResolve — verify this is compliant (CLIOverrides is not a Runtime struct).

NON-SCOPE
  - Changes to agent_run or agent_run_overrides command behavior.
  - Changes to the fizeau or ladder routing logic.
  - Lint rules for non-routing fields on Runtime structs (covered by runtimelint).

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadRejectsCommitPlusNoChangesRationale` or equivalent proves an attempt with a synthesized implementation commit and `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/no_changes_rationale.txt` does not return `status=success`, `outcome=merged`, or close the bead.
2. `TestExecuteBeadMixedCommitAndBlockedNoChangesRationalePreservesEvidence` or equivalent proves a mixed result containing `status: blocked` preserves the rationale text in result/evidence and exposes a clear `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`-style detail.
3. Existing legitimate zero-diff no_changes coverage still passes, including `TestNoChangesRationaleWithVerificationCommandClosesAlreadySatisfied` or the local equivalent for verification_command behavior.
4. Existing normal commit success coverage still passes when no `no_changes_rationale.txt` is present.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecuteBead.*NoChanges|TestNoChanges.*|Test.*Mixed.*NoChanges" -count=1` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passes or any pre-existing unrelated failure is recorded in the bead evidence without being converted to success.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A DDx execute-bead attempt can produce both a substantive implementation commit and `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/no_changes_rationale.txt`, and DDx still records the attempt as `success`/`merged`. Live evidence: attempt `20260512T050728-bb18a61f` for `ddx-ef9e8aec` landed commit `15772fc85`, merged evidence commit `9e1ab8506`, and closed the bead, while `.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/no_changes_rationale.txt` says `status: blocked` because the required `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` gate timed out/failed. That turns a self-reported blocked verification into a successful close.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1413-1428` classifies `task_succeeded` solely from `resultRev != baseRev`; it only reads `no_changes_rationale.txt` when `resultRev == baseRev`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:615-616` deliberately excludes `.ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt` from synthesized code commits, so the file can coexist with a synthesized implementation commit without affecting `resultRev`.
- `cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:56-90` already parses structured `status: blocked|open|proposed` no_changes rationales, but that parser is bypassed when any implementation commit exists.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1360-1407` treats the returned report as authoritative; by the time the loop sees the result, the contradictory no_changes signal has been lost behind a success/merged outcome.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make execute-bead treat an implementation diff plus `no_changes_rationale.txt` as a non-successful mixed-signal result before close/merge success is reported. A worker must choose one lane: commit green implementation, or no_changes/status rationale.
- Preserve the rationale text in evidence and surface a clear failure/detail such as `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` so the worker can retry or a follow-up bead can handle the stated blocker.
- Ensure `status: blocked` in the rationale cannot close a bead as success when a commit also exists.
- Keep legitimate `no_changes` behavior unchanged for zero-diff attempts and keep ordinary green implementation commits unchanged when no rationale file exists.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_*test.go` or `cli/internal/agent/try/*test.go` as needed for focused coverage

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rewrite the already-landed `ddx-ef9e8aec` history.
- Do not change Fizeau routing/model/provider selection.
- Do not weaken legitimate already-satisfied no_changes verification.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 5 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Existing tests in `cli/internal/bead/` (store_test.go, chaos_test.go, backend_conformance_test.go) continue to pass unchanged. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` is green.
- `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx skill prompts are part of the execution contract. The bead-lifecycle skill currently teaches agents to classify work into needs_human and needs_investigation outcomes, which will recreate the broken behavior even after core code is fixed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:75,126,208,227,246,256-257 refers to needs_human, needs-investigation, and recommended_action outputs as lifecycle decisions.
- .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md mirrors the same contract for Claude harnesses.
- .agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md and .claude/skills/ddx/SKILL.md may need queue/status wording updates after the specs change.
- AGENTS.md currently names queue_steward allowed commands but does not yet reflect the new status=proposed operator-intervention contract.

PROPOSED FIX
Update DDx skill contracts and mirrored skill files so agents use status-owned lifecycle decisions. The bead-lifecycle skill should classify operator-required outcomes as proposed, autonomous smart-agent investigation as open/continue, external recheckable blockers as blocked, satisfied work as closed, and not-doing as cancelled. It must not ask subagents to output needs_human or needs_investigation as lifecycle labels. Keep any legacy terms only as input-compatibility notes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change core Go behavior in this bead. Do not hand-edit generated/cached plugin copies unless the repo's skill sync process requires it.

DEPS
Depends on the spec update and the agent outcome mapping beads so prompt wording matches implemented behavior.

description:
PROBLEM
Several otherwise scoped beads cannot be retried or closed because their required verification gates fail in shared agent/cmd execution tests unrelated to the bead under attempt. The queue currently parks those beads as needs-investigation even when their focused tests pass.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:969-971 calls excludeCleanupMetadataFromWorktreeGit(wtPath) after worktree creation and wraps failures as `excluding execute-bead cleanup metadata: ...`.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go:90-109 fakeExecuteBeadGit.WorktreeAdd creates an ordinary directory and copies tracker/docs files, but does not initialize a git repository in the fake worktree. Cmd tests that call ExecuteBeadWithConfig now hit the real cleanup-metadata exclusion path against a non-git directory and fail with exit status 128.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_test.go:40 and cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_e2e_test.go:32 are named in failed queue attempts as part of the red cmd execute-bead coverage.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_cancel_test.go:166 defines TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA; recent failed attempts report this test timing out after 10m with the worker stuck in cancellation/probe loops, blocking broader `go test ./internal/agent/...` gates.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make cmd fake execute-bead worktrees compatible with the production cleanup-metadata exclusion path, either by initializing enough git metadata in fakeExecuteBeadGit.WorktreeAdd or by injecting/seaming the exclusion operation for tests while keeping production behavior covered.
- Stabilize TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA so it deterministically observes cancellation and returns within its SLA timeout under package/race test runs.
- Re-run the specific cmd and agent tests that blocked the parked beads, then the broader package gates used by those beads.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change candidate-cycle, review-gate, lifecycle-hook, or production reachability behavior.
- Do not weaken production cleanup metadata exclusion; tests may use a seam only if production behavior remains covered by an integration test.
- Do not close or mark the dependent beads complete in this bead; it only restores the shared verification floor.

description:
PROBLEM
The intake parent bead ddx-f3bbcfce was too broad and bounced because it combined hook contracts, safe rewrites, decomposition, AC maps, depth overflow, and no-progress policy. The first executable slice is the typed pre-claim intake hook contract and loop insertion point.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:680 invokes PreDispatchLintHook before claim, but there is no typed PreClaimIntakeHook with the finalized intake outcomes.
- cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:86 constructs lint-only behavior, not actionability/decomposition decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add PreClaimIntakeHook (or equivalent guard) to ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.
- Define typed outcomes actionable_atomic, actionable_but_rewritten, too_large_decomposed, ambiguous_needs_human, and intake_error.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/bead/lock.go WithLock records wait-duration and hold-duration via agentmetrics or equivalent observable channel.
2. cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock records wait, hold, and retry-count metrics.
3. When tracker_lock.go exhausts DefaultLockRetryPolicy.MaxElapsed, the resulting error message includes the lock identity and the holder's claimant-PID (not just a generic timeout).
4. TestWithLock_RecordsWaitDuration in cli/internal/bead/lock_test.go (or new test file) asserts the metric is captured for a contended lock.
5. TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount in cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock_test.go (or new test file) asserts retry count metric is captured under simulated contention.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  When parallel-worker drain mode is enabled (multiple ddx work --once
  shells running concurrently), bead.Store.WithLock and the agent-layer
  tracker locks become a contention surface. Today there is no
  observability into lock wait time, lock-hold duration, or contention
  rate — failures manifest as 30s MaxElapsed exhaustion and
  resource_exhausted outcomes, with no telemetry to debug.

  The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus self-review identified this as a
  prerequisite for safely scaling up to N=2..4 parallel workers: without
  per-lock metrics, we cannot tell whether a 10s WithLock wait
  (lock.go:18-27 default) is approaching its budget under load, or
  whether 30s tracker-lock MaxElapsed (tracker_lock.go:51) is being
  exhausted by a long-tail of concurrent acquisitions vs occasional
  hot-path blocking.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/lock.go:18-27 WithLock has no instrumentation; it
    spins at 50ms intervals and waits up to 10s by default. Wait time is
    not measured or emitted.
  - cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:105-183 withTrackerLock /
    withMainGitLock acquire/release path has no instrumentation.
  - There is no operator-facing dashboard, log, or metric for lock
    contention.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add lightweight metrics around three lock surfaces:

  1. bead.Store.WithLock (cli/internal/bead/lock.go:18-27):
     - Record lock-wait duration (time from WithLock entry to lock
       acquisition).
     - Record lock-hold duration (time the callback held the lock).
     - Emit via the existing agentmetrics package (or a new bead-metrics
       channel) keyed by call site.

  2. withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock (cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:105-183):
     - Same wait/hold instrumentation.
     - Additional: record retry-attempt count when DefaultLockRetryPolicy
       is consumed (so we see "needed 3 retries" vs "got it on first
       attempt").

  3. Exit code / stop reason aggregation:
     - When a worker times out at MaxElapsed, capture the lock identity
       and the holder's claimant-PID at point of exhaustion. Today the
       error message is generic.

  Surface format:
     - Existing agentmetrics output (likely .ddx/agent-metrics or
       similar; check cli/internal/agentmetrics for existing API).
     - Or stdout JSON line per lock acquire/release if no agentmetrics
       sink exists.

  Add tests:
     - TestWithLock_RecordsWaitDuration: assert metric is captured for a
       contended lock.
     - TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount: assert retry count metric is
       captured under simulated contention.

NON-SCOPE
  - Fixing lock contention itself (sister bead ddx-1f774edf removes the
    big contributor; this bead is observability for what's left).
  - Building a lock-contention dashboard (separate concern; just emit
    the data).
  - Reducing the 10s/30s budgets (those are correct; the metric tells
    us if they're tight).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (network-free Land()) drops the big
    contention cause; this bead provides visibility into what remains.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a (chaos for stale-lock recovery) covers
    correctness; this bead covers visibility.
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent/chaos: cover concurrent-drain
    landing under shared-main contention" needs this metrics surface to
    write meaningful assertions.
title: agent/lock: instrument bead.Store WithLock wait time + tracker-lock acquisition metrics

All execute-bead tests still pass. prompt_sha (from B3) measurably increases cache hit rate across attempts in dogfood metrics (or at minimum, manifest comparisons across attempts of the same bead show identical prompt_sha prefix).
description: Optional / not on Story 12 critical path per /tmp/story-12-final.md §B5. buildPrompt at execute_bead.go:1392-1461 places dynamic bead metadata FIRST and static instructions LAST, so Anthropic's prefix-cache cannot help — every attempt cache-misses on the static suffix. Reordering to put static instructions first would unlock cache hits across attempts with the same prompt_sha. Reordering changes XML semantics and merits its own change with separate review. File now; prioritize separately.
