acceptance:
1. TestReviewRouting_MissingActualPowerUsesSmartFloor verifies reviewer MinPower is smart/top when implementer actual_power is zero or missing.
2. TestReviewRouting_KnownActualPowerUsesNextFloor verifies reviewer MinPower is above implementer actual power when catalog data is available.
3. TestRepairRouting_FixableGapCanRaiseMinPower verifies a repair pass for review_fixable_gap requests a higher MinPower when eligible.
4. TestRouting_PassthroughUnsatisfiedStops verifies sticky harness/provider/model constraints that cannot satisfy the requested floor emit operator-action evidence and do not silently downgrade.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestReviewRouting|TestRepairRouting|TestRouting_PassthroughUnsatisfiedStops" green.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Reviewer and repair attempts must be allowed to use stronger reasoning when the failure is capability-sensitive, but DDx must preserve ADR-024 by expressing that only as MinPower bounds rather than concrete model fallback. Current reviewer MinPower can collapse when implementer actual_power is missing.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:661-664 sets reviewer minPower to impl.ActualPower + 1 only when ActualPower > 0; otherwise it leaves MinPowerOverride at 0.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:148-152 SelectReviewerTier returns smart, but that tier choice is not enough if review dispatch uses MinPowerOverride=0 and no explicit smart/top floor.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:40-68 raises MinPower for primary attempts on selected statuses, but review/repair candidate-cycle routing does not yet have explicit power-floor policy.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:38-41 forbids DDx from choosing/substituting concrete models, so the fix must use Fizeau power bounds.

PROPOSED FIX
- Reviewer routing: if implementer ActualPower is known, request the next higher available floor; if missing/zero, request the configured smart/top review floor.
- Repair routing: for review_fixable_gap or capability-sensitive check failures, allow the coordinator to raise MinPower for the repair pass within MaxPower and sticky passthrough constraints.
- If passthrough constraints make the requested floor unsatisfiable, emit agent_power_unsatisfied or blocked_by_passthrough_constraint and stop without silent downgrade.
- Add tests using fake model catalogs / fizeau responses.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement concrete provider/model fallback chains.
- Do not change Fizeau internals.
- Do not enable two-slot review.

DEPS
Depends on repair loop so repair routing has a caller and on reviewer runtime/profile work so reviewer routing is explicit.
title: routing: raise review and repair MinPower through Fizeau bounds

acceptance:
1. cli/cmd/testutils_test.go no longer contains a sync.Once package-level fixture; GetTestLibraryPath is removed or replaced with a per-test fixture call.
2. Every former GetTestLibraryPath caller uses testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, ...) or a t.TempDir()-scoped fixture with t.Cleanup registered.
3. After running cd cli && go test ./cmd/..., neither /tmp/.test-library nor /tmp/.test-library.git exists on disk (verified by a test that asserts os.Stat returns ErrNotExist for both paths after the package finishes).
4. TestNoSharedTmpFixtureLeaks in cli/cmd/testutils_test.go (or equivalent) asserts the cleanup invariant.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/testutils/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  cli/cmd/testutils_test.go GetTestLibraryPath uses a sync.Once package-level
  helper that creates /tmp/.test-library (working repo) and /tmp/.test-library.git
  (bare repo) and never registers cleanup. These directories accumulate on the
  developer's machine permanently because there is no t.Cleanup, defer
  os.RemoveAll, or trap-style teardown anywhere in the chain. Discovered by
  the 2026-05-09 test-cleanup audit.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/cmd/testutils_test.go:88-90 hardcodes:
      workingRepo := filepath.Join(tempBase, ".test-library")
      bareRepo    := filepath.Join(tempBase, ".test-library.git")
      testLibraryPath = bareRepo
  - tempBase falls through to "/tmp" when TMP and TMPDIR are unset, so on
    a vanilla `go test ./cmd/...` invocation without TestMain in scope (or
    with env vars cleared by a shell wrapper) the dirs land at literal
    /tmp/.test-library and /tmp/.test-library.git.
  - There is no os.RemoveAll, no t.Cleanup hook, and the sync.Once design
    is explicitly intended to "reuse across runs" — making the leak
    architectural rather than a forgotten cleanup.
  - cli/cmd/testutils_test.go isolateCmdTestTempRoot only redirects TMPDIR
    inside TestMain; if any subtest or parallel package calls
    GetTestLibraryPath before TestMain has fired (e.g. independent test
    binaries) the /tmp literal wins.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Delete the package-level sync.Once shared-fixture pattern.
  - Each call site that needs a fresh ddx-initialized repo should call
    cli/internal/testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, "standard") (already exists,
    auto-registers t.Cleanup, uses scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh under the
    hood per CLAUDE.md).
  - For tests that genuinely need a shared bare repo within ONE package's
    run, build it under t.TempDir() in a TestMain helper that registers
    cleanup via the returned func from func TestMain(m *testing.M).
  - Remove tempBase env var detection now that t.TempDir() is in use.

NON-SCOPE
  - Migrating other test packages that use their own private fixtures.
    This bead is scoped to GetTestLibraryPath and its callers in cli/cmd/.
  - Performance optimization (reusing fixtures across tests within one
    package via TestMain is fine; cross-process reuse is what's banned).
  - Frontend test cleanup.

INTERSECTIONS
  - testutils.NewFixtureRepo (cli/internal/testutils/) is the canonical
    replacement. CLAUDE.md already documents it as the preferred path.
title: tests: replace shared /tmp/.test-library fixture with t.TempDir-scoped repo

acceptance:
1. Every *_test.go file in cli/internal/server/perf/ has a //go:build perf tag at the top.
2. cd cli && go test ./... (no -tags=perf) reports zero tests run from cli/internal/server/perf/...
3. cd cli && go test -tags=perf ./internal/server/perf/... runs the perf tests as before.
4. Makefile contains a test-perf target invoking the tagged build.
5. .github/workflows/ci.yml contains a step or job that invokes make test-perf on an appropriate trigger (schedule, perf-touching PR, or manual workflow dispatch).
6. cd cli && go test ./... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  The cli/internal/server/perf package contains performance benchmarks
  that run for ~80 seconds during the default lefthook pre-push
  full-tests step. These are perf tests by intent, not unit tests; they
  should not block every push. Today they have no build tag or skip
  gate, so they run on every full-test invocation.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/server/perf/ contains benchmark tests (e.g.
    TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline) that take ~80s when not skipped.
  - The package has a testing.Short() skip in some tests but not all,
    and pre-push runs without -short.
  - Makefile has a bench-graphql target (lines ~121-127) that writes a
    Markdown baseline report when DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR is set, but it is
    not invoked from CI today.
  - .github/workflows/ci.yml does not invoke `make bench-graphql` or
    otherwise run the perf package as a dedicated step.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add `//go:build perf` tag to every test file in
    cli/internal/server/perf/. Files to edit (run `ls
    cli/internal/server/perf/*_test.go` and tag each).
  - Update Makefile:
      a. Add a `test-perf` target that runs `go test -tags=perf
         ./internal/server/perf/...` with an appropriate timeout (e.g.
         5 minutes).
      b. Document that `make test` excludes perf; `make test-perf` runs
         them.
  - Update .github/workflows/ci.yml:
      a. Add a new step or job that runs `make test-perf` on a schedule
         OR on pull requests touching cli/internal/server/perf/. Don't
         run on every PR by default; perf is non-gating.
  - Update lefthook.yml ci block (or full-tests step) only if it
    explicitly references perf (it doesn't appear to).
  - Verify default `cd cli && go test ./...` no longer pulls perf tests
    in (they should be excluded by the build tag).

NON-SCOPE
  - Other test packages outside cli/internal/server/perf/ (covered by
    sister beads).
  - Designing a perf-regression dashboard (separate concern; this bead
    just gates the slow tests behind a tag).
  - Removing the perf tests; they remain valuable, just not on the
    default path.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead: "lefthook pre-push: add -short gate" (independent — the
    -short flip and the perf-tag move both reduce pre-push time, by
    different mechanisms; they compose).
title: tests: move internal/server/perf behind //go:build perf tag; add make test-perf target

acceptance:
1. .gitignore no longer contains lines 438-445 (the explicit un-ignore allow-list for execution evidence).
2. .gitignore contains an explicit ignore for .ddx/executions/ (or relies on the broader .ddx/ pattern with no un-ignores).
3. git ls-files .ddx/executions/ returns zero matches.
4. After the bead lands, a fresh execute-bead attempt produces evidence files on disk under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ that are NOT tracked by git.
5. CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/) documents that .ddx/executions/ is per-machine working state; the events stream in .ddx/attachments/ is the durable audit.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  .gitignore lines 438-445 explicitly UN-IGNORE several file types
  inside .ddx/executions/, forcing every per-attempt evidence dir
  into git. This was a deliberate choice to make execution evidence
  durable — but it has produced 12,096 tracked files (52% of the
  repo) with no retention bound.

  The events stream is already externalized to .ddx/attachments/
  per the existing migrate path. That's the durable audit trail.
  The per-attempt prompt.md / result.json / manifest.json
  duplicate evidence in git are working state — like node_modules,
  per-machine, recreatable.

  After sister bead "feat(executions): wire RetainDays" lands and
  bounds the in-tree dir count, this bead completes the cleanup by
  removing the un-ignore lines so future attempt dirs are NOT
  tracked in git AT ALL. Existing tracked dirs are removed via
  git rm --cached -r.

CURRENT STATE
  - .gitignore:438-445:
        # Allow tracked execution evidence
        !.ddx/executions/
        !.ddx/executions/*/
        !.ddx/executions/*/prompt.md
        !.ddx/executions/*/manifest.json
        !.ddx/executions/*/result.json
        !.ddx/executions/*/usage.json
        !.ddx/executions/*/checks.json
  - These un-ignores override the broader .ddx/ ignore pattern.
  - Without them, .ddx/executions/ would be untracked.

PROPOSED FIX
  - .gitignore: delete lines 438-445 (the un-ignore allow-list
    for execution evidence file types).
  - Add a single explicit ignore line:
        .ddx/executions/
  - Run `git rm --cached -r .ddx/executions/` to remove all
    currently-tracked execution dirs from the index (they remain
    on disk).
  - Update CLAUDE.md to reflect: .ddx/executions/ is now untracked
    working state, like node_modules. The events stream in
    .ddx/attachments/ is the durable audit.
  - Verify the pre-commit / pre-push hooks still pass after the
    change.

NON-SCOPE
  - Migrating evidence to a mirror backend (sister bead).
  - Implementing retention (sister bead).
  - Auditing every reference to .ddx/executions in tests / docs
    that may need updating.

INTERSECTIONS
  - DEPENDS ON: sister bead "feat(executions): wire RetainDays"
    landing first. Otherwise dirs accumulate on disk without
    bound (just untracked instead of tracked).
  - Sister bead "feat(mirror): wire executions.mirror" optional;
    not required.
  - Memory note: Execution Evidence Convention in CLAUDE.md
    states agents must write reports to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/.
    That convention still works after this change (working
    state is per-machine; the attached events stream captures
    the durable audit signal).
title: chore(gitignore): stop tracking .ddx/executions/ in git

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:236 has a 2-3 line comment quoting the amended TD-031 §5 reconcile-close invariant and citing TD-031 by section.
2. cli/internal/bead/store.go:471 has a one-line comment noting Create is the lone status write outside transitionLifecycleInPlace.
3. cli/internal/bead/store.go:1245 has a 1-2 line comment stating ClosureGate guards the evidence-close path only.
4. TestReconcileCloseSkipsClosureGate (or equivalently named existing test) in cli/internal/bead/reconcile_test.go asserts a bead with no session_id and no closing_commit_sha but all deps closed reaches StatusClosed via reconcile.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  After TD-031 §5 declares reconcile-close a distinct meta-close that
  intentionally bypasses ClosureGate (sister bead, doc-only), the production
  code at the two relevant call sites must reference the amended invariant
  inline so the spec/code linkage is observable to future readers and to
  alignment-review tooling. Today both sites are silent about why one
  transition path uses the gate and the other does not.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:471 in Create assigns b.Status = DefaultStatus
    directly without going through transitionLifecycleInPlace. This is the
    single legitimate exception to the "all status writes go through the
    transition gate" rule and is not documented at the assignment.
  - cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:236-242 calls UpdateWithLifecycleStatus
    (StatusClosed, ManualClose=true, ...) without referencing TD-031 or
    explaining why ClosureGate is skipped.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1245 invokes ClosureGate inside closeWithEvidence
    but does not state that reconcile-close is the documented bypass.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a one-line comment at cli/internal/bead/store.go:471 noting that
    Create is the lone status write that does not go through
    transitionLifecycleInPlace (creation is not a transition; reference TD-031
    §3 transition matrix).
  - Add a 2-3 line comment at cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:236 quoting the
    amended TD-031 §5 invariant: reconcile-close is a meta-close that
    intentionally bypasses ClosureGate because the bead has no execution
    session; dependency closures supply transitive evidence.
  - Add a 1-2 line comment at cli/internal/bead/store.go:1245 stating that
    ClosureGate guards the evidence-close path only; reconcile-close skips it
    by design (per TD-031 §5).
  - Add a regression test TestReconcileCloseSkipsClosureGate in
    cli/internal/bead/reconcile_test.go that constructs a bead with no
    session_id / closing_commit_sha, all deps closed, runs reconcile, and
    asserts the bead lands in StatusClosed (proving the gate is bypassed
    by design, not by accident). If a near-equivalent test already exists
    in reconcile_test.go, retrofit its name and assertion instead of adding
    a duplicate.

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing reconcile.go runtime behavior. The TD-031 amendment declared
    current behavior correct; this bead only documents and locks it down
    with a regression test.
  - Adding ClosureGate variants for reconcile.
  - Refactoring the broader reconcile control flow.

INTERSECTIONS
  - ddx-ca1c66ee (epic parent).
  - Depends on the TD-031 amendment bead landing first (the comments quote
    the amended spec text).
title: bead/reconcile: align with amended TD-031 reconcile-close invariant

acceptance:
1. CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/parallel-drain-playbook.md) contains a "Parallel drain operator playbook" section covering the 6 items in PROPOSED FIX.
2. The doc explicitly notes that parallel drain is unsafe before ddx-1f774edf lands (because of withMainGitLock spanning network I/O).
3. The doc gives a concrete example of mixing harnesses (claude + codex + openrouter or equivalent) across N shells.
4. The doc cross-references the chaos test bead and the lock-metrics bead.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes (markdown lint + secrets + ddx-config validation are the relevant gates for a doc-only change).
description:
PROBLEM
  The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus self-review identified a gap in the
  operator playbook: when running multiple ddx work --once shells in
  parallel (after ddx-1f774edf lands), operators must understand that
  using the same harness across all N workers will throttle on the
  per-account rate limit (e.g., Anthropic 5-tier rate limits for the
  claude harness). Mixing harnesses across workers (claude + codex +
  openrouter, etc.) dodges per-harness ceilings and delivers true N×
  throughput. There is no operator documentation today that says this.

CURRENT STATE
  - CLAUDE.md describes the project structure and per-CLI commands but
    not the operator playbook for parallel drains.
  - docs/helix/06-iterate/ contains reliability principles and alignment
    reviews but no operator-facing parallel-drain guide.
  - .agents/skills/ddx/reference/ and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/
    contain skill documentation but not an explicit
    parallel-drain-playbook section.
  - CLAUDE.md memory note feedback_models.md says "prefer claude harness
    for execute-loop; avoid agent harness for real work" — the harness
    selection guidance exists for single-worker drain but not for
    parallel.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a short section to CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/ if
  preferred for separation of concerns) titled "Parallel drain
  operator playbook" covering:

  1. When to use parallel drain: after ddx-1f774edf lands; not before
     (lock contention defeats the parallelism today).

  2. How to start: open N tmux panes / shells, each running a
     `ddx work --once` loop. No code change required.

  3. Harness mixing: example of running claude on one shell, codex on
     another, openrouter on a third — mixing dodges per-harness rate
     limits and avoids serializing on Anthropic quota.

  4. Cost ceiling: each ddx work invocation accepts --max-cost; budget
     across N shells.

  5. Conflict expectations: parallel landing produces some preservation
     under load (covered by the chaos sister bead); set operator
     expectation that occasional preservation is normal and the
     preserved iteration refs can be merged manually.

  6. Observability: point at the lock-metrics sister bead's output
     channels (once landed) for debugging contention.

NON-SCOPE
  - Building a `ddx work --parallel N` flag (deliberately YAGNI per
    plan review; external shells deliver the same throughput).
  - Server-spawned-worker design (ADR-022 step 7, separate work).
  - Harness-selection algorithm (single-worker memory note already
    covers default selection).

INTERSECTIONS

    safe), the chaos test bead, the lock-metrics bead.

description:
PROBLEM
Story 16.3 needs the run-detail page, evidence whitelist, resolver contract, and review deep-link documented together. The current docs already cover parts of that surface, but the wording is split across three specs and still needs to be normalized into one coherent Story 16.3 contract.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md:835-841 documents the run-detail evidence whitelist and `run_detail_view`, but it does not present the Story 16.3 trust-boundary language as a dedicated subsection.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:1215-1236 defines `run_detail_view` audit semantics, but the run resolver contract and drain-time persistence notes need to be spelled out in the same place.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md:64-73 ties evaluation drill-down to the shared run-detail surface, but the review surface still needs an explicit `/runs/[runId]` deep-page link.

PROPOSED FIX
- FEAT-008: add or normalize the Run-detail-page subsection so the trust boundary and evidence whitelist are explicit.
- FEAT-010: extend the run resolver contract text with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path), bundle-root confinement, and normalized ToolCallEntry persistence at drain time.
- FEAT-019: point the review surface at `/runs/[runId]` so the deep page is called out directly.

NON-SCOPE
- Server implementation changes.
- Playwright or Bun test work.
- Any new auth or RBAC semantics.

ACCEPTANCE

2. The updated prose includes the trust boundary, evidence whitelist, resolver contract, drain-time persistence note, ToolCallEntry definition, and `/runs/[runId]` deep-page reference.
3. `cd cli && go test ./...` green.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
Execute-loop execution fields are duplicated across cobra extraction, REST/GraphQL request structs, server ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec persistence, and runWorker consumption. Adding a worker mode or idle interval can silently drop it on one transport.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as a server-local struct while cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts a wider flag set, cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 has another request struct, and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 has a third. The type boundary is not shared, and the old polling field name conflicts with the desired public semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small import-safe package under cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ that defines ExecuteLoopSpec with JSON tags and fields ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, and SpecVersion.
- Define Mode values for once, drain, and watch. CLI parsing may keep --once, but the persisted/API spec uses Mode rather than a boolean once field.
- IdleInterval is the sleep duration used only when Mode=watch and the queue has no execution-ready work.
- Do not add or preserve a PollInterval field, poll_interval JSON field, or public compatibility alias in the new spec.
- Add DispatchOptions with only Local and JSON control-plane fields; do not persist it.
- Add Duration JSON support that marshals as strings and unmarshals both strings and numeric nanoseconds.
- Add ApplyDefaults and Validate methods, including SpecVersion support and a code comment documenting the single-version client/server forward-compat stance.
- Do not wire cobra, REST, GraphQL, or runWorker callers in this bead; later children consume this package.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change LabelFilter from string.
- Do not change execute-loop validation policy beyond moving existing checks behind the spec lifecycle.
- Do not modify server or cobra call sites except imports needed by tests.
notes: Unblocked after fresh-eyes cleanup removed stale PollInterval/poll_interval compatibility-alias requirements. The bead is now a standalone canonical ExecuteLoopSpec package slice with Mode + IdleInterval semantics and no unresolved investigation item.
title: agent: introduce canonical ExecuteLoopSpec package

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` and `ddx try` execute beads inline without writing any worker-status record to `.ddx/workers/`. The server's WorkerManager writes those records only for server-started workers. For inline workers, operators cannot see worker_id, current bead, attempt_id, current phase, or last_activity_at — the only visible signal is `extra.claimed-at` on the bead, which never refreshes. This is the core information gap for "is this worker alive and what is it doing?" during a long-running attempt.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go:13-35` periodically calls `store.TouchClaimHeartbeat` but has no hook for writing operator-facing worker status.
- `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:154-186` constructs the inline `ExecuteBeadWorker` and `workerprobe.Probe` but never generates a local worker_id or writes a `.ddx/workers/<id>/status.json` sidecar.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1585-1620` tracks `provAttemptID` and `phaseSeq` per attempt but does not propagate them to any persistent status file.
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:1688-1713` defines the WorkerRecord schema used by the server; inline workers should use a compatible subset (worker_id, current_bead, current_attempt, started_at, last_activity_at, pid) so existing consumers (`workerDirPID` at `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:1250-1262`, `pruneWorkerDirs` at line 1267) keep working.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a `LivenessReporter` in `cli/internal/agent/work/` (or a thin helper package under `cli/internal/agent/workerstatus/`) with:
- `Update(now time.Time, info LivenessInfo)` — atomic write to `.ddx/workers/<worker-id>/status.json` (write to tmp + rename); concurrent-safe; idempotent for repeated identical state
- Fields persisted: worker_id, project_root, current_bead, attempt_id, phase, started_at, last_activity_at, harness/model/profile summary, pid
Wire a tick callback into `work.WithHeartbeat` so each tick calls `Update`. Propagate current_bead/attempt_id/phase from the execute-bead loop (e.g. via a setter on the reporter called from the phaseEmitter and around the WithHeartbeat fn). In `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go`, generate a local `worker-id` (e.g. `worker-<ts>-<short-rand>`) when the inline worker starts and pre-create `.ddx/workers/<id>/status.json` with starting state. Treat the local sidecar as the source of truth for inline workers; the server's WorkerManager remains authoritative only for server-started workers (`StartExecuteLoop` and friends are untouched).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not emit any new server events or change probe behavior (covered by sibling bead).
- Do not change focus/status consumers (covered by sibling bead).
- Do not modify the server-side WorkerManager file layout.

Parent: ddx-a3589116. Dependencies: none.
title: worker: ddx work writes liveness sidecar at .ddx/workers/<id>/status.json on heartbeat tick

description:
PROBLEM
Operators see confusing `ddx work` messages such as `pre-claim intake: pre-claim intake: empty output (continuing to claim ddx-cce893be)` and `pre-dispatch lint: lint hook: missing-harness (continuing to claim ddx-cce893be)`. The wording exposes internal hook names, repeats prefixes, and fails to distinguish bead-readiness warnings from system-readiness warnings such as missing harness or empty hook output.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:86-107` defines `ComplexityGuard` and prints `complexity gate missing; allowing bead`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:670-710` prints `pre-claim intake` messages directly to the operator log, including fail-open `(continuing to claim ...)` text.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:192-205` returns errors already prefixed with `pre-claim intake: ...`; the execute-loop log then prepends another `pre-claim intake:` prefix.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-751` prints `pre-dispatch lint` messages directly to the operator log.
- `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:22-28` and `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:95-97` classify missing harness as `lint hook: missing-harness`, but the user-facing loop message does not explain that readiness checks were unavailable due to agent/harness configuration.
- `cli/internal/config/types.go:307` describes triage config as the pre-Claim complexity gate, and `cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json:651` carries generated stale wording.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace operator-facing terminology with bead readiness language while preserving compatibility for persisted events, prompt sources, and internal structs where unsafe to rename immediately:
- Render hook infrastructure failures as actionable readiness-check warnings, for example `readiness check unavailable: no harness configured; continuing with ddx-cce893be`.
- Render empty hook output as a system/check failure, not as a bead defect, for example `readiness check unavailable: intake returned empty output; continuing with ddx-cce893be`.
- Avoid doubled prefixes by formatting hook errors once at the loop boundary.
- Keep fail-open semantics intact for infrastructure failures and keep blocking semantics intact for real bead-readiness failures.
- Update config comments/schema/docs so user-facing text no longer refers to complexity gates, pre-claim intake, or pre-dispatch lint except where explicitly marked legacy/debug.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not perform large internal type/function renames or event migrations.
- Do not change terminal result-line status markers such as `✓ ddx-id → error`; that is owned by `ddx-f326f001`.
- Do not implement the full readiness/system classification taxonomy; that is owned by `ddx-e2479b81`.

description:
PROBLEM
When the post-attempt triage hook cannot find the nested `bead-lifecycle` skill, the operator sees `post-attempt triage error (<bead> execution_failed): triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)`. The message is technically true but does not say which project root was inspected, which paths were checked, or what command remediates the install.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:90-92` returns `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle` when `hasBeadLifecycleSkill(projectRoot)` is false. `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:218-227` hard-codes the checked paths but returns only a boolean, so callers cannot include the checked path list in diagnostics. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:175-179` logs the hook error verbatim and appends `(continuing)`, which confirms fail-open behavior but hides the actionable remediation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the boolean-only missing-skill path with a diagnostic helper that returns whether the lifecycle skill exists, the resolved project root, and the exact checked paths.
- Update post-attempt triage errors to say the hook was skipped/degraded because `bead-lifecycle` was missing under the project root.
- Include the checked paths `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` in the error text.
- Include remediation in the hook-time error: run `ddx update --force`, then `ddx doctor`.
- Preserve fail-open behavior: missing lifecycle skill must not change the underlying attempt status or close/park behavior by itself.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not run full `ddx doctor` from inside the hook.
- Do not dispatch the lifecycle hook through a fallback global skill path.
- Do not change lint/readiness/triage JSON output contracts.
- Do not change retry or cooldown policy in this bead.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/types.go contains a new ToolCallTimeout constant (default 1800000 ms = 30 min) alongside DefaultWallClockMS and DefaultTimeoutMS, with a comment explaining its sanity-cap role (NOT loop detection).
2. cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go drainServiceEventsWithRenderer (or equivalent) parses stream-json events and identifies meaningful-event types: tool_use, distinct tool_result, response_complete. Idle timer resets ONLY on these.
3. cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go contains a loopDetector helper that tracks the last 8 tool calls and triggers EarlyCancel via the existing mechanism when ≥4 identical (canonicalized command, exit-code + first-256B-output) pairs are seen.
4. TestIdleTimer_ResetsOnlyOnMeaningfulEvents asserts the idle timer does NOT reset on a stream-json line that is neither tool_use nor distinct tool_result.
5. TestLoopDetector_FiresAfterRepeatedIdenticalCommands asserts EarlyCancel fires after 4 identical command/result pairs in the last 8.
6. TestToolCallTimeout_KillsLongHungSubprocess asserts a single tool_use that exceeds ToolCallTimeout without a tool_result triggers a structured tool_timeout cancel.
7. TestWedgeImmune_HardWorkRunsToCompletion: simulate a 4-hour stream of regularly-progressing tool_use → distinct tool_result events; assert the harness completes naturally without being cancelled.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  The 2026-05-09 drain wedge investigation found:
    - DefaultWallClockMS = 3h (cli/internal/agent/types.go:441) is the
      ONLY bound that fires on wedged workers. It is unconditional —
      kills active and inactive work alike.
    - DefaultTimeoutMS = 2h (idle, line 434) resets on every
      stdout/stderr byte. Stream-json tool_result events are bytes,
      so a model in a tight loop (e.g., 168s test rerun × 6
      iterations) never trips the idle timer even though no real
      progress is being made.

  The policy we WANT:
    - True wedge (no token exchange AND no running subprocess for an
      extended period) → kill.
    - Loop (repeated tool calls without real diff/test/state progress)
      → kill.
    - Hard work in progress (active tokens, real subprocess work, real
      file diffs / test outcomes evolving) → DO NOT kill, no matter
      how long it takes.

  An absolute wall-clock cap kills hard work alongside wedges. The
  sister bead that proposed reducing wall-clock from 3h to 1h has
  been cancelled as wrong policy. This bead is the structural
  prevention.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:434 DefaultTimeoutMS = 2h, resets on
    byte stream — not on meaningful events. A loop that produces
    regular tool_result events keeps the timer alive without
    meaningful progress.
  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:179-207
    drainServiceEventsWithRenderer parses stream-json events but does
    not track event type for idle-reset purposes (treats all events
    equally) and does not track repeated-command patterns.
  - There is no per-tool-call timeout, so a single hung subprocess
    can absorb arbitrary wall-clock without surfacing.

PROPOSED FIX
  Three layered prevention mechanisms (compose, don't replace):

  1. Meaningful-event idle reset:
     Define a "meaningful event" set: tool_use start, tool_result with
     distinct content from prior, file_edit, test_pass/test_fail with
     changed outcome, response_complete. Reset the idle timer ONLY on
     these — not on all stream-json events. Keep the byte-stream as a
     liveness check separate from idle.
     Land: cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:179-207 +
     cli/internal/agent/executor.go idle-tracking path.

  2. Loop detection (repeated-command guard):
     Maintain a recent-tool-call window (last 8 tool calls). Track:
       - canonicalized Bash command string
       - tool_result outcome (exit code + first 256B of output)
     If the same (command, outcome) pair appears N≥4 times in the
     window, surface a structured loop_detected event and request
     early cancel via the existing EarlyCancel mechanism
     (cli/internal/agent/executor.go:102-125 has authCancelPatterns
     as a precedent for triggering EarlyCancel from a stream watcher).
     Land: cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go new
     loopDetector helper.

  3. Per-tool-call sanity cap:
     Set a generous per-tool-call wall-clock (default 30 min, much
     larger than typical) so a genuinely hung subprocess (e.g.,
     network deadlock in `git push`) cannot indefinitely starve the
     drain. NOT for catching loops — that's mechanism 2's job. Just
     for catching individual stuck tool calls.
     Land: cli/internal/agent/types.go new ToolCallTimeout constant +
     cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go enforces it via the
     stream-json watcher.

  Critically:
    - DO NOT reduce DefaultWallClockMS (3h backstop is fine — it
      fires only when all three new mechanisms have somehow been
      bypassed).
    - DO NOT change behavior for legitimately-hard-but-active work.
      All three mechanisms target real failure modes: byte-only
      activity, repeated unproductive commands, and hung individual
      subprocesses.

  Tests:
    - TestIdleTimer_ResetsOnlyOnMeaningfulEvents
    - TestLoopDetector_FiresAfterRepeatedIdenticalCommands
    - TestToolCallTimeout_KillsLongHungSubprocess
    - TestWedgeImmune_HardWorkRunsToCompletion (negative test:
      simulate 4h of regularly-progressing tool calls; assert it does
      NOT get killed)

NON-SCOPE
  - Heuristic about file-diff progress beyond what mechanism 1
    captures (defer; could be a follow-up bead).
  - Surface the loop_detected event to the operator UI (logging
    suffices for now; UI is a separate concern).
  - Tuning the canonicalization rules for what counts as the "same
    command" in mechanism 2 (start with simple string-equality after
    trimming whitespace; refine if needed).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-85b544f3 (CANCELLED): proposed reducing
    wall-clock 3h→1h. Wrong policy; this bead supersedes.
  - Sister beads ddx-1f774edf + ddx-f1c476c3 + ddx-1fda33d2 +
    ddx-150758e7 reduce the SOURCE of long-running Bash calls (test
    cost on every push). Once those land, wedge frequency drops by
    starvation. This bead provides the safety net for whatever
    long-running patterns remain.
title: feat(agent): wedge prevention via loop-detection + meaningful-event idle reset

description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go was named when the command was 'ddx agent execute-loop'. The command is now 'ddx work' (with ddx agent execute-loop as a back-compat alias), and the FEAT-010 refactor (ddx-5cb6e6cd) moves to a work package. The filename no longer matches the command surface or the package intent.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: the file contains the Drain loop logic. Its name derives from the pre-refactor 'execute-bead-loop' command, which has been superseded by 'ddx work'.
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) plans 'file/identifier rename per refactor proposal §3'. If C13 only renames identifiers (not the file), this bead handles the file rename. If C13 subsumes the file rename, this bead should be closed as duplicate — verify after C13 lands.

Evidence committed at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/fizeau-execute-routing-investigation.md with file:line citations into fizeau's source. Outcome A: gap confirmed → continue; Outcome B: gap doesn't exist → close bead as already-resolved with the report as evidence.

2. (Outcome A only) Upstream fizeau change: file an issue/PR against github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau so that explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower triggers the routing engine within that harness's eligible models. For Class 2 harnesses (AutoRoutingEligible=false: gemini/opencode/pi), the engine returns an explicit "no auto-resolution available for harness=X" error, NOT silent empty Model.

3. (Outcome A only) Bump fizeau in cli/go.mod once the upstream change ships.

4. Integration test (in fizeau, or DDx-side stub if upstream test coverage is sufficient): TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness — assert ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"claude", Model:"", Profile:"code-economy"} → response RoutingActual.Model is non-empty AND is a member of the claude family per catalog.

5. Companion negative test: TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly — assert empty Model + empty Profile/MinPower returns a clear "under-specified routing" error instead of silently empty Model.

6. End-to-end verification (after cfedee8e/C9 also landed): ddx work --harness=claude on a small queue. Assert via:
   - .ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json: Model field non-empty after first attempt
   - .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json: routing_decision evidence shows resolved model from the claude family
   - ddx server workers show <id>: Model field non-empty
   - NOT via process-list inspection

7. Use current catalog model names in tests/assertions: opus-4.7, sonnet-4.6, claude-haiku-5.5 (NOT stale Haiku/Sonnet/Opus literals). Fizeau may normalize to family aliases (haiku/sonnet/opus) for the actual --model arg via service_models.go:164-185.

8. NO local-config "fallback when fizeau unavailable" path. Empty fizeau response = fail the attempt; DDx retry policy escalates next attempt.

9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.

10. Documentation: code comment on fizeau Execute call site (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go) noting that empty Model + Profile/MinPower is the well-formed request shape and what fizeau is expected to return.
description:
Upstream fizeau gap: when ServiceExecuteRequest carries an explicit Harness pin AND empty Model AND a non-empty Profile/MinPower, fizeau v0.10.3's Execute (service_execute.go:193-224) treats Harness as a hard pin and skips the routing engine entirely (it only invokes routing when req.Harness == ""). Result: request validates with empty Model, fizeau returns Model="", DDx passes no --model arg to the claude CLI, and Anthropic's CLI uses whatever the local user's session default is.

OBSERVED
- Active worker on ddx-23c67ca4 (PID 4065353): claude --print -p --verbose ... — no --model arg
- ddx server workers show worker-20260503T004125-0b67 → Model: empty, Harness: claude
- Operators cannot control which Claude model executes a bead via DDx routing for the claude harness; UI/spec.json reports Model="" (operator sees "claude" but not which Claude model)

SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY

This bead is HALF A of the original ddx-1e516bc9 problem. HALF B (DDx supplying Profile/MinPower in ServiceExecuteRequest) is owned by ddx-cfedee8e + C9 (ddx-9228a484), which wire the ladder into the executor closure and bump MinPower per each call. Without the ladder wiring, fizeau wouldn't have anything to route on even if this fix lands. With the ladder wiring AND this fizeau fix, the full path works.

INVESTIGATION FIRST
Confirm the gap before implementing. The hypothesis is that fizeau v0.10.3 service_execute.go:193-224 only runs routing when Harness == "". If that's wrong (e.g., fizeau already routes within harness when Profile/MinPower is supplied), this bead is a no-op and gets closed as already-resolved.

UPSTREAM CHANGE (if confirmed)
Modify fizeau's Execute so that when Harness is set and Model is empty and Profile or MinPower is non-empty, the routing engine runs WITHIN that harness's eligible models. Fizeau already has the catalog (models.yaml: opus-4.7 code-high :31-60; sonnet-4.6 code-medium :141-170; claude-haiku-5.5 code-economy :222-251) and harness eligibility (registry.go: claude/codex AutoRoutingEligible=true; gemini/opencode/pi false). The change is: skip the "Harness != '' means skip routing" gate when Model is empty AND a routing input (Profile/MinPower) is present.

For Class 2 harnesses (gemini/opencode/pi, AutoRoutingEligible=false): the engine should still surface a clear "no auto-resolution available" error — NOT silently return empty Model. DDx should treat that as a fail-fast on the attempt.

DDx-SIDE WORK (verification only — not implementation)
Once the ladder is wired (cfedee8e → C9), DDx will already submit Profile/MinPower for each Execute call. This bead's verification phase asserts that with cfedee8e landed AND fizeau bumped to the version with this fix, ddx work --harness=claude produces a non-empty resolved model in spec.json / result.json / server worker UI.

NOT IN SCOPE
- DDx-side ladder wiring or MinPower-supply changes (owned by ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-9228a484 C9)
- Operator-explicit --model passes (already work; pinned by operator intent)
- HTTP providers openrouter/lmstudio (no subprocess --model arg; not subprocess invocations)
- Stale resolveModel() in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:380 (legacy path; codex confirmed production uses RunWithConfigViaService → svc.Execute, not the legacy Run() path)

WHY P1 (was P0)
Demoted because:
- The DDx-side enabling work (cfedee8e/C9) is already P1/P2 in the queue
- This bead's effect is only observable after cfedee8e/C9 land
- It's an upstream fizeau bump, not a DDx code change
- Original P0 framing assumed a DDx-side fix in the dispatch path; that part is the refactor's job

DEPS
- ddx-cfedee8e (or its absorber ddx-9228a484 C9): the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on
title: fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models (upstream gap)

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go Land() invokes no git fetch and no git push; LandingGitOps interface no longer declares FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, or FetchOriginAncestryCheck methods.
2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go file-header comment (formerly lines 27-29) describes a network-free flow with no push step.
3. landPushAutoRecover function and any LandResult fields naming push outcomes (PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered) are removed; fetchOriginError type is removed.
4. withMainGitLock in Land() wraps only local-index operations (merge or update-ref + evidence commit + worktree sync); a unit test asserts the lock is released within 1s under a fake LandingGitOps with simulated 5s of post-Land work in the caller (proving the lock does not extend beyond Land() return).
5. TestLand_IsNetworkFree in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go wires a fake LandingGitOps with no fetch/push methods (compile-time proof) and asserts Land() succeeds end-to-end.
6. Existing tests that asserted push-race recovery (e.g. TestLandPushAutoRecover_*) are removed or rewritten; no test references LandResult.PushFailed.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go invokes git fetch
  origin, git push --ff-only, and a push-race auto-recovery loop as part of
  the core drain lifecycle. This couples Land() to remote I/O, holding
  withMainGitLock for 30-120s on remote round-trips, which produced the
  observed contention failures (ddx-08e90869, ddx-15a51f8c). Per the user's
  architectural position and the sister doc-only bead amending FEAT-010,
  Land() must perform ONLY the local worktree-merge into local main; origin
  sync (fetch) and push are separate operations, never invoked from Land()
  or the drain loop.

  This bead removes all network I/O from Land() and from the LandingGitOps
  interface. Origin-sync and push become operator responsibilities (raw
  git commands today; a future ddx sync helper is downstream of multi-node
  work and explicitly out of scope per operator direction).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:27-29 file-header comment
    declares "If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip" as
    step 5 of the canonical Land flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1036-1043 Land() wraps the
    entire landLocked() call under withMainGitLock, including push.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1146-1216 landLocked merge
    path issues git fetch origin (FetchBranch), then git push (PushFFOnly),
    then on push race calls landPushAutoRecover.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1479 landPush triggers
    landPushAutoRecover on non-FF rejection.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1538-1580 landPushAutoRecover
    runs fetch + temp-worktree merge + update-ref + push, all inside the
    same lock.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:239-297 LandingGitOps
    interface includes FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, and FetchOriginAncestryCheck
    methods that exist solely to support the in-Land network I/O path.
  - LandResult fields PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered, and the
    fetchOriginError type all exist to communicate network-I/O outcomes
    that Land() should no longer be responsible for.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Strip Land() to local-only operations:
      a. fetch-origin call (FetchBranch) at the start of landLocked:
         REMOVE.
      b. FetchOriginAncestryCheck before merge: REMOVE (the merge proceeds
         on local main as-is; if origin diverged, that is discovered when
         the operator pushes, by raw git push rejecting non-FF, which is
         the operator's problem).
      c. PushFFOnly at end of landLocked: REMOVE.
      d. landPushAutoRecover function: REMOVE entirely.
  - Update LandingGitOps interface:
      a. Remove FetchBranch method.
      b. Remove PushFFOnly method.
      c. Remove FetchOriginAncestryCheck method.
      d. Keep merge / update-ref / worktree / commit methods.
  - Update LandResult:
      a. Remove PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered fields.
      b. Remove fetchOriginError type.
      c. Keep merge / commit / preserve outcomes.
  - Update the file-header comment at lines 27-29 to reflect the
    network-free flow.
  - Update Land() critical section: withMainGitLock now wraps only the
    local-index slice (merge-or-update-ref + evidence commit + worktree
    sync). Sub-second hold expected.
  - Existing tests for push-race recovery (and any LandResult.PushFailed
    assertions) MUST be removed or rewritten as no-network tests.
  - Add a regression test TestLand_IsNetworkFree in
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go that wires a
    LandingGitOps with no Fetch/Push methods (proving they are no longer
    called) and asserts Land() succeeds.

  Operator-side note (NOT in scope of this bead): users push with raw
  `git push origin main` after a drain session. A future ddx sync helper
  is downstream of multi-node coordination work and is explicitly
  deferred.

NON-SCOPE
  - Designing/building a ddx sync helper (deferred until multi-node).
  - Federation work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is single-node
    only.
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact; file separate beads if any
    surface friction).
  - Changing TD-031 bead state machine.
  - Reducing the bead.Store WithLock primitive (different layer).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on doc-only spec amendment bead ddx-3d5197d8 landing first.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-c019e48a (now scoped to local-only stale-lock
    recovery) depends on this implementation landing.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-eab41b94 (push-race scenarios) is now obsolete
    and should be cancelled.
title: agent/land: release withMainGitLock around network I/O (push, fetch, merge)

description:
PROBLEM
  Four tests in cli/internal/server collectively block ~55 seconds per
  pre-push run waiting for unreachable agent-provider endpoints to time
  out. Each calls a server handler that runs ListProviders / ListModels
  with a 10s context timeout; with no providers reachable in the test
  environment, the handler waits for the full timeout before returning
  empty. Pre-push lefthook runs without -short, so all four fire on
  every push.

ROOT CAUSE
  Affected tests + 10s probe timeouts:
    - cli/internal/server/agent_models_test.go:11 TestHandleAgentModelsNoProviders
      → handleAgentModels at agent_models.go:28 wraps context.WithTimeout(10s)
      around ListProviders/ListModels. Test waits ~10s for empty result.
    - cli/internal/server/agent_models_test.go:60 TestMCPAgentModels
      → mcpAgentModels at agent_models.go:147 same pattern. ~10s.
    - cli/internal/server/agent_models_test.go:119 TestMCPAgentModelsSingleProvider
      → mcpAgentModels with single-provider path at agent_models.go:224
      same context.WithTimeout(10s) pattern. ~5s observed.
    - cli/internal/server/server_test.go:3419 TestMCPAgentDispatchTrustedAllowed
      → mcpAgentDispatch at server.go:4731 calls
      agent.RunWithConfigViaService which constructs a real agent.Service
      and waits for provider discovery; ~40s observed.

  None of these have testing.Short() gates. Pre-push runs the full set
  without -short, so the ~55s aggregate cost lands on every push.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a testing.Short() skip at the top of each of the four tests:
        if testing.Short() {
            t.Skip("provider-probe test; runs only in non-short mode")
        }
  - File:line edits required:
        cli/internal/server/agent_models_test.go:11 (TestHandleAgentModelsNoProviders) — add skip inside func
        cli/internal/server/agent_models_test.go:60 (TestMCPAgentModels) — add skip
        cli/internal/server/agent_models_test.go:119 (TestMCPAgentModelsSingleProvider) — add skip
        cli/internal/server/server_test.go:3419 (TestMCPAgentDispatchTrustedAllowed) — add skip

  - The skip alone does not save pre-push time today because pre-push runs
    without -short. The companion sister bead "lefthook pre-push: add
    -short gate" (filed separately) flips pre-push to use -short. After
    both land, pre-push wall-clock drops by ~55s for these 4 tests.

  - Coverage for "no providers returns empty array" is independently
    exercised by the integration job in CI (which runs without -short).

NON-SCOPE
  - Mocking the provider service — the timeout is the real-world
    behavior; gating with testing.Short() preserves the test's existence
    for non-short / CI runs.
  - Reducing the 10s context timeout — that timeout is the production
    behavior under real network latency.
  - Modifying lefthook.yml itself (the sister bead handles the pre-push
    -short flip).
  - Other slow tests in cli/internal/server (covered by independent beads).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead: "lefthook pre-push: add -short gate" — depends on this
    bead landing first so the four tests have skips before -short flips.
title: fix(server): short-circuit agent-models provider probe in tests; gate with testing.Short

No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
The drain loop still treats no-progress mostly as base_rev == result_rev after a failed implementation attempt. That is too broad now that ddx work has pre-claim intake, pre-dispatch lint, routing preflight, review malfunctions, operator parks, and other non-implementation outcomes. Those states should not consume no-progress retry budget or trigger long cooldowns.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1964-1978 implements shouldSuppressNoProgress with only three checks: disrupted, transient outcome reason, and base/result equality.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1980-1987 only treats transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict, lock contention, and no viable provider as transient; it omits intake blocks, decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, auth/tool setup, review errors, and needs_human/operator states.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:230-249 increments no_changes metadata before the work layer has a reusable valid-implementation-attempt classifier.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1199-1215 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1275 apply no-progress cooldowns from separate branches instead of a shared eligibility decision.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a reusable no-progress eligibility helper, either in cli/internal/agent/try or cli/internal/agent/work, that answers whether the implementer had a real opportunity to inspect/modify/verify code.
- Count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts with checkout/context established and an implementer result available.
- Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota, transport, auth/tool setup, review errors, needs_human/operator-required states, and other system failures.
- Route eligible no-progress stops through the StopCondition NoProgress vocabulary from ddx-89ab3fda.
- Preserve existing behavior for genuine no-commit implementation attempts.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review verdict taxonomy beyond marking review/system outcomes no-progress-ineligible.
- Do not schedule repair cycles.
- Do not alter max-cost handling.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-89ab3fda for the NoProgress StopCondition vocabulary.
title: try: count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts

description:
PROBLEM
  After a successful bead land, the post-land
  syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths step overwrites the live
  .ddx/beads.jsonl with the version committed in the landed worktree.
  That committed version is a snapshot from when the agent claimed
  the bead — not the authoritative current state. Any operator
  writes to beads.jsonl that happened after the claim (e.g.,
  `ddx bead update <id> --set queue-rank=N`, dep edits, priority
  bumps) are silently reverted on the next land.

  Observed: I set queue-rank on ~12 beads via `ddx bead update --set
  queue-rank=N`. Later, after the drain landed unrelated beads, all
  queue-rank values had vanished (parseQueueRank returned empty).
  The drain reverted to default sort (priority + creation date),
  ignoring my prioritization.

  This is a silent data loss. No error is surfaced. The operator's
  intended ordering simply stops working after the next land.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:384 RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead
    delegates to syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:429
    syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths runs `git checkout-index -f` for
    every file in the fromRev..HEAD diff, except those listed in
    skipPaths (today: only files dirty-before-land are skipped via
    dirtyBefore guard).
  - .ddx/beads.jsonl is committed inside the landing worktree as
    part of agent evidence (claim, heartbeat, close events). It
    therefore appears in the fromRev..HEAD diff at land time.
  - The dirty-file guard at land time does NOT save it: if the
    operator's `--set` write was already committed via
    beadAutoCommit (which the agent itself runs to checkpoint
    tracker state), the file appears "clean" at land time → not
    skipped → got overwritten with the older landed version.
  - Same bug applies to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl by the same
    mechanism.

  No code path in cli/internal/bead/ explicitly clears queue-rank;
  the marshal round-trip preserves unknown Extra keys correctly.
  This is purely a git-overwrite issue at the agent/land seam.

PROPOSED FIX
  Treat .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl as live-state
  files managed by the bead.Store lock — never authoritative from a
  landed commit. They must be UNCONDITIONALLY skipped by
  SyncWorkTreeToHead, regardless of dirty-before-land state.

  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead
    (line 384 area): change call signature to pass an unconditional
    skip list:
        return syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev, []string{
            ".ddx/beads.jsonl",
            ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl",
        })
    Or: introduce a new constant
        var alwaysSkipSyncPaths = []string{".ddx/beads.jsonl", ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl"}
    and merge with the existing dirtyBefore set inside
    syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths.
  - Document at the call site why these paths are special: they are
    governed by bead.Store WithLock; the committed worktree version
    is a stale snapshot.
  - Add the regression test described in AC.

  ADDITIONALLY, add comprehensive lifecycle preservation tests for
  queue-rank to cover the full set of transitions that operators
  expect to be safe (zero tests exist today for this).

NON-SCOPE
  - Refactoring SyncWorkTreeToHead beyond the always-skip list.
  - Other "live state" files in .ddx/ that might have similar
    issues (executions/, attachments/) — those are append-only or
    per-attempt and don't share the snapshot problem; only the
    JSONL stores do.
  - The network-free Land() refactor (sister bead ddx-1f774edf will
    rewrite this file; this fix should land first or be merged into
    that work).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (network-free Land code) will refactor
    execute_bead_land.go heavily. This bead's fix should land first
    so subsequent drain prioritization is reliable; otherwise
    ddx-1f774edf's own queue-rank may be wiped before it gets
    picked up.
title: fix(agent/land): SyncWorkTreeToHead clobbers .ddx/beads.jsonl, wiping live state

description:
PROBLEM
The parent Fizeau-boundary ratification bead mixes durable policy docs with source FEAT docs. ADR-024 and docs/migrations/routing-config.md need their own focused audit so DDx retry/review policy and configuration migration both say the same thing: DDx may pass operator constraints through, but Fizeau owns concrete route selection.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:26-41 says Fizeau owns harness/provider/model routing and DDx changes only MinPower/request facts.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:69-79 says `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are sticky passthrough constraints and DDx must not substitute or widen them.
- docs/migrations/routing-config.md:5-17 says old `agent.routing.*` defaults are retired and DDx execution config is distinct from Fizeau routing config.
- The parent bead requires these docs to reject DDx-owned normalization, fuzzy matching, fallback routing, and compatibility aliases.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit ADR-024 for any remaining desired-state language where DDx chooses concrete provider/model/harness routes rather than setting power bounds and passing request facts to Fizeau.
- Audit docs/migrations/routing-config.md so removed `agent.*` keys and the DDx-execution/Fizeau-routing split are explicit enough for an operator migrating config.
- Add one short cross-reference between the two docs if needed so policy and migration instructions are not divergent.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit FEAT source docs here; sibling ddx-e2586179 owns PRD/FEAT alignment.
- Do not edit TP-020; a sibling child owns the test-plan surface.
- Do not change config parser behavior.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-424fdac4. No deps; this is one decomposition child of the broad Fizeau-boundary ratification bead.

description:
Today reviewer defaults to implementer harness (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:521) — directly violating R4 (different reviewer). Build the reviewer ExecuteRequest with: Role='reviewer'; SAME CorrelationID as the implementer call (joins them in events / session log / aggregations); MinPower = implementer.RoutingActual.Power + 1.

'Different provider' Day 1 is best-effort emergent — the MinPower bump usually forces Fizeau to pick a different candidate, but not guaranteed. When same-provider results, log a 'review-pairing-degraded' event with implementer + reviewer routing details so operators can see degradation.

description:
PROBLEM
When a pre-land reviewer finds a fixable implementation or test gap, DDx should repair in the same still-live attempt worktree instead of landing a rejected candidate or starting from a cold worktree. No same-worktree repair loop exists today.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:233-244 records REQUEST_CHANGES as a non-approved review outcome but does not launch an implementer repair pass.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1481-1487 includes a review gate prompt block, but the normal implementation prompt also says commit exactly once at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1520-1523 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1550-1555; repair needs a distinct prompt mode that permits one append-only repair commit.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1123 counts review outcomes but has no loop that appends repair commits and re-runs checks/review before final land.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a RepairPass that runs in the same attempt worktree when ReviewFindingClassifier returns review_fixable_gap.
- Build a repair prompt that includes prior review findings, per-AC failures, failed candidate_rev, base_rev, required verification commands, and explicit append-only commit rules.
- Permit one repair commit per repair cycle while forbidding reset, amend, squash, rebase, or history rewrite.
- After each repair commit, pin a new candidate ref, re-run checks, re-run review against base_rev..new_candidate_rev, and stop on approval, manual class, repair_max_cycles, no-progress, provider/cost budget, or context cancellation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not enable two-slot review; this uses the transitional single-slot review path.
- Do not implement concrete model fallback; routing power floors are a separate bead.

DEPS
Depends on classification so only review_fixable_gap enters repair.
title: repair: run same-worktree append-only repair cycles

description:
PROBLEM
BLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement. In the coordinator topology (Story 14), the originating peer identity attestation is not forwarded from coordinator to client — any non-localhost write goes through the hub without carrying who initiated it. The spoke cannot authorize on both origin and coordinator identity because neither is transmitted.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: current federation supports query fan-out only. No RouteMutationToProjectOwner, no ForwardMutation, no identity header propagation.
- Risk ('Multi-node authorization gap'): A compromised or misconfigured hub can forward mutations on behalf of any origin, with the spoke unable to distinguish legitimate coordinator-forwarded requests from hub-originated attacks. Without dual authorization, spoke trusts the hub implicitly.
- The /tmp/story-15-final.md plan file cited in the original bead is ephemeral and not available to sub-agents on other machines; relevant content is inlined below.

PLAN-LOCKED DECISIONS (from story-15 planning):
- Multi-node routing (point 6): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations.
- Identity forwarding: X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified WhoIs peer at coordinator edge (NOT coordinator self); X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Both mandatory on forwarded mutations.
- Dual authorization on spoke: requireTrusted augmented to check BOTH origin (WhoIs / loopback policy) AND coordinator (registered federation hub); 403 if either fails.

This bead has been decomposed into 4 children:
- ddx-468d800f — S15-7a: ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers.
- ddx-eb75a32d — S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation).
- ddx-ba31fec8 — S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization.
- ddx-ea1ada47 — S15-7d: Integration test with two in-process servers.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT spec; changes go into FEAT-010 and FEAT-011 extensions.
- Performance optimization of federation routing.
- Story 14 query fan-out behavior (already shipped in fanout.go).

title: S15-7: Multi-node trust attestation forwarding (Story-14 coupling)

acceptance:
1. TestApplyLandResult_PreservesImplementationRevAndSetsLandedRev proves a successful land keeps the original implementation/candidate revision and records the target branch tip separately.
2. TestBuildLandRequest_UsesImplementationRevNotEvidenceRev proves LandRequest.ResultRev is built from the candidate/implementation revision even after evidence or landed fields are populated.
3. TestExecutionBundle_ExposesRevisionTriplet proves manifest/result loading exposes implementation/candidate, landed, and evidence revisions while preserving `result_rev` compatibility.
4. TestWorkerReport_DistinguishesCandidateAndLandedRev proves server worker state and operator output no longer collapse candidate and landed revision into one field.
5. TestNoChangesRevisionSemantics_PreservesBaseRev verifies no-change attempts do not fabricate landed/evidence revisions and keep base/candidate semantics clear.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run "TestApplyLandResult_PreservesImplementationRevAndSetsLandedRev|TestBuildLandRequest_UsesImplementationRevNotEvidenceRev|TestExecutionBundle_ExposesRevisionTriplet|TestWorkerReport_DistinguishesCandidateAndLandedRev|TestNoChangesRevisionSemantics_PreservesBaseRev" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ExecuteBeadResult.ResultRev` is overloaded. Depending on where the caller observes it, the field can mean the worker implementation commit, a merge commit, or the trailing evidence commit. That makes candidate review, landing, replay, server state, and UI drill-down ambiguous.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:26-37 defines `ExecuteBeadResult` with only `BaseRev` and `ResultRev`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1549-1584 mutates `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`, replacing the original worker result revision with the target branch tip after fast-forward or merge.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1614-1623 builds `LandRequest.ResultRev` from `res.ResultRev`, so callers must know whether the field still names the candidate commit or has already been rewritten.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:756-792 uses `ResultRev` both to decide whether to land and to report the final worker result.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:979-992 persists `WorkerExecutionResult.ResultRev` without distinguishing candidate, landed, or evidence revisions.
- cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:23-75 and 175-182 expose only `result_rev` in execution manifests/results.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add explicit fields for the revision roles, for example `ImplementationRev` or `CandidateRev`, `LandedRev`, and `EvidenceRev`.
- Define `ResultRev` as a backwards-compatible alias during migration only; document which field it mirrors and stop using it for new internal decisions.
- LandRequest construction must use the implementation/candidate revision, never an evidence or already-landed revision.
- ApplyLandResult must preserve the implementation/candidate revision and set landed/evidence fields separately.
- Server worker reports, state persistence, execution manifests, and UI/query loaders must expose the revision triplet so operators can trace implementation, branch landing, and evidence publication independently.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change merge strategy, candidate review policy, or the execute-bead evidence file format beyond additive fields.
- Do not rewrite existing historical execution bundles.
- Do not remove `result_rev` until a later compatibility cleanup.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-31e07fdc. Depends on ddx-7cb3153d so the new semantics line up with candidate refs instead of preserving the ambiguous raw worktree SHA model.
