acceptance:
1. ExecuteLoopSpec struct defined in cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go. Exported fields include at minimum: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion. Audit: any field present in cobra extraction (agent_cmd.go:1582-1605) or workers.go:25-49 not in this list is a bug.

2. The unified spec does not contain a PollInterval field, a poll_interval JSON tag, or any public compatibility field for the removed polling flag. New CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.

3. parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) exists in the new package. Called only from cobra RunE in agent_cmd.go. No parallel cobra-extraction block remains. DispatchOptions holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) and is not persisted to spec.json.

4. parseExecuteLoopSpec maps --once to Mode=once, maps --watch to Mode=watch, defaults to Mode=drain, maps --idle-interval to IdleInterval only for Mode=watch, and returns a usage error when --idle-interval is provided without --watch.

5. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods exist. Lifecycle enforced: (a) client calls Validate() before HTTP submit or inline dispatch; (b) server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults() then Validate() after decode, before StartExecuteLoop; (c) spec.json on disk is fully resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran at write time).

6. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2390) decodes directly into ExecuteLoopSpec; the anonymous request struct (currently lines 2395-2409) is deleted. Mode, IdleInterval, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, and FromRev survive the REST round-trip.

7. graphql_adapters.go anonymous request struct (lines 63-76) and its parallel StartExecuteLoop literal (lines 127-141) are deleted. GraphQL dispatch goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.

8. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is replaced by or aliased to ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json files written by StartExecuteLoop use the unified struct and emit Mode plus IdleInterval.

9. runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD as the cost-cap ceiling, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD fallback when zero. No hardcoded DefaultMaxCostUSD reference outside the fallback case.

10. OpaquePassthrough: server-submitted spec with OpaquePassthrough=true skips both validation gates - workers.go:357-361 (StartExecuteLoop pre-flight) and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch inside runWorker. TestStartExecuteLoop_OpaquePassthrough_SkipsValidation asserts that StartExecuteLoop with OpaquePassthrough=true does not call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService and returns a WorkerRecord without error even when the harness name is unresolvable.

11. Reflection round-trip test: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection in cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or executeloop sub-package. Uses reflect to iterate exported fields of ExecuteLoopSpec; for each field sets a non-zero sentinel value; marshals to JSON; unmarshals; asserts equality. Test fails if any exported field has a zero-value sentinel after unmarshal. A future added field automatically falls under coverage.

12. Backward compatibility for disk only: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ load without error. Files containing unknown legacy fields are tolerated and ignored by json.Unmarshal default behavior. Do not enable json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads.

13. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field present. Server returns HTTP 400 with a capability description when incoming SpecVersion > server-supported version. SpecVersion==0 (unset) is treated as the current version. TestHandleStartExecuteLoopWorker_HighSpecVersion_Returns400 covers this.

14. Regression: inline execution path behavior is unchanged except for the explicit once/drain/watch mapping. Existing tests that cover the inline path pass without modification.

15. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green.

16. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ExecuteLoopSpec fields are declared in parallel structs across three independent code sites. When a flag or worker mode is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. The new work-loop contract requires one vocabulary across CLI parsing, worker persistence, REST, and GraphQL: mode plus idle_interval, with no public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag.

ROOT CAUSE
Each execute-loop field traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:

1. cobra flag extraction plus local vars: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once/watch mode, idleInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, and maxPower as naked locals.

2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 closes over these locals and assembles config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them.

3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition: cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is hand-maintained in parallel with cobra and has historically missed fields.

4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct: cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is a separate anonymous struct, so newly added fields can be silently lost for server-spawned workers.

5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 plus cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 hand-assembles the worker spec; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here.

6. GraphQL adapter request struct plus StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141 is a third parallel declaration; fixes to REST can leave GraphQL broken.

The consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has two validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).

PROPOSED FIX
- cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go: define ExecuteLoopSpec with all fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.
- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. Default is Mode=drain.
- IdleInterval is meaningful only for Mode=watch. --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error in the cobra layer and must not be represented as a separate polling mode in the persisted spec.
- Do not add, accept, document, or emit a public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag. Historical worker records may be read best-effort as unknown/legacy input, but new CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.
- Add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults(). Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) as the single cobra-to-spec conversion.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as REST.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD, with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero.
- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec_test.go: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. New field = automatic coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7) owns replacing runWorker with exec ddx work; this bead does not change the subprocess vs in-process dispatch decision.
- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, MCP agent dispatch) may have the same smell; covered by sister bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string is owned by ddx-9d55601f; keep LabelFilter as string.

SEQUENCING
ddx-29058e2a (this bead) vs ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7 - server-spawn migration to exec ddx work): lands independently and before ddx-eccc6efb. ADR-022 says ddx-29058e2a remains valuable as worker-side struct unification (cobra to in-process flow) and should land independently. When ddx-eccc6efb lands later, it will delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec from workers.go as part of the exec migration; the unified ExecuteLoopSpec should already live in the durable agent package. If ddx-eccc6efb lands first, the scope of this bead narrows to the cobra and worker-facing layers that still exist.

{"attempt_id":"20260503T110552-7129c1c3","harness":"claude","input_tokens":17,"output_tokens":8486,"total_tokens":8503,"cost_usd":0.86102725,"duration_ms":206193,"exit_code":0}

Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits.

## Summary

The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.

These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:
  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or
  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.

The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.

## Evidence

1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).
2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.
3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).
4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.
5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.

## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)

The bead also fails the size check on its own:

- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).
- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.
- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages.
- Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE.

## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt

The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:

  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.
  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.
  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.

Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.

## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage

1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.
2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).
3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.
4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.

## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)

- No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice.
- No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above.
- No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

acceptance:
1. `TestBeadLifecycleSkillReadinessDocumentsRewriteContract` or equivalent verifies `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` documents exact readiness classifications, `rewrite.changed_fields`, `rewrite.description`, `rewrite.acceptance`, and the advisory-vs-machine distinction from `suggested_fixes`.
2. The same test or a paired assertion verifies `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` contains the same readiness rewrite/classification contract.
3. `TestBeadLifecycleSkillLintDocumentsRationaleShape` or equivalent verifies both skill copies document lint-mode `rationale` in the shape expected by `LintResult` and do not imply array/string interchangeability across modes.
4. `TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesDocumentedNeedsRefineRewrite` or equivalent verifies a `classification=needs_refine` readiness payload with `rewrite.changed_fields=["description","acceptance"]`, string `description`, and string `acceptance` decodes to `actionable_but_rewritten`.
5. `TestPreClaimReadiness_RejectsUnsupportedClassification` or equivalent verifies observed unsupported classifications such as `safely_refinable` fail with a clear error that lists the accepted classification values.
6. `TestPreClaimReadiness_RejectsArrayAcceptanceRewrite` or equivalent verifies malformed array-shaped `rewrite.acceptance` fails with a clear decode error.
7. `TestLintHook_RejectsMalformedRationaleShape` or equivalent verifies array/string rationale drift is caught with a clear lint-hook error.
8. `ddx skills check .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle` passes.
9. `make skill-schema` is run if the skill schema/generated artifacts require it after editing `SKILL.md`.
10. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestBeadLifecycleSkill.*|TestPreClaimReadiness_.*|TestLintHook_.*Rationale" -count=1` passes.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The bead-lifecycle readiness/lint skill documents advisory fields such as `suggested_fixes`, but it does not fully document the JSON contracts that DDx lifecycle hooks enforce. Agents following only the skill can emit unsupported classifications, array-shaped fields where strings are required, or omit the machine-consumable rewrite evidence. The result is repeated lifecycle hook decode warnings, lost safe refinements, and workers continuing only because DDx fail-opens unavailable hooks.

LIVE EVIDENCE
- `ddx-cf8fb41c` worker log: `readiness check unavailable: decode result: json: cannot unmarshal array into Go struct field preClaimIntakePromptRewrite.rewrite.acceptance of type string`.
- `ddx-ef9e8aec` worker log: `readiness check unavailable: unknown classification "safely_refinable"`.
- `ddx-ef9e8aec` worker log: `lint hook: bad-json: json: cannot unmarshal array into Go struct field LintResult.rationale of type string`.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:73-118` defines the READINESS MODE JSON shape with `suggested_fixes`, `suggested_child_beads`, and `waivers_applied`, but no `rewrite` object or `rewrite.changed_fields` guidance.
- `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:73-118` has the same missing readiness rewrite schema text.
- `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:156-188` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:156-188` document lint output but do not stress that `rationale` is an array of objects only in lint mode, while readiness `rationale` is a string and hook parser fields must match mode-specific schema exactly.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go:72-76` defines `PreClaimIntakeRewrite` with string `description`, string `acceptance`, and `changed_fields`.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:399-424` accepts only known readiness classifications and promotes `classification=needs_refine` to `actionable_but_rewritten` only when a valid rewrite is present.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update both shipped bead-lifecycle skill copies to document the exact mode-specific JSON contracts for readiness and lint.
- State that readiness classification values are exactly `ready`, `needs_refine`, `needs_split`, `operator_required`, or `system_unready`; do not emit synonyms such as `safely_refinable`.
- State that readiness `rewrite.changed_fields` is required when proposing `needs_refine` as an intent-preserving replacement rewrite, and that `rewrite.description` and `rewrite.acceptance` are strings, not arrays.
- Clarify the distinction: `suggested_fixes` is advisory diagnostic output; `rewrite` is the machine-consumable replacement contract DDx may apply before claim.
- Clarify mode-specific `rationale` shape so lint output and readiness output do not cross-contaminate each other.
- Add or update tests that verify the skill prompt/schema text contains these contracts and that parsers accept the documented shape and reject the observed malformed shapes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change bead lifecycle state transitions.
- Do not weaken operator-required handling for rejected or lossy rewrites.
- Do not change DDx-side routing or Fizeau integration.

DEPS
No deps.

1. .ddx/config.yaml documents bead_tracker.backend: axon plus the Axon connection block.
2. TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL, TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig, and TestNewStore_PreservesExternalBackends are added or updated.

description:
PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.

acceptance:
1. GraphQL schema (cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql):
   - Bead.status doc lists all six values: proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, cancelled.
   - BeadStatusCounts has these Int! fields exactly: open, inProgress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled, total, ready, workerReady, dependencyWaiting, externalBlocked, operatorAttention. Field doc strings describe the lifecycle/derivation.
   - The needsHuman, needsHumanReason, needsHumanSuggestedAction, needsHumanSummary fields are REMOVED from the Bead type (no aliases).
   - The beadsNeedsHuman query and the BeadStatusCounts.needsHuman field are REMOVED (replaced by beadsByStatus(status: "proposed") or operatorAttention count).
   - The beadHumanResolve mutation and HumanResolveAction enum are REMOVED.

2. New mutations replace beadHumanResolve:
   - beadApprove(id: ID!, note: String!): Bead!  -- proposed→open via TransitionLifecycle with ManualReopen=true; appends a human-resolution event (kind=human-resolution, summary=approve, body=note); empty note rejected.
   - beadCancel(id: ID!, reason: String!): Bead! -- (open|in_progress|blocked|proposed)→cancelled via TransitionLifecycle; appends a human-resolution event; empty reason rejected.
   - beadBlock(id: ID!, externalBlockerReason: String!): Bead! -- (open|in_progress|proposed)→blocked via TransitionLifecycle with ExternalBlockerReason set; empty reason rejected.
   - Existing beadClose(id, reason) routes through TransitionLifecycle with ManualClose=true and accepts proposed→closed.

3. REST/MCP status filter and update:
   - cli/internal/server/server.go MCP ddx_bead_list status property description enumerates all six states.
   - ddx_bead_update status property description enumerates all six states; the handler routes status writes through the lifecycle transition API (TransitionLifecycle), not direct field writes; transitions that require OperatorRequired/ExternalBlockerReason fail with a clear error when those fields are missing.
   - GET /api/beads?status=<x> accepts all six values.

4. Blocked vs dependency-waiting:
   - GET /api/beads/blocked and ddx_bead_blocked return ONLY beads in the external-blocker bucket (status=blocked with externalBlockerReason set), NOT dependency-waiting open beads.
   - A new GET /api/beads/dependency-waiting and ddx_bead_dependency_waiting tool return open/in_progress beads with unmet dependencies (the prior /api/beads/blocked semantics).
   - GraphQL beadsBlocked semantics align with the REST endpoint (external blocker only); the dependency-waiting bucket is exposed via beadsDependencyWaiting (new query) returning a BeadConnection.

5. Tests REMOVED:
   - TestGraphQLBeadsNeedsHumanQuery (cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads_needs_human_test.go)
   - TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolveRetry, TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolveRequiresNote
   - TestGraphQLBeadStatusCountsNeedsHuman
   - The whole resolver_beads_needs_human_test.go file may be deleted; existing TestGraphQLBeadsReadyAndBlockedUseLifecycleBuckets must be preserved (move to a new file if needed) and updated so its blocked branch asserts external-blocker semantics, not dependency waiting.

6. Tests ADDED (or replacing the above):
   - TestGraphQLBeadStatusCountsLifecycleStates (cli/internal/server/graphql/): seeds two beads in each of open/in_progress/closed/blocked(externalBlockerReason)/proposed/cancelled, plus one open with an unclosed dep and one open in-flight; asserts every Int! field matches store.Status() result exactly and verifies operatorAttention==proposed and dependencyWaiting>0.
   - TestGraphQLBeadProposedApprove: creates a proposed bead, calls beadApprove with a note, asserts status=open and a human-resolution(approve) event appended.
   - TestGraphQLBeadProposedApproveRejectsEmptyNote.
   - TestGraphQLBeadCancelTerminal: cancels an open bead, asserts cancelled status, terminal (next transitions rejected), and dependents are NOT marked closed.
   - TestGraphQLBeadBlockExternal: blocks an open bead with reason, asserts status=blocked and externalBlockerReason persisted; and that the bead now appears in beadsBlocked but NOT beadsDependencyWaiting.
   - TestGraphQLBeadBlockRequiresReason.
   - TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolveRemoved: introspection or direct query for beadHumanResolve / HumanResolveAction / beadsNeedsHuman returns a schema error.
   - TestMCPBeadStatusSchemasIncludeSixLifecycleStatuses (cli/internal/server/server_test.go): inspects the MCP tools/list response and asserts the descriptions of ddx_bead_list and ddx_bead_update enumerate all six statuses (\"open\", \"in_progress\", \"closed\", \"blocked\", \"proposed\", \"cancelled\").
   - TestBeadsBlocked updated: seeds an external-blocked bead and a dependency-waiting bead; GET /api/beads/blocked returns ONLY the external one; GET /api/beads/dependency-waiting returns ONLY the dep-waiting one.
   - TestMCPBeadBlocked updated: ddx_bead_blocked returns only external-blocked; new TestMCPBeadDependencyWaiting covers ddx_bead_dependency_waiting.

7. cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/graphql is regenerated (houdini codegen) so the tracked artifacts match the new schema; keep changes minimal — anything that becomes a compile error in frontend code is out of scope for this bead and should fail fast (separate frontend bead ddx-9ca8c995 follows).

8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestGraphQLBead.*Proposed|TestGraphQLBeadStatusCountsLifecycleStates|TestGraphQLBeadCancelTerminal|TestGraphQLBeadBlock|TestGraphQLBeadHumanResolveRemoved|TestMCPBeadStatusSchemasIncludeSixLifecycleStatuses|TestBeadsBlocked|TestMCPBeadBlocked|TestMCPBeadDependencyWaiting' -count=1 passes.

9. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/bead/... passes.

10. rg -n 'beadHumanResolve|HumanResolveAction|beadsNeedsHuman|BeadStatusCounts.*needsHuman' cli/internal/server returns no canonical (non-test, non-migration) references; tests asserting removal are exempt.

11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Server, REST/MCP, and GraphQL surfaces expose needsHuman as if it were a lifecycle lane and use blocked endpoints for dependency waits. That will keep external clients and the web UI tied to old semantics unless the API contract is updated with the state machine.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:100-140 documents Bead status without proposed/cancelled and includes needsHuman fields.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:177-192 defines BeadStatusCounts.needsHuman and says workerReady excludes needs_human/needs_investigation.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:114-125 computes NeedsHuman from labels.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:167-238 exposes BeadsNeedsHuman as open plus label.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:228-273 implements beadHumanResolve by removing needs_human labels or closing.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:3181 and cli/internal/server/server.go:3418 advertise only open/in_progress/closed status filters/updates in MCP schemas.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:3220-3222 describes ddx_bead_blocked as open beads with unmet dependencies.

PROPOSED FIX
Make API contracts use the six-status lifecycle and derived buckets. Remove needsHuman fields/queries/mutations from the canonical schema rather than keeping long-lived aliases. REST/MCP and GraphQL status filters/updates must accept proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled, and status mutations must call the transition API. Blocked endpoints must mean external recheckable blockers; dependency-waiting is a distinct derived bucket. Old clients fail clearly after the one-way migration instead of receiving label-based compatibility behavior.

NON-SCOPE
Do not update Svelte UI in this bead except generated schema/types required by the server contract. Frontend has a separate bead.

DEPS
Depends on store/query wiring and migration semantics.
title: server: expose proposed lifecycle over API and GraphQL

acceptance:
AC1. ddx try/ddx work treats success outcome and bead-state propagation as a single recoverable unit: if the finalize step fails after outcome=task_succeeded is written, the next pick of the same bead detects the dangling result_rev and either lands it or surfaces a structured error (not silent no_evidence_produced).
AC2. A regression test exercises the dangling-success scenario by simulating a finalize failure and asserting the bead lands cleanly on retry.
AC3. ddx doctor (or a new diagnostic command) detects executions where result.json claims success but result_rev is unreachable from any branch, and reports them with the recovery command (git merge --no-ff <sha> + ddx bead close --commit <sha> --session <sid>).
AC4. Documentation in the ddx skill or work.md describes this failure mode and how operators can recover manually until AC1 ships.
description:
PROBLEM: A ddx try/ddx work attempt that produces outcome=task_succeeded and a clean result_rev may fail to auto-merge or update the bead state, leaving the success commit unreachable from any branch and the bead stuck in_progress. Operators must manually recover by checking out the dangling SHA, verifying ACs, and merging.

REPRO (observed in axon project, 2026-05-08):
- Bead axon-d323acc9 ("refactor(axon-schema): import named-query parser from axon-cypher-ast")
- Attempt 20260508T190516-8d61a244, session eb-b068c4db
- result.json fields: outcome=task_succeeded, status=success, result_rev=c3386193ab1c18fdf5739901c41d2e69093f1030, base_rev=9194da47097b2d0d05899a51688950084b3995b8, duration_ms=1179708, exit_code=0
- Master HEAD never advanced to include c3386193; bead remained in_progress until manual recovery
- A subsequent attempt 20260508T192511-c4a200de fired 15 seconds later and recorded outcome=task_no_evidence with detail "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt", which suggests the worker re-ran and saw no work to do (the success was already in the dangling commit) but did not connect that to the prior success record.

CURRENT STATE:
- Both attempt result.json files are present and parsable.
- Recovery required: git merge --no-ff <result_rev> + ddx bead close --commit <result_rev> --session <session_id>.
- No evidence in the bead events indicated the attempt had succeeded; the success was discovered by inspecting result.json on disk.

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION (owned by ddx-loop/finalize maintainer):
1. The post-execute finalize step (merge + bead update with success event) should be transactional with attempt outcome, or at minimum idempotent so a follow-up ddx work cycle can complete it.
2. On bead pick, if status=in_progress and the most-recent attempt outcome=success but no merge commit references the result_rev, attempt to land that result before claiming new work or starting a fresh attempt.
3. A startup self-check could enumerate result.json files where outcome=task_succeeded and ensure the corresponding result_rev is reachable from a branch; surface inconsistencies as recoverable state.

NON-SCOPE: defining the merge strategy (no-ff vs ff-only is already settled by skill guidance); changing result.json schema.
title: bug: ddx try success outcome leaves dangling commit when finalize step fails

acceptance:
1. .ddx/executions/<run-id>/tmp-leak-investigation.md exists, listing every confirmed /tmp write surface from exec.Command subprocesses (or explicitly stating "no additional leaks confirmed").
2. The report names the instrumentation method used (strace, exec wrapper, fs-watcher, etc.) and the test packages exercised.
3. Each confirmed-leak finding either has a follow-on bead filed (id captured in the report) or is fixed inline if trivial (one-liner t.TempDir() swap).
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  The 2026-05-09 test-cleanup audit identified two follow-up investigations
  it could not complete with read-only static analysis. Both relate to
  potential /tmp leak surfaces that may or may not be real:

    1. Tests that spawn exec.Command subprocesses passing hardcoded /tmp
       paths as args (e.g. cli/cmd/agent_test.go:150 area). Static analysis
       confirmed the paths are struct-literal usage; a runtime check is
       needed to confirm none of those subprocesses actually write files
       at those paths.
    2. The testLibraryOnce race window — concurrent test packages calling
       GetTestLibraryPath simultaneously from different test binaries.
       This is largely subsumed by the sister bead that removes the
       sync.Once shared-fixture pattern entirely, but should be verified
       once that bead lands.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/cmd/agent_test.go:150 (and similar sites) pass /tmp-rooted args to
    exec.Command. Static read of the call sites suggests they're
    placeholders, but a dynamic test would confirm.
  - The sister bead "tests: replace shared /tmp/.test-library fixture with
    t.TempDir-scoped repo" addresses the architectural fix that eliminates
    the race window. Verification that no race-condition leak remained
    pre-fix is a separate confirmation step.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Run cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run '.*' under strace or fs-watcher
    instrumentation (or instrument with a small exec.LookPath wrapper that
    records subprocess argv) and capture every /tmp path written by a
    test binary or its children.
  - For each confirmed leak surface (if any), file a follow-on bead with
    the specific file:line and a t.TempDir() fix.
  - For the testLibraryOnce race: after the shared-fixture bead lands,
    grep cli/cmd for any residual sync.Once-around-fs-state pattern;
    document that none remain or file a follow-on if any are found.
  - This bead's deliverable is a short investigation report under
    .ddx/executions/<run-id>/tmp-leak-investigation.md naming each
    confirmed leak (or stating "no additional leaks confirmed").

NON-SCOPE
  - Fixing newly-discovered leaks (file a follow-on bead per finding).
  - Investigating non-Go tests or frontend tests.
  - Re-running the original static audit.
title: tests: investigate exec.Command subprocesses with hardcoded /tmp paths

2. TestAutoCommit_TargetOnlyPreservesUnrelatedStagedChanges verifies AutoCommit commits only the requested file and leaves unrelated staged changes staged.
3. .ddx/config.yaml contains git.auto_commit: always and git.commit_prefix: chore for this repository.

description:
PROBLEM
Tasks are still able to leave .ddx/beads.jsonl dirty even after tracker mutations. The pre-commit guard catches later commits that would leave tracker dirt behind, but it cannot catch a task that mutates the tracker and exits before another commit. This repository also does not currently opt into git.auto_commit, so bead-mutating commands only change the JSONL file unless the operator remembers to commit it manually.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- .ddx/config.yaml:1-30 has no git.auto_commit setting, so cli/cmd/bead.go:78-97 returns without committing tracker mutations by default.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:74-97 documents that beadAutoCommit errors are silently ignored and drops the error returned by internal/git.AutoCommit.
- cli/internal/git/autocommit.go:58-72 stages the target file and runs git commit without path limiting, so if auto-commit is enabled it can include unrelated staged changes instead of being a tracker-only safety action.

PROPOSED FIX
- Set this repo's .ddx/config.yaml git.auto_commit to always with a chore commit prefix so bead commands create tracker commits by default.
- Change beadAutoCommit to return errors and make mutating bead commands fail loudly when the configured tracker auto-commit fails.
- Restrict AutoCommit's git commit to the target file so auto-commit cannot accidentally include unrelated staged work.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the pre-commit beads-tracker-health guard; it remains the fallback for manual commits.
- Do not require all .ddx runtime/evidence artifacts to be auto-committed.
- Do not change the bead storage format.
title: bead: hard-fail tracker auto-commit misses

acceptance:
1. The root cause of the 60s wall clock is identified and documented in the bead's commit message (one of: kill path not firing, process-group cleanup, reader-goroutine block, or other).
2. The fix is applied to cli/internal/agent/agent.go (or wherever OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir lives) so the test completes in well under 15 seconds wall clock.
3. TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel in cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go:1012 contains an assertion that the test body completes in under 15 seconds (require.Less on time.Since(start)).
4. cd cli && go test -count=1 -run TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel ./internal/agent/... completes in under 15s wall clock (verified by an external `time` measurement or test elapsed-time output).
5. result.EarlyCancel == true and result.ExitCode == -1 still hold.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel at cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go:1012
  spawns `sleep 60` with a 10-second context timeout and expects the
  early-cancel path to fire when the context expires. In the most recent
  audit run the test ran for 60.0 seconds, not the expected ~10 seconds.
  Either (a) the context cancel is not propagating to the subprocess kill
  signal, (b) the kill is firing but process-group cleanup waits for the
  full sleep, or (c) the early-cancel branch is wired up correctly but a
  separate wait loop is blocking the test goroutine until natural exit.
  Whichever the root cause, this is a real correctness gap: production
  agent execution relies on context cancellation killing runaway
  subprocesses promptly.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go:1012 TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel
    invokes OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir with a 10s ctx timeout against
    `sleep 60`.
  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go:1019-1023 asserts
    result.EarlyCancel == true and result.ExitCode == -1.
  - The test PASSES (assertions are met) but takes ~60s wall-clock,
    suggesting one of:
      a. OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir's kill path is not signalling SIGKILL
         to the subprocess on context cancel.
      b. The kill signal fires but cmd.Wait() blocks until the OS reaps
         the process tree (no setsid, no -PGID kill).
      c. The function returns at 10s but the test goroutine is held by a
         separate mechanism (e.g. captured stdout reader not closing).
  - Read OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir at cli/internal/agent/agent.go (or
    wherever ExecuteInDir is defined) to determine which.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Investigate OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir; identify which of (a), (b), (c)
    explains the 60s wall-clock.
  - For each plausible cause:
      a. Add explicit kill on context cancel: when ctx.Done() fires before
         cmd.Wait() returns, send syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, SIGKILL)
         (requires cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}
         on Unix).
      b. Use process-group kill so descendants of the spawned subprocess
         also die.
      c. Close stdout/stderr pipes when ctx fires so any reader goroutines
         unblock.
  - Add explicit assertion that the test completes in under 15s wall
    clock: wrap test body with start := time.Now(); … ; require.Less(
    time.Since(start), 15*time.Second, "TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel must
    not run longer than 15s").
  - Verify on Linux and macOS (cli is multi-platform per Makefile).

NON-SCOPE
  - General refactor of OSExecutor; only the early-cancel path.
  - Replacing OSExecutor with a different executor implementation.
  - Adding testing.Short() skip — the test SHOULD complete fast; if it
    doesn't, that's a bug. The fix is to make it fast, not to skip it.

INTERSECTIONS
  - No deps.
title: fix(agent): TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel should cancel in <=10s not 60s

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedStopsLoop creates two ready beads and an executor returning resource_exhausted for the first; verifies the second bead is never claimed or executed.
2. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedUnclaimsAndNoCooldown verifies any claimed bead is open/re-claimable and no execute-loop-retry-after/no-progress cooldown is written.
3. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedLoopEndEvent verifies loop.end includes exit_reason=resource_exhausted and cleanup/resource detail is emitted.
4. TestWorkResourceExhausted_PrintsOperatorMessage verifies the ddx work command output includes resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop or the finalized exact message.
5. Resource exhaustion remains distinct from review transport/quota and provider rate-limit retry in status/detail handling.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhausted|TestWorkResourceExhausted" -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ddx work currently continues scanning and claiming ready beads after setup/infrastructure failures. During the May 6 ENOSPC incident, ddx work failed to create one isolated worktree, then immediately claimed another production-reachability bead and failed again. Resource exhaustion must stop the drain because continuing expands the blast radius.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 initializes loop events and exitReason but does not include resource_exhausted as a recognized loop-fatal exit reason.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:529 onward continues iterating after non-success reports unless existing retry/no-progress/once logic stops it; there is no host-resource circuit breaker.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:263-283 defines status taxonomy around execution_failed/no_evidence/etc., but there is no distinct resource_exhausted status/reason for loop policy to inspect.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:434-440 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:281-284 require ddx work to stop claiming new beads after resource_exhausted.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a stable resource_exhausted attempt/loop classification that can be returned by ddx try/resource preflight and recognized by ExecuteBeadWorker.
- On resource_exhausted, release any non-terminal claim, emit cleanup/resource events, set loop.end exit_reason=resource_exhausted, and return without scanning the next ready bead.
- Print an operator-visible message such as resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop.
- Ensure resource_exhausted is not counted as model no-progress, capability retry, or review/quota retry.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the resource checker itself here; this bead consumes the outcome from the try/resource-preflight bead.
- Do not change queue ranking or production-reachability bead priority.
- Do not change provider 429 in-attempt retry behavior.

acceptance:
1. TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs creates stale allowlisted DDx scratch prefixes outside the execution worktree root and verifies cleanup removes them only after the configured age/liveness checks pass.
2. TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveDDXScratchDirs verifies fresh mtime, live PID/liveness, and non-DDx prefixes are preserved.
3. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees verifies stale DDx metadata from temp test project roots can be reclaimed when no active git worktree/liveness remains, while active foreign project worktrees remain preserved.
4. TestCleanupCommand_ReportsScratchReclamation verifies ddx cleanup --json and human output include scratch dir counts plus bytes/inodes reclaimed.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs|TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveDDXScratchDirs|TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees|TestCleanupCommand_ReportsScratchReclamation" -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The production cleanup manager is too narrow for the resource failure we just had to repair by hand. ddx cleanup reported little or no reclaimable space because it only owns execution worktree directories, while DDx-generated e2e/test scratch directories and generated test binaries can consume many GiB in the same temp filesystem.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:202-212 reads entries only under the execution temp root and skips anything without ExecuteBeadWtPrefix. This excludes DDx-owned scratch prefixes created by command/e2e tests and generated binaries.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:234-240 preserves ownership mismatches unconditionally, which is correct for foreign projects but means test-owned DDx execution worktrees are never reclaimed by an operator running cleanup from the main project.
- cli/cmd/cleanup.go:64-67 constructs the cleanup manager with default scope only; there is no CLI or manager option to include safe DDx scratch prefixes.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:69-88 summary fields only count execution temp dirs, registered worktrees, and run-state files, so broader scratch cleanup would not be observable without new fields.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend ExecutionCleanupManager with an explicit DDx scratch cleanup scope driven by allowlisted prefixes/metadata, minimum age, and liveness/process checks.
- Include DDx-created e2e/test scratch roots and generated test binary directories in that scope without touching arbitrary temp directories.
- Keep foreign-project execution worktree preservation by default, but add a safe test-owned/expired-DDx metadata path when liveness is stale and the directory is not registered active.
- Extend cleanup summaries and CLI JSON/human output with scratch dirs removed, scratch bytes/inodes reclaimed, preserved active scratch count, and warnings.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not delete non-DDx temp directories or user-managed caches.
- Do not remove complete .ddx/executions, .ddx/runs, refs/ddx/iterations/*, active registered worktrees, or live liveness-marked paths.
- Do not change work-loop stop policy; ddx-fc6b42d2 wires cleanup into the claim cycle after this bead.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-cf37253d for the updated spec contract. Builds on closed beads ddx-5d154c5d (cleanup manager) and ddx-ef2a7638 (operator cleanup command); those are contextual predecessors, not active blocking deps.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx has a partial tier-label mechanism, but durable bead-level execution hints are underspecified. Agents can currently copy route-shaped settings into bead metadata without a clear lint/audit contract, and smart-tier usage is not tied to a required justification.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:23-39 documents that `ddx try` infers tier from labels today, but arbitrary custom fields are preserved without a routing contract. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md:62-92 requires smart justification, and lines 112-161 require durable route-pin rejection plus execution-routing-intent evidence.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: This bead defines and implements the durable execution-hint contract that later prose beads rely on. Weak implementation would let agents persist concrete model or harness pins into queue metadata, directly violating ADR-024.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add typed parsing for `tier:cheap`, `tier:standard`, and `tier:smart` labels.
- Extract and validate a `SMART JUSTIFICATION:` section for smart-tier beads.
- Add bead lifecycle lint findings for missing smart justification and durable route-pin fields/labels.
- Record `execution-routing-intent` evidence with source, requested tier/power, smart justification, actual route facts, and degradation notes.
- Extend agent metrics projection enough to audit smart-hint usage and rejected durable route pins.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add durable bead-level harness/provider/model/model-ref pins.
- Do not change Fizeau routing internals.
- Do not implement a materialized metrics rollup store.

DEPS
No deps. This must land before the Vale/prose child beads rely on tier hints.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx pre-close review and review repair currently exist as partially overlapping ideas instead of one candidate-cycle pipeline. Recent tracker evidence shows review errors and review_malfunction outcomes can be recorded after implementation commits without a reliable same-worktree repair loop, which makes it unclear when to land, preserve, retry, or close.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:668-680 defines ExecuteBeadWithConfig as a worker that creates an isolated worktree, returns a task result, and leaves merge/preserve/recovery to the parent.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:765-767 removes the implementation worktree via a defer before any later post-merge review path could reuse it.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1104 invokes RunPostMergeReview only after the loop has a success report from the implementation/landing path.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:53-77 describes the review state machine as post-merge/pre-close and explicitly notes repair-cycle extraction is still future work.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:337-356 already wants adversarial review and repair-cycle behavior, but does not yet define the same-worktree candidate-cycle pipeline in implementable detail.

PROPOSED FIX
Create a decomposed implementation epic for the candidate-cycle refactor. The child beads will specify and then implement: candidate vocabulary, read-only reviewer enforcement, attempt-cycle coordinator interfaces, project-root candidate refs, checks-before-review, same-worktree pre-land review, finding classification, same-worktree repair, power-floor routing, two-slot review, retirement of post-land review semantics, and cleanup/recovery hardening.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not directly edit implementation code in this epic bead.
- Do not manually edit .ddx/beads.jsonl; all child work must be created and managed through ddx bead commands.
- Do not remove existing related beads such as ddx-639af4ef or ddx-9228a484; this epic coordinates the refined replacement/dependency path.

2. Path A run: each fixture's bead replayed via ddx try (or ddx work --once) on a branch from base-rev~1; outcomes captured.
3. Path B run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with bead's auto-prompt verbatim; outcomes captured.
4. Path C run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with operator hand-curated prompt; outcomes captured.

description:
Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.
notes: Partial Path B-vs-C evidence: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome, supporting the bead-quality hypothesis side of the study.
title: study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)

description:
PROBLEM
The bead lifecycle is currently controlled by a mix of status values, lifecycle labels, ad hoc events, retry metadata, and command-specific conditionals. That makes ddx work unreliable: execution-ready work, operator-required work, dependency waits, and terminal outcomes can drift apart depending on which surface is queried.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:38 defines the six upstream status values, but later sections still make labels such as needs_human and triage:needs-investigation lifecycle-driving states.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1450 filters needs-investigation and needs_human labels in readiness logic, while cli/internal/bead/store.go:1548 treats ordinary dependency waiting as Blocked().
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1961 parks intake rejections by adding a needs_human label instead of moving the bead lifecycle into a single status field.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:60 parses status: needs_investigation even though that value is not an upstream bead status.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:177 exposes needsHuman counts as lifecycle output, and the Svelte bead page hard-codes derived ready/incomplete status options.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace conflicting lifecycle behavior with one DDx-owned status state machine. Status is the sole lifecycle field. The accepted lifecycle values are proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled. proposed means operator decision required; open means accepted active work and may be dependency-waiting; in_progress means claimed/running; blocked means accepted work paused by an external recheckable blocker; closed means terminal satisfied and satisfies dependents; cancelled means terminal not-doing and does not satisfy dependents. Dependency waiting is derived from dependency closure, not status=blocked. needs_human and triage:needs-investigation stop being queue labels and become legacy metadata/events only where needed for migration or compatibility.

NON-SCOPE
Do not execute the refactor in this epic. Child beads own specs, pure state machine code, store/query wiring, agent outcome mapping, API/UI surfaces, skill contracts, and final conformance.

CHILD WORK
The children under this epic must keep the same invariant: lifecycle decisions call the pure state-machine contract rather than adding new one-off conditionals.
title: bead: replace lifecycle labels with status-owned state machine

description:
PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.

4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerReadinessRejectReleasesOrParksClaim` covers intake error, ambiguous/needs-human, and lint-block outcomes: retryable errors release the bead or set a bounded cooldown; terminal readiness outcomes park with explicit labels/metadata; actionable beads proceed to implementation.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` now runs model-backed bead readiness intake and bead-lifecycle lint after selecting a ready bead but before atomically claiming it. With two workers on the same project, both workers can select the same bead, both spend model calls on intake/lint, and both can append pre-dispatch events before one loses `Store.Claim`. If intake returns `actionable_but_rewritten`, the loser can also mutate bead description/acceptance before owning the bead. Atomic `Store.Claim` still prevents duplicate implementation, but it no longer protects the expensive and stateful pre-claim phase.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:686-765 calls `runtime.PreClaimIntakeHook` and handles rewrite/block outcomes before `w.Store.Claim` is attempted.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:768-807 calls `runtime.PreDispatchLintHook` and `appendPreDispatchLintEvent` before the claim, so concurrent workers can append duplicate lint events for the same open bead.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:809-823 only detects the ownership race after intake/lint have already run.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:39-58 loads and updates bead description/acceptance from the intake rewrite path without a prior worker claim/ownership check.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:588-635 makes `ClaimWithOptions` the atomic ownership boundary, but `ReadyExecution` at cli/internal/bead/store.go:1352-1355 only lists candidates and does not reserve them.

PROPOSED FIX
- Move the expensive/model-backed readiness and lint gates behind an atomic worker reservation, or add an equivalent atomic `ReserveForReadiness` lease that removes the bead from other workers while intake/lint runs.
- If the chosen design uses ordinary claim before readiness: claim first, start heartbeat, run intake and lint, then unclaim/release on retryable intake errors, park with `needs_human`/triage metadata on terminal readiness outcomes, and continue to implementation only for actionable beads.
- If the chosen design uses a separate readiness lease: make `ReadyExecution` exclude fresh readiness leases, make stale leases reclaimable, and require rewrite/lint event mutations to verify lease ownership.
- Ensure `actionable_but_rewritten` applies metadata under the same ownership/lease used by the worker, so concurrent workers cannot double-rewrite or append conflicting events.
- Preserve the existing atomic `Store.Claim` duplicate-implementation guarantee and existing `picker.claim_race` telemetry for true claim races.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the bead-lifecycle rubric or model prompt content.
- Removing readiness/lint checks entirely.
- Changing final implementation landing/review semantics.

DEPS
No deps. This is a queue safety bug introduced by the current pre-claim intake ordering and can be fixed independently of the parked-bead triage work.

acceptance:
1. A single store-level lifecycle transition API exists and all status-writing code paths call it or a narrow adapter that delegates to it.
2. Store.Update rejects or ignores direct Status mutations except from the transition API, with tests proving invalid direct writes cannot bypass the state machine.
3. Claim, unclaim, close, close-with-evidence, reopen, operator prompt approve/cancel, CLI update --status, GraphQL mutation, REST/MCP update, and migration application paths are wired through the transition API.
4. TestLifecycleTransitionWriteAPI_AllowsDocumentedTransitions covers proposed→open, proposed→cancelled, open→in_progress, in_progress→open, in_progress→closed, in_progress→proposed, open→blocked, blocked→open, and closed→open/reopen if the spec permits it.
5. TestLifecycleTransitionWriteAPI_RejectsInvalidTransitions covers cancelled satisfying no deps, arbitrary needs_human/needs_investigation pseudo-statuses, and status=blocked without external blocker metadata.
6. TestBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition, TestGraphQLBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition, and TestMCPBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition cover the public mutation paths.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... -run 'TestLifecycleTransitionWriteAPI|TestBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition|TestGraphQLBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition|TestMCPBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition' -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/...` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Even after a pure lifecycle state machine exists, DDx can still drift if status writers bypass it. Today commands and store helpers assign statuses directly, so invalid or semantically wrong transitions can keep entering the tracker from CLI, GraphQL, REST/MCP, claim/unclaim, close/reopen, or operator-prompt paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:535-562 exposes Store.Update, where callers can mutate b.Status directly before generic validation.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:525-526 assigns `b.Status = v` for `ddx bead update --status`.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:580-612 implements ClaimWithOptions with direct status checks and assignment.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:730-736 makes Unclaim set in_progress back to open directly.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1024-1027 and cli/internal/bead/store.go:1192-1209 close/reopen by assigning statuses directly.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:228-256 currently resolves human-review state by mutating labels/closing rather than lifecycle transitions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a status-transition write API, for example Store.TransitionLifecycle or Store.SetLifecycleStatus, that delegates to the pure lifecycle transition function and records transition reason/evidence. Route every status-writing path through it: update --status, claim, unclaim, close, close-with-evidence, reopen, operator prompt approve/cancel, GraphQL/REST/MCP update, migration application, and agent parking paths. Leave Store.Update for non-lifecycle fields but prevent arbitrary status assignment outside the transition API.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change ready/blocked query derivation in this bead. Do not add broad backcompat for legacy labels; old lifecycle labels are handled only by the one-way migrator.

DEPS
Depends on the pure lifecycle core and storage status preservation.
title: bead: enforce lifecycle transitions on every status write

description:
PROBLEM
DDx does not have a written contract that separates broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. An agent opened in the repo can interpret queue questions as permission to start edits, while ddx work is meant to pick up execution-ready beads.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy without declaring a default interactive queue-steward mode. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work layers but not the interactive mode boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", and "what's ready" through the ddx skill without specifying whether those prompts mean human intervention triage or worker execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Update AGENTS.md, FEAT-010, and FEAT-011 with an explicit mode taxonomy: queue_steward for broad interactive DDx questions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead worker prompts, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Define precedence so bead_execution supersedes only the queue-steward default, while tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI behavior beyond static guidance tests.
- Changing tracker status enums or storage.
- Starting or managing workers automatically from interactive sessions.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: none.

description:
PROBLEM
  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.
- P7: the refactor aligns names and prompt surface with the bead-as-prompt contract.
