description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

Deps: ddx-c6317784.

description:
PROBLEM
The no-changes contract and parser still accept a pseudo-status needs_investigation, then park the bead via labels and cooldown metadata. That bypasses the upstream status set and prevents ddx work from rerunning investigation-quality work with a smart agent when autonomous progress is possible.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:16-23 defines NoChangesKindNeedsInvestigation.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:25-35 defines NoChangesLabelNeedsInvestigation and related events/extra keys.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:60-64 parses `status: needs_investigation`.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:136-140 defines NoChangesActionNeedsHumanNoCooldown.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:203-215 maps needs-investigation to a needs-human action and label.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1786-1796 instructs workers to output `status: needs_investigation` for ambiguous no-change cases.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the no-changes pseudo-status contract with status-owned outcomes. If the bead is already satisfied, close it. If no change happened but autonomous smart-agent work remains possible, leave status=open with explanatory no-changes metadata and route the next retry with smart power. If operator decision is required, transition to status=proposed with explicit reason/suggested action metadata. Reserve status=blocked only for external recheckable blockers. `status: needs_investigation` is not accepted in new live no-changes output; stored historical values are handled by the one-way lifecycle migrator.

NON-SCOPE
Do not rewrite preclaim intake, terminal review, GraphQL, or frontend surfaces in this bead. Those have separate integration beads.

DEPS
Depends on store/query semantics because no-changes outcomes must be visible in the new worker-ready/operator-attention buckets.
title: agent: map no-changes outcomes to status-owned lifecycle states

4. Worker recommendation logic is conservative: it can suggest another worker when ready depth is high and active/in-progress capacity is observable, and it reports unknown capacity instead of inventing a blocker when signals are absent.

description:
PROBLEM
Interactive queue stewardship needs a higher-level surface than ddx bead ready. Ready beads are worker-actionable and should usually be left to ddx work; operators need a command that exposes work ddx ready/work will not pick up plus a conservative signal when the ready queue is deep enough to justify starting another worker.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:7-16 lists ready, blocked, status, and list as queue inspection commands but has no human-intervention view. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 answers "What should I work on next?" with ddx bead ready. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:119-160 prints execution plan rows and skipped epics, but not a first-class intervention queue or worker-capacity recommendation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a read-only command surface, proposed as ddx work focus, that summarizes human-required intervention separately from worker-ready work. It should show needs_human/review-blocked beads, blocked beads that require planning or dependency decisions, malformed or underspecified beads if lifecycle signals are available, ready-depth summary, active-worker/in-progress signals when observable, and unknown capacity hazards when not observable. It may recommend starting another worker only from observable queue depth/capacity signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Automatically starting workers.
- Closing, updating, or splitting beads.
- Replacing ddx work plan or ddx bead ready.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92.

description:
PROBLEM
  Today's Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go performs git
  fetch, push --ff-only, and push-race auto-recovery as part of the core
  drain flow. This causes 30-120s lock holds spanning network I/O, which
  in turn produced ddx-08e90869 (work landed only on a preserved iteration
  ref because tracker lock contention prevented the main merge) and
  ddx-15a51f8c (push-race remediation commit). The user's architectural
  position is: Land() should perform only the worktree-merge into local
  main; origin-sync (fetch) and push are separate concerns, operator-driven
  or background-deferred, never coupled to Land() or the drain.

  The spec corpus partially reflects this position already:
  FEAT-012-git-awareness.md:134 declares "DDx does not invoke `git push` at
  all." But FEAT-010 (the governing feature for task execution) does not
  state the network-free boundary as a requirement, and the multi-machine
  docs (API-001 §"Land And Preserve Semantics", SD-020) describe a
  push/fetch-coupled lifecycle that is correct for distributed mode but
  contradicts the single-node invariant.

  This is a doc-only bead. It lands the requirement in the right anchor
  spec, then reconciles every cross-reference so the spec corpus is
  coherent and complete. Implementation removal of push/fetch from Land()
  is a separate sister bead (ddx-1f774edf, rewritten in parallel).

ROOT CAUSE
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md ##Requirements
    section (around line 1057) enumerates functional + non-functional
    requirements but has no "Network isolation" or "Sync decoupling"
    requirement.
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-git-awareness.md:134 declares the
    correct invariant ("DDx does not invoke `git push` at all") but has no
    cross-reference to FEAT-010 or to the implementation status.
  - docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md
    Layer 2/3 sections do not mention push/fetch — silent on the boundary,
    so implementers may inadvertently reintroduce network I/O.
  - docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:221-225
    §"Land And Preserve Semantics" prescribes "fetch origin before rebase"
    and "push --ff-only ... atomic ref-update is the serialization point".
    This was written for the multi-machine coordinator topology and
    contradicts the single-node invariant.
  - docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md
    lines 51, 60, 79, 83, 117, 123, 136, 155 reference git push/fetch as
    part of the lifecycle; these are correctly scoped to multi-machine but
    need an explicit preamble distinguishing single-node from multi-machine.
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-023-sync.md exists but does not
    explicitly position itself as the designated home for origin-sync
    semantics complementing FEAT-010's drain boundary.
  - docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md enumerates P1-P8
    execution-loop invariants; the network-free drain is the natural P9.

PROPOSED FIX
  All edits are doc-only:

  1. FEAT-010: under ##Requirements, add a new functional requirement:
     "Network-free drain boundary: Land() performs only the worktree-merge
     into local main, under a brief local lock. Origin-sync (git fetch +
     merging origin/main into local main) and origin-push (git push) are
     separate operations, operator-driven or background-deferred, and are
     never invoked from Land() or from the drain loop."
     Plus a non-functional requirement bullet:
     "Network isolation: single-developer/single-node drain does not block
     on remote I/O; origin sync and push are decoupled and may run in
     parallel or in the background without holding the drain or land lock."

  2. FEAT-012:134 — keep the existing line; add a cross-reference sentence
     pointing to FEAT-010's new requirement and noting the implementation
     gap is being closed by the sister implementation bead.

  3. SD-025 — under Layer 2 (bead attempt) and Layer 3 (queue drain), add a
     one-sentence cross-reference: "Per FEAT-010 network-free drain
     boundary, this layer performs no network I/O; origin-sync and push are
     out-of-band per FEAT-023."

  4. API-001:221-225 §"Land And Preserve Semantics" — add an explicit
     preamble: "These semantics describe the multi-machine coordinator
     topology (SD-020). For single-node mode (FEAT-010 default), Land() is
     local-only; the fetch+push lifecycle described below applies only when
     multi-machine coordination is active."

  5. SD-020 — at the top of the doc, add a one-paragraph preamble making
     the single-node vs multi-machine distinction explicit, with a pointer
     to FEAT-010.

  6. FEAT-023-sync.md — add a sentence positioning it as the designated
     home for origin-sync semantics, complementing FEAT-010's drain
     boundary. Defer the actual `ddx sync` helper to the future
     multi-machine work; this is just a naming/scoping clarification.

  7. reliability-principles.md — add P9 "Network-free drain": short
     principle text, file:line citations to FEAT-010 and Land(),
     justification (single-node drain must not block on network I/O).

NON-SCOPE
  - The implementation removal of push/fetch from Land() and the
    LandingGitOps interface (sister bead ddx-1f774edf, rewritten).
  - Designing or building a `ddx sync` helper (downstream of multi-node
    work; the user explicitly deferred this).
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact, separate beads).
  - Changing TD-031 (state machine is unaffected).
  - Changing ADR-022 (worker client-server is orthogonal).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister implementation bead ddx-1f774edf depends on this landing.
  - Future multi-node work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is a temporary
    scoping that explicitly says "single-node only" so future-spec authors
    can layer multi-node semantics on top.
title: docs(FEAT-010): declare network-free drain boundary; reconcile cross-refs across spec corpus

description:
PROBLEM
  Fizeau (the agent-routing service DDx embeds for harness execution
  and routing) was moved from github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau to a
  new home at easel/fizeau, with a new published release. DDx today
  imports github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.15 (cli/go.mod:7)
  across 53 .go files in cli/. The integration is mechanical (import
  path swap + go.mod update + go.sum refresh + verify build/tests).

  Top-priority because: (a) the old import path may be deprecated or
  receive no further releases; (b) we want the latest fizeau release
  features/fixes (likely includes routing or wedge-relevant
  improvements); (c) this is a blocking integration — every other
  agent-side fix depends on stable fizeau access.

  Per user memory feedback_no_local_replace.md: ddx must always import
  a properly tagged release; no go.mod replace directives, even
  temporarily.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/go.mod:7 has `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.15`.
  - 53 .go files in cli/ import github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau or
    a sub-package thereof. Verify with:
        grep -rn "DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau" cli/ --include='*.go' | wc -l
  - The new module path is at easel/fizeau (likely
    github.com/easel/fizeau — confirm via `go list -m
    github.com/easel/fizeau@latest` or by checking the upstream repo
    directly). The latest published release tag should be discovered
    at integration time, not assumed from this bead body.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Discover the new module path and latest tag:
        - Try: `go list -m github.com/easel/fizeau@latest`
        - If that fails, check upstream README / GitHub for the
          canonical Go import path.
        - Confirm a Go-module-friendly tag (e.g., v0.11.x or higher).

  2. Update cli/go.mod:
        - Remove `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.15`.
        - Add `<new-module-path> <new-version>`.
        - Run `go mod tidy` to refresh go.sum.
        - DO NOT add a go.mod replace directive — must import the
          properly tagged release directly.

  3. Sweep the 53 .go files:
        - Replace `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau` →
          `<new-module-path>` in every import statement.
        - Verify no stale references remain:
            grep -rn "DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau" cli/ → must return zero.

  4. Verify the build:
        - cd cli && go build ./... must succeed.
        - cd cli && go test ./... must pass (or at minimum match the
          pre-change baseline; document any breaking changes from the
          new release in the commit message).

  5. If the new release introduces API changes (e.g., renamed types,
     changed function signatures, new required fields):
        - Adapt the call sites minimally to satisfy the new API.
        - Document each adaptation in the commit message.
        - If the API churn is large enough to merit a separate bead,
          file it and note in this bead's commit.

NON-SCOPE
  - Adopting any new fizeau features beyond what's needed to compile
    against the new release.
  - Refactoring DDx's fizeau integration patterns (out of scope; do
    only what's needed to compile and pass existing tests).
  - Changes to ddx-server-side fizeau usage (covered if any; flag if
    out of repo).

INTERSECTIONS
  - All other agent-side beads (ddx-1f774edf, ddx-1c3cad6f, etc.)
    depend on a working fizeau integration. Land this first.
  - User memory feedback_no_local_replace.md applies: no replace
    directives, even as a stepping stone.
title: feat(deps): integrate easel/fizeau replacement for DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau

description:
PROBLEM
The public DDx execution vocabulary is supposed to be `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`, but large parts of the codebase still use legacy `execute-bead` / `execute-loop` names in public help, comments, server worker kinds, GraphQL models, tests, docs, tracker metadata helpers, and internal symbols. Operators now see mixed terms and developers keep adding new code under the old names.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent execute-loop` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent execute-loop`, and deprecation text still names execute-loop rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `execute-loop` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `execute-loop-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent execute-loop` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "execute-loop|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.

- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent execute-loop`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `execute-loop-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE

- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS

- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.
- ddx-eccc6efb: server-spawn migration may delete some `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` paths; if it lands first, shrink this bead to remaining public/API/doc names.

description:
PROBLEM
Implementation agents need durable specs before removing `ddx agent`, renaming DDx internals, or consuming Fizeau contract changes. Today the specs still mix the old `agent` vocabulary with the desired DDx/Fizeau boundary, so agents can reasonably follow stale instructions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
FEAT-001 still describes an `Agent Passthrough` structural mount for `ddx agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:70-84. FEAT-006 still describes the upstream invocation boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` and `DDx Agent Service` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:1-23. FEAT-010 still says layer 1 consumes upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. TP-020 remains a historical DDx-side routing test plan even though Fizeau owns routing. docs/migrations/routing-config.md:1-13 removes some old routing keys but does not yet document the full `agent.*` removal / Fizeau-vs-execution config split.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the specs before implementation. Required docs: FEAT-001, FEAT-006 or its renamed replacement, FEAT-010, ADR-024, TP-020, docs/migrations/routing-config.md, docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md, and DDx skill references. The specs must state: DDx owns task execution (`run`, `try`, `work`); Fizeau owns routing, provider/model discovery, native transcript/session rendering, and fuzzy model constraint resolution; `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are explicit operator passthrough only; DDx passes raw strings unchanged; `ddx agent` is removed without deprecation; and `agent` is allowed only for actual external AI agent concepts.

NON-SCOPE
No production code removal in this bead. No Fizeau implementation work. No compatibility alias design. No generated CLI docs rebuild unless the docs generator is part of the spec-doc workflow.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES

- fizeau-400b57fe for Fizeau-owned model alias/fuzzy matching

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go (new file or extending tracker_lock_test.go) contains TestConcurrentLand_NonOverlappingChanges_BothLandClean asserting all parallel non-conflicting lands succeed.
2. The same file contains TestConcurrentLand_OverlappingChanges_OneLandsRestPreserve asserting exactly one of three conflicting lands succeeds; the other two preserve correctly.
3. The same file contains TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded with N=4 workers over M=10 cycles, asserting preservation rate < 50%.
4. Tests use deterministic timing (channels or synctest) and per-test sandbox via testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir().
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestConcurrentLand_' green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  After sister bead ddx-1f774edf removes network I/O from Land(), the
  drain becomes safe for external parallelism (multiple ddx work --once
  shells running concurrently). However, parallel workers will land their
  own bead's worktree against the same local main and may produce
  overlapping changes — multi-worker conflict frequency on Land()'s
  git merge step is unmeasured. The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus
  self-review identified this as a key risk: under high parallelism,
  preservation (resource_exhausted to iteration ref) could become the
  common path rather than the exception, defeating the gain.

  This bead adds chaos coverage for the parallel-landing case and a
  sentinel that fails if the preservation rate exceeds an acceptable
  threshold under simulated load.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go and chaos2_test.go cover
    bead.Store-layer concurrency only.
  - cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go covers single-worker
    semantics.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a covers stale-lock recovery for a single
    worker's local-only land. It does not cover N>1 workers landing in
    parallel.
  - No test verifies what happens when worker A lands branch-A into
    main, then worker B (whose worktree was branched from pre-A main)
    tries to land branch-B with overlapping changes.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add three chaos tests in cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go (new
  file, or extend tracker_lock_test.go):

  1. TestConcurrentLand_NonOverlappingChanges_BothLandClean:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each with its own bead/worktree
       touching disjoint files.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: all three land on local main, in some order; no
       preservation; main contains all three commits.

  2. TestConcurrentLand_OverlappingChanges_OneLandsRestPreserve:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each touching the same file with
       different content.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: exactly one lands; the other two are preserved to
       iteration refs; the preserved branches are intact and contain
       their bead's full work.

  3. TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded:
     - Spawn N worker goroutines (N=4) over M cycles (M=10), each with
       a randomized mix of overlapping/non-overlapping changes.
     - Assert: preservation rate is < 50% (i.e., at least half of
       attempts land cleanly). This is a smoke check that parallel
       drain is viable, not a hard SLA.

  Use deterministic timing (channels or synctest), not wall-clock.
  Use testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir() per test.
  Depends on sister bead ddx-1f774edf so the lock holds are sub-second
  (otherwise the test exercises today's contention bug, not the
  parallel-landing concern).

NON-SCOPE
  - Multi-process chaos (these are goroutine-level concurrent landings
    using fake LandingGitOps that mirror the real merge logic).
  - Coordinating workers via a "land queue" abstraction (separate
    architectural concern; this bead establishes the baseline measurement
    against which any such work would be evaluated).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (depends on it landing first).
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent/lock: instrument bead.Store
    WithLock wait time" — its metrics are referenced by these tests'
    assertions if available.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a complements this for the single-worker
    stale-lock case.
title: agent/chaos: cover concurrent-drain landing under shared-main contention

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: when implementer returns no_changes (BaseRev == ResultRev and zero commits), reviewer dispatch is skipped.
2. The existing no_changes triage path handles routing; behavior is preserved (labels, cooldowns, status transitions match pre-change baseline).
3. A "reviewer-skipped-empty-diff" event is emitted on each skip so operators can see the optimization.
4. TestNoChanges_SkipsReviewer asserts reviewer is NOT invoked when outcome is no_changes.
5. TestSomeChanges_StillInvokesReviewer asserts reviewer IS invoked when implementer produced any commit.
6. TestNoChangesWithReviewSpecGapClassification_RoutesToProposed asserts spec-gap path still routes to proposed without reviewer gating.
7. TestReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEvent asserts the structured event is emitted.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  When an implementer attempt produces no_changes (BaseRev ==
  ResultRev, no commits), the drain still dispatches the reviewer
  agent. The reviewer is paid for an empty-diff review; it
  contributes nothing because there are no changes to evaluate.
  This is pure cost waste — the no_changes triage path (existing,
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go around the no_changes
  outcome handling) already routes the bead correctly without
  needing a reviewer signal.

  Per ADR-024 P1 (cheapest first), the reviewer's cost on an
  empty-diff dispatch is unjustified. Cost optimization includes
  "skip the unnecessary".

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: when implementer
    returns no_changes, the dispatch flow proceeds to reviewer
    invocation regardless.
  - No early-exit / lazy-dispatch logic.
  - Reviewer cost: typically smart-tier, $0.30-1.00 per invocation.
  - Multiplied across N no_changes attempts per bead (N=2-3 in
    smart-retry path), this is real money on no-op operations.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: before invoking the
    reviewer, check the implementer outcome:
      - If outcome is no_changes (BaseRev == ResultRev and zero
        commits produced): skip reviewer entirely.
      - Route directly to the existing no_changes triage path
        (which classifies, applies labels, sets cooldowns or
        re-status as needed).
      - Emit a "reviewer-skipped-empty-diff" event so operators
        can see the optimization fired.
  - Preserve all existing no_changes handling logic — the only
    change is "don't pay the reviewer when there's nothing to
    review."
  - Edge case: review_spec_gap diagnosis happens at the IMPLEMENTER
    level (the implementer says "I can't because the spec is
    gappy"); the reviewer doesn't gate this. Confirm the no_changes
    triage path handles the spec-gap routing without reviewer.

  Tests:
     - TestNoChanges_SkipsReviewer: assert reviewer is NOT invoked
       when implementer outcome is no_changes.
     - TestSomeChanges_StillInvokesReviewer: assert reviewer IS
       invoked when implementer produced any commit.
     - TestNoChangesWithReviewSpecGapClassification_RoutesToProposed:
       assert the spec-gap path still routes to proposed without
       reviewer gating.
     - TestReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEvent: assert event is emitted.

NON-SCOPE
  - Skipping reviewer for non-no_changes outcomes (out of scope;
    those genuinely need review).
  - Tuning reviewer dispatch for partial-changes cases (out of
    scope).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-451cdead (ADR-024 amendment) — P1 cost
    optimization principle.
  - Sister bead ddx-cf79f10c (no_changes stepwise) — same
    no_changes path; this bead skips the reviewer cost; that
    bead handles the implementer escalation. Compose cleanly.
title: feat(review): skip reviewer dispatch when implementer produced no commits

description:
PROBLEM
  ddx try needs post-attempt triage after the loop has adjudicated an attempt outcome and before cooldown decisions, so transient failures can be classified without mutating the underlying status. Today the loop records status/detail/rationale but has no triage hook invocation path that can populate OutcomeReason.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:940-1090 contains the outcome adjudication and cooldown branch chain. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:976 and 1084 call shouldSuppressNoProgress before applying no-progress cooldown. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1099 appends the report to the loop result after cooldown handling. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:30 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PostAttemptTriageHook and cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go defines TriageResult, but no cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go exists and the loop never calls the hook.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go with a constructor, e.g. NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot string, store BeadReader, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, sessionLogReader SessionLogExcerptReader) func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error). If package boundaries require a different name, preserve the explicit injected inputs.
  - The hook builds a prompt whose first line is MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes the bead body, ExecuteBeadReport as JSON, the most relevant recent bead events, and a bounded session-log excerpt when report.SessionID is non-empty and a log can be found under the project agent log dir.
  - Invoke dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, rcfg, AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: prompt, WorkDir: projectRoot, PromptSource: "bead-lifecycle-triage"}) or an equivalent package-local wrapper that still uses AgentRunner/ResolvedConfig and not os/exec.
  - In execute_bead_loop.go, call runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook after report.Status/Detail/Rationale/BaseRev/ResultRev are final for the attempt and before any shouldSuppressNoProgress or SetExecutionCooldown branch. A valid classification sets report.OutcomeReason = TriageResult.Classification and records triage details in the execute-bead event body or a dedicated bead-quality.triage event.
  - On hook failure, fail open: leave OutcomeReason empty and keep legacy cooldown behavior.

NON-SCOPE
  - Defining OutcomeReason and TriageResult; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.
  - CLI wiring for ddx try; covered by sibling/parent wiring work.
  - GraphQL exposure of OutcomeReason.
  - Automatically creating SuggestedFollowupBeads; record/surface only unless a later bead opts in.

PARENT
  ddx-e1a576a7.

DEPS
  No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and the OutcomeReason/TriageResult contract exists.

acceptance:
1. `TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtAllowsTrackerAndEvidencePaths` or a dedicated `TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtAllowsMetricsPaths` writes `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` and proves `checkpointPreDispatchDirt` commits it.
2. `TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtRejectsImplementationPaths` still proves dirty non-DDx implementation files are rejected.

description:
PROBLEM
After an unpinned `ddx work` successfully merged ddx-c3219628, later ready beads failed before agent execution because `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` remained dirty and the pre-execute checkpoint guard treated it as implementation work. The worker failed ddx-6cde5ffd, ddx-9d39d510, and ddx-c019e48a with `checkpoint refused to absorb implementation changes outside DDx bookkeeping: .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:719-733` defines `preDispatchCheckpointAllowedPath` for DDx bookkeeping paths, but it includes beads, executions, runs, and run-state paths only.
- Attempt metrics written to `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` are durable DDx bookkeeping produced by the worker after an attempt, but they are not included in the checkpoint allowlist.
- The next queue iteration therefore fails before execution even though the dirty path is DDx-owned evidence, not bead implementation work.

PROPOSED FIX
Allow `.ddx/metrics/` paths in the pre-dispatch checkpoint guard so metrics dirt is checkpointed with the same DDx bookkeeping treatment as `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions/`, `.ddx/runs/`, and `.ddx/run-state/`. Add regression coverage that writes `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` and proves `checkpointPreDispatchDirt` commits it instead of rejecting it.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken rejection of non-DDx implementation files. Do not change metrics schema or attempt cost accounting. Do not ignore metrics silently; they should remain durable evidence.
title: execute-bead: checkpoint attempt metrics bookkeeping

acceptance:
1. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md contains a Principles section (P1-P5), a Sequencing section (within-cycle → cross-cycle → proposed), a Per-bead budget subsection, and an Operator escape hatches subsection (labels recovery:manual and budget:<USD>).
2. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md §5 contains 4 new outcome rows: reframe_applied, decompose_applied, auto_recovery_failed, per_bead_budget_exhausted.
3. TD-031 §6 lists reframer and decomposer role types with their dispatch contracts.
4. TD-031 documents a new Extra field consecutive_ladder_exhaustions with semantics (counter, clears on success/operator-action, drives auto-recovery trigger threshold).
5. docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md contains a Layer 3.5 (auto-recovery) section between Layer 3 and the status=proposed escape, with cross-references to TD-031 §5 and ADR-024 P4.
6. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md Requirements section contains a functional requirement on automatic recovery and an NFR on per-bead cost budget.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  The escalation+auto-recovery design (sister beads 20-25 implement)
  introduces new behavior that requires updates to FOUR governing
  spec documents, not just ADR-024:

  - ADR-024: power escalation + reviewer routing — needs explicit
    P1-P5 principles and within-cycle vs cross-cycle sequencing.
  - TD-031 §5: outcome → label/event/Extra mapping — needs new rows
    for reframe_applied, decompose_applied, auto_recovery_failed,
    per_bead_budget_exhausted.
  - TD-031 §6: claim semantics + role catalogue — needs reframer +
    decomposer roles defined with their dispatch contracts.
  - TD-031: state tracking — needs a new Extra field
    consecutive_ladder_exhaustions to drive the auto-recovery
    trigger.
  - SD-025: 3-layer execution lifecycle — auto-recovery is a NEW
    stage (between Layer 3 and status=proposed); SD-025 is silent.
  - FEAT-010: task execution feature spec — auto-recovery is a
    functional requirement, parallel to the "network-free drain
    boundary" added by sister bead ddx-3d5197d8.

  Without coherent spec coverage, implementers (sister beads 20-25)
  have nothing to anchor against; later operators can't tell
  whether observed behaviors are intentional. This bead is doc-only;
  it lands the full spec frame so the implementation beads can
  reference and conform.

CURRENT STATE
  - docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md
    governs power escalation but has no explicit principles
    enumeration, no within/cross-cycle sequencing rule.
  - docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
    §5 has 14 outcome rows; missing reframe_applied,
    decompose_applied, auto_recovery_failed,
    per_bead_budget_exhausted, push_failed, push_conflict (last two
    covered by sister bead at rank 14).
  - TD-031 §6 (Claim Semantics) does not list reframer or
    decomposer as legitimate role types.
  - docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md
    Layer 3 (queue drain) does not mention auto-recovery; the
    only escape from drain is described as status=proposed.
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md
    Requirements section enumerates execution requirements but no
    auto-recovery requirement.
  - User memory feedback_models.md captures the cost-tiered
    intent but is not in spec form.

PROPOSED FIX

  1. ADR-024 amendments (primary anchor):
     - Add Principles section enumerating P1-P5 (cheapest first,
       escalate on failure, reviewer always stronger, reframe +
       decompose on persistent failure, no human-in-the-loop).
     - Add Sequencing section with explicit ordering:
       within-cycle (ladder climb) → cross-cycle (reframe) →
       cross-cycle (decompose) → status=proposed (final escape).
     - Add Per-bead budget subsection: a bead's escalation must
       stop when its per-bead cost exceeds the configured limit.
     - Add Operator escape hatches subsection: labels
       recovery:manual (skip auto-recovery), budget:<USD>
       (override default per-bead cost cap).

  2. TD-031 §5 amendments (outcome → mapping table):
     - New row: reframe_applied — bead description/AC rewritten by
       reframer agent; status stays open; events: reframe-applied
       with from-rev, to-rev, reframer-cost.
     - New row: decompose_applied — children filed via Store.Create
       with parent linked; parent status=open with
       execution-eligible=false; events: decompose-applied with
       child-IDs and reframer-cost.
     - New row: auto_recovery_failed — both reframe and decompose
       attempts failed; status=proposed; events:
       auto-recovery-failed with both attempt-cost values.
     - New row: per_bead_budget_exhausted — bead unclaimed without
       cooldown; events: per-bead-budget-exhausted with total cost.

  3. TD-031 §6 amendments (Claim semantics + role catalogue):
     - Add reframer role: dispatched with strong-tier MinPower (per
       ADR-024 P3); claim is held by drain proxy not the agent;
       output: structured edits to bead description/AC.
     - Add decomposer role: same dispatch rules; output: list of
       2-5 child bead specs to Store.Create.
     - Add new Extra field: consecutive_ladder_exhaustions —
       integer counter incremented when ladder exhausts on a
       drain cycle; cleared on success or operator action; drives
       auto-recovery trigger threshold (default 2).

  4. SD-025 amendments (3-layer execution lifecycle):
     - Add a new section "Layer 3.5: auto-recovery" between Layer
       3 (queue drain) and the proposed status escape:
       - Triggered after consecutive_ladder_exhaustions >= 2.
       - Routes to reframer first; on reframer failure or no
         change, routes to decomposer; on decomposer failure,
         falls through to status=proposed.
     - Cross-reference TD-031 §5 new rows.
     - Cross-reference ADR-024 P4.

  5. FEAT-010 amendments (Requirements section):
     - Add functional requirement: "Automatic recovery from
       persistent failure: when a bead's ladder is exhausted on
       N consecutive cycles, the system attempts (a) reframe via
       a strong-tier reframer agent, then (b) decomposition via
       a strong-tier decomposer agent, before parking to
       status=proposed."
     - Add NFR: "Per-bead cost budget: every bead's escalation
       attempts are bounded by a per-bead cost cap; budget
       exhaustion is a recheckable signal, not a final state."

  6. Cross-references throughout: each new section/row should
     point to the relevant sister bead and to the other affected
     specs.

NON-SCOPE

    rows) — same TD-031 file, coordinate the two amendments.

    FEAT-010 file, same Requirements section.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-215b08a2 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
  Reopen and Unclaim each maintain their own inlined list of claim-related
  Extra keys to clear. When a new claim metadata key is added (recently:
  claimed-worktree), it must be added in both places or stale metadata
  leaks into reopened/unclaimed beads. A consolidated key list would make
  the cleanup atomic and audit-friendly.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:823-831 Unclaim deletes claimed-at,
    claimed-pid, claimed-machine, claimed-session, claimed-worktree,
    plus assignee, plus other inline keys.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1306-1317 Reopen deletes the same set plus
    execute-loop-heartbeat-at and walks an additional inline-event cleanup
    block at lines 1329-1334.
  - cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:9-26 already declares some Extra-key
    constants (ExtraExecutionEligible, ExtraExecutionReason, etc.) but
    not the claim metadata set.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a single canonical declaration of claim-metadata Extra keys in
    cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go (alongside the lifecycle constants) or
    cli/internal/bead/types.go (alongside CanonicalStatuses):
        var ClaimMetadataExtraKeys = []string{
            "claimed-at",
            "claimed-pid",
            "claimed-machine",
            "claimed-session",
            "claimed-worktree",
        }
    plus separate constants for ClaimAssigneeExtraKey ("assignee") and
    ClaimHeartbeatExtraKey ("execute-loop-heartbeat-at") if they need
    distinct cleanup rules.
  - Refactor Unclaim (store.go:823-831) to iterate ClaimMetadataExtraKeys
    instead of inlining delete calls. Preserve the order of operations:
    clear assignee, clear claim metadata, then clear heartbeat.
  - Refactor Reopen (store.go:1306-1317) similarly, plus preserve the
    inline-event cleanup at 1329-1334 unchanged (different concern).
  - Add a unit test TestClaimMetadataKeys_SharedByReopenAndUnclaim in
    cli/internal/bead/store_test.go that:
      a. Claims a bead, populates every key in ClaimMetadataExtraKeys plus
         assignee and execute-loop-heartbeat-at.
      b. Calls Unclaim; asserts every key in ClaimMetadataExtraKeys is
         absent from b.Extra.
      c. Re-claims, then calls Reopen; asserts the same.
      d. Adds a deliberately new key to b.Extra not in
         ClaimMetadataExtraKeys (e.g. "claim-test-extra"); asserts it
         survives both Unclaim and Reopen (proving the cleanup is bounded
         by the canonical list).

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the actual fields the system writes during claim acquisition.
  - Renaming or moving the Extra keys; their string values are unchanged.
  - Touching the inline-event cleanup at store.go:1329-1334 (separate
    concern: events, not claim metadata).
  - Migrating existing beads with stale metadata; that is a separate
    reconcile path.

INTERSECTIONS
  - ddx-ca1c66ee (epic parent).
  - No deps on B1/B2/B3/B4/B5; can run in parallel.
title: bead/store: consolidate claim-Extra key list shared by Reopen and Unclaim

2. ExecuteBeadWithConfig or an extracted ImplementationPass can return a live worktree candidate result without owning terminal cleanup.

4. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_NoChangesBehaviorUnchanged verifies no_changes adjudication remains unchanged.

description:
PROBLEM
The current execute-bead implementation conflates worktree lifetime, implementation dispatch, artifact writing, landing, and post-merge review across ExecuteBeadWithConfig and the execute loop. Candidate-cycle review/repair needs a coordinator that owns the full live-worktree lifecycle before final land.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:668-680 documents ExecuteBeadWithConfig as the thin worker and says merge/update-ref/gate/preserve are parent responsibilities.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:765-767 removes the worktree via defer inside ExecuteBeadWithConfig, preventing same-worktree review/repair after the function returns.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:888-900 calls agenttry.Attempt around the executor, then handles review later in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1104.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:146-299 owns generic attempt outcome classification, but not candidate-cycle review/repair.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce narrow interfaces for AttemptCycleCoordinator, ImplementationPass, CandidateRefStore, CandidateCheckRunner, CandidateReviewer, RepairPass, and CandidateLander or equivalent names.
- Split the implementation pass from terminal worktree cleanup: implementation produces a candidate result inside a live worktree; the coordinator owns cleanup after terminal disposition.
- Preserve existing no_changes, no_evidence, and execution_failed behavior while creating seams for candidate checks, review, repair, and final land.
- Add basic cleanup liveness protection for active candidate-cycle worktrees before the deferred removal is moved.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not yet run pre-land review.
- Do not yet implement repair classification or repair loops.
- Do not enable two-slot review.

DEPS
Depends on the spec bead for vocabulary and on the read-only reviewer profile only for interface shape; this bead may define the coordinator before reviewer execution is moved.

description:
PROBLEM
The server documents resolver rebuilds the full artifact graph on every page request, even when the underlying graph has not changed. On a 2000-artifact corpus, this produces visible latency on every load and makes concurrent requests redundantly expensive.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go (or equivalent resolver file): the documents resolver calls the full graph-build pipeline on every GraphQL request without checking if the graph revision has changed since the last build.
- No normalized artifact cache (by graph revision) exists — every request starts cold.
- The search path in the documents resolver also rebuilds the full artifact list on every search call.
- ddx-5681cc57 (2k fixture) is a dep: cache design must be validated against the 2k scale fixture.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a cache keyed by graph revision (mtime-based, as recommended in the perf epic): store normalized artifact list (lowercased title/path/id/mediaType) keyed by revision string.
- Add search to documents resolver: filter the cached normalized list by search query.
- Add invalidation on file change: detect mtime changes and invalidate cache entry.
- Ensure concurrent requests don't double-build (single-flight pattern or sync.Once per revision).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching for resolvers other than documents.
- CI performance gate (that is a later bead).
title: perf: cache graph/artifact metadata + add search to documents resolver + invalidation

description:
PROBLEM
The runs/[runId] page (built in S16.1) has no Evidence tab. Users cannot browse or download execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json, stderr logs, etc.) from the run-detail view. Whitelisting logic for safe inline rendering must be enforced to avoid serving binary or executable content inline.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte (added by ddx-f04faa86) has no Evidence tab.
- No bundleFiles[] or bundleFile(path) resolver exists yet for the run-detail page (that resolver is ddx-6d132f62, a dep).
- No whitelist enforcement is implemented; any file could be rendered inline without size or extension checks.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add Evidence tab to runs/[runId]/+page.svelte (as a sibling tab to Overview/Prompt/Response/Tools/Session).
- List every file in run's bundle with: path, size, mimeType.
- Whitelist: *.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB — render inline as raw text.
- Non-whitelisted or > 64KB: download link only. No Markdown rendering, no syntax highlighting, no hex preview.
- Evidence tab lazy-loads on activation.
- Large files (> 64KB): show size + download button only.
- Bundle-root confinement enforced server-side on bundleFile(path) resolver.

NON-SCOPE
- Markdown rendering (deliberate: whitelist is raw-text only).
- Syntax highlighting.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go EscalatableStatuses contains "review_request_changes" and "review_block" (only when classification is fixable).
2. AttemptCycleCoordinator (or the repair-exhaustion handler) bumps implementer MinPower by one ladder step before the final retry.
3. The final escalated retry's reviewer is at implementer.ActualPower + 1 (matching the existing reviewer asymmetry rule).
4. TestReviewBlock_EscalatesImplementerOnRepairExhaustion asserts the MinPower bump happens.
5. TestReviewBlock_StillFailsAtTopTier_ParkProposed asserts top-tier failures still park properly.
6. TestReviewBlock_NonFixableClassification_DoesNotEscalate asserts spec_gap / too_large do not escalate (they need reframe/decompose instead).
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/escalation/... green.

description:
PROBLEM
  When a reviewer returns review_fixable_gap or review_block (with
  fixable classification), the AttemptCycleCoordinator runs a
  repair cycle in the same worktree. But the repair cycle runs the
  SAME implementer model as the failed attempt — no MinPower bump.

  This violates ADR-024 P2 (escalate on failure) at exactly the
  moment escalation matters most: the reviewer has identified
  specific gaps, and the SAME model is being asked to fix them
  with the SAME limitations that produced the gaps.

  Net effect: repair-cycle exhausts retry budget, bead parks to
  status=proposed. Operator opens it, manually re-routes to a
  stronger model. Pure operational drag.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:20-25 EscalatableStatuses
    does NOT contain review_request_changes or review_block.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go AttemptCycleCoordinator
    runs repair cycles at the same MinPower as the original attempt.
  - On repair-cycle exhaustion, status=proposed without bumping
    MinPower for a final escalated retry.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go: add
    "review_request_changes" and "review_block" to EscalatableStatuses
    (only when classification is fixable; not for spec_gap or
    too_large).
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go AttemptCycleCoordinator:
    plumb the previous attempt's ActualPower through the repair
    cycle. After RepairMaxCycles is exhausted, before parking to
    proposed, do ONE final escalated retry at next-tier MinPower.
  - The escalated final retry runs the implementer at higher tier
    AND a stronger reviewer (reviewer.MinPower = new
    implementer.ActualPower + 1).

  Tests:
     - TestReviewBlock_EscalatesImplementerOnRepairExhaustion:
       assert after RepairMaxCycles failures, the next attempt runs
       at implementer MinPower one ladder step higher.
     - TestReviewBlock_StillFailsAtTopTier_ParkProposed: assert
       genuine top-tier failures park as today (no infinite loop).
     - TestReviewBlock_NonFixableClassification_DoesNotEscalate:
       review_spec_gap and review_too_large continue to park
       directly (those need reframe/decompose, not bigger model).

NON-SCOPE
  - Reframe (sister bead handles spec_gap auto-reframe).
  - Decompose (sister bead handles too_large auto-decompose).
  - Reviewer escalation on transport errors (sister bead R4).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR-024)" P2.
  - Sister bead R5 (auto-reframe) for the spec_gap path.
title: fix(escalation): repair cycle bumps implementer MinPower after review BLOCK

description:
PROBLEM
DDx should run prose checks during normal docs-changing attempts without users reminding the agent. Findings need to be captured as evidence and fed back while the attempt is still editable.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts that change Markdown under `docs/` to run `ddx doc prose --changed`, attach evidence, and keep prose advisory. No workflow hook currently implements that contract.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Workflow-hook policy is high-judgment because it touches execution finalization and evidence. A weak implementation could block correct work on advisory prose findings or run too late for agents to repair obvious prose issues.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect docs Markdown changes in `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts.
- Run `ddx doc prose --changed` before finalization when docs changed.
- Attach prose-check evidence to the attempt.
- Feed findings back to the agent before final response when editable.
- Keep prose findings advisory by default and separate from correctness review.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make prose checks mandatory blockers.
- Do not run full-doc cleanup.
- Do not alter Fizeau routing.

DEPS
Depends on the Vale-backed command and the upgraded writing skill.
title: try: attach prose-check evidence for docs-changing attempts

cli/cmd/cleanup.go:64-134 delegates to the cleanup manager and prints the preservation summary, but there is no end-to-end test that seeds all three published-evidence forms and verifies cleanup leaves them untouched.

PROPOSED FIX

NON-SCOPE

description:
PROBLEM
No frontend component renders the ArtifactType definitions for an artifact. When an artifact matches a type definition (by prefix), the operator has no UI surface to view the associated template, prompt, and examples. The detail-view of any artifact is missing the type-aware panel.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/ contains artifact detail pages but no ArtifactTypePanel.svelte component.
- The typeDefinitions resolver (ddx-9ca4b5bf, a dep) provides the data, but nothing in the frontend consumes it.
- On prefix collision (multiple type definitions matching the same prefix), no selector dropdown or ?typeDef= URL state exists.
- prefixOf utility at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/artifacts/grouping.ts (from AC #10 of parent) is the expected import source.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/ArtifactTypePanel.svelte:
  - Tabbed layout: Reference Prompt | Template | Examples tabs (labels neutral, not 'Generate from').
  - On prefix collision: selector dropdown with ?typeDef= URL round-trip.
  - Imports prefixOf from artifacts/grouping.ts (single source of truth).
- Integrate ArtifactTypePanel into the artifact detail-view page.
- Add vitest unit tests for the component.

NON-SCOPE
- The typeDefinitions GraphQL resolver (that's ddx-9ca4b5bf, a dep).
- Generating artifacts from a prompt (not in this bead; labels must say 'Reference Prompt').

description:
# PROBLEM
The legacy `*Store.MigrateToAxon` method (now exposed via `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` in MIG bead, returning `ErrDeprecated`) writes JSONL files under `.ddx/axon/` rather than performing an actual JSONL→Postgres migration. Per the audit in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`, production AxonStore deployment requires migrating existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl` corpora into Lakebase Postgres. The real importer is unimplemented.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/migrate.go` (function previously at `*Store.MigrateToAxon`, now folded into `*storeMigrator` per MIG bead) writes JSONL into a sidecar directory. The function does not connect to Postgres or invoke `AxonStore.Apply`/`AxonStore.Create`. Production deployment cannot use it to populate Lakebase.

# PROPOSED FIX

1. Implement a real importer in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent post-BL location). The importer:
   - Reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` if present) — uses existing JSONL parser.
   - For each bead, calls `axonStore.Create(ctx, &b)` to insert into Postgres. Idempotent on `ErrConflict` (skip + log).
   - For each bead, appends events via `axonStore.Apply(ctx, id, AppendEventOp{...})` per TD-027 §13 vocabulary (events come from `Extra["events"]` on the JSONL bead or from sidecar `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/events.jsonl`).
   - Tracks progress, supports `--dry-run` (counts without writes), `--limit N` (batched migration).
   - Verifies post-migration: read back beads from Postgres, assert field-for-field equality with source JSONL (sample-based for large corpora).

2. Expose via `Migrator` interface (added in MIG): rename/replace `MigrateToAxon` with the real implementation; same method name, different (correct) behavior.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` to support the importer with appropriate flags:
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --dry-run`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --apply [--limit N]`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --verify` (read-back + checksum)

4. Remove the deprecated JSONL-writing behavior. `ErrDeprecated` return from MIG bead is replaced by the real implementation.

# NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder (separate concern; deferred until first v1→v2 breaking change).
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres — separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres — not in scope; this is one-shot migration.
- AxonStore itself — implemented in AX (`ddx-9c5bca8f`).

# Acceptance criteria

1. New file `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent) implements the JSONL→Postgres importer. Uses `axonStore.Create` + `axonStore.Apply` for inserts + event appends.
2. Existing `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` method returns the real importer's result (not `ErrDeprecated`).
3. CLI flags wired: `--to-axon --dry-run`, `--to-axon --apply`, `--to-axon --verify`. Test: `TestImporter_DryRun_CountsOnly`, `TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres`, `TestImporter_Verify_DetectsDrift`.
4. Idempotency: re-running `--apply` on an already-imported corpus is a no-op (skips conflicts). Test: `TestImporter_Apply_Idempotent_OnReRun`.
5. Field-fidelity: every JSONL bead field is preserved in Postgres, including `extra` preserved-keys. Test: `TestImporter_PreservesExtras`.
6. Event ordering: events imported preserve monotonic `created_at` (TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8). Test: `TestImporter_PreservesEventOrdering`.
7. Attachment migration: sidecar files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/` are uploaded to Unity Catalog Volumes (or local-equivalent for testing). Test: `TestImporter_MigratesAttachments`.
8. Verification mode: post-import, sample N beads, read back from AxonStore, assert equality with source. Test: `TestImporter_Verify_RoundTripPassesOnCleanImport`.
9. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green (with httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint or in-memory backend).
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

acceptance:
1. TP-020 title and scope describe DDx boundary/pass-through behavior over Fizeau routing, not DDx-owned route planning.
2. TP-020 test cases cover raw `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` passthrough, no DDx-side normalization, no DDx-side fuzzy matching, no DDx-side route selection, and config boundary rejection of retired routing defaults.
3. `rg -n "agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution|DDx-side routing planner|catalog ranking|fuzzy matching|route selection" docs/helix/03-test/test-plans docs/helix/01-frame docs/helix/02-design` returns only TP-020 historical/superseded-context hits or explicitly audited references.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/docgraph/... -run "TestDocValidateCommand|TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract" -count=1` passes.
5. `ddx doc validate` passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are listed in the bead notes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The parent Fizeau-boundary bead originally referenced the old TP-020 routing planner test plan. That filename has since moved to docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-020-fizeau-boundary-and-pass-through.md, but the plan still needs a focused verification pass so it is clearly a DDx boundary plan rather than a DDx-side routing planner plan.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-020-fizeau-boundary-and-pass-through.md:8-14 says the plan supersedes DDx-side routing planner coverage and now tests raw passthrough/no local routing guarantees.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-020-fizeau-boundary-and-pass-through.md:24-49 lists named tests for raw constraint passthrough, no DDx-side normalization, no DDx-side routing, and config boundary behavior.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-020-fizeau-boundary-and-pass-through.md:58-64 defines exit criteria that DDx forwards strings unchanged and leaves Fizeau routing to Fizeau-owned tests.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit TP-020 so it no longer asks DDx to test model catalog ranking, fuzzy matching, provider fallback, or concrete route selection.
- Ensure every listed test name maps to a DDx boundary invariant: parse/forward unchanged, do not normalize locally, do not route locally, and reject old DDx routing defaults.
- Update references to the retired TP-020 filename if any nearby docs still point at the old agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the tests in this bead; this is the plan-contract bead.
- Do not edit Fizeau-owned routing tests.
- Do not update generated website command pages.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-424fdac4. No deps; this is one decomposition child of the broad Fizeau-boundary ratification bead.

acceptance:
1. A new renderer/helper formats human work stdout lifecycle lines with `HH:MM:SS` timestamps and no subsecond precision.
2. `TestWorkLogRenderer_FormatLifecycleLine` covers a representative phase line such as readiness with timestamp, phase, message, and optional route/model fields.
3. `TestWorkLogRenderer_PreservesHeaderTitlePunctuation` proves a title like `spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle` remains punctuated as authored.
4. `TestWorkLogRenderer_SuppressesOnlyCurrentScopedBeadID` proves the current bead id is omitted from scoped follow-on lines while a different bead id is preserved.
5. Existing progress formatter tests still pass without changing Fizeau event schemas.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkLogRenderer'` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The `ddx work` stdout sequence is assembled by several call sites instead of one renderer, so timestamp shape, bead scoping, route/model display, and phase wording cannot be enforced consistently.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:722-737 writes lifecycle/header lines directly with `fmt.Fprintf`.
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go:135-144 formats service progress rows without timestamp, phase, or scoped bead awareness.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:185-195 writes route/progress entries directly to the same writer.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a reusable work stdout renderer for human `ddx work` output. It should own line timestamping (`HH:MM:SS`), current-bead scoping, phase labels, route/model formatting, and header/title formatting. Keep it small and local to agent/work stdout behavior so JSONL/event persistence remains untouched.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/work_log_renderer.go
- cli/internal/agent/work_log_renderer_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go only for narrow helper integration if needed

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rewrite all logging in the repository.
- Do not alter structured event timestamps or storage schemas.
- Do not change model routing decisions.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-eeced112. Depends on the stdout spec bead.

acceptance:
1. triage.ActionNeedsHuman is removed from canonical live policy in favor of an action whose lifecycle effect is status=proposed/operator-required.
2. Config values of needs_human fail validation after lifecycle migration with an actionable error; they are not silently accepted as runtime aliases.
3. Unknown policy fallback is explicit and documented; it does not silently recreate a needs_human lifecycle lane.
4. Default retry ladders bias toward autonomous repair/investigation before status=proposed where a smart-agent attempt is possible.
5. TestPolicy_UnknownModeFallsBackToNeedsHuman is replaced with a test for the new proposed/operator-required fallback behavior.
6. Config triage tests cover rejection of legacy needs_human action spelling and acceptance of the new canonical action spelling.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/triage/... ./internal/config/... -run 'TestPolicy|TestTriage|Test.*Triage' -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/triage/... ./internal/config/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The triage policy layer still exposes needs_human as a terminal action. That action name leaks into config, retry ladders, execution events, and review routing, so agent code can keep producing old lifecycle labels even after status=proposed is introduced.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/triage/triage.go:31-43 defines ActionNeedsHuman.
- cli/internal/triage/triage.go:53-77 ends default ladders in needs_human.
- cli/internal/triage/triage.go:86-100 falls unknown modes back to needs_human.
- cli/internal/triage/triage.go:103-115 parses needs_human from config/text.
- cli/internal/config/types.go:76-104 resolves triage policy actions from config, including existing needs_human fixtures.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go:9-13 defines TriageNeedsHumanLabel.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace needs_human with a canonical proposed/operator-required action whose lifecycle effect is status=proposed and no active needs_human label. Old needs_human config/action values are not normal compatibility aliases; after lifecycle migration they should fail validation with a clear upgrade/migration error. Default ladders must bias toward autonomous repair/investigation before status=proposed where smart-agent work is possible.

NON-SCOPE
Do not wire preclaim intake or terminal review parking in this bead except where they compile against the renamed triage action. Dedicated agent integration beads own those paths.

DEPS
Depends on store/query semantics because triage outcomes must target the new lifecycle buckets.
title: agent: replace needs-human triage action with proposed lifecycle action

description:
PROBLEM
No performance baseline exists for the documents/artifacts UI at realistic corpus size (2000 artifacts). Without a baseline, the caching work (ddx-4a7eed8c) cannot demonstrate improvement, and CI has no reference point for regression detection.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/ does not contain a scale fixture for 2000 artifacts.
- No measurement of first-paint cold/warm latency, scroll smoothness, or search latency at this scale has been recorded.
- ddx-b9993722 (FEAT-008 + TP-002 measurement contract, a dep) established the measurement methodology; this bead applies it to the 2k scale.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create a synthetic 2000-artifact fixture under cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/scale/.
- Measure: first paint cold/warm, scroll smoothness (frame rate under scroll), search latency (50ms goal from parent perf epic).
- Record baseline measurements in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/perf-baseline.md.
- Do NOT add a CI gate (baseline only — gate added in a later bead after caching lands).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching implementation (ddx-4a7eed8c).
- CI gate (deferred).

acceptance:
1. `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run` reports counts for needs_human labels, triage:needs-investigation labels, needs_investigation pseudo-statuses, rows that will become proposed/open/blocked/closed/cancelled, and whether the schema marker is missing.
2. `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` performs the one-way conversion through the lifecycle transition API, removes active legacy labels, records explanatory events/Extra, writes the lifecycle schema marker, and auto-commits tracker changes.
3. The migration is idempotent: a second apply reports no changes and preserves the schema marker.
4. TestLifecycleMigrate_NeedsHumanLabelBecomesProposed covers open+needs_human rows.
5. TestLifecycleMigrate_NeedsInvestigationSmartRunnableRemainsOpen covers investigation labels that should be rerun by a smart agent rather than parked.
6. TestLifecycleMigrate_NeedsInvestigationOperatorRequiredBecomesProposed covers investigation labels that genuinely require operator input.
7. TestLifecycleMigrate_ExternalBlockerBecomesBlocked covers rare external recheckable blockers and proves dependency-only waits do not become blocked.

description:
PROBLEM
Existing beads and lifecycle metadata can carry needs_human, triage:needs-investigation, no_changes_needs_investigation, cooldowns, and lifecycle_reconciled labels. Under the new state machine those labels cannot remain active runtime controls, and DDx startup must refuse to run until old queues are migrated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:8-27 defines lifecycle labels and extra keys including LabelNeedsInvestigation and LabelNeedsHuman.
- cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:156-223 adds or clears needs-investigation labels/cooldown metadata during lifecycle reconciliation.
- cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:281-284 includes triage labels in currentLifecycleFields.
- cli/internal/bead/types.go:206-216 defines BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation and BlockerKindNeedsHuman as first-class blocker kinds.
- cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:10-108 and cli/internal/bead/migrate.go:23-40 implement archive/event/backend migration but no one-way lifecycle migration.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a one-way lifecycle migration command/path, for example `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run|--apply`, that converts legacy lifecycle labels/metadata into status-owned state. open+needs_human and equivalent human-required outcomes become status=proposed with reason/suggested-action metadata. triage:needs-investigation is removed as an active lane; each row becomes status=proposed when operator input is required, remains status=open when autonomous smart-agent work should proceed, becomes status=blocked only for external recheckable blockers, or terminal status where evidence already proves closed/cancelled. After apply, record a lifecycle schema marker used by the startup gate. This is not backwards compatibility: old labels are migration input only.

NON-SCOPE
Do not preserve needs_human or triage:needs-investigation as worker-exclusion labels. Do not auto-apply migration at startup. Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` outside the migration command.

DEPS
Depends on store/query wiring and transition write API so migration output validates against the new queues.
title: bead: migrate legacy lifecycle labels to status-owned states

description:
PROBLEM
The lifecycle contract must specify how existing repositories move from label-owned lifecycle state to status-owned lifecycle state. Without a hard startup gate, DDx can start against an old queue, reinterpret stale needs_human / triage:needs-investigation labels under new code, and either run beads that were parked under old semantics or keep old queue lanes alive indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:110-120 constructs the root command without any project-local bead queue format preflight.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:67-92 chooses store backend/prefix but does not identify legacy lifecycle queue state before callers read or mutate beads.
- cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:10-108 currently defines `ddx bead migrate` only for event externalization/archive and axon backend migration.
- cli/internal/bead/migrate.go:23-40 implements archive/event migration, not lifecycle status migration.
- The live tracker currently contains open beads with needs_human and triage:needs-investigation labels, so this is not a hypothetical upgrade path.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the governing docs with a one-way lifecycle migration contract. DDx startup must detect unmigrated lifecycle queues and refuse to run normal commands until migration is applied. The allowed escape hatches are only diagnostic/help/version commands and the lifecycle migration command itself. The migration is one-way: old labels and pseudo-statuses are input to the migrator, not a compatibility surface for normal runtime. Because beads are git-tracked, rollback is git rollback rather than maintaining dual semantics.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the startup gate or migrator in this docs bead. Do not preserve needs_human or triage:needs-investigation as active runtime compatibility aliases.

DEPS
Depends on the base lifecycle contract bead so this amendment lands after the six-status state machine spec edit.
title: docs: require one-way lifecycle migration and startup gate

acceptance:

4. attempted/hookFailed maps deleted; pick derives exclusion from drain.result.
5. Routing preflight runs once at Drain startup, never per iteration.
6. ddx work mode semantics preserved: default is drain-until-no-ready-work; --once runs one ready bead then exits; --watch stays alive and sleeps for the idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist; --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error; no public compatibility alias remains for the removed polling flag.
7. ddx try <bead-id> CLI command exists.

10. Server worker integration (cli/internal/server/) compiles and behaves identically except for the explicit once/drain/watch mode contract; tests green at every phase.
11. ddx-c6e3db02, ddx-b24e9630, ddx-cfedee8e, ddx-a8718bec, ddx-dc157075, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, and ddx-dc300be9 behaviors preserved per C5/C9/C10 absorption ACs.
