description:
ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() is a 700-line function with 24 returns: 4 legitimate terminal exits, 1 legacy idle-loop exit, 1 routing-preflight-kills-worker exit, and 18 store-error escalations from the same handleOutcomeStoreError pattern repeated across outcome paths. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick -> Gate -> Claim -> Execute -> Classify -> Reviewing -> Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.

## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010

  cli/internal/agent/run/   - Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)
  cli/internal/agent/try/   - Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)
  cli/internal/agent/work/  - Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)

try.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes approximately 30 lines: pick -> guard -> claim -> attemptWithHeartbeat -> applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.

## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision

In-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. The stdout/watch-mode beads (ddx-7d9e1e82, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, ddx-dc300be9) define the public work-loop contract this refactor must preserve. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.

## Naming and public semantics

Package = try (not try_bead - Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface is ddx run / ddx try / ddx work. ddx work has three modes: once (one ready bead then exit), drain (default: run ready work until no execution-ready beads remain, then exit), and watch (stay alive indefinitely, sleep for idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist, and poll for newly arriving work). The removed polling flag is not preserved as a public compatibility alias; the durable vocabulary is mode plus idle_interval.

## Children

  C0  Pre-refactor JSONL fixture baseline for event-shape regression detection
  C1  try.Outcome sealed sum type + Disposition enum + ParkReason vocabulary enumeration
  C2  try.Attempt function shell (wraps existing executor; no behavior change)
  C3  Move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)
  C4  Move conflict recovery into try.Attempt
  C5  Move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict (absorbs ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)
  C6  Single commitOutcome helper; deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError
  C7  Uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps; pick() derives from drain.result
  C8  Routing preflight moves to Drain startup (runs once, not per iteration)
  C9  First-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation + downstream-consumer audit for execution_failed:max-cost grep before relocation. Includes queue snapshot plumbing and human-review blocker pressure from ddx-7dcbd40b.
  C10 ddx work once/drain/watch modes + transient idle substate. Watch mode owns idle interval sleeps, per-idle queue status, and next-ready transition messaging from ddx-f9acb86d and ddx-8b7ed960.
  C11 Heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper
  C12 Single phase-event emission (replaces 4 sites)
  C13 File and identifier rename per package split + ADD ddx try CLI command + handle dc157075 file-path migration if it landed first
  C14 Delete ExecuteBead* shims; verify all callers ported off (grep audit)
notes: Fresh-eyes cleanup: removed the old compatibility-alias/back-compat requirement from the refactor epic and aligned it with once/drain/watch plus idle_interval semantics.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Context is king · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Use ddx bead commands for tracker changes · Preserve audit evidence · Keep prompts fit for execution and self-contained · Go implementation follow-up must use real/stubbed domain interfaces and go test plus lefthook gates</practices>
<governing>FEAT-010 / ADR-023 readiness policy currently permits safe intent-preserving updates before claim; this evolve pass specifies the stronger validated replacement rewrite contract so readiness can improve prompt fitness while preserving explicit commitments.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Safe rewrite policy is currently ambiguous. The append-only preservation guard prevents deletion of stale or noisy wording, but appending more text can make future bead prompts longer and more confusing. DDx needs specs that define validated replacement rewrites as a first-class readiness capability, including both expansion of underspecified beads and compression of noisy/stale beads.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:176-196 defines actionable_but_rewritten but does not say replacement rewrites are allowed or how they are validated.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:136-144 says readiness may update beads but does not distinguish append-only amendments from validated replacement rewrites.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:17-26 describes safe rewrites but does not require prompt fitness or audit evidence for replaced text.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:392-407 names readiness transitions but does not model successful replacement rewrite as an open-to-open readiness mutation with evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the governing specs to define safe rewrites as validated replacement rewrites: replacement bead text is allowed when a deterministic validator preserves intent, acceptance criteria, non-scope, durable evidence, and unresolved product choices; original text is retained in audit evidence, not duplicated into the prompt body. Replacement can expand an underspecified bead or compress a noisy one when prompt fitness requires it.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement code changes in this evolve pass.
- Do not relax acceptance criteria preservation.
- Do not permit rewrites that choose missing product semantics or delete unresolved conflicts.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: none. No deps.
notes:
<measure-results>
- FEAT-010 updated to define validated replacement safe rewrites, prompt fitness, durable-anchor validation, and audit evidence.
- ADR-023 updated to prefer fit-for-purpose replacement over append-only amendment when it improves the implementation prompt.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md updated with operational intake contract for replacement rewrites.
- TD-031 updated with open-to-open readiness rewrite transition and evidence requirements.
- Downstream beads created: ddx-a41e6fbb (validated replacement validator/prompt/evidence) and ddx-d5ae126c (park rejected rewrite attempts).
- Verification: lefthook run pre-commit passed for staged docs; commit ef22894a1.
</measure-results>
title: evolve: specify validated safe rewrites

description:
PROBLEM
DDx-created temporary execution resources do not have one shared cleanup owner. Inline attempt cleanup, orphan recovery, and liveness cleanup are spread across attempt code and ad hoc test helpers, which let stale /tmp/ddx-exec-wt directories and registered orphan worktrees accumulate until /tmp ran out of inodes.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:257-279 defines the DDx temp worktree root and naming convention, but there is no cleanup manager that owns that root.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:296-324 exposes WorktreeAdd/Remove/List/Prune primitives, but no production code uses them to reconcile stale registered vs unregistered DDx worktrees before attempts.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:16-29 records one live attempt path, but it does not model cleanup ownership, preserved state, stale PID/session liveness, or partial setup directories.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:202-266 defines the cleanup manager roots, ownership metadata, entry points, and conservative deletion rules now missing in code.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a focused cleanup manager under cli/internal/agent, e.g. execution_cleanup.go, with injectable filesystem/git/liveness probes for tests.
- Teach it to inspect the configured DDx temp worktree root, project git worktree list, run-state/liveness files, and legacy .ddx/executions/.ddx/runs evidence roots.
- Implement conservative deletion: remove only DDx-owned stale unregistered dirs, stale registered DDx worktrees, stale liveness files, and partial setup dirs; preserve published evidence, preserve refs, and active liveness.
- Return a structured cleanup summary with removed path counts, bytes/inodes where measurable, warnings, and blocked errors.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not wire the manager into ddx try/work loops here; that is covered by sibling beads.
- Do not add the operator CLI command here; that is covered by a sibling bead.
- Do not implement .ddx/executions to .ddx/runs migration.

The current cleanup tests in cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go:55-159 cover dry-run/apply and evidence preservation, and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_worktree_cleanup_test.go:48-65 only proves failed worktree setup removes a partial directory. That still leaves the real registered-worktree pruning path unexercised.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/cleanup.go:53-134 runs the production cleanup manager against the filesystem, but there is no command-level end-to-end test that seeds real git-tracked stale worktrees plus stale unregistered dirs and then proves the cleanup pass removes only the stale DDx-owned resources.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a cleanup end-to-end test in cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go or a sibling e2e test file that creates a temp project, a temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, and real stale registered + unregistered DDx worktree directories.

description:
PROBLEM
  The PostLadderExhaustionHook infrastructure (ddx-e012af90) routes TooLarge
  failure class to a DECOMPOSE path, but no decomposer prompt template and no
  decomposer dispatch logic exist. When the hook fires for TooLarge (or as a
  fallback from reframe failure on PersistentExecutionFailed), it must dispatch
  a strong-tier agent to produce 2-5 child bead specs, then file them via
  Store.Create with the parent linked.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has PostAttemptDecompositionHook for
  the no_changes/orchestrator_action:decompose path, but no general decomposer
  for post-ladder-exhaustion scenarios.
  library/prompts/agents/decomposer.md does not exist.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Add library/prompts/agents/decomposer.md defining:
     - Input contract: original bead body and failure history (same as reframer).
     - Output contract: JSON array of 2-5 child specs, each with: title (string),
       description (string), acceptance (string), labels ([]string).
     - Role guidance: each child must be independently executable, ~3-6 ACs,
       with explicit deps if ordering required.
  2. Add decomposer dispatch in cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:
     - runDecomposer(ctx, store, executor, beadID, failureHistory) → DecomposeResult
     - Dispatches a smart+ tier agent.
     - Parses JSON array output; validates 1-5 children, each passes intake
       validation (non-empty title, description, acceptance).
     - Creates children via store.Create with Parent=beadID.
     - Sets execution-eligible=false on parent via bead.Extra key.
     - Emits decompose-applied event with child IDs and costUSD.
  3. Failure modes explicitly handled (each returns DecomposeResult{failed:true}):
     - Timeout exceeded.
     - Empty output.
     - <1 or >5 children in parsed result.

acceptance:
1. .github/workflows/ci.yml contains a step that runs go test -json on the full suite (or a dedicated job that does so) and uploads the JSON output as a GitHub Actions artifact.
2. The artifact retention is at least 30 days (verify in the upload-artifact step config).
3. (Optional but encouraged) cli/tools/test-timing/ contains a small parser that emits a top-N-slowest-tests Markdown summary, also uploaded as an artifact.
4. CLAUDE.md or docs/helix/06-iterate/ documents the convention and where to find the artifact.
5. The CI job completes successfully on a test PR and the artifact is downloadable.
6. cd cli && go test ./... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Today no test runner emits durable timing data. lefthook pre-push,
  pre-commit, Makefile test target, and the GitHub Actions ci.yml
  full-suite step all run `go test ./...` and emit elapsed-time only to
  stdout. There is no JUnit, no `go test -json`, no aggregated artifact,
  no dashboard. The result: when a test gets slow (e.g.
  TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel becomes a 60s test instead of a 10s test),
  the regression goes unnoticed until a developer manually inspects
  pre-push output. Splitting into unit / integration / e2e tiers is hard
  without baseline timing data.

CURRENT STATE
  - .github/workflows/ci.yml runs `go test -race -cover ./...` and
    emits stdout only.
  - .github/workflows/ci.yml uploads two artifacts (e2e-test-output,
    prompt-size-report) but neither is structured timing data.
  - No `go test -json` invocation anywhere in the repository.
  - Makefile has no test-with-json target.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a CI step (in .github/workflows/ci.yml, in the existing test
    job or a new test-timing job) that runs:
        cd cli && go test -json -race -cover ./... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-output.json
    Then upload /tmp/test-output.json as a GitHub Actions artifact named
    `test-timing-<run-id>.json` retained for at least 30 days.
  - Optionally add a small Go program under cli/tools/test-timing/ that
    parses go-test-json events and emits a top-N-slowest-tests Markdown
    summary; write the summary as `test-timing-summary.md` and upload
    alongside.
  - Document in CLAUDE.md: "CI uploads test timing as a JSON artifact;
    use it to investigate slow-test regressions."
  - Do NOT modify the lefthook hooks — local pre-push timing is not
    durable and a JSON dump there would just clutter the developer's
    machine.

NON-SCOPE
  - Building a test-timing dashboard or a regression-detection alarm
    (separate concern; this bead just produces durable data).
  - JUnit format (the audit recommended JSON; JUnit is a separate
    concern if test-result reporters are needed).
  - Any change to `go test` invocation in lefthook.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Independent of all sister beads. Composes with the perf-tag and
    -short-gate beads (this bead measures; those beads optimize).
title: ci: emit go test -json across the test suite and upload as artifact for timing visibility

acceptance:
1. `PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks()` (or equivalent code path) writes a `pre_merge_check_failed` event onto the bead with check name, exit code, and output excerpt.
2. The event surfaces in `ddx bead evidence list <bead-id>` and `ddx bead show <bead-id>` events list.
3. After preservation, result.json carries either an updated status or a new `orchestrator_status` field that distinguishes worker success from final landing failure.
4. Unit/integration test covers: worker returns task_succeeded → pre-merge check blocks → evidence event present, iteration ref written, bead unclaimed for retry.
description:
Context:
When `SubmitWithPreMergeChecks()` (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:131-178) calls `RunPreMergeChecks()` and the outcome is Blocked, `PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks()` writes the result under `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<basesha>` and skips the merge. The bead's evidence list never receives a `pre_merge_check_failed` event with the check name, output, or rationale.

This made a real incident on 2026-05-08 invisible: bead `fizeau-bd5193ca` (in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) was redriven 4 times across ~50 minutes / ~$9 of agent cost. Each attempt's result.json reported `outcome: task_succeeded, status: success` (worker classification, written before the gate) and the orchestrator silently preserved the result. `ddx bead evidence list fizeau-bd5193ca` only showed routing + cost events; `ddx bead show` had no hint that pre-merge checks had blocked anything. Operator had no way to discover the rejection without reading orchestrator source.

Reproduction:
1. Claim any bead via `ddx work` whose worker output trips a pre-merge check.
2. Observe result.json status=success, iteration ref preserved, bead remains open, evidence list devoid of pre-merge-check info.

Proposed fix:
- Emit a structured `pre_merge_check_failed` evidence event from `PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks()` (or its caller) with at least: check_id/name, exit_code, stdout/stderr tail, attempt_id, base_rev, result_rev. Append via the same evidence path used by routing/cost events so it shows up in `ddx bead evidence list` and `ddx bead show`.
- The orchestrator's terminal status (post worker + gate) should also be reflected back into result.json's status field — currently it stays `success` regardless of the gate outcome, which is misleading. Either rewrite result.json with the orchestrator's verdict, or add a separate `orchestrator_status` field.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go contains a PostLadderExhaustionHook (or equivalent) that fires when consecutive_ladder_exhaustions >= 2 OR when triggered by per_bead_budget_exhausted (per sister bead ddx-8645c5d4 chain).
2. The hook respects the recovery:manual label (skip auto-recovery; park to proposed directly).
3. The hook routes to REFRAME or DECOMPOSE based on failure class (review_spec_gap → reframe; review_too_large → decompose; persistent execution_failed → reframe first then decompose).
4. library/prompts/agents/reframer.md and library/prompts/agents/decomposer.md exist with input + output contracts.
5. The reframer dispatch uses a strong-tier agent (smart+); the decomposer dispatch uses a strong-tier agent.
6. Reframer failure modes are explicitly defined (any of these counts as failure): timeout exceeded, empty output, structurally-invalid edits (cannot parse), no-op edits (description and AC unchanged).
7. Decomposer failure modes are explicitly defined: timeout exceeded, empty output, returning <1 or >5 children, malformed child specs (failing intake validation).
8. Circuit-breaker: if recovery hooks (across both reframer and decomposer attempts) accumulate cost > $2 (configurable via --max-recovery-cost), abort recovery and route to status=proposed with auto-recovery-failed event including total recovery cost.
9. Reframe success: bead description/AC updated via actionable_but_rewritten code path; status=open; consecutive_ladder_exhaustions cleared; reframe-applied event emitted.
10. Decompose success: 2-5 child beads created via Store.Create with parent linked; parent execution-eligible=false; decompose-applied event emitted.
11. Recovery hooks consume per-bead budget; per-bead budget exhaustion takes precedence over auto-recovery (falls through to per_bead_budget_exhausted).
12. Only when reframe AND decompose both fail (and per-bead budget remains, and circuit-breaker not tripped) does the bead park to status=proposed with auto-recovery-failed event.
13. TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersReframe, TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose_ReviewTooLarge, TestReframeFailure_FallsBackToDecompose, TestBothFail_ParkProposed, TestRecoveryEvents_StructuredOutput, TestRecoveryManualLabel_SkipsAutoRecovery, TestConsecutiveLadderExhaustionsCounter, TestReframerTimeout_CountsAsFailure, TestReframerNoOpEdits_CountsAsFailure, TestDecomposerInvalidChildren_CountsAsFailure, TestRecoveryCircuitBreaker_AbortsAtCostCap, TestPerBeadBudgetExhausted_TriggersAutoRecovery all pass.
14. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
15. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Today, when a bead persistently fails (ladder exhausted on
  multiple drain cycles, repair cycles exhausted, or
  review_spec_gap / review_too_large fires), the only path is
  status=proposed → operator action.

  ADR-024 P5 (no human-in-the-loop, sister bead rank 19) requires
  automatic recovery: before parking to proposed, the system must
  attempt automatic reframe (rewrite description + AC) AND/OR
  decompose (split into child beads). Only if both fail does the
  bead reach proposed.

  Today only intake-time reframe (PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten
  in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go) can rewrite the bead.
  Post-failure reframe does not exist. Decomposition only fires
  for review_too_large, not for "implementer keeps failing"
  patterns.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go: intake can apply
    actionable_but_rewritten edits. ONE-shot, before claim.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has hooks like
    PostAttemptDecompositionHook for review_too_large but NOT a
    general post-ladder-exhaustion hook.
  - status=proposed is the default escape hatch for spec gaps,
    too-large, ladder-exhausted, repair-cycle-exhausted.
  - No consecutive_ladder_exhaustions counter; the system can't
    distinguish "first ladder exhaustion" from "third".
  - No reframer or decomposer prompt templates exist.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a new post-failure recovery pipeline. After ladder exhaustion
  triggers consecutive_ladder_exhaustions >= 2 (per TD-031 §6 new
  field, sister bead rank 19):

  1. Determine the failure class:
     - review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance → REFRAME path.
     - review_too_large or repeated execution_failed at top tier →
       DECOMPOSE path.
     - Other persistent execution_failed → REFRAME first, then if
       reframe doesn't help on next cycle, DECOMPOSE.

  2. Operator opt-out: if bead has label "recovery:manual",
     skip auto-recovery entirely and park to status=proposed
     (matches ADR-024 Operator escape hatches subsection).

  3. REFRAME path:
     - Add prompt template at library/prompts/agents/reframer.md
       — defines the reframer role's input contract (bead body +
       failure history) and output contract (structured edits to
       description and AC).
     - Dispatch a strong-tier (smart+) agent with role=reframer.
     - Apply the edits via the same actionable_but_rewritten path
       intake uses (preserves history, records reframe-applied
       event per TD-031 §5 row from sister bead rank 19).
     - Set status=open; clear consecutive_ladder_exhaustions;
       bead re-enters queue at next cycle.

  4. DECOMPOSE path:
     - Add prompt template at library/prompts/agents/decomposer.md
       — defines the decomposer role's input contract (bead body
       + failure history) and output contract (2-5 child bead
       specs in JSON).
     - Dispatch a strong-tier (smart+) agent with role=decomposer.
     - File the children via Store.Create (with parent set), wire
       deps, set parent's execution-eligible=false.
     - Existing parent → status=open with execution-eligible=false
       so the children get worked first.
     - Emit decompose-applied event per TD-031 §5 row.

  5. Both paths emit structured events (TD-031 §5 rows) with:
     from-rev, to-rev (if any), reframer-cost, reviewer-confidence-score.

  6. ESCAPE HATCH: only if reframe AND decompose BOTH fail (e.g.,
     reframer agent times out, decomposer returns no children) does
     the bead reach status=proposed with auto-recovery-failed event.

  7. Cost: recovery-hook costs accumulate against the per-bead
     budget (sister bead rank 22). If budget exhausts during
     recovery, fall through to per_bead_budget_exhausted (NOT
     status=proposed).

  Tests:
     - TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersReframe
     - TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose_ReviewTooLarge
     - TestReframeFailure_FallsBackToDecompose
     - TestBothFail_ParkProposed
     - TestRecoveryEvents_StructuredOutput
     - TestRecoveryManualLabel_SkipsAutoRecovery
     - TestConsecutiveLadderExhaustionsCounter

NON-SCOPE
  - The intake-time reframe path (existing).
  - Manual reframe / decompose tools (operators can still trigger
    via CLI; this is automation, not replacement).
  - Tuning when "persistent" triggers (start with N=2 cycles).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(specs)" rank 19 — ADR-024 P4, TD-031 §5
    new rows, TD-031 §6 reframer/decomposer roles + counter,
    SD-025 Layer 3.5, FEAT-010 auto-recovery requirement.
  - Sister bead "per-bead budget" rank 22 — recovery hooks count
    against the budget.
  - Sister bead "repair-cycle escalation" rank 23 — only triggers
    AFTER repair-cycle exhaustion AND ladder exhaustion.
  - Existing intake actionable_but_rewritten code path is the
    template for the apply-edits step.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx service-run code still understands too much of Fizeau's inner event stream. It should forward/store Fizeau ServiceEvents opaquely and adapt only the final data needed for DDx Result bookkeeping.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:243-355 switches on raw service-event names and reconstructs transcript/progress/tool-call records.

PROPOSED FIX
Collapse DDx's Fizeau service consumption to two responsibilities: store/forward opaque ServiceEvents unchanged, and decode only final-result/routing/session artifact fields required to populate DDx Result and indexes. Prefer Fizeau typed decoders such as agentlib.DecodeServiceEvent where decoding is necessary; remove local stringly-typed event constants and custom partial structs that duplicate Fizeau schema.

description:
PROBLEM
The May 7 incident cleanup exposed a local Git configuration corruption: the primary checkout's .git/config had core.worktree pointing at /tmp/ddx-c2a66f45-wt. After that, git status and git commit run from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx evaluated a detached temp worktree instead of the real checkout. ddx doctor and pre-commit validation should fail fast on this class before hooks or commits operate on the wrong worktree.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:111 runs Git Repo Health, and cli/cmd/doctor.go:721-839 documents checks for core.bare, stray core.worktree, and extensions.worktreeConfig.
- lefthook.yml:291-302 runs ddx doctor in the ddx-validate pre-commit hook, but the hook did not protect the operator from a primary checkout whose local core.worktree redirected Git operations to /tmp/ddx-c2a66f45-wt.
- cli/internal/git/exec.go:20-48 scrubs inherited Git environment variables, but local .git/config core.worktree is persistent repo state, not an inherited environment variable.

PROPOSED FIX
- Harden ddx doctor so it detects a primary checkout .git/config core.worktree value that points anywhere other than the physical current checkout root, even when Git itself reports the redirected worktree as top-level.
- Add a pre-commit-facing fast check or ensure ddx-validate surfaces the doctor failure before other hooks run expensive tests.
- Keep ddx doctor --fix behavior for safely unsetting stray core.worktree.
- Add regression tests that create a repo with .git/config core.worktree pointing at a different temp worktree and prove doctor reports git_stray_core_worktree from the physical invocation root.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change execute-bead worktree creation policy.
- Do not change normal linked worktree support when extensions.worktreeConfig is enabled and the command is actually running inside that linked worktree.
- Do not remove existing Git environment scrubbing.

DEPS
Depends on the incident evidence containment bead so the repo is clean enough to validate hook behavior.
title: doctor: fail on primary checkout core.worktree redirects

description:
PROBLEM
DDx currently duplicates Fizeau policy intent with numeric power on initial zero-config implementation dispatch. In the current code path, DDx selects the `default` Fizeau policy for ordinary implementation work and then also sets `MinPower` to that policy floor. That makes the request shape `Policy=default, MinPower=7` instead of letting Fizeau fully interpret the selected policy.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:396-401` selects an implementation profile and raises `initialMinPower` to `selection.MinPower` on the first attempt. `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:426-442` then passes `requestedMinPower` into `singleTierAttempt`, which writes it into `config.CLIOverrides.MinPower` at `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:289-294`. `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:241-258` sends both `Policy` and `MinPower` to Fizeau. The same first-attempt pattern exists for `ddx try` at `cli/cmd/try.go:293-322`. Current command tests assert this duplicated shape in `cli/cmd/work_test.go` and `cli/cmd/try_test.go`, so the tests encode the wrong contract.

DESIRED ROUTING CONTRACT
For initial zero-config implementation work, DDx should select an opaque Fizeau policy by metadata and send only that policy. Example: ordinary work selects `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`. Fizeau owns the model choice and may choose a lower-power model inside that policy if the policy permits it.

For implementation fallback escalation, DDx should keep policy intent and add a numeric floor only after evidence of failure. Example: DDx selects `Policy=default`; Fizeau chooses a power-5 model; the attempt fails; DDx retries with `Policy=default, MinPower=6`. If repeated failures or ladder exhaustion require moving policy bands, DDx may reselect the appropriate policy by metadata, but numeric floors are evidence-driven retry constraints, not initial policy duplication.

For review routing, DDx may use `MinPower` immediately when the reviewer must be stronger than the implementation route or when review pairing evidence requires it.

PROPOSED FIX
Change zero-config implementation routing so first attempts do not copy the selected policy floor into `MinPower`. Preserve explicit operator `--min-power`, bead numeric tier hints, reviewer escalation, and retry escalation. On retry, derive the next floor from the actual route power when available; otherwise use the selected policy floor/ladder only as an escalation basis, not as an initial dispatch floor.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hard-code Fizeau policy names. Do not add DDx-side provider/model selection. Do not change Fizeau policy definitions. Do not remove MinPower support for explicit user flags, review escalation, or implementation retry escalation.
title: routing: send MinPower only for review and fallback escalation

description:
PROBLEM
  The DDx skill tree is still managed through parallel bootstrap copies instead of a single package-owned source. The governing plan at docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:16-23 requires the default `ddx` plugin package to own the DDx skill, requires `ddx init` and `ddx plugin install ddx` to share one package installer path, and keeps home-directory skill installs out of scope.

ROOT CAUSE
  Current state is split across several bootstrap paths: cli/cmd/init.go:256-287 copies embedded skills with `skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, ...)`, registers `.ddx/skills/ddx` separately, then installs the default plugin through `registry.InstallPackage`; cli/internal/registry/installer.go:110-132 handles package skills by calling `skills.Install` directly instead of applying manifest skill mappings; cli/cmd/install.go:254-372 has a separate local symlink implementation; cli/Makefile:65-84 keeps copied skill mirrors in sync. The plan requires these paths to collapse into package install semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
  Break the migration into child beads that first make package install semantics correct, then route init through the embedded package installer, then move the skill source into the default package layout, and finally update docs/specs/help.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not execute the child implementation work in this epic bead.
  - Do not restore global or home-directory skill installs.
  - Do not change public plugin CLI names beyond the separate CLI rationalization work.

PARENT + DEPS
  Root epic. No deps.
title: skills package: migrate DDx skill ownership to the default plugin package

description:

Update process metrics to parse and account for all six lifecycle statuses. Reports should preserve existing fields where needed and add proposed/blocked/cancelled timing and transition counts. description:
PROBLEM
  `ddx init` must install the default `ddx` package offline from embedded package content, using the same installer semantics as `ddx plugin install ddx`. Today the binary embeds a special skill-only tree instead of the default package layout.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/registry/installer.go:20-185 only installs packages by downloading a GitHub tarball. cli/cmd/init.go:276-299 works around offline/default-package needs by separately copying embedded skills and then attempting `registry.InstallPackage(pkg, workingDir)`, which downloads package content and may fail offline. cli/Makefile:65-84 syncs `skills/ddx` into `cli/internal/skills/ddx` for `go:embed`, not a package-rooted `library/` tree. The plan requires `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, sourceFS, projectRoot)` or equivalent embedded-package support rooted at the default package.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add an embedded default-package FS under the registry package, rooted at the package layout that will be copied from `library/`.
  - Split package install entrypoints into remote, local-dir, and embedded-FS paths that share the same core manifest/mapping implementation.
  - Preserve the existing `InstallPackage(pkg, projectRoot)` public wrapper as the remote path only if callers still need it during the migration.
  - Add offline tests proving an embedded package install can create `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` without network.

NON-SCOPE
  - Rewriting `ddx init` to call the embedded package installer; a dependent bead owns the init call site.
  - Moving the source skill tree into `library/skills/ddx`; a later bead owns the content move.
  - Removing old embedded skill files before offline init is green.

PARENT + DEPS
  Parent: ddx-6862f2ac. Depends on the registry mapping bead so embedded installs use the same mapping implementation.
title: registry: install default package from embedded package FS

description:
PROBLEM
Long-running DDx entrypoints can start work even when the project-local skill install is broken. In /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, post-attempt triage logged `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)` only after an execution failed. The operator had to infer that the project still had a pre-migration skill layout and needed `ddx update --force` / `ddx doctor`.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/work.go:106` delegates directly to `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl` without a lightweight runtime preflight. `cli/cmd/try.go:102-120` resolves execution flags and proceeds toward store/runtime setup without checking project-local skill prerequisites. `cli/cmd/server.go:98-146` starts the server without surfacing degraded project-local skill layout. `cli/cmd/doctor.go:281-287` already detects legacy skill symlinks and prints `run: ddx update --force`, but that diagnostic is only available when the operator explicitly runs `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a shared lightweight project-runtime preflight helper for long-running entrypoints. It should reuse existing doctor/install helper logic where practical, but must not run the full `ddx doctor` checks.
- Wire the helper into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, compatibility `ddx agent execute-loop`, and `ddx server` before execution begins.
- For work/try/execute-loop: fail on missing required project/tracker state, warn once per process for optional lifecycle-hook degradation in WARN-ONLY mode, and include remediation.
- For server: fail on required server project state, but log degraded startup diagnostics for missing lifecycle skills or legacy skill symlink layout.
- Checked paths for `bead-lifecycle` must be `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` under the resolved project root.
- Remediation text must include `ddx update --force` first, then `ddx doctor`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add network, package-manager, route/provider, stale-doc, or Fizeau health probes to this preflight.
- Do not change bead-lifecycle hook classification semantics.
- Do not make `ddx server` refuse to start solely because `bead-lifecycle` is missing.
- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually; tracker mutation is handled by this bead record only.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
Stopping a live `ddx work --watch` process while an implementation attempt is in flight can dirty `.ddx/beads.jsonl` with misleading terminal/no-progress metadata. Observed on 2026-05-14: interrupting workers appended `disruption_detected`, `execution-routing-intent`, `no_changes_unjustified`, `execute-bead`, and `loop-error` events to active beads, and wrote `execute-loop-last-status=loop-error` plus `execute-loop-retry-after` on at least one bead. Operator cancellation is not model evidence and must not create queue noise or cooldowns.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:277-296 adjudicates any report whose status is `no_changes`, even if the parent worker context has already been canceled. That can emit `no_changes_unjustified` for an interrupted attempt that never produced a meaningful no-changes rationale.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1877-1893 marks context-canceled attempts as disrupted, but then continues through normal outcome handling, including routing-intent evidence and no-changes handling.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2001-2047 applies no-changes unclaim/event/label mutations before checking whether the worker context was canceled.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2249-2265 appends the late `execute-bead` event and may write loop-error cooldown metadata after the attempt has already been interrupted.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce an interrupted-attempt path that, once `ctx.Err()` is set before durable terminal outcome handling, only releases claim/liveness and returns the cancellation. It may emit in-memory/server progress, but it must not write terminal attempt evidence, no-changes labels/events, retry cooldowns, or loop-error metadata to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. Preserve successful terminal mutations that completed before cancellation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not suppress legitimate model no_changes results when the worker context is still active. Do not remove disruption classification for transport/provider errors. Do not change successful close semantics. Do not manually edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
title: work: suppress tracker mutations after interrupted attempts

acceptance:

Add `TestWorkerProbe_PeriodicHeartbeatRefreshesServerLastEventAt` (under `cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/...` or `cli/internal/server/...`): with a reachable httptest server, a simulated long-running attempt with a short heartbeat tick produces multiple `worker.heartbeat` POSTs to `/api/workers/<id>/event`; the server-derived `last_event_at` (per `worker_ingest.go:110-120`) advances between claim and the eventual `bead.result`.
2. Add `TestWorkerProbe_ServerUnavailableDoesNotBlockWork`: with `AddrFunc` returning "" or a connection-refused server, the same heartbeat path produces no errors propagated to the attempt; no new failure status, cooldown, or stop is introduced; the attempt completes normally.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/workerprobe/... ./internal/agent/work/... ./internal/server/... -run "TestWorkerProbe_PeriodicHeartbeatRefreshesServerLastEventAt|TestWorkerProbe_ServerUnavailableDoesNotBlockWork" -count=1` green.

description:
PROBLEM
The workerprobe mirrors loop events via `TeeJSONL`, so during a long-running attempt with no intermediate loop event, the server's `last_event_at` stays at claim time. The server cannot distinguish an actively-working worker from a silent/stale one. The new periodic liveness mechanism must also fail open: when no server address is configured or the server refuses connections, work must continue without errors, status churn, new failure states, or cooldowns.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go:13-35` ticks but emits no event to the probe.
- `cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:209-228` `RecordEvent` already best-effort posts to `/api/workers/<id>/event` when connected and buffers when not — but nobody calls it for heartbeat-cadence events.
- `cli/internal/server/worker_ingest.go:110-120` updates `last_event_at` only when `/api/workers/<id>/event` receives an event.
- `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:174-186` connects the probe but only `TeeJSONL`-mirrored loop events flow through it.

PROPOSED FIX
Wire a probe-aware liveness emitter into the `WithHeartbeat` tick: on each tick, when a `*workerprobe.Probe` (or a minimal `RecordEvent`-shaped interface) is available, call `probe.RecordEvent(Event{Kind: "worker.heartbeat", BeadID: ..., AttemptID: ..., Body: <small JSON>})`. The body should be a compact envelope (worker_id, current_bead, attempt_id, phase, ts) — enough to refresh `last_event_at` server-side and provide minimal context. `RecordEvent` is already non-blocking and best-effort. When no addr / connection refused / no probe, the call must be silent (a single debug-level log line is acceptable) and must not propagate errors to the attempt path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the probe's connection lifecycle, register/backfill behavior, or backoff/jitter constants.
- Do not add a new server endpoint; reuse the existing `/api/workers/<id>/event` path.
- Do not write the local sidecar (covered by sibling bead ddx-1be8df2b).

Parent: ddx-a3589116. Dependencies: none (independent of ddx-1be8df2b — the two together complete the parent).

description:
PROBLEM
DDx cannot safely assume Fizeau native harness identity `fiz` or Fizeau-owned `--model qwen36` fuzzy matching until those upstream contract changes land in a Fizeau release consumed by cli/go.mod.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
DDx currently imports github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau from cli/go.mod:7. The consumed Fizeau v0.10.9 public interface already has ServiceExecuteRequest.Model/Provider/Harness/ModelRef/MinPower/MaxPower fields, but its contract still names native behavior around `agent` and its current matching only partially normalizes model IDs. Upstream work is tracked in ../fizeau: fizeau-61668fd3 for native harness `agent` -> `fiz`, and fizeau-400b57fe for fuzzy model constraint resolution.

PROPOSED FIX
After upstream Fizeau closes fizeau-61668fd3 and fizeau-400b57fe and cuts a release, bump cli/go.mod/go.sum to that release. Update DDx Fizeau adapter tests and any test fixtures that assert native harness `agent` so they use `fiz`. Add DDx-side tests proving DDx passes raw model strings such as `qwen36` through to ServiceExecuteRequest.Model unchanged and records the actual model returned by Fizeau without implementing matching.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement fuzzy matching in DDx. Do not add `agent` compatibility aliases. Do not remove the `ddx agent` CLI in this bead; that is ddx-d8474e0e. Do not perform broad internal package renames; that is the internal-rename child.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-61668fd3 must be closed in ../fizeau before this bead can complete.
- fizeau-400b57fe must be closed in ../fizeau before this bead can complete.

description:
PROBLEM
  Package installs declare skill source/target mappings in `package.yaml`, but the remote package installer does not apply those mappings. The plan requires `install.skills[*].source` to be honored relative to the package root and child skill directories to be installed under each target without replacing unrelated skills.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/registry/installer.go:110-132 ignores `pkg.Install.Skills` mapping sources and targets by calling `skills.Install(os.DirFS(absExtracted), absProject, ...)`, then guesses installed skill names with `recordInstalledSkills`. cli/internal/registry/installer.go:191-218 also hard-codes `.agents/skills` and `skills` as discovery roots and always records `.agents/skills` plus `.claude/skills`, regardless of manifest targets. The plan's manifest contract at docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:77-100 requires mapping-driven install behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/registry/installer.go: add a shared helper that applies every `InstallMapping` in `pkg.Install.Skills` from the extracted/package root to the mapping target.
  - Keep directory-to-directory semantics: `source: skills/`, `target: .agents/skills/` copies or records each child skill directory, such as `skills/ddx -> .agents/skills/ddx`, and does not replace `.agents/skills` itself.
  - Update installed-file recording to reflect actual mapping targets.
  - Add focused registry tests for manifest skill source/target behavior and preservation of unrelated existing project skills.

NON-SCOPE
  - Local `--local` symlink overlays; a sibling bead owns that path.
  - `ddx init` routing through an embedded default package; a sibling bead owns that path.
  - Moving the DDx skill tree into `library/skills/ddx`; a later bead owns the content move.

PARENT + DEPS
  Parent: ddx-6862f2ac. No deps.
title: registry: honor package skill source and target mappings

description:
PROBLEM
The candidate-cycle review/repair pipeline is not specified with enough precision for independent implementation beads. The current docs say review and repair should happen, but do not define the vocabulary, same-worktree lifecycle, candidate ref retention, read-only reviewer capability, transitional single-slot review allowance, or terminal dispositions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:337-356 describes adversarial pre-close review and repair outcomes, but it does not state that review should run pre-land in the still-live attempt worktree.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:96-185 defines pre-close review policy, but still allows interpretation as post-land review and does not define read-only tool reviewer mechanics.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:231 mentions pre-close review as a close prerequisite, but does not define candidate-cycle events such as candidate-pinned, repair-cycle-started, approved-land-conflict, or repair-cycle-exhausted.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-022-prompt-evidence-assembly.md:310-354 discusses review evidence assembly, but does not clarify canonical bounded evidence versus supplemental same-worktree read-only access.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-010 to define the layer-2 candidate-cycle pipeline: create worktree, implementation pass, project-root candidate ref, candidate checks, same-worktree read-only review, repair loop, final land, close/preserve/park, cleanup.
- Update ADR-024 to choose read-only tool reviewer as the review capability model and define same-worktree repair as append-only.
- Update FEAT-022/TD-033 references so bounded evidence remains canonical even when reviewer workdir is the attempt worktree.
- Update TD-031 with candidate-cycle event names, terminal dispositions, budgets, and claim/cooldown behavior.
- Add an explicit transitional allowance for single-slot review until the two-slot bead lands, with a removal condition.

NON-SCOPE
- No Go implementation changes.
- No new CLI flags.
- No manual edits to .ddx/beads.jsonl.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-639af4ef currently covers repair-cycle policy at a high level; this bead supersedes its ambiguous post-land framing by specifying the candidate-cycle pipeline.
- ddx-9228a484 covers retry budgets; this bead names the budget axes so implementation can be decomposed safely.

DEPS
None. ddx-639af4ef and ddx-9228a484 are informational cross-references/intersections, not blocking dependencies for this spec-definition bead.

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 6 archive/interchange methods (`Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile`). Per TD-027 §6 archive belongs on `BeadArchive` interface; interchange on `BeadInterchangeReader`/`BeadInterchangeWriter`. All take ctx per AC #1. These are signature-only changes — no helper migration needed; the methods stay as direct storage primitives.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go`, `cli/internal/bead/archive.go`, `cli/internal/bead/import.go` define these 6 methods without ctx. Callers: `cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go` (export/import subcommands).

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update `*Store.Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile` to take `ctx context.Context` first.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadArchive = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers (`cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Archive policy (TD-027 §16) — not modified.
- Attachment storage layout (TD-027 §17) — not modified.
- bd/br interchange shape (TD-027 §20) — wire format unchanged.
- Migration tooling (`MigrateLifecycle`/`MigrateFromHelix`/etc.) — separate concern, handled in MIG bead.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. The 6 methods take ctx as first parameter. Compile-time assertions for `BeadArchive`, `BeadInterchangeReader`, `BeadInterchangeWriter` compile.
2. Callers thread ctx. Existing archive/import/export tests pass.
3. `ddx bead archive`, `ddx bead export`, `ddx bead import` CLI commands continue to work end-to-end. Test: `TestBeadArchiveCommand_RoundTrip`, `TestBeadExportImport_RoundTripsAllFields`.
4. Schema round-trip preserved (per TD-027 §20 — bd/br interchange compatibility). Test: `TestBead_ExportImportRoundTrip_PreservesExtras`.
5. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:archive, area:interchange, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.

2. TestEvidenceCommitSucceedsWhenGitignored: Land() end-to-end in a fixture repo whose .gitignore covers .ddx/executions/; verify result.Status equals "landed". `cd cli && go test -run TestEvidenceCommitSucceedsWhenGitignored ./internal/agent/...`

description:
## Problem

Projects that include .ddx/executions/ in their .gitignore (including fizeau and any repo using the standard DDx .gitignore template) silently fail to land any bead. The failure is invisible: implementation is correct, tests pass, but agent.Land() preserves instead of landing on every attempt.

## Root Cause

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:717 — RealLandingGitOps.StageDir:

  args := append([]string{"add", "--", relPath}, EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs()...)
  out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()

git add without --force silently skips gitignored paths (exit 0, no output, no error). When .ddx/executions/ is gitignored, StageDir stages nothing. CommitStaged sees an empty index and returns sha="". landEvidence calls evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles which also returns false (gitignored files not tracked), so it returns error "no staged evidence files under .ddx/executions/...". preserveAfterEvidenceFailure fires, rolls back master, and creates a preserve ref.

Compounding issue: ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps ALL non-pre-merge-check preserve reasons (including "evidence commit failed") to reasonForStatus="merge conflict", which ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus returns as "land_conflict" (escalatable). This causes runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts to burn the entire model escalation ladder (min_power 5→7→8→9) before exhausting.

Confirmed in fizeau on multiple beads — every attempt preserved by evidence commit failure. Originally filed as easel/fizeau bead fizeau-af9d5f1c; refiled here because the source files live in the DDx CLI repo, not in fizeau.

## Proposed Fix

Part 1 (fix): In cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:717, change `args := append([]string{"add", "--", relPath}, ...)` to `args := append([]string{"add", "--force", "--", relPath}, ...)`. Evidence files are DDx infrastructure, not user source. They must be committed even when the project's .gitignore covers .ddx/executions/.

Part 2 (defensive): In execute_bead_status.go, add "evidence commit failed:" as a non-escalatable prefix in isPreservedNeedsReviewReason so infrastructure failures do not burn the escalation ladder.

## Origin

Refiled from easel/fizeau bead fizeau-af9d5f1c (was filed in the wrong repo — fizeau vendored a copy of DDx CLI source which has since been removed).
title: fix: StageDir must use git add --force so evidence commits succeed in projects that gitignore .ddx/executions/

description:
PROBLEM
  `ddx init` still installs shipped DDx skills through a bootstrap-only path and then separately attempts the default plugin install. The plan requires `ddx init` to install the default DDx package through the embedded package installer, with no separate `.ddx/skills/ddx` bootstrap mirror.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/cmd/init.go:250-274 creates `.ddx/skills`, cleans skill surfaces, calls `skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, ...)`, and calls `registerBootstrapDDxSkills`. cli/cmd/init.go:276-299 then separately installs the default package through `registry.InstallPackage`, which is not the offline embedded path. cli/cmd/init.go:415-450 contains the dedicated bootstrap skill copier. This duplicates ownership and produces the copied mirrors the plan removes.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/cmd/init.go: replace the direct bootstrap skill install and default-plugin download path with the embedded default package installer.
  - Remove `registerBootstrapDDxSkills` and the `.ddx/skills/ddx` bootstrap-only mirror once replacement tests pass.

  - Update init output/errors so offline package install warnings are precise.

NON-SCOPE

  - Changing non-skill init behavior such as config generation, AGENTS block generation, or versions file writing beyond install-path references.

acceptance:
1. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md §5 contains a new row for push_failed declaring "in_progress → open" transition, no execute-loop-retry-after, and event push-failed.
2. The same §5 contains a new row for push_conflict declaring "in_progress → open", optional 15min execute-loop-retry-after (recheckable cooldown), and event push-conflict.

description:
PROBLEM
  TD-031 §5 outcome→Extra mapping table is missing rows for
  push_failed and push_conflict. Today's implementation parks
  push_failed at 24h cooldown (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:272-283),
  which violates the §5 invariant: "execute-loop-retry-after may be
  set only when retrying the same bead after time passes could
  plausibly succeed without human/spec/dependency changes."

  push_failed is caused by branch protection, auth-token expiry,
  pre-push test failures (which require code fixes), or a data race
  in executor.go (also a code fix). NONE of these are recheckable
  by time elapsed alone. Without the spec row, implementers add
  cooldowns that survive long after their cause is resolved. Today's
  drain idled ~10h because of stale push_failed cooldowns set during
  a data-race incident that was fixed within the hour.

  This is a doc-only bead. It lands the spec row + the invariant
  before the implementation fixes (sister beads handle code).

CURRENT STATE
  - docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
    §5 has rows for review_pass, already_satisfied, review_fixable_gap,
    review_spec_gap, review_too_large, review_error, execution_failed,
    no_changes (multiple variants), parent/epic, external blocker,
    superseded, transient infra/quota/transport, stale no_changes
    metadata.
  - No row for push_failed.
  - No row for push_conflict.
  - The §5 preamble invariant about execute-loop-retry-after is
    correctly stated but lacks a concrete enumeration of which
    outcomes may set the field vs which must not.

PROPOSED FIX
  Edit docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
  §5:

  1. Add row for push_failed:
     - Status transition: in_progress → open (claim released).
     - Label changes: optional explanatory label (e.g.,
       blocked-on-branch-protection if applicable).
     - Events: push-failed with stderr detail and base-rev.
     - Extra updates: Extra["last-run"] only; DO NOT set
       execute-loop-retry-after. Operator action OR a code fix on
       origin clears the implicit block.

  2. Add row for push_conflict (remote diverged, fast-forward
     rejected by non-FF):
     - Status transition: in_progress → open (claim released).
     - Label changes: (none).
     - Events: push-conflict.
     - Extra updates: may set execute-loop-retry-after at 15min
       (recheckable — remote advanced; a brief wait + re-fetch
       resolves naturally).

  3. Strengthen the §5 invariant prose:
     - Add an explicit list of outcomes that MUST NOT set
       execute-loop-retry-after: push_failed,
       declined_needs_decomposition, review_spec_gap,
       review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap.
     - Note: push_conflict, no_viable_provider, land_conflict,
       transient infra/quota/transport ARE recheckable and may
       use cooldowns.
     - Reinforce: cooldown lifetime should match the recheckable
       window (15min for transient, NOT 24h).

NON-SCOPE
  - Implementation fixes (sister beads).
  - Bulk operator clear command (sister bead).
  - Rev-bound auto-invalidation logic (sister bead).
  - Other sections of TD-031.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "fix(try): push_failed must not set cooldown" (R1)
    depends on this row landing first.
  - Sister bead "fix(try): declined_needs_decomposition use
    execution-eligible=false" (R4) is referenced by the new
    invariant prose.
title: docs(TD-031): codify push_failed/push_conflict outcome rows + cooldown invariant

acceptance:

2. New execution dirs are created with names matching <attempt-id>; readers accept both new and legacy formats.
3. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md, docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md, and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md use attempt-id terminology consistently.
4. TestVocabConsistency_AttemptIDOnly passes (grep across cli/internal/agent/ and cli/internal/bead/ for run-id terms).
5. TestExecutionDirs_AcceptBothNames asserts readers handle both legacy and new naming.
6. cd cli && go test ./... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  The DDx codebase uses two names for the same concept:
    - run-id: directory name in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/
    - attempt_id: field name in events bodies (cost event, etc.)

  These refer to the same thing — one full cycle of trying to
  complete a bead. The split vocabulary creates confusion,
  inconsistency in docs, and friction for newcomers.

  Per sister bead "feat(metrics): extract per-attempt rows",
  the metrics file is named attempts.jsonl, locking in
  "attempt" as the canonical term going forward. This bead
  rename run-id → attempt-id everywhere to align.

CURRENT STATE
  - .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ directory naming.
  - Event bodies: attempt_id (already correct).
  - Code: runID variables, RunID constants, etc.
  - Spec docs: TD-031, SD-025, ADR-024 reference both terms.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Codebase: rename runID → attemptID, run_id → attempt_id,
    RunID → AttemptID, etc., across cli/.
  - File-system: rename .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ to
    .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ for new dirs. Old dirs
    remain with their existing names (backward-compat readers
    accept both).
  - Reader compatibility: code that reads execution dirs must
    accept both naming conventions (attempt-id, run-id) for
    existing dirs.
  - Spec docs: update TD-031, SD-025, ADR-024 to use
    attempt-id consistently. Cross-references updated.
  - Tests: update test fixtures.
  - Migration of existing data: no rename needed (readers
    accept both forms).

  Tests:
    - TestVocabConsistency_AttemptIDOnly: assert no run-id or
      runID identifiers remain in cli/internal/agent/.
    - TestExecutionDirs_AcceptBothNames: assert readers handle
      both legacy run-id and new attempt-id named dirs.

NON-SCOPE
  - Renaming session_id (separate concept; refers to agent
    harness session, not bead-attempt).
  - Renaming .ddx/executions/ → .ddx/attempts/ (could be done
    in a follow-up; this bead just aligns the inner ID name).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "feat(metrics): extract per-attempt rows"
    locks in "attempt" terminology.
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR): formalize 3-tier evidence
    storage" should use attempt-id consistently.
title: refactor(vocab): rename run-id → attempt-id across code, dirs, events, specs

acceptance:

2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go emits a "review-ac-override" event when the reviewer's per-AC grade disagrees with the ac-check.json mechanical result.
3. The metrics row schema (per ddx-054b2449) gains reviewer_acmismatch_count, reviewer_override_reasons, reviewer_accuracy_signal fields.
4. `ddx work analyze --by reviewer-model` (per ddx-09ceb430) surfaces per-model false-positive and false-negative rates.

description:
PROBLEM
  The reviewer is a load-bearing gate: it BLOCKs fake-work and
  APPROVEs real-work. But the reviewer is itself an AI model
  whose performance can drift (prompt regressions, model
  version changes, distribution shifts in bead content).
  Today there is no signal on reviewer accuracy — we cannot
  tell whether the reviewer is BLOCKing real work as
  false-positives or APPROVing fake work as false-negatives.

  Three observed fake-work cases (ddx-1f774edf, ddx-94a368b6,
  ddx-d22293ba/ddx-cccb4763) on 2026-05-10 were reviewer
  false-negatives. We caught them by manual coherency review;
  there's no system signal that would have alerted us.

  Closing this loop requires a feedback signal: when the
  reviewer's verdict diverges from the eventual truth, record
  it.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go produces verdicts
    APPROVE | BLOCK. No accuracy telemetry.
  - The metrics extraction bead (ddx-054b2449, queued) will
    record review_verdict per attempt, but not whether that
    verdict was correct.
  - No mechanism captures the operator's eventual override
    (e.g., a BLOCKed bead the operator manually closes; an
    APPROVEd bead the operator later reopens for fake-work).

PROPOSED FIX
  Add an "review accuracy event" emitted when the reviewer's
  verdict is contradicted by a later authoritative signal.

  Sources of authoritative signal:

  1. **Operator override**: operator manually transitions a
     reviewer-BLOCKed bead to status=closed (without further
     work). Or operator reopens a reviewer-APPROVEd bead with
     a "fake-work" or "rework" reason.
     - Hook: cli/internal/bead/store.go Close/Reopen paths.
     - Emit: "review-accuracy-override" event on the bead, with
       fields {verdict_was, operator_action, reason}.

  2. **Downstream test/build failure on landed work**: if a
     reviewer-APPROVED bead's land introduces a build break or
     test failure within N days, that's a false-negative.
     - Hook: post-merge CI signal (when available) OR a
       periodic sweep that diffs landed beads against
       eventual main HEAD.
     - Out of scope for v1 — rely on operator-override signal.

  3. **AC-check disagreement**: when ac-check.json says fail
     but the reviewer issued APPROVE, that is a likely
     false-negative. The sister bead ddx-a9ff01a1 (reviewer
     ratifies ac-check) requires the reviewer to OVERRIDE
     ac-check with explicit reason; this bead records those
     overrides for later auditing.
     - Hook: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go,
       at verdict emission time.
     - Emit: "review-ac-override" event when reviewer's
       per-AC grade contradicts ac-check.json's mechanical
       result.

  Per-attempt fields added to the metrics row (extends
  ddx-054b2449's schema):
    - reviewer_acmismatch_count: int — number of ACs where
      reviewer and ac-check disagree
    - reviewer_override_reasons: list[string] — reasons cited
      in judgment_override
    - reviewer_accuracy_signal: enum {confirmed, override,
      contested, unknown} — set by the operator-override hook
      after the fact

  `ddx work analyze --by reviewer-model` (extends ddx-09ceb430):
  query the metrics file to surface reviewer false-positive /
  false-negative rates by model. Cross-reference signals tell
  us when to iterate on the reviewer prompt or escalate
  reviewer routing.

  Tests:
    - TestReviewAccuracy_OperatorOverride_EmitsEvent
    - TestReviewAccuracy_ACCheckDisagreement_RecordedInMetrics
    - TestAnalyze_ByReviewerModel_SurfacesFPRate

NON-SCOPE
  - Downstream test/build failure as a signal (v1 uses
    operator-override + ac-check disagreement; CI integration
    is a future bead).
  - Auto-tuning reviewer routing based on accuracy (separate
    concern).
  - UI/dashboard for accuracy time series (separate concern).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on ddx-054b2449 (metrics extraction) for the row
    schema.
  - Depends on ddx-09ceb430 (analyze CLI) for the query
    interface.
  - Depends on ddx-a9ff01a1 (reviewer ratifies ac-check) for
    the AC-check disagreement signal.
title: feat(metrics): reviewer accuracy tracking — record false-positive/negative for reviewer-quality drift detection

description:
# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027, reliability

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: all per-concern beads must complete before this bead can execute:
- ddx-bca628fa (L)
- ddx-e1c743d3 (C)
- ddx-f39b41b3 (E)
- ddx-aed7c7ab (X)
- ddx-bc07270f (Q)

description:
PROBLEM
When readiness parks or warns on a bead, the operator and subsequent workers cannot reliably see the rule that fired, whether it was advisory or blocking, or what action would resolve it. This makes downgrade loops hard to diagnose and makes operator acceptance non-auditable.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1217-1222 emits pre_claim_intake.blocked with bead_id, outcome, reason, and detail only.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2804-2815 appends intake.blocked with intake_outcome and detail only. It omits rule_id, decision_source, fingerprint, suggested_action, and whether the decision is WARN-ONLY advisory or BLOCK/factory hard stop.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md:785-792 treats event kind as a closed vocabulary. Any new intake.warned kind must be documented first; otherwise the implementation should reuse existing event kinds with richer structured bodies.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small structured readiness decision event body shared by park and warn paths. Include rule_id, normalized reason, decision_source, policy_mode, decision (warn/proceed, park/proposed, override_honored), fingerprint when applicable, and suggested_action.
- Prefer existing event kinds unless the spec bead explicitly amends TD-027/TD-031. If introducing intake.warned or override_honored as durable event kinds, update TD-027 event vocabulary and TD-031 mappings in the same implementation.
- Ensure ddx work log output or bead event output surfaces enough of the decision to explain why the bead parked or proceeded.

NON-SCOPE
- No readiness policy changes; ddx-91898f19 owns warn/proceed versus park.
- No operator override behavior; ddx-cd69c24 owns idempotency.
- No UI redesign beyond existing CLI/event rendering needed to expose the new fields.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on ddx-ef9e8aec.

acceptance:
1. `TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtRejectsImplementationPaths` or equivalent proves dirty `cli/internal/...` code/test files in the parent checkout are not committed as `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead ...`.
2. `TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtAllowsTrackerAndEvidencePaths` or equivalent proves legitimate tracker/evidence bookkeeping still checkpoints when needed.
3. `TestExecuteBeadCheckpointDoesNotAbsorbSubstantiveWork` or equivalent exercises `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` and verifies the checkpoint commit contains only allowed paths while the bead implementation remains in a `[ddx-<id>]` substantive commit or the attempt fails with actionable evidence.
4. Existing concurrency regressions including `TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace` and `TestRun_LockScopeIncludesSynthesizeCommit` remain green.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirt|TestExecuteBeadCheckpointDoesNotAbsorbSubstantiveWork|TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace|TestRun_LockScopeIncludesSynthesizeCommit" -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Live `ddx work` attempts are landing substantial implementation changes inside `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead ...` commits instead of the required conventional substantive commit tagged with `[ddx-<id>]`. This weakens the execute-bead audit trail: cost, latency, retry, and review tooling can no longer reliably treat the checkpoint as tracker/caller-dirt bookkeeping and the tagged commit as the bead implementation.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:542-599` defines `checkpointPreDispatchDirt`, which stages `git add -A -- .` through a temporary index and creates a `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead <attempt>` commit.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1050-1064` runs `commitTrackerLocked` and then `checkpointPreDispatchDirt` before resolving the base revision and creating the isolated execute-bead worktree.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:17-29` and `:124-138` assert the lock-scope and HEAD-race behavior, but not the content contract for checkpoint commits.
- Observed evidence: `df5b591ec chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260513T175830-e116edd8` contains 305 lines of readiness-timeout implementation across seven code/test files, and `c53f2b0fe chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260513T183205-80b61705` contains 170 lines of `readiness_checks` implementation across three files. The later tagged commit for `ddx-90b84d73` contained only follow-on CLI/docs plumbing, so the main implementation was attributed to the checkpoint.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a checkpoint content guard so pre-dispatch checkpoint commits cannot silently absorb ordinary implementation files. The guard should either: (a) restrict checkpoints to known durable tracker/evidence/caller-dirt paths and reject code/test paths with an actionable execution failure, or (b) require dirty parent checkout implementation files to be committed under the active bead's `[ddx-<id>]` commit before checkpointing. Preserve the existing serialized lock behavior that prevents concurrent HEAD CAS failures.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove the pre-dispatch checkpoint mechanism entirely. Do not weaken the existing concurrent worker lock tests. Do not rewrite existing history to repair already-landed checkpoint commits.
title: execute-bead: prevent checkpoints from absorbing implementation changes
