acceptance:
1. Add or update a test named `TestTryZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauProfileByMetadata` using arbitrary Fizeau policy names such as `p-low`, `p-balanced`, and `p-max`; a zero-config ordinary implementation bead sends `ServiceExecuteRequest.Policy == "p-balanced"`, does not send a concrete model/provider/harness pin, and does not promote the request to `p-max`.
2. Add or update a work-loop test named `TestWorkZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauProfileByMetadata` proving unpinned `ddx work` uses the metadata-selected Fizeau profile/policy for an ordinary implementation bead.
3. Add or update selector tests covering weak-first, criteria-based profile choice from Fizeau metadata: ordinary implementation chooses the cheapest/fastest viable medium-capability profile over the strongest band when both are available; unavailable, exact-pin-only, non-auto-routable, or disallowed-local candidates do not make a profile satisfiable; lower cost and better speed/perf signal can break ties within the appropriate capability band when exposed by Fizeau metadata.
4. Add or update a degraded-route test proving that when the ideal capability band has no satisfiable profile but a weaker free/available provider can attempt the task, DDx dispatches the weaker/free profile instead of failing pre-dispatch, and records the request as degraded.
5. Add or update a no-provider test proving DDx returns an execution/provider-availability error only when no Fizeau profile/provider can attempt the work at all.
6. Add or update a fallback test proving that when Fizeau `ListPolicies` is unavailable, DDx falls back to a lower-bound `MinPower` for the inferred task need instead of top-power for an ordinary implementation bead.
7. Add or update an escalation test proving strongest-profile selection for implementation dispatch occurs only for explicit strong-task signals, breakdown/debug/high-risk criteria, or a retry path with concrete lower-tier failure evidence.
8. Existing explicit passthrough tests still pass: `--profile`, `--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--min-power`, and `--max-power` must prevent automatic profile selection for implementation dispatch.
9. Routing-intent evidence for a zero-config inferred task records the inferred need, selected Fizeau profile/policy name when selected, and degradation note when DDx tries a weaker/free profile than ideal. Actual harness/provider/model remain Fizeau-reported facts.
10. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTryZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauProfileByMetadata|TestWorkZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauProfileByMetadata|TestSelect.*Profile|TestLoadProfileSnapshot' -count=1` passes.
11. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
12. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Unpinned `ddx work --watch` on a medium Story 16 server/GraphQL bead inferred ordinary implementation work, but the implementation route still ran under Fizeau `policy=default` and selected `codex/gpt-5.5`. DDx should introspect Fizeau profiles/policies and choose the best request-level profile for the task. Profile names are configurable Fizeau data, so DDx must not hard-code names such as `cheap`, `standard`, `smart`, or `frontier`; those are shortcuts only when a Fizeau installation chooses to expose them.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/profile_select.go:27-37 defines `ProfileSnapshot` and `LoadProfileSnapshot`, fetching Fizeau `ListPolicies` plus `ListModels`.
- cli/internal/agent/profile_select.go:99-157 already has profile selectors, but they are only used by lifecycle/reviewer dispatch paths and currently prefer fixed named concepts rather than a general implementation selector.
- Fizeau `PolicyInfo` currently exposes `Name`, `MinPower`, `MaxPower`, `AllowLocal`, `Require`, and manifest metadata; `ModelInfo` exposes `Power`, `Cost`, `PerfSignal`, `Billing`, `Available`, `AutoRoutable`, and related route facts.
- cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:138-144 detects zero-config auto-inference, but cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:351-361 loads only models into a MinPower ladder.
- cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:377-391 computes `initialMinPower` and calls `singleTierAttempt(..., spec.Harness, spec.Provider, spec.Model)` without setting `spec.Profile`/Fizeau policy for the inferred task need.
- cli/cmd/try.go:230-250 has the same model-ladder-only path for `ddx try`.
- cli/cmd/try.go:267-293 then sends `Profile: profile` (empty for unpinned zero-config invocations) plus inferred `MinPower`, so Fizeau receives no selected policy/profile and falls back to its default policy.
- cli/cmd/try_test.go:345-385 currently codifies the wrong behavior by asserting inferred work becomes a numeric MinPower rather than selecting a Fizeau profile by metadata.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md now specifies that DDx should infer task need, introspect Fizeau profiles/models, choose the cheapest/fastest viable request profile, reserve strongest profiles for hard work or evidence-backed escalation, and treat profile names as opaque.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md now specifies that DDx chooses request-level profile/power intent while Fizeau owns concrete routing.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a profile selector for implementation dispatch that maps inferred task need to a satisfiable Fizeau policy/profile returned by `ListPolicies`, considering available auto-routable models and preserving DDx's existing precedence rules.
- The selector must be criteria-based, not name-based: infer what the task needs, classify profiles by metadata (power range, availability, cost/billing, speed/perf signal, locality policy, and future diversity data), then choose the cheapest/fastest profile that is likely to get the work done.
- Default execution should start with weaker viable models/profiles and work upward only when evidence justifies escalation. Strongest profiles should be reserved for bead breakdown, debugging, difficult reviews, high-risk or architecture-sensitive work, and retries where lower tiers produced concrete failures.
- In zero-config `ddx try` and `ddx work`, set `Profile`/Fizeau policy to the selected profile name returned by Fizeau when no explicit `--profile`, `--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--min-power`, `--max-power`, or project routing config is present.
- Preserve explicit operator passthrough exactly: if a profile or other routing flag/config is supplied, do not override it with task inference.
- Keep concrete route selection in Fizeau. DDx may choose the abstract profile/policy name and power bounds but must not select a model/provider/harness.
- If the ideal profile is unavailable but any free/available provider can try the bead, dispatch the attempt through the best available weaker/free profile and record a degraded routing intent rather than blocking before execution.
- Return an execution/provider-availability error only when no provider/profile can attempt the work at all.
- If Fizeau profiles are unavailable and DDx falls back to `MinPower`, derive the fallback floor from the inferred task need as a reasonable lower bound, not the strongest available model.
- Record routing-intent evidence with inferred task need, selected Fizeau profile/policy name when selected, fallback MinPower when used, and degradation notes when DDx tries a weaker/free profile than the ideal.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/profile_select.go and profile_select_test.go
- cli/cmd/try.go and try_test.go
- cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go and work/execute-loop tests
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go routing-intent evidence if a requested profile field is needed
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-037-bead-execution-hints.md and FEAT-006 only if wording must be reconciled

NON-SCOPE
- Do not pin a concrete model/provider/harness in DDx.
- Do not hard-code Fizeau profile names.
- Do not reintroduce legacy `agent.routing.profile_ladders`, `model_overrides`, or DDx-owned provider fallback.
- Do not change Fizeau's route ranking algorithm inside a selected profile.
- Do not change lifecycle/reviewer hook routing except to share the same selector if useful.
- Do not enable local providers by default merely because they appear in `.ddx/config.yaml`; only Fizeau profiles with available auto-routable models and appropriate policy metadata should be selected.

OBSERVED LIVE EVIDENCE
On 2026-05-13, an unpinned worker started as `ddx work --watch --idle-interval 30s` on ddx-7c256284. The worker printed `route codex/gpt-5.5 reason=policy=default` and `route codex/gpt-5.5 power=10 cost_source=subscription`, even though the bead's durable metadata inferred ordinary implementation work rather than a strong-profile request.
title: routing: select Fizeau profile for inferred implementation tier

description:
PROBLEM
The run-detail page needs a single GraphQL/data-layer contract that can return transcript bodies, normalized tool calls, and evidence bundle file metadata without exposing arbitrary filesystem paths.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:1208-1216 defines the run-detail transcript and bundleFiles fields on Run.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:1232-1266 defines RunToolCall and RunToolCallConnection, and cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2794-2822 defines run(id:), runToolCalls, and runBundleFile.
- cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go:16-30 defines the 64 KB cap and inline whitelist; cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go:102-140 is the bundle path confinement boundary.
- cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go:247-290 loads prompt, response, stderr, and bundleFiles; cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go:308-384 resolves persisted tool calls and bundle files.

2. TestCandidateRefStore_ProjectRootReachable verifies git -C <projectRoot> rev-parse <candidate_ref> succeeds for a candidate produced in a temp worktree, including after that temp worktree is removed.
3. TestCandidateRefStore_MetadataRecorded verifies result.json / ExecuteBeadResult includes candidate_ref and cycle_index plus existing base_rev, result_rev, and attempt_id for the cycle.
4. TestCandidateRefStore_EventRecorded verifies the bead event emitted for the candidate-cycle step includes candidate_ref, cycle_index, base_rev, result_rev, and attempt_id.
5. TestCandidateRefStore_RetentionPolicy verifies approved-landed temporary refs are cleaned only after candidate metadata is durable, while preserved/manual/parked/conflicted candidates retain refs.

description:
PROBLEM
Candidate commits created in a temp worktree must be resolvable from the project repository before checks, review, repair, landing, or crash recovery can rely on them. Today the worker returns a raw result_rev from the temp worktree, but no dedicated project-root candidate ref lifecycle exists for the FEAT-010 candidate-cycle pipeline.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:32-103 defines ExecuteBeadResult with base_rev/result_rev/preserve_ref fields but no candidate_ref or cycle_index metadata.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:397-410 in FEAT-010's sequence says candidate refs must be pinned before candidate checks and read-only review, but the loop currently goes from implementation result toward checks/review/land without a project-root candidate ref record.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:696-708 creates land-time preserve refs under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>, but that only covers failed/deferred landing; it is not the per-cycle candidate ref required before checks/review.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:333-365 defines each cycle as recording cycle_index, attempt_id, base_rev, result_rev, and a candidate ref under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add CandidateRefStore or equivalent agent package helper that pins each close-eligible candidate commit in the project repo under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index> before candidate checks and read-only review run.
- Use cycle_index starting at 0 for the first implementation cycle in an attempt and increment for each append-only repair cycle.
- Record candidate_ref and cycle_index alongside existing attempt metadata. The concrete storage target is ExecuteBeadResult/result.json (add JSON fields) plus the append-only bead event emitted for the candidate-cycle step; include base_rev and result_rev so the cycle can be reconstructed after worktree cleanup.
- Retention policy: landed temporary candidate refs may be deleted only after result.json and the bead event/landed evidence containing candidate_ref, base_rev, result_rev, attempt_id, and cycle_index are durable in the project branch. Preserved/manual/parked/conflicted candidates retain the candidate ref for operator inspection and recovery.
- Ensure git -C <projectRoot> rev-parse <candidate_ref> resolves even after the temp worktree is removed.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not move review or repair into the worktree yet.
- Do not implement stale ref pruning beyond the retention behavior above.
- Do not rename existing land-time preserve refs in execute_bead_land.go except where tests need to distinguish preserve_ref from candidate_ref.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-31e07fdc. Depends on ddx-49d8d6cb for coordinator interfaces.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` does not have a crisp human-facing contract for run modes or stdout. The current docs and code use `--poll-interval` as if it were the mode selector, but every queue worker scans the queue between attempts. Operators need explicit semantics for one-shot work, drain-and-exit work, and long-running watch work, plus output that answers whether the worker exited, why it exited, what queue state remains, and why an idle watcher is sleeping.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:685-702 currently describes the behavior as a `--poll-interval` default, conflating idle sleep tuning with the operator's chosen work mode.
- cli/cmd/work.go:37-50 presents `ddx work --poll-interval 30s` as the continuous-worker example rather than naming the mode directly.
- cli/cmd/work.go:75 exposes `--poll-interval` as the primary stay-alive control, even though `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:649-682` treats any positive interval as idle/watch behavior.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1885-1937 prints terminal summaries without stop reason, already-satisfied breakdown, remaining queue-state context, or blocked-behind-human-review explanation.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:656-688 emits idle progress internally but does not define the human stdout contract for idle or next-ready-bead transitions.

PROPOSED FIX
Update FEAT-010 and FEAT-006 with an explicit stdout and mode contract:
- `once`: process at most one execution-ready bead and exit. Public flag remains `--once`.
- `drain`: process the currently execution-ready queue until a terminal stop condition, then exit. Direct CLI `ddx work` uses this unless `--watch` is supplied.
- `watch`: process ready work, then remain idle and rescan for newly arriving execution-ready work until signal, fatal config, budget/resource stop, or operator stop. Public flag is `--watch`.
- `idle interval`: the sleep duration between empty-queue scans while in watch mode. Public flag is `--idle-interval <duration>` with a 30s default in watch mode.
- `--poll-interval` is removed from the public CLI and generated docs. There is no compatibility alias. New server/API inputs use `mode` + `idle_interval`, not `poll_interval`.
- Server-managed workers default to watch mode with a 30s idle interval unless the request explicitly asks for `once` or drain mode.

Also define the human stdout contract:
- Terminal summaries state attempts, closed count, changed/already-satisfied breakdown, failures, stop reason, and remaining queue state.
- Remaining queue state distinguishes execution-ready, blocked, needs-human, cooldown/deferred, not-eligible, superseded, and epic/closure-candidate buckets where DDx can compute them.
- Queue summaries explain dependency pressure from human-review blockers: if 30 unique open beads are transitively blocked behind 3 open `needs_human` / needs-investigation blockers, say so and list the blocker IDs/titles with blocked counts.
- Watch idle output says the worker is still running, gives the idle interval, includes queue-state summary, and uses direct wording such as `idle: no execution-ready beads; sleeping 30s`.
- Every idle poll prints a compact queue-state line. The full blocker list may be suppressed on unchanged repeated polls, but counts and sleep duration remain visible every poll.
- When watch mode wakes up and claims new work, stdout separates the new bead from the prior block and says `taking next ready bead from queue: <id> — <title>` before the bead block starts.
- Fizeau typed service events remain opaque; DDx may project route/model/tool summaries into phase-labeled human stdout without mutating the event schema.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement CLI flags or renderers in this docs bead.
- Do not change Fizeau event schemas, routing policy, structured JSONL timestamps, or stored event names.
- Do not create or modify Go tests in this docs bead; the Go command below is a regression gate only.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-eeced112. No deps.

2. Report explains the failure (real defect vs false-positive in the check itself).

4. If real defect: report explains why the operator's manual cherry-pick of ee719835 still passes `go test ./cmd/fiz ./agentcli`.
description:
Context:
On 2026-05-08, bead `fizeau-bd5193ca` ("Fix fiz version JSON output") in the fizeau repo went through 4 successive `ddx work` execute-bead attempts:
- 20260508T175640-b12741e5 (15m, $2.59, sonnet) → task_succeeded → result preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/fizeau-bd5193ca/…/2c3f896a
- 20260508T181204-20d01e6d (18m, $2.68, sonnet) → task_succeeded → preserved as b673f48a
- 20260508T183001-483c8a6e (15m, $1.74, sonnet) → task_succeeded → preserved as ee719835
- 20260508T184500-34ccc16f (~10m, sonnet) → task_succeeded → preserved as ebbeb0ad

None merged onto master; only `chore: update tracker (execute-bead …)` commits landed. Bead stayed in_progress until the loop was killed manually.

Operator manually cherry-picked attempt 3 (ee719835) onto fizeau master as 44d85287 and verified all acceptance criteria pass:
- `go test ./cmd/fiz ./agentcli` → both packages OK
- `fiz --version` → human-readable with non-empty commit
- `fiz version --json` → valid JSON with non-empty version, non-empty commit, boolean dirty

So the implementations themselves were correct. Some pre-merge check blocked them anyway. We don't yet know which check or why — see ddx-62edcb10 for the orthogonal observability bug that hid the rejection.

Investigation goals:
1. Read `RunPreMergeChecks` (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:148-area) and enumerate every check it runs by default for a fizeau-style repo.
2. Replay one of the preserved iteration refs (e.g. `refs/ddx/iterations/fizeau-bd5193ca/20260508T183001-483c8a6e-e9e33bba16bb`) through the pre-merge check pipeline against the matching base_rev to reproduce the block locally.
3. Identify which check returned Blocked=true and why. Determine whether it was a real defect in the implementation (and the cherry-pick happened to skip the check) or a false positive in the check itself.
4. If false positive, file a follow-up bead to fix the check; if real defect, document why the cherry-pick still passed go test.

Useful artifacts (in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau):
- `.ddx/executions/20260508T17{5640,1204},18{3001,4500}-*/result.json` — worker outputs
- `refs/ddx/iterations/fizeau-bd5193ca/*` — preserved result trees
- `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-2026-05.jsonl` — session metadata
- `.ddx/attachments/fizeau-bd5193ca/events.jsonl` — overflow events

Depends on: ddx-62edcb10 only insofar as fixing that bug would have made this investigation a one-line lookup instead of a code dive. They can be tackled independently.

acceptance:
1. `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` includes stop condition/exit reason fields that are populated for drain, once, signal/context, budget, blocked, no-progress, preflight, fatal config, and resource-exhausted exits.
2. `loop.end.exit_reason` remains emitted with the same values as before for existing consumers.
3. `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` includes a queue snapshot populated on every no-candidate path, including after one or more successful attempts.
4. The queue snapshot preserves compatibility with existing `no_ready_work` / `no_ready_work_detail` JSON fields while adding structured counts usable by the human summary renderer.
5. The queue snapshot includes human-review dependency-pressure fields: total open beads blocked transitively by human blockers, plus blocker entries with ID, title, priority, and downstream blocked count.
6. `TestExecuteBeadLoopResult_IncludesStopReasonOnDrainExit` covers explicit drain exit.
7. `TestExecuteBeadLoopResult_IncludesQueueSnapshotAfterSuccessfulDrain` covers a run that closes at least one bead and then reaches no ready work.
8. `TestQueueSnapshot_HumanReviewBlockersAggregateTransitiveBlockedBeads` builds a fixture with 30 open beads blocked behind 3 needs-human blockers and asserts the total and per-blocker counts/IDs/titles are present.
9. `TestExecuteBeadLoopResult_PreservesLoopEndExitReason` proves the structured event still carries the same exit reason.
10. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestExecuteBeadLoopResult|TestQueueSnapshot_HumanReviewBlockers|TestLoop_DrainExitsOnNoReadyWork|TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoReadyWork|TestExecuteBeadWorkerEmitsLoopEventsWithNoReadyWork' -count=1` passes.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Human and JSON summaries cannot reliably answer why `ddx work` stopped or what queue state remained when it stopped. Idle watch output also cannot explain why the worker is sleeping beyond a flat "no ready work" signal. The loop emits `exit_reason` only in the structured `loop.end` event, while `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` exposes attempts/successes/failures and a partial `NoReadyWorkDetail` only for the zero-attempt no-ready path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:531-542 keeps `exitReason` as a local variable and writes it only through the deferred `loop.end` event at lines 577-581.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:399-407 defines `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` without stop condition, exit reason, or a reusable queue-state summary.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:632-647 populates `NoReadyWorkDetail` only when `result.Attempts == 0`, so a drain that completed work and then emptied the queue cannot summarize the remaining queue state.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1576-1715 exposes `BlockedAll` with dependency blockers, retry cooldowns, needs-human/investigation blockers, execution-ineligible beads, superseded beads, and epic-only blockers, but the work loop does not aggregate those into a queue snapshot.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1885-1937 can only print attempts/successes/failures and bare route lines because the loop result does not carry stop and queue-state fields.

PROPOSED FIX
Expose stop and queue state as first-class loop-result data:
- Add stable fields to `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` for stop condition/exit reason and a compact queue snapshot.
- Populate stop condition/exit reason whenever `applyStop` classifies a terminal stop, and preserve existing `loop.end.exit_reason` behavior.
- Populate queue snapshot whenever the picker reaches the no-candidate path, including after successful attempts, not just the zero-attempt case.
- The queue snapshot should distinguish at least: execution-ready count, blocked count, needs-human/investigation count, cooldown/deferred count and next retry time, execution-ineligible count, superseded count, skipped epics, and epic closure candidates where the store can compute them.
- Add a transitive dependency-pressure summary for human-review blockers. A human blocker is an open bead that carries `needs_human`, `needs_investigation`, or the existing no-changes triage labels. For each such blocker, compute how many open downstream beads are transitively blocked by it. Expose total blocked-by-human count plus blocker entries with ID, title, priority, and downstream count.
- Keep existing JSON field names for `no_ready_work` and `no_ready_work_detail` compatible; add new fields rather than removing old ones.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the human renderer in this bead; the idle and terminal summary renderers consume the new fields.
- Do not change bead picker ordering, retry policy, or readiness classification.
- Do not implement watch/drain CLI flag naming; ddx-f9acb86d owns that.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-eeced112. Depends on ddx-7d9e1e82 for the documented summary contract.

3. TestExecutionTrace_AdapterPreservesCycleFields proves cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go preserves all cycle trace fields in both conversion directions.
4. Existing bead.result/review event consumers remain compatible; if durable field names are retained for compatibility, the test documents those retained names explicitly.
5. No trace writer overwrites or rewrites an older cycle entry for the same result_rev/review_group_id.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecutionTrace|TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Once work supports initial and repair cycles, operators need a durable append-only trace for every implementation and repair cycle. Today the loop emits bead.result and review events, but there is no single cycle trace schema that connects cycle_index, attempt_id, implementer route facts, review group slots, review verdicts, and final decision.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-67 defines the try.Report shape without cycle_index, review_group_id, reviewer slot, or final cycle decision fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:9-46 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:49-86 copy report fields between legacy and try shapes, so any new trace fields must be wired through both directions while the compatibility layer exists.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1339-1349 emits bead.result without cycle trace fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:56-94 builds review_group_id and reviewer_index slot data, but that data remains review-local rather than being attached to a durable cycle trace.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:200-227 records the reduced review verdict and closes on APPROVE without appending a unified initial/repair cycle record.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an append-only execution cycle trace data shape for initial implementation cycles and review repair cycles.
- Required fields: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, review result, and final decision.
- Persist the trace in execution evidence and/or bead events using an append-only format that can survive retries and process restarts.
- Wire the trace through the try.Report/legacy ExecuteBeadReport adapter while both shapes exist.
- Cover both the first implementation cycle and the bounded repair cycle introduced by ddx-ece97605.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement repair-cycle scheduling; this bead consumes the repair behavior from ddx-ece97605.
- Do not build frontend UI for cycle traces.
- Do not prune, compact, or rewrite older execution evidence.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-ece97605 so the trace test can cover both initial and repair cycles.
title: evidence: record append-only implementation and repair cycle trace

description:
Fizeau v0.10.10 is published at https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau/releases/tag/v0.10.10. DDx currently depends on github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.9 in cli/go.mod, so service progress rendering still shows duplicate tool rows and uses turn_index as the visible counter. Fizeau v0.10.10 changes normal tool progress to emit only the completed progress row and adds ServiceProgressData.tool_call_id plus ServiceProgressData.tool_call_index. Update DDx to consume v0.10.10 and render tool progress counters from tool_call_index when present. Keep start/finish semantics available for any future delayed-running display, but normal fast tool calls should appear as one row containing the command/action and output summary.

description:
PROBLEM: After ddx work auto-merges a beads worktree branch into master (cb300d7-style merge commit), the main worktrees git INDEX ends up with the OLD pre-merge content of the beads modified files staged. Verified twice in axon project, 2026-05-08.

SYMPTOM: git status shows M in column 1 (staged-vs-HEAD) for the very files the bead just modified; working tree matches index; both differ from HEAD. Any subsequent operator git add of an unrelated file followed by git commit then sweeps in those staged reverts, undoing the beads work.

REPRO (axon, 2026-05-08):
- 333d1a1 [axon-044a5b5b] modified ui/src/.../audit/+page.svelte and ui/tests/e2e/intent-audit-lineage.spec.ts
- cb300d7 merged into master; master HEAD content is correct
- After merge, git status -s in main worktree:
  M  ui/src/routes/.../audit/+page.svelte
  M  ui/tests/e2e/intent-audit-lineage.spec.ts
  D  .ddx/executions/20260508T204742-afe71867/manifest.json (and others)
- git diff HEAD reports the index differs from HEAD by reverting the bead changes and deleting the exec dir files
- Operator recovery: git checkout HEAD -- <file paths> resets both index and working tree

LIKELY ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go around RealLandingGitOps.MergeInto and landEvidence/StageDir. The merge updates working tree but the index appears to be left at the pre-merge state for the touched files. Then StageDir runs git add on the exec dir, which flips the index entries for those files to "staged for deletion" (since the worktree was deleted) but leaves the audit-page entries alone in their pre-merge state.

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION (owned by ddx maintainers):
- After MergeInto succeeds, ensure the index in the main worktree is fully synchronized with HEAD before any subsequent StageDir or add operations.
- Or: verify that git merge --no-ff is being run with the main worktree as cwd (not a different worktree) so the index updates correctly.
- Or: explicit git reset HEAD or git read-tree HEAD between merge and staging to defensively reset the index.

NON-SCOPE: changing merge strategy (--no-ff vs ff-only is fixed by skill guidance).
title: bug: ddx merge leaks pre-merge file content into operator's git index

acceptance:
1. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-NNN-evidence-storage-architecture.md exists with sections 1-8 as described in PROPOSED FIX.
2. ADR documents principles P1-P4 with rationale.
3. ADR contains the per-bead git footprint summary (~5-8 KB tracked for 3-attempt bead vs ~95 KB today).
4. ADR documents the 90-day local retention default and operator override mechanism.
5. ADR explicitly states single-machine assumption for .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl with a forward-pointer to federation-time partitioning.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  DDx accumulates per-attempt evidence (manifest.json, prompt.md,
  result.json, reviewer-stream.log, agent stream logs in
  .ddx/agent-logs/, worker state in .ddx/workers/) without a
  documented retention or storage architecture. Today the
  executions/ dirs are explicitly tracked in git via un-ignore
  rules; agent-logs/ are local-only but unbounded. After today's
  cleanup discoveries (12,096 tracked execution files, 860 MB
  agent-logs locally) we need a formal design that balances
  reconstruct-the-past against repo bloat.

  The architecture must enable optimization analysis (model /
  prompt / harness / spec performance) WITHOUT forcing all
  detailed evidence into git.

CURRENT STATE
  - No ADR or spec describes the storage tiers, retention
    policy, or the operator interface for archive.
  - Implementation is inconsistent: executions/ tracked via
    un-ignore, agent-logs/ gitignored, workers/ not in gitignore
    but also not actively tracked.
  - No central "metrics" extraction; queries across attempts
    require fanning out reads.

PROPOSED FIX
  New ADR at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-NNN-evidence-storage-architecture.md.

  Principles:
    P1. Git holds durable, small, structured state.
        - bead rows (.ddx/beads.jsonl)
        - closed-bead events (.ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl)
        - aggregate per-attempt metrics (.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl)
        - code, docs, ADRs/TDs/FEATs
    P2. Local disk holds detailed, ephemeral, retention-bounded
        evidence (per-machine, never in git).
        - .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ (full evidence bundle —
          prompt.md, manifest.json, result.json, usage.json,
          checks.json, reviewer-stream.log, decisions.md, etc.)
        - .ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl (raw model stream)
        - .ddx/workers/worker-*/ (per-worker runtime state)
        Default retention: 90 days. Configurable.
    P3. External archive is operator-configured (mirror hook),
        not a built-in. Backends: local-dir (default), remote
        (e.g., S3) deferred.
    P4. Aggregate metrics are denormalized, queryable, durable.
        .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl is the analytics table; each
        row is one bead-attempt with structured fields for
        cross-bead queries.

  Per-bead git footprint after this architecture:
    - 1 row in .ddx/beads.jsonl (~2-5 KB)
    - 1 file in .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl
      (~1-3 KB, lifecycle events for the bead)
    - N rows in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl (~300 bytes/attempt)
    Total for a typical 3-attempt bead: ~5-8 KB tracked. 20x
    reduction vs current ~95 KB.

  ADR sections:
    1. Context (why this decision is needed)
    2. Decision (the 3-tier architecture)
    3. Principles P1-P4 with rationale
    4. Per-bead git footprint summary
    5. Local retention default and operator override
    6. External archive interface (hook-point, no built-in
       backend mandate)
    7. Cross-machine considerations (single-machine assumption
       for the attempts.jsonl; partition when federation lands)
    8. Migration plan (sister beads B29/B30/B31/metrics-extraction)

NON-SCOPE
  - Implementing retention (sister bead B29).
  - Removing gitignore un-ignores (sister bead B30).
  - Implementing the mirror backend (sister bead B31).
  - Implementing metrics extraction (sister bead).
  - Implementing the analyze CLI (sister bead).

INTERSECTIONS
  - All other architecture beads (B29 retention, B30 gitignore
    flip, B31 mirror, metrics extraction, analyze CLI) anchor
    against this ADR.
  - Cross-refs TD-031 §5 (existing event vocabulary) and
    SD-025 (existing execution lifecycle).
title: docs(ADR): formalize 3-tier evidence storage architecture (git/local/archive)

description:
PROBLEM
CLI queue surfaces present needs-human and blocked lanes that are derived from labels or ordinary dependency waits. After the status-owned state machine lands, operators need commands that show proposed, open/ready, dependency-waiting, in-progress, external-blocked, closed, and cancelled consistently.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/work_focus.go:57-83 builds a human_required list from store.NeedsHuman.
- cli/cmd/work_focus.go:86-115 excludes needs_human from blocked output using old label semantics.
- cli/cmd/work_focus.go:181-198 documents needs_human/review-blocked help text.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:916,957,979 consumes ReadyExecution/BlockedAll/BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation/BlockerKindNeedsHuman for bead ready/status output.
- cli/cmd/try_test.go currently accepts only selected status update values, while the help text for bead update is stale versus the six upstream statuses.

PROPOSED FIX
Update CLI commands and help to use status-owned lifecycle vocabulary. `ddx bead ready` and `ddx work focus` should show execution-ready open beads separately from dependency-waiting open beads and proposed operator-attention beads. `ddx bead blocked` should mean external recheckable blockers, not ordinary dependency waits. Status commands should report the six statuses and derived buckets. Any old needs-human command surface must either be removed, renamed, or explicitly deprecated as an alias for proposed/operator-attention.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change server GraphQL/API or frontend UI in this bead. Do not mutate live tracker rows; migration has a separate bead.

DEPS
Depends on store/query wiring and legacy migration behavior.
title: cli: update bead and work-focus commands for proposed lifecycle

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go declined_needs_decomposition branch (around line 256-269) sets execution-eligible=false instead of cooldown; no execute-loop-retry-after is written.
2. TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NoCooldown asserts no execute-loop-retry-after.

description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:256-269 sets a 24h cooldown on
  declined_needs_decomposition outcomes. This violates TD-031 §5
  invariant: "execute-loop-retry-after may be set only when
  retrying the same bead after time passes could plausibly succeed
  without human/spec/dependency changes."

  Decomposition requires operator action: filing sub-beads, wiring
  dependency edges, possibly amending the parent's AC. Time elapsed
  alone changes nothing. After 24h, the drain re-attempts, fails
  again with the same "needs decomposition", and re-parks for
  another 24h. Infinite cycle until operator acts.

  Per TD-031 §5 row "parent/epic/decomposed container": the correct
  signal is execution-eligible=false (which excludes the bead from
  ordinary drain claim) plus optional decomposed label, NOT a
  time-based cooldown.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:256-269: returns OutcomePark
    with maxAttemptCooldown (24h) for declined_needs_decomposition.
  - The bead exits ready queue for 24h based on wall-clock, then
    re-enters and fails again with the same outcome.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:256-269: change the
    declined_needs_decomposition branch:
      - Set Extra["execution-eligible"] = false (matches TD-031 §5
        parent/epic container row).
      - Optionally add label "needs-decomposition" or
        "triage:decomposition-required" for operator visibility.
      - Status transition: in_progress → open (claim released)
        with execution-eligible=false flag.
      - DO NOT set execute-loop-retry-after.
  - The bead drops out of ordinary drain claim (execution-eligible
    filter excludes it) but is visible in `ddx work focus` for
    operator triage.
  - When the operator decomposes the bead (files children, wires
    deps), they unset execution-eligible=false; bead re-enters the
    queue.

  Tests:
     - TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NoCooldown: assert no
       execute-loop-retry-after is set.
     - TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NotEligible: assert
       execution-eligible=false is set.
     - TestDeclinedNeedsDecomposition_NotInReady: assert the bead
       is excluded from Store.Ready() output.

NON-SCOPE
  - Implementing the operator decomposition workflow (separate
    concern; assume operators already do this).
  - Auto-decomposition (separate beads, e.g., ddx-008d288f).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(TD-031): codify push_failed/push_conflict
    outcome rows" — the same spec invariant motivates this fix.
  - Sister bead R1 (push_failed no cooldown) — same pattern.
title: fix(try): declined_needs_decomposition uses execution-eligible=false not time-park

description:
PROBLEM
Cleanup cannot rely only on the next ddx try setup path. Long-lived ddx work processes and server-managed workers can sit idle or run many attempts while stale DDx temp worktrees, heartbeat files, and partial setup directories accumulate in the background.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 starts the work loop and emits loop.start/loop.end, but there is no periodic cleanup worker tied to long-lived polling or graceful shutdown.
- cli/cmd/work.go:101 and related command plumbing start ddx work without a cleanup cadence or cleanup lock.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:49 documents server/FEAT-010 worker state, but server-managed worker paths do not share a background cleanup contract.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:476-497 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:232-240 require startup, periodic, shutdown, and background cleanup with jitter and a cleanup lock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add background cleanup orchestration for long-lived ddx work/server worker processes using the cleanup manager from the base cleanup bead.
- Run cleanup at ddx work startup, periodically while polling, after setup/finalization failures before the next claim, and during graceful signal shutdown.
- Use jitter plus a project cleanup lock so multiple workers do not prune simultaneously.
- Emit structured cleanup events and operator-visible summaries only when cleanup reclaims meaningful resources or blocks progress.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the cleanup manager primitives here.
- Do not replace server-spawn migration work in ddx-eccc6efb; if server workers exec ddx work, they may inherit this behavior instead of duplicating it.
- Do not delete complete evidence or preserved worktrees.
title: work: run periodic background cleanup for long-lived workers

acceptance:
1. TestBeadsTrackerHookAllowsCleanTracker verifies the new guard exits 0 when .ddx/beads.jsonl has no changes.
2. TestBeadsTrackerHookAllowsFullyStagedTrackerChange verifies the guard exits 0 when .ddx/beads.jsonl changes are staged and there are no unstaged tracker changes.
3. TestBeadsTrackerHookFailsOnUnstagedTrackerChange verifies the guard exits non-zero and prints git add .ddx/beads.jsonl when the tracker has unstaged changes.
4. TestBeadsTrackerHookFailsOnPartiallyStagedTrackerChange verifies staged plus unstaged tracker changes are rejected so partial tracker commits cannot leave state behind.
5. lefthook.yml runs the guard during pre-commit next to other fast repo-health guards.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestBeadsTrackerHook" -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Tracker mutations are still easy to leave behind in the operator checkout. Several ddx task paths either rely on best-effort auto-commit or create implementation commits that do not include an already-dirty .ddx/beads.jsonl, leaving the queue state uncommitted after work appears done.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- AGENTS.md:35-39 says tracker mutations must be committed by default, but this is policy guidance rather than a mechanical guard.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:74-97 makes bead auto-commit best-effort and silently ignores AutoCommit errors, so failed tracker commits can leave .ddx/beads.jsonl dirty.
- lefthook.yml:28-50 has fast pre-commit guards for conflicts and local git config, but no guard that fails a commit when .ddx/beads.jsonl has unstaged or partially staged changes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a fast pre-commit guard that inspects .ddx/beads.jsonl directly with git diff.
- Fail when .ddx/beads.jsonl has unstaged changes, or when it has both staged and unstaged changes, so commits cannot leave tracker mutations behind accidentally.
- Allow a commit when .ddx/beads.jsonl is clean, or when all tracker changes are staged in the commit.
- Keep the guard narrow to .ddx/beads.jsonl; do not require all .ddx evidence artifacts to be staged.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the bead storage format.
- Do not make every .ddx/ path mandatory in every commit.
- Do not remove existing beadAutoCommit behavior; this is a commit-time safety net.

DEPS
No deps. This is a direct prevention follow-up from the May 7 worker-collision cleanup.
title: hooks: fail commits that leave beads tracker dirty

description:
Once REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE
- The protocol or check implementation itself (separate beads).

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-215b08a2 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 1

description:
PROBLEM
The try command needs an end-to-end regression that proves a resource_exhausted failure leaves the target bead open and reclaimable instead of parking it behind execute-loop-retry-after or consuming the claim. Existing tests in cli/cmd/try_test.go exercise not-found, closed, unmet-deps, happy-path, and interruption, but they do not cover the command-level resource exhaustion path.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/try.go:324-333 checks the resource checker before the worker run and returns an ExitError on failure. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:695-715 turns a ResourceExhaustedError into a structured result, but there is no command-level test that verifies the failing bead remains open/unclaimed and does not receive a retry cooldown marker.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable in cli/cmd/try_test.go or a sibling e2e test file.
- Seed one ready bead in a temp project, inject a resource checker failure through the command factory, and run ddx try on that bead.
- Assert the command exits non-zero, prints the resource_exhausted stop message, leaves the bead open with no owner, and does not write execute-loop-retry-after.
- Use temp dirs and fakes only; do not fill real disk or inodes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not test unrelated claim-approval or review flows.
- Do not add new retry-policy behavior.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable exists and invokes ddx try with an injected resource_exhausted failure.
2. The command exits non-zero, prints resource_exhausted, and leaves the bead open and unclaimed.
3. The test verifies no execute-loop-retry-after marker is written.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: test: keep ddx try resource exhaustion reclaimable

description:
PROBLEM
The ddx skill is the gateway most interactive agents read before deciding what to do. Without explicit mode guidance there, the agent can still interpret broad queue questions as implementation requests even after AGENTS.md and specs are updated.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" directly to ddx bead ready, which is the worker-ready queue. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:1-16 defines doing work as draining the ready queue and recommends ddx work, but it does not say broad interactive sessions should manage intervention/planning instead of manually executing ready beads. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:43-49 tells agents to pick the next ready bead by default without distinguishing direct worker mode from broad interactive queue management.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the canonical ddx skill references and shipped copies so broad interactive DDx prompts enter queue_steward mode. The skill should tell agents to inspect intervention/focus status, summarize blockers, propose bead/spec changes, and suggest a worker when ready depth warrants it; explicit ddx work/try/execute-bead prompts remain bead_execution and may implement bead AC. Keep review prompts read-only unless the user asks to fix findings.

NON-SCOPE
- Editing SKILL.md frontmatter unless required by existing schema.
- Changing skill install/discovery mechanics.
- Implementing ddx work focus itself; this bead consumes that command surface.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92 and ddx-3c6ca63f.
notes: SUPERSEDED by ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238. The interactive-steward routing bead now owns the first-stop interactive.md work, existing status/work/review reference cleanup, route fixtures, eval-driver schema update, and shipped-copy verification. Do not dispatch this bead independently.

Move to Drain startup: run once before the for-loop. If it fails, return one worker-level execution_failed record and exit (legitimate startup failure). Never run again per iteration.

INTERSECTIONS

- P2: preflight only owns startup advisory checks, not eligibility or provider availability.

- P4: a preflight failure on one bead must not affect later beads.

3. TestExecutor_WallClockCapsAtConfiguredBudget in cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go asserts ExecuteInDir cancels at the configured wall-clock budget (use a much shorter budget for the test, e.g., 200ms, to keep it fast).

description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/agent/types.go:441 sets DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000
  (3 hours). This is the per-attempt absolute wall-clock cap on a
  harness invocation. Two drain wedges observed in the 2026-05-09
  drain consumed the full 3 hours before timing out:

    ddx-c113a116: silent from 16:21 to 19:13:54 (3h0m0.018s),
                  then "do response complete 8472 tok in 3h0m0.018s,
                  0.8 tok/s" → timeout: context deadline exceeded.

    ddx-94a368b6: silent from 19:36 onward (drain killed before
                  natural timeout fired).

  3 hours is too long: a wedged worker burns operator time and budget
  before the timeout fires. 1 hour catches the same wedge class
  before they accumulate substantial cost.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441
        DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
  - Comment at line 438: "Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout
    — so normal long tasks still complete while genuinely hung workers
    free themselves."
  - The 1.5x ratio against the 2h idle timeout (DefaultTimeoutMS at
    line 434) is preserved by halving both proportionally OR by
    halving wall-clock alone (idle timer protects against true silence
    independently).

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441: change DefaultWallClockMS to
    3600000 (1 hour).
  - Update the comment at line 438 to reflect the 1h cap and explain
    the wedge-detection rationale (citing this bead).
  - DO NOT change DefaultTimeoutMS (idle timer); it remains at 2h,
    which now exceeds wall-clock — but that's fine, idle and
    wall-clock are separate triggers (whichever fires first wins).
    Document this in the comment.
  - Verify nothing in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:45-46 or
    cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:158-159 hardcodes the 3h value
    elsewhere; they should pull from DefaultWallClockMS.
  - Add a regression test: TestExecutor_WallClockCapsAtConfiguredBudget
    in cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go (or extend an existing
    test) that asserts ExecuteInDir cancels at the configured budget.

NON-SCOPE
  - Per-tool-call timeout (sister bead handles that as a structural
    prevention; this bead is the quick symptom guard).
  - Loop detection via stream-json watcher (separate concern).
  - Removing the underlying source of long Bash calls (sister beads
    ddx-1fda33d2, ddx-150758e7, ddx-f1c476c3 reduce per-push test
    cost; ddx-1f774edf removes per-bead pushes entirely).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent: per-tool-call wall-clock for
    Bash..." — structural prevention; this bead is the immediate
    symptom mitigation.
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf — once landed, drain stops pushing per
    bead, eliminating the 168s test tax that fuels wedges.
notes: Cancelled per user direction 2026-05-09: wall-clock cap is wrong policy. Reducing 3h to 1h would kill legitimately-hard-but-still-active work along with wedges. Right policy: detect 'no real progress' (loops + true idle) rather than capping absolute wall-clock. See sister bead ddx-1c3cad6f (reshaped to cover loop detection + meaningful-event idle reset).
title: fix(agent): reduce DefaultWallClockMS from 3h to 1h (faster wedge detection)

acceptance:
1. cli/cmd/work.go (or equivalent) exposes a --max-bead-cost flag (default $5, configurable; 0 = unlimited).
2. cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go contains a PerBeadCostTracker (alongside the existing global CostCapTracker).
3. runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts instantiates a PerBeadCostTracker per bead and stops escalation when the budget is exceeded.
4. When a bead has a label of the form budget:<float>, that value overrides the default per-bead budget.
5. On budget exhaustion: (a) bead is unclaimed; (b) "per_bead_budget_exhausted" event is appended (matches TD-031 §5 row); (c) consecutive_ladder_exhaustions is incremented (chains into sister bead ddx-63155d5c auto-recovery trigger); (d) NO cooldown is set.
6. The auto-recovery hook (sister bead ddx-63155d5c) reads consecutive_ladder_exhaustions and treats per_bead_budget_exhausted as a valid trigger source (alongside ladder exhaustion).
7. TestPerBeadCostTracker_StopsEscalationAtLimit, TestPerBeadCostTracker_NoLimit_RunsToLadderExhaustion, TestPerBeadCostTracker_BudgetLabelOverridesDefault, TestPerBeadCostTracker_InvalidBudgetLabel_FallsBackToDefault, TestPerBeadCostTracker_ChainsIntoAutoRecovery all pass.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Today's cost cap is ONLY a global drain-level CostCapTracker
  (cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go:128). There is no
  per-bead budget. A bead that fails repeatedly can burn unbounded
  budget escalating up the ladder before the global cap fires —
  one expensive bead can exhaust the entire drain's budget while
  cheap beads sit unworked.

  ADR-024 P1 (cheapest first, sister bead at rank 19) only works
  in practice if there is a per-bead "stop" condition to bound the
  escalation cost. Without it, "cheapest first" devolves into
  "cheapest first, then the most expensive thing imaginable,
  infinitely."

  Per ADR-024 Operator escape hatches subsection (sister bead
  rank 19), operators must be able to override the per-bead
  budget on a specific bead via a label budget:<USD>.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go:128 CostCapTracker is
    a single drain-level accumulator.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go and
    cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts
    do NOT instantiate per-bead trackers.
  - There's no --max-bead-cost flag.
  - There's no budget:<USD> label override mechanism.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a new flag --max-bead-cost (default $5; configurable):
    cli/cmd/work.go (or wherever drain flags live).
  - cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go: rename
    CostCapTracker to GlobalCostCapTracker (or split). Add a
    PerBeadCostTracker that takes a budget at construction.
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go
    runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts: instantiate a
    PerBeadCostTracker per bead claim, accumulate per-attempt cost,
    stop escalation (return ErrPerBeadBudgetExceeded) when budget
    is exceeded.
  - Operator escape hatch: when a bead has label budget:<USD>
    (e.g. budget:25.0), use that as the per-bead budget instead
    of --max-bead-cost. Parse from labels; emit a budget-override
    event when used.
  - On per-bead budget exhaustion: unclaim, leave bead open, log
    a "per_bead_budget_exhausted" event with cost details (matches
    TD-031 §5 row defined in sister bead rank 19). NOT a cooldown.

  Tests:
     - TestPerBeadCostTracker_StopsEscalationAtLimit
     - TestPerBeadCostTracker_NoLimit_RunsToLadderExhaustion
     - TestPerBeadCostTracker_BudgetLabelOverridesDefault: bead
       with label "budget:25.0" uses $25 instead of default $5.
     - TestPerBeadCostTracker_InvalidBudgetLabel_FallsBackToDefault.

NON-SCOPE
  - Adjusting per-bead default ($5 is starting point; tunable).
  - Smart per-bead budgeting (e.g., complexity-based) — out of
    scope.
  - The recovery:manual label (sister bead rank 25 handles).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(specs)" rank 19 — Per-bead budget +
    Operator escape hatches subsections.

description:
PROBLEM
The C9 bead ddx-9228a484 is blocked because it bundles every stop/retry concern into one oversized pass. The first executable slice is to create the work-layer StopCondition vocabulary and wire the drain loop to use it for terminal drain exits before moving budget, no-progress, review repair, or trace behavior.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:504-508 documents exit reasons as ad hoc strings inside ExecuteBeadWorker.Run instead of using a typed stop classifier.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:550-560 checks context cancellation inline during every drain iteration and sets exit_reason directly.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1355-1357 handles --once completion inline with another direct return.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1789-1813 currently lets the executor closure synthesize a cost-cap failure report, so budget stopping is not represented as a first-class work-layer stop condition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with a StopCondition enum and helper that classifies drain exit decisions.
- Required enum values: Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.
- Wire ExecuteBeadWorker.Run to call the helper for the already-existing terminal decisions it owns today: context cancellation/signal, --once completion, explicit poll-zero/drained behavior, and budget stop handoff if a budget stop is provided by the caller.
- Keep behavior-compatible loop.end exit_reason strings, but derive them from the typed stop condition instead of scattering literals.
- Leave cost-cap relocation, no-progress eligibility, review terminal taxonomy, repair cycles, and execution trace schema to sibling split beads.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change retry budgets or no-progress eligibility in this bead.
- Do not implement review repair cycles.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model selection, or MinPower escalation.
- Do not rename ExecuteBeadWorker or move the whole drain loop.

DEPS
No deps. The closed dependencies on ddx-9228a484 already landed; this is the first split slice.

acceptance:
1. In watch mode, every idle poll with no execution-ready work prints `idle: no execution-ready beads; sleeping <duration>` with an `HH:MM:SS` timestamp.
2. Every idle poll output includes compact queue-state counts from the loop queue snapshot: execution-ready, blocked, needs-human/investigation, cooldown/deferred with next retry when available, execution-ineligible, superseded, epics, and epic closure candidates.
3. Every idle poll output includes human-review dependency pressure when present: `N beads blocked behind M needs-human blockers`.
4. The first idle poll after a queue-state change includes numbered blocker entries containing each blocker ID, title, and downstream blocked count; unchanged repeated polls may omit the full numbered list but must keep the compact counts and total blocked-behind-human summary.
5. `TestWorkWatchIdleStdout_PrintsQueueStatusAndHumanBlockers` covers a fixture with 30 beads blocked behind 3 needs-human blockers and asserts the idle output names all 3 blockers on the first idle poll.
6. `TestWorkWatchIdleStdout_RepeatedPollKeepsCompactQueueStatus` proves an unchanged repeated idle poll still prints queue counts and the total blocked-behind-human summary even when the full numbered blocker list is suppressed.
7. When a new candidate appears after idle, stdout prints a blank line followed by `taking next ready bead from queue: <id> — <title>` before the bead header; `TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle` covers this.
8. Existing `loop.idle` and `loop.active` progress events still fire so server-managed workers keep their running/idle substate behavior.
9. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatchIdleStdout|TestWorkWatchStdout|TestSleepOrWake|TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue' -count=1` passes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
In watch mode, an idle `ddx work` process should explain why it is sleeping and what queue state remains on each idle poll. Current behavior only emits an internal `loop.idle` progress signal and leaves human stdout without enough context. When new work later appears, the next bead can start abruptly after an idle period without a visible transition.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:656-688 emits `loop.idle` and sleeps, but it does not print a human idle line with queue-state context.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:689-699 clears idle state on `loop.active`, but it does not separate the next bead block from the prior idle block or announce the bead selected from the queue.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:722-737 currently owns direct bead header logging; the scoped renderer introduced by ddx-55900861 should own the human transition lines.
- ddx-7dcbd40b will expose the queue snapshot and human-review blocker pressure that this renderer needs.

PROPOSED FIX
Render watch-mode idle and wake transitions through the scoped work-log renderer:
- On every idle poll where no execution-ready bead exists, print a concise line such as `HH:MM:SS idle: no execution-ready beads; sleeping 30s`.
- Every idle poll line must include compact queue-state counts: execution-ready count, blocked count, needs-human count, cooldown/deferred count with next retry when known, and other non-executable buckets available in the queue snapshot.
- If open work is transitively blocked behind human-review blockers, include a sentence such as `30 beads blocked behind 3 needs-human blockers:` followed by numbered blocker entries with ID, title, and downstream blocked count.
- The full numbered blocker list may be rate-limited when unchanged, but the compact counts and the total `N beads blocked behind M needs-human blockers` summary must remain visible on every idle poll.
- Cap very long blocker lists with an explicit `and N more` suffix, but do not omit all blocker IDs when the list is short.
- When watch mode later finds work after an idle period, print a blank line and `taking next ready bead from queue: <id> — <title>` before the bead header/block starts.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/work_log_renderer.go
- cli/internal/agent/work_log_renderer_test.go or execute_bead_loop stdout tests

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the queue snapshot fields; ddx-7dcbd40b owns them.
- Do not change server worker state/substate semantics beyond preserving the existing `loop.idle` / `loop.active` progress signals.
- Do not change queue selection, readiness, retry, or cost policy.
- Do not change final terminal summary output; ddx-dc300be9 owns that.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-eeced112. Depends on ddx-7d9e1e82 (contract), ddx-55900861 (renderer), ddx-f9acb86d (watch mode), and ddx-7dcbd40b (queue snapshot).

description:
PROBLEM
The Axon backend contract requires a generated GraphQL client plus a WebSocket subscription client, but the current implementation is an in-process JSONL emulation instead of a typed Axon package. The gap is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-26, which says the backend is a local emulation and not the wire-path implementation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 explicitly keeps the backend as a JSONL-shaped emulation, so there is nowhere for genqlient-generated bindings, schema snapshots, query documents, or a subscription transport to live. The repo has no Axon client package to satisfy TD-030's GraphQL-only requirement.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql with the pinned introspection snapshot.
- Add query/mutation/subscription documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/.
- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Add a small subscription transport in the same package for GraphQL WS changeEvents.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing backend_axon.go itself; that is the follow-up implementation bead.
- Config routing and package-level test matrix changes.
- Axon server-side schema work.

description:
PROBLEM
  TD-031 §5 lists 14 outcome rows for execute-bead-driven status transitions
  but does not address the dependency-satisfied auto-close path executed by
  ddx bead reconcile. As a result, the spec is silent on whether ClosureGate
  applies to reconciliation closes, and the implementation diverges from the
  evidence-close path without that divergence being declared.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1234-1257 closeWithEvidence enforces ClosureGate(b)
    before the in_progress -> closed transition. ClosureGate requires evidence
    in the form of session_id + closing_commit_sha or equivalent attachments
    per the gate's contract.
  - cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:236-242 closes beads via
    UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(StatusClosed, {ManualClose: true, Reason, Source})
    when applyReconcilePlan sees CloseSatisfied=true, then calls
    externalizeEvents at line 252. The ClosureGate is never invoked.
  - Reconcile-close happens when all dependencies are closed and the satisfied
    work is recognised post-hoc; it has no execution session of its own.
  - TD-031 §5 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
    lines ~264-316) does not enumerate this path, leaving readers to infer it
    from store.go and reconcile.go.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Edit docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:
    - Add a new outcome row in §5 covering "dependency-satisfied reconcile
      close" with status transition open -> closed (via UpdateWithLifecycleStatus,
      ManualClose=true), event lifecycle_reconciled (already emitted by
      reconcile.go:227), and Extra updates clearing the listed
      execute-loop-* fields.
    - Add an explicit invariant: ClosureGate applies to evidence-bearing
      execute-bead closes (CloseWithEvidence path); reconcile-close is a
      distinct meta-close that intentionally bypasses the gate because the
      bead has no execution session and no closing_commit_sha to provide.
      The justification is that all dependencies are closed (each of which
      individually passed ClosureGate or was a meta-close itself), so the
      parent's closure inherits its evidence by reference through the
      dependency edges.
    - Reference reconcile.go:208-254 and ClosureGate at store.go:1245 by file:line.

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the runtime behavior of reconcile.go or closeWithEvidence (B2
    handles any code-side follow-up if the spec amendment requires it).
  - Adding new ClosureGate variants or evidence types.
  - Updating ADR-023; this is purely a TD-031 §5 amendment.

INTERSECTIONS
  - ddx-ca1c66ee (epic parent).
  - B2 depends on this bead landing because the call-site comment B2 adds
    quotes the amended TD-031 invariant.
title: docs(TD-031): specify reconcile-close vs evidence-close semantics

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go EscalatableStatuses no longer contains "structural_validation_failed".
2. TestStructuralValidationFailed_DoesNotEscalate asserts ShouldEscalate returns false for this status.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:24 includes
  structural_validation_failed in EscalatableStatuses, but the
  surrounding comment (lines 27-29) explicitly says "structural
  failures do not escalate because a smarter model cannot fix a
  malformed prompt."

  This internal inconsistency wastes escalation budget: a bead with
  a structural defect (corrupted prompt, malformed bead body, etc.)
  will fail the same way at every tier, but escalation will keep
  retrying at higher and higher cost.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:20-25
      var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
          "execution_failed":               true,
          "post_run_check_failed":          true,
          "land_conflict":                  true,
          "structural_validation_failed":   true, // <-- WRONG per comment below
      }
  The comment at lines 27-29 contradicts this entry.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:24: delete the
    "structural_validation_failed": true entry.
  - Update any test in cli/internal/escalation/escalation_test.go
    that asserts the status escalates (likely
    TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab or similar).
  - Add positive test TestStructuralValidationFailed_DoesNotEscalate
    asserting ShouldEscalate("structural_validation_failed") == false.

NON-SCOPE
  - Other entries in EscalatableStatuses (separate analysis).
  - Refactoring the escalatable-status registry.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR-024)" P3 sequencing rule covers this.
title: fix(escalation): remove structural_validation_failed from EscalatableStatuses

description:
Per the TD from B_TD: implement the axon backend in cli/internal/bead/backend_axon.go (or per the existing backend.go shape). Use genqlient to generate a typed Go client from Axon's GraphQL introspection schema — no protoc/protobuf toolchain. Wire as a selectable backend (config flag .ddx/config.yaml backend: axon|jsonl|bd). Default stays jsonl Day 1. Existing tests must pass against both backends.

Subscription support: implement a subscription client (genqlient supports subscriptions via separate library, or use a simple WebSocket GraphQL subscriptions client) so that DDx server can subscribe to Axon ChangeEvents on ddx_beads / ddx_bead_events for live UI updates.

description:
PROBLEM
TD-030 requires a live GraphQL subscription path so DDx can subscribe to Axon changeEvents for bead and bead-event updates. The current test suite in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go covers CRUD, deps, events, and import/export, but there is no subscription smoke test yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go:131-179 verifies the split event collection, but nothing in the package opens a WebSocket subscription and asserts that changeEvents are delivered in order. That leaves the live-update path unexercised.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an integration test in cli/internal/bead/axon/ that opens the subscription endpoint, performs a small bead mutation sequence, and asserts the expected changeEvents arrive.
- Reuse the generated Axon client and subscription transport from the client-scaffolding bead.
- Keep the test isolated and deterministic so it can run in the normal package suite.

NON-SCOPE
- Core backend CRUD logic.
- Config selection.
- Package-wide backend matrix work.
