notes: REFERENCE UPDATE 2026-05-11: design + integration plan consolidated into TD-027 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md). TD-027 is now the canonical acceptance criteria source — its §28 (Acceptance Criteria) supersedes any AC enumerated in the prior plan doc. TD-031 also folded into TD-027. The bead's design hold (cooldown until 2027-01-01 for ddx-c6317784) remains in effect until the consolidated spec package is reviewed and committed.
title: bead/axon: add WebSocket subscription integration smoke test

acceptance:
1. Legacy format handling: beads with `schema_version` absent in JSONL read as `schema_version=1` per TD-027 §11.4. Test: `TestBead_LegacyFormat_ReadsAsV1`.
2. Every caller that uses Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID passes ctx through. No `*Store.Init()` (no-ctx) calls remain in production code outside test fixtures. Event callers are intentionally excluded and handled by E.
3. `RawBackend` docstring updated with the warning text from TD-027 §6.2 directing new backends to implement `Backend` directly.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Legacy format handling: beads with `schema_version` absent in JSONL read as `schema_version=1` per TD-027 §11.4. Test: `TestBead_LegacyFormat_ReadsAsV1`.
- Every caller that uses Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID passes ctx through. No `*Store.Init()` (no-ctx) calls remain in production code outside test fixtures. Event callers are intentionally excluded and handled by E.
- `RawBackend` docstring updated with the warning text from TD-027 §6.2 directing new backends to implement `Backend` directly.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 4

acceptance: 1. agent-3bb96bf5 tagged release identified (e.g., fizeau v0.10.0). 2. cli/go.mod updated to that tagged version. 3. cd cli && go build ./... succeeds. 4. cd cli && go test ./... passes (no test consumes the new fields yet — that's S10_2/3/5). 5. acceptance:
1. TestTryResourcePreflight_FailsBeforeClaim verifies a failing injected resource checker runs cleanup once, returns resource_exhausted, and leaves the bead open/unclaimed.
2. TestTryResourcePreflight_RechecksAfterCleanup verifies ddx try proceeds when cleanup restores healthy resource status.
3. TestExecuteBeadWorktreeAddFailure_RemovesPartialDir verifies a simulated WorktreeAdd ENOSPC failure removes the attempt partial directory under DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR.
4. TestTryResourcePreflight_ChecksEvidenceAndTempRoots verifies both the temp worktree root and durable evidence root are checked for writability and resource health.
5. Production ddx try/work paths call the resource preflight before claim/worktree creation; tests assert the new code path is wired, not just unit-tested.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestTryResourcePreflight|TestExecuteBeadWorktreeAddFailure" -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ddx try can claim/checkpoint and then fail during worktree setup when the temp worktree or evidence root is out of bytes/inodes or unwritable. In the May 6 incident, git worktree add failed with No space left on device after creating partial execution artifacts, and the failure was treated as ordinary execution_failed instead of a resource guardrail.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:686-714 creates the temp worktree parent, commits/checkpoints, resolves base, and calls WorktreeAdd before any resource-health check of $TMPDIR/ddx-exec-wt or .ddx/executions/.ddx/runs.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:713-714 returns immediately on WorktreeAdd failure, so partial unregistered directories can remain outside the deferred WorktreeRemove path.
- cli/cmd/try.go:306-342 routes ddx try through ExecuteBeadWorker without a pre-claim resource gate that can fail before claiming the target bead.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:454-467 requires ddx try to validate roots, run one cleanup pass, re-check, and return resource_exhausted without claiming when still unhealthy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a resource checker abstraction that validates temp worktree root and durable evidence root writability, free bytes, and free inodes where supported.
- Run resource preflight before claim/worktree creation in ddx try paths, using the cleanup manager once before final failure.
- Classify persistent resource failure as resource_exhausted with structured detail, not generic execution_failed/quota.
- Ensure WorktreeAdd failure removes partial unregistered worktree directories created for the attempt.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not decide ddx work loop stop behavior here; sibling ddx work bead consumes the resource_exhausted outcome.
- Do not add background cleanup cadence here.
- Do not implement a real low-disk integration test that fills a filesystem; use injectable resource checker/fake filesystem boundaries.

description:
# PROBLEM
`*bead.WatcherHub` (`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:36-66`) constructs its own `*Store` internally via `NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. This bypasses any factory-level capability validation or backend selection. Today everything is JSONL so this is invisible; once AxonStore ships and `bead.backend: axon` is configured for production, the WatcherHub still polls a local JSONL file while the authoritative bead state lives in Lakebase Postgres. Result: silent stale-data bug — `beadLifecycle` GraphQL subscriptions return empty or stale events.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:54-66` — `SubscribeLifecycle` instantiates a `*Store` directly: `store := NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. No injection point for the configured backend.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:60-65` — `newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)` ties the watcher to that locally-constructed Store.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go` — constructs `*WatcherHub` and passes it to subscription resolvers. The hub is constructed once at server startup; the project Stores it creates internally are detached from any per-project factory.

# PROPOSED FIX
Change `WatcherHub` to take a `bead.BeadReader` (or `Backend`) factory function at construction:

```go
type StoreFactory func(projectID string) (bead.BeadReader, error)

type WatcherHub struct {
    factory  StoreFactory
    watchers map[string]*projectWatcher
    interval time.Duration
    ...
}

func NewWatcherHub(factory StoreFactory, interval time.Duration) *WatcherHub { ... }

func (h *WatcherHub) SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error) {
    // ...
    store, err := h.factory(projectID)
    if err != nil { return nil, nil, err }
    pw = newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)
    // ...
}
```

`cli/internal/server/server.go` is updated to pass a factory that uses the project's configured backend (selected via the same config pathway that the rest of ddx-server uses).

# NON-SCOPE
- Native subscription transport on AxonStore (separate work — would replace the polling-based `projectWatcher` with native push when available).
- Changing `LifecycleEvent` shape or semantics.
- Multi-tenant identity propagation (handled separately via the `bead.WithIdentity` ctx accessor pattern).
- Modifying callers other than `cli/internal/server/server.go` and the subscription resolver.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `WatcherHub` constructor signature changes to accept a `StoreFactory` parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ LifecycleSubscriber = (*WatcherHub)(nil)` still holds.
2. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` no longer contains a direct `NewStore(...)` call.
3. `cli/internal/server/server.go` constructs `WatcherHub` with a factory that delegates to the same backend-selection path the rest of the server uses.
4. Test `TestWatcherHub_UsesProvidedFactory` injects a fake factory, asserts the factory is invoked when SubscribeLifecycle is called, and asserts the watcher polls the injected `bead.BeadReader` rather than the filesystem.
5. Test `TestWatcherHub_PropagatesFactoryErrors` injects a factory that returns an error and asserts SubscribeLifecycle returns the error.
6. Existing `TestWatcherHub_*` tests in `cli/internal/bead/watcher_test.go` are updated to use the factory pattern; they continue to pass.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:server, kind:refactor, spec:FEAT-002, spec:FEAT-004, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049 (axon backend prototype umbrella).
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (interface refinement, which adds `BeadReader` and the LifecycleSubscriber ctx-aware signature).
notes: REFERENCE UPDATE 2026-05-11: design + integration plan consolidated into TD-027 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md). TD-027 is now the canonical acceptance criteria source — its §28 (Acceptance Criteria) supersedes any AC enumerated in the prior plan doc. TD-031 also folded into TD-027. The bead's design hold (cooldown until 2027-01-01 for ddx-c6317784) remains in effect until the consolidated spec package is reviewed and committed.
title: WatcherHub takes a BeadReader factory at construction (interface-refactor S5 follow-up)

description:
PROBLEM
  Multiple tests in cli/cmd/ pass the literal "/tmp" as the working-directory
  argument to NewCommandFactory(...). Today these tests don't write files at
  that path, but a future change to NewCommandFactory or its commands could
  silently start writing into /tmp without any cleanup. Discovered by the
  2026-05-09 test-cleanup audit.

ROOT CAUSE
  Hardcoded "/tmp" appears in NewCommandFactory calls in (at minimum):
    - cli/cmd/version_acceptance_test.go:15
    - cli/cmd/status_test.go:20
    - cli/cmd/prompts_test.go:16
    - cli/cmd/help_acceptance_test.go:15
    - cli/cmd/security_test.go:19
    - cli/cmd/contract_test.go:21,268,366
    - cli/cmd/list_test.go:184
    - cli/cmd/config_test.go:180
  Each call passes "/tmp" because the tests verify CLI output (version
  strings, help text, contract shapes, list output, etc.) where the
  working-dir traversal is currently a no-op. There is no defensive
  guarantee that this remains true.

PROPOSED FIX
  - In each listed file:line, replace the literal "/tmp" with t.TempDir()
    (Go's stdlib testing helper, auto-registers cleanup).
  - Verify no other behavior changes; the working dir is still a unique
    temp path, just one that's guaranteed empty and auto-cleaned.
  - Add a CI/lint check: a small grep-based assertion (or a new linter
    rule) that flags any future NewCommandFactory("/tmp") literal in
    cli/cmd/*_test.go. The simplest implementation is a one-line addition
    to lefthook's no-debug or no-tmp pre-commit hook.

NON-SCOPE
  - Tests in cli/cmd/server_state_test.go and cli/internal/server/state_test.go
    that embed /tmp/TestFooBar* paths in JSON test data (no filesystem I/O;
    test-data only).
  - Test files outside cli/cmd/.
  - The genuine /tmp leak in cli/cmd/testutils_test.go GetTestLibraryPath
    (covered by the sister bead on shared-fixture cleanup).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead: shared-fixture cleanup in testutils_test.go.
title: tests: replace NewCommandFactory("/tmp") placeholders with t.TempDir()

acceptance:
1. `ddx work` readiness/preflight hooks run under a bounded timeout with a clear default and, if configurable, documented config/flag behavior.
2. On readiness timeout, the attempt records evidence containing the readiness command/context, timeout duration, and resulting bead outcome.
3. A timed-out readiness check does not leave the bead indefinitely claimed/in_progress.
4. In watch mode, after handling the timed-out readiness outcome, the worker can continue to other eligible ready beads.
5. Regression coverage proves a hanging readiness hook cannot block the worker indefinitely.
description:
In Niflheim, `ddx work --watch --profile smart` claimed bead `niflheim-edcb2b7c` and then parked at `readiness check: starting` without spawning an agent child, timing out, recording a terminal non-success outcome, or releasing/blocking the bead. The readiness check was for a live soak bead whose previous evidence already showed PgWire unavailable on `127.0.0.1:5433`.

Observed behavior on 2026-05-13:
- Worker was alive in tmux (`ddx` pane process), but no child agent process existed.
- Worker log stopped after `▶ niflheim-edcb2b7c ...` and `readiness check: starting`.
- Bead remained `in_progress`/claimed.
- Queue had many ready beads, but the worker could not move past this readiness phase.

Expected behavior: readiness hooks must be bounded. If readiness cannot complete inside a configured/default timeout, `ddx work` should write evidence explaining the failed readiness command, map the outcome to blocked/open according to the no_changes contract, unclaim the bead when appropriate, and continue draining other ready work instead of parking the whole worker.

This is separate from the underlying Niflheim PgWire availability bug; DDx should not require the target service bug to be fixed before the queue can keep moving.
title: Bound ddx work readiness hooks so blocked beads cannot hang a worker forever

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-70a432b5 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- FEAT-008 spec updated with Run-detail-page subsection + trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist.
- FEAT-010 spec updated with extended Run resolver contract + drain-time persistence note + ToolCallEntry definition.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: Story 16.3: Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit even: part 1

description:
PROBLEM
The `ddx work` bead lifecycle stdout starts with vague and repetitive lines such as `pre-claim intake: starting ddx-cf37253d`, which hides what the system is actually doing and repeats the bead id already shown in the header.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:722-737 prints the bead header and readiness/pre-claim start text directly.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:737 uses `pre-claim intake: starting %s`, which leaks an internal hook name and repeats the current bead id.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:742-750 records a `ProgressEvent` with phase `pre_claim_intake` and message `pre-claim intake`, which is useful internally but not acceptable human stdout.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:149-165 describes the behavior as readiness and claim gating.

PROPOSED FIX
Route DDx lifecycle stdout through the scoped work-log renderer. Human output should describe the work as readiness checking/claim gating, include `HH:MM:SS`, suppress the current bead id after the header, and include route/model facts when a lifecycle line reports model-backed work.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/work_log_renderer.go only for narrow helper additions if required

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rename persisted event kinds or remove compatibility fields consumed by existing tests or tools.
- Do not change claim ownership, intake policy, routing, or retry behavior.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-eeced112. Depends on renderer foundation.

description:
PROBLEM
Pre-claim readiness currently treats recoverable quality/refinement findings as operator-required, which can park otherwise executable open beads and interrupt continuous forward progress. In default WARN-ONLY behavior, these findings should warn and proceed unless DDx has a hard manual-decision blocker.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:90-94 maps ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine to PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired when no validated rewrite is present. That causes refinement needs to follow the proposed parking path instead of advisory warning.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_ac_quality.go:115-124 documents and implements the AC quality gate as execution-ineligible plus PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired when score < threshold, even though ADR-023 default mode is WARN-ONLY.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 parks every terminal non-actionable intake outcome to proposed, without a policy distinction between recoverable rubric noise and hard operator_required findings.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add/consult the readiness quality mode defined by the spec bead: WARN-ONLY by default, explicit BLOCK/factory mode when configured.
- In WARN-ONLY mode, recoverable rubric gaps, needs_refine findings, low AC-quality scores, and readiness infrastructure noise append warning evidence and proceed to claim/implementation.
- Keep parking for true hard blockers: explicit operator_required from ambiguity/contradiction, lossy decomposition, depth cap with no executable path, unsafe commitment loss, or BLOCK/factory mode low-quality findings.
- Preserve existing post-attempt review and no_changes operator-required behavior unless this pre-claim path directly calls it.

NON-SCOPE
- No operator-override history detection here; that is ddx operator-override child work.
- No new event vocabulary; structured evidence is covered by the observability child.
- No Fizeau routing/profile/model behavior.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on ddx-ef9e8aec.
notes: Operator reviewed pre-claim parking on 2026-05-12. Bead is execution-ready as written; readiness rewrite dropped part of the ROOT CAUSE file:line citation cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:90-94. Preserve the original prompt and skip lifecycle rewrite for execution.
title: agent: make WARN-ONLY readiness findings proceed

description:
PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0: bead-lifecycle quality framework needs an ADR documenting the policy + amendments to existing FEATs to formally adopt the lint/triage hooks and the sub-skill packaging change.

ROOT CAUSE
Today no policy artifact exists for bead-quality enforcement. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 documents P7 as a principle but does not specify the enforcement mechanism, waiver model, staged rollout, or recovery UX. Existing FEAT docs are stale on relevant points:
- FEAT-011 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:180 says DDx ships exactly one skill, but .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md already demonstrates nested sub-skill packaging.
- FEAT-004 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:150 owns bead validation hooks but doesn't describe authoring-quality lint or post-attempt triage.
- FEAT-010 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:21 documents the layer-1/2/3 invocation model but doesn't spec hook insertion points for ddx try / ddx work.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Author ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md covering:
   - Policy: lint pre-dispatch + triage post-attempt; both invoke the same bead-lifecycle sub-skill
   - Staged rollout: WARN-ONLY default → BLOCK opt-in after baseline confirms
   - Waiver model: rubric-level by bead-type (doc/epic/deletion skip certain criteria, per docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md:219); per-bead lint-waiver:<criterion> label for rare exceptions; --force --reason records an event (does not silently bypass)
   - Fail-open hook behavior (P1 from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES): infrastructure failure proceeds; only valid low lint score blocks (in BLOCK mode)
   - Operator recovery UX: blocked dispatch prints missing fields + suggested ddx bead update commands

2. Amend FEAT-004 (beads): add §"Authoring quality lint" describing the rubric, sub-skill location, waiver storage (label-based), and tracker metadata (no new schema fields; lint output ephemeral in evidence dir).

3. Amend FEAT-010 (executions): add §"Quality hooks" describing PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook insertion points in ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime; OutcomeReason field beside existing Disrupted in ExecuteBeadReport.

4. Amend FEAT-011 (skills): replace "ships exactly one skill" with the nested workflow-skill model demonstrated by bead-breakdown/, replay-bead/, etc. Document the 5 shipped-skill paths per codex review (skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/).

5. Cross-link: ADR-023 references RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652) + evidence (ddx-f339c399) + bead-authoring-template.md as the canonical template.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT (codex: not warranted; existing FEATs cover the surface)
- ADR for the in_progress-eligibility bug (separate concern)
- Cross-project skill packaging (deferred per FEAT-015)
- Schema changes to bead JSON (waivers via labels; no new fields)

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defines StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked, and drain.stop(state) is the only production budget/terminal stop classifier.
2. Cost-cap checks are removed from executor closure call sites and reviewer group cost is charged to the same budget accumulator before close eligibility is evaluated.
3. No-progress retry budget is incremented only for valid implementation attempts; TestStopCondition_NoProgress_IgnoresIntakeRoutingReviewAndOperatorStates covers intake block, decomposition, claim race, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review-error, and needs_human cases.
4. Review REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK no longer reopens a closed bead. A review_fixable_gap prevents close, records review-fixable-gap, and schedules one bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers this.
5. review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error transition to blocked with needs_human and do not consume no-progress budget. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress covers this.
6. MinPower retry/escalation behavior from ddx-cfedee8e is wired in the attempt/retry path without mutating explicit harness/model/profile pins. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins covers the path.
7. Execution records include append-only cycle trace data for initial and repair cycles: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, and final decision. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsInitialAndRepairCycles covers both cycles.
8. Downstream-consumer audit for cost-cap and stop-condition event names is committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The execution loop now needs a single durable stop/retry policy for the reliable factory workflow. Current code still exposes older post-merge review/reopen semantics and scattered stop logic: ExecuteBeadReport.ReviewVerdict is documented as post-merge in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226, Reopen is documented for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300, no-progress suppression only excludes a narrow transient set at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576, and C9 previously depended on ddx-cfedee8e even though C9 absorbs that scope.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818 contain historical cost-cap stop sites outside a first-class StopCondition gate.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 still describe post-merge reviewer close/reopen behavior, which conflicts with ADR-024 default adversarial pre-close review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 counts no-progress by base/result equality but only excludes transport/quota/routing/timeout/merge_conflict outcome reasons; intake blocks, decomposition, review errors, and operator-required states must not consume no-progress retry budget.
- ddx-9228a484 previously listed ddx-cfedee8e as a dependency while also absorbing it, creating a queue deadlock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Implement cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with StopCondition values Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.
- Move all budget/cost-cap decisions, including implementer plus adversarial reviewer group cost, into drain.stop(state).
- Relocate MinPower retry behavior from ddx-cfedee8e into the attempt/retry path without mutating requested harness/model/profile pins. A review_fixable_gap may raise MinPower for the repair attempt; spec gaps, missing acceptance, too-large, unsafe/out-of-scope, review errors beyond cap, intake ambiguity, and decomposition overflow block for the operator instead.
- Make no-progress budget count only valid implementation attempts where checkout/context existed and the implementer had an opportunity to modify or verify code. Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review errors, and operator-action states.
- Keep append-only cycle trace fields for every implementation cycle and repair cycle: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer slots, review result, final decision.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the adversarial review gate itself; that is tracked separately.
- Frontend display of stop reasons.
- Poll-interval defaults and idle workers; already handled by ddx-dc157075 / C10.

1. Sub-skill bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md exists in all 5 shipped paths: skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/. Identical content (or symlinked per existing bead-breakdown/ packaging convention).

2. SKILL.md frontmatter has name=bead-lifecycle + description matching the proposed wording.

3. SKILL.md body has 4 sections: LINT MODE, TRIAGE MODE, REFINE MODE, WAIVER TABLE. Each MODE section has explicit JSON output contract with named fields.

4. bead-lifecycle/examples/ directory has 5-10 example files (curated by epic owner from closed beads; one per bead-type at minimum).

5. WIRED-IN: parent ddx skill (.agents/skills/ddx/SKILL.md router section) gains a line pointing operators/agents at bead-lifecycle/ for "score a bead" / "triage a failed attempt" / "refine a bead" intents.

6. Manual verification: 'find . -path "*/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md" -not -path "./.git/*"' returns 5 paths.

7. Manual verification: invoke skill via 'ddx agent run --harness=sonnet --text="MODE: lint\nUse the bead-lifecycle skill to lint bead ddx-1e516bc9. Return JSON only."' and observe valid JSON output with score field.

8. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>]. Stage the 5 SKILL.md paths + bead-lifecycle/examples/* only.

9. lefthook run pre-commit passes (skill-schema check exists at lefthook config; will validate SKILL.md frontmatter).
description:
PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0 + ADR-023 (filed as ddx-XXXX): a sub-skill is needed at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ (and 4 other shipped paths) carrying the rubric, examples, and judge prompts for lint + triage + refine modes. ddx try will compose this sub-skill via the runner library.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md (existing) is the precedent for nested workflow sub-skills under the parent ddx skill. No equivalent exists for bead authoring quality. The bead-authoring-template.md at docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md is the canonical template (per codex review) but only has 1 strong example + 1 weak example (line 98 of template).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Create sub-skill directory bead-lifecycle/ under each of 5 shipped paths (codex amendment): skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/. SKILL.md + supporting files identical across all 5 (or symlinked if the build process supports it; check current bead-breakdown/ packaging).

2. SKILL.md frontmatter: name=bead-lifecycle, description="Score, classify, and refine ddx beads. Used by ddx try hooks pre-dispatch (lint mode) and post-attempt (triage mode); operator-invocable for refine mode."

3. SKILL.md body sections:
   - LINT MODE: input=bead JSON; output=JSON {score 0-8, rationale, suggested_fixes, waivers_applied}. References the 8-criterion rubric from bead-authoring-template.md.
   - TRIAGE MODE: input=bead + outcome event + session log excerpt; output=JSON {classification ∈ {already_satisfied, ambiguous, investigated_no_path, decomposed, operator_input_needed, routing, quota, transport, tests_red, merge_conflict, review_block, timeout}, recommended_action ∈ {refine_bead_and_retry, retry_with_more_context, file_children_and_supersede, escalate_to_operator, close_as_already_done, wait_and_retry, give_up}, rationale, suggested_amendments, suggested_followup_beads}.
   - REFINE MODE: input=bead + (optional) prior triage output; output=YAML diff suggesting amendments to bead description + AC.
   - WAIVER TABLE: per-bead-type criterion skips (doc-only skips named-tests + test-name; epic skips concrete-implementation; deletion skips wired-in-assertion). Codex: rubric-first, per-bead label override second.
   - 5-10 CURATED EXAMPLES under bead-lifecycle/examples/: code bug, feature, doc-only, epic, deletion/rename, no-op investigation, upstream/external work. Operator (epic owner ddx-a9d130d0) curates from existing closed beads; LLM does not pick.

4. Mode selection: invocation prompt MUST include "MODE: lint|triage|refine" as first line; SKILL.md instructs the LLM to read mode and apply only that mode's contract.

NON-SCOPE
- LLM judgment baked into Go (this bead is data + markdown only; no code)
- ddx try hook integration (separate child bead)
- Tooling to validate skill output JSON (deferred; LLMs are usually well-behaved with structured-output prompts)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first per FEAT-015)
title: skill: bead-lifecycle sub-skill (lint + triage + refine modes) shipped to all 5 paths + 5-10 curated examples

2. The new test asserts forbidden transitions (closed -> *, cancelled -> *, in_progress -> cancelled) return the expected ValidateLifecycleTransition error against both backends.
3. The new test passes against both the JSONL backend and the Axon backend via the existing conformance parameterization.

description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/bead/backend_conformance_test.go is the single shared test
  suite that must pass against both the JSONL backend (backend_jsonl.go) and
  the Axon backend (axon_backend.go). It covers create, update, claim, deps,
  events, and close - but it does NOT exercise the TD-031 §3 transition
  matrix. Two backends could silently diverge on transition side effects
  (external_blocker_reason persistence on Blocked, claim-Extra cleanup on
  Reopen, label management) without the conformance suite catching it.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/backend_conformance_test.go:53-166 enumerates test
    cases for the existing surface but skips status-transition flows.
  - cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:244-256 declares the legal transition
    matrix; cli/internal/bead/lifecycle_conformance_test.go covers the
    transition validator in isolation but does not run it through both
    backends end-to-end.
  - axon_backend.go and backend_jsonl.go each implement their own metadata
    side effects; drift is undetected.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extend cli/internal/bead/backend_conformance_test.go with a new test
    block TransitionMatrix that, for each backend instance under test:
      a. Walks every legal transition from lifecycle.go:244-256:
         proposed -> {open, cancelled}
         open -> {in_progress, blocked, proposed, cancelled}
         in_progress -> {open, closed, blocked, proposed}
         blocked -> {open, proposed, cancelled}
         closed -> open (with ManualReopen)
      b. For each transition, asserts:
         - The persisted Status field changed.
         - Required Extra side effects landed (external_blocker_reason on
           Blocked, claim-Extra cleared on Reopen, etc.).
         - Forbidden transitions return the expected validation error from
           ValidateLifecycleTransition.
  - Use existing test helpers in backend_conformance_test.go (the
    parameterized factory pattern); do not introduce a new harness.
  - Run the suite against both JSONL and Axon backends via the existing
    parameterization at backend_conformance_test.go top.

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the transition matrix or validator.
  - Adding new transitions.
  - Refactoring the conformance suite structure beyond appending the new
    test block.
  - Drift in event vocabulary (TD-031 §4); covered by separate event tests.

INTERSECTIONS
  - ddx-ca1c66ee (epic parent).
  - No deps on B1/B2/B3; can run in parallel.
title: bead/conformance: add transition-matrix coverage to backend_conformance_test

acceptance:

2. The package conformance helpers make the backend selector explicit instead of silently defaulting in only one path.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.

description:
PROBLEM
The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Backend selection/config plumbing.
- Subscription integration smoke tests.

acceptance:
1. Add `TestCoverageRatchetReadsBaselineFromConfig` proving the ratchet threshold/baseline is loaded from a checked-in config/data file and is not a compiled fallback.
2. Add `TestCoverageRatchetFailsBelowBaseline` proving a measured aggregate below the configured baseline returns a non-zero/failing result with a useful message.
3. Add `TestCoverageRatchetAllowsEqualOrImprovedCoverage` proving equal or improved measured coverage passes without updating the baseline.
4. Add a reproducible command, for example `cd cli && make coverage-ratchet`, that runs `go test` with a cover profile, computes aggregate coverage, and enforces the configured ratchet.
5. Run `cd cli && go test ./...` and the new coverage-ratchet command.
6. Run `lefthook run pre-commit`.
description:
PROBLEM
The default DDx test path currently answers only pass/fail. It does not produce an aggregate coverage number, does not persist a baseline, and does not fail when coverage regresses. That made it easy to miss that the interrupted work/watch tracker-noise path had no real integration coverage until production behavior exposed it.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- Makefile:23-24 delegates `make test` to `cli-test` without any coverage gate.
- Makefile:59-61 delegates CLI tests to `cd cli && $(MAKE) test`, so root-level default testing inherits the CLI target behavior.
- cli/Makefile:111-114 runs `go test -v ./...` with no `-coverprofile`, aggregate coverage parsing, baseline comparison, or ratchet persistence.
- cli/Makefile:116-120 has `test-full` run `go test -race -cover ./...`, but it only prints package coverage and has no enforced ratchet.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a coverage measurement and ratchet workflow for the default Go test suite. The implementation must read its baseline/threshold from a checked-in repo config or data file, not from hardcoded defaults in Go source. Add a Make target that runs coverage, reports aggregate coverage, compares it to the configured baseline, and fails on regression. Decide whether `make test` should invoke the ratchet directly or whether CI/default-test documentation should make the ratchet target mandatory; if not wired into `make test`, document the reason in the bead implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not require live harness tests, perf tests, or networked integration tests for the coverage ratchet. Do not make developers update a baseline implicitly during ordinary test runs. Do not hide coverage failures behind verbose-only output. Do not manually edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- Makefile
- cli/Makefile
- cli/tools/ or cli/internal/ test-support package for coverage-ratchet parsing/comparison
- a checked-in coverage ratchet config/baseline file under cli/ or .ddx/

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Fizeau routing policy
- worker execution semantics
- live_harness and perf test inclusion policy
title: test: measure coverage and enforce a ratchet

acceptance:
1. Probe goroutine; cadence + jitter + backoff per ADR §Probe + freshness state model.
2. Register on NotConnected→Connected; clear worker_id on Connected→NotConnected.
3. Ring buffer for backfill (200 cap; oldest dropped silently; had_dropped_backfill flag set if overflow).
4. Best-effort event mirror; failures logged + continue.
5. Tests: TestWorker_ProbeImmediateFirst, TestWorker_ProbeJitter, TestWorker_BackfillOnReconnect, TestWorker_BackoffOnFailures, TestWorker_NoServer_StillWorks.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
7. WIRED-IN: integration test (TestWorker_RealAttemptEvents_FlowToServer) starts a server (from step 1), starts a real ddx work worker process against a fixture bead, observes that the worker's bead-attempt events appear in the server's derived view within 5s. Asserts the entire worker-server flow end-to-end, not just the probe goroutine in isolation.
description: Add server-probe goroutine to worker; periodic reachability check on ~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr with jittered 30s interval, immediate first probe, exponential backoff on consecutive failures (5min cap); state transitions emit register/disconnect; in-memory ring buffer (200 events) for backfill on Connected transition; event POSTs are best-effort. ~250 LOC.

acceptance:
1. Child bead for root SIGINT/SIGTERM context and user-facing shutdown message exists and is closed.
2. Child bead for interrupted attempt cleanup, unclaim, liveness cleanup, and regression tests exists and is closed.
3. `ddx work` and `ddx try` both print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully` on first Ctrl-C/SIGTERM.
4. Interrupted non-terminal beads are open/re-claimable after cleanup and are not parked behind `execute-loop-retry-after`.
5. Second-signal hard-abort behavior is documented and tested or explicitly documented as best-effort.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestSignal|TestInterrupt|TestCancel|TestShutdown|TestDisrupted|TestClaim" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators routinely stop long-running `ddx work` / `ddx try` with Ctrl-C. The governing specs now require the first SIGINT/SIGTERM to cancel work cooperatively, print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully`, preserve available evidence, release non-terminal claims, and leave unfinished beads re-claimable. The implementation beads need to land as one coherent interrupt story.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/main.go:31 calls cmd.Execute, and cli/cmd/root.go:31 calls rootCmd.Execute without a signal-backed context, so terminal SIGINT/SIGTERM kills the process instead of driving cooperative cancellation.
- cli/cmd/work.go:37-40 documents signal as a stop condition, but there is no root signal handler that turns Ctrl-C into that condition.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 can classify context cancellation as disrupted, but normal Ctrl-C does not currently reach this path.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:423-434 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:177-190 now define the intended first-signal behavior and user-facing message.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate two implementation slices: root CLI signal context/user messaging, and work/try attempt cleanup/reclaim tests. When both children close, verify `ddx work` and `ddx try` behave consistently under Ctrl-C.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement resumable worktrees by default. Interrupted work may be preserved as evidence, but the safe default is cleanup and retry from scratch.
- Do not change queue ordering, routing policy, review policy, or cost-cap policy.
- Do not remove legacy execute-loop/execute-bead compatibility commands.
notes:
RELATED QUEUE ALIGNMENT
- ddx-9228a484 (StopCondition enum) now depends on this epic so Signal semantics are implemented after first-signal cooperative cancellation is defined.
- ddx-9f20a4bd (external claim liveness) intersects with interrupted heartbeat/liveness cleanup; do not reintroduce beads.jsonl heartbeat writes while implementing the cleanup child.
- ddx-eccc6efb (server-spawn ddx work subprocess) should rely on the same root signal behavior once server workers exec ddx work; keep server-specific SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation as a supervisor layer, not a second queue-drain implementation.
title: work: graceful interrupt shutdown for work and try

acceptance:
1. ExecuteBeadLoopOptions has MaxRecoveryCostUSD float64 field (default 2.00) wired to --max-recovery-cost flag on the execute-loop command.
2. Circuit breaker: when cumulative reframe+decompose costUSD > MaxRecoveryCostUSD, bead parks to status=proposed; event "auto-recovery-failed" emitted with reason="circuit-breaker" and total_cost_usd field.
3. PersistentExecutionFailed two-stage: reframe attempted first; if reframe fails, decompose attempted; if decompose also fails, escape hatch fires.
4. Escape hatch: both reframe and decompose failed → status=proposed; event "auto-recovery-failed" with reason="both_failed".
5. Budget integration: per-bead budget exhaustion during recovery routes to per_bead_budget_exhausted (not status=proposed).
6. TestReframeFailure_FallsBackToDecompose passes: PersistentExecutionFailed, reframer stub returns failed=true; verifies decomposer is called next.
7. TestBothFail_ParkProposed passes: both reframer and decomposer stubs return failed=true; verifies bead parks to proposed with auto-recovery-failed event reason="both_failed".
8. TestRecoveryEvents_StructuredOutput passes: verifies reframe-applied and decompose-applied events include costUSD field.
9. TestRecoveryCircuitBreaker_AbortsAtCostCap passes: first attempt costs $1.50, second attempt costs $0.60; verifies circuit breaker fires before second attempt exceeds cap, event reason="circuit-breaker" with total_cost_usd.
10. TestPerBeadBudgetExhausted_TriggersAutoRecovery passes: per-bead budget hits 0 during recovery; verifies per_bead_budget_exhausted path taken, NOT status=proposed.
11. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  With reframe (ddx-ca3e0d37) and decompose (ddx-60bc5e9a) paths in place, two
  gaps remain:
  1. No circuit breaker: recovery hooks can run multiple times accumulating
     unbounded cost before either succeeds or falls through to proposed.
  2. No escape hatch wiring: when BOTH reframe and decompose fail (or are
     skipped), the bead must park to status=proposed with an auto-recovery-failed
     event. PersistentExecutionFailed class must try reframe first, then if
     reframe fails, try decompose; only then escape.
  3. Per-bead budget interaction: if the per-bead budget exhausts during a
     recovery attempt, it must fall through to per_bead_budget_exhausted (not
     status=proposed).

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/recovery_hook.go (ddx-e012af90) stubs the hook without
  a cost accumulator. No --max-recovery-cost flag or config key exists.
  The two-path sequencing for PersistentExecutionFailed is not wired.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Add circuit breaker to recovery_hook.go:
     - Track cumulative recovery costUSD across both reframe and decompose attempts.
     - If costUSD > maxRecoveryCost (default $2.00, configurable via
       ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.MaxRecoveryCostUSD), abort recovery and park to
       status=proposed with event type "auto-recovery-failed" and reason
       "circuit-breaker" plus total_cost_usd field.
  2. Wire PersistentExecutionFailed two-stage sequencing:
     - Stage 1: run reframer. If succeeds → done.
     - Stage 2: if reframer fails, run decomposer. If succeeds → done.
     - If both fail → escape hatch.
  3. Escape hatch (both fail OR circuit-breaker not triggered): park to
     status=proposed with event type "auto-recovery-failed" reason "both_failed".
  4. Budget integration: pass per-bead budget remaining into runReframer and
     runDecomposer; if budget exhaustion is detected during recovery, return
     DecomposeResult/ReframeResult{budgetExhausted:true}; hook routes to
     per_bead_budget_exhausted path instead of proposed.
  5. Add --max-recovery-cost flag to execute-loop command (and
     ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) defaulting to 2.00.
  6. Integration tests in cli/internal/agent/recovery_integration_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  Reframe implementation (ddx-ca3e0d37). Decompose implementation (ddx-60bc5e9a).
  Hook infrastructure (ddx-e012af90).
title: feat(escalation): add recovery circuit breaker, escape hatch, and integration tests

acceptance:
1. TestAxonBackend_CreateAndGet, TestAxonBackend_UpdateMutatesAndPersists, TestAxonBackend_ClaimAndUnclaim, TestAxonBackend_DepAddRemoveAndTree, TestAxonBackend_AppendEventSplitsIntoEventsCollection, and TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip pass against the real Axon-backed implementation.
2. backend_axon.go no longer uses local JSONL snapshots as the primary storage path.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.

description:
PROBLEM
  ddx try constructs ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with PreClaimHook only, so even after quality hooks exist in the agent package, direct ddx try invocations will not run lint or triage.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/cmd/try.go:320-329 constructs ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime for ddx try and currently sets Once, Log, EventSink, WorkerID, ProjectRoot, SessionID, PreClaimHook, and NoReview. There are no PreDispatchLintHook or PostAttemptTriageHook assignments at this wire site.

PROPOSED FIX
  - In cli/cmd/try.go, build and assign PreDispatchLintHook and PostAttemptTriageHook when constructing ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.
  - Reuse the agent-package hook constructors from the lint and triage child beads and pass the resolved config/project root/runner plumbing they require.
  - Default behavior remains warn-only/fail-open as implemented by the loop and hook layers.
  - Add --no-lint and --no-triage flags only if needed by the implementation; otherwise keep defaults with no extra flags.
  - Ensure operator output remains clear when lint blocks under opt-in block mode.

NON-SCOPE
  - Implementing hook contracts or hook bodies; depends on child beads.
  - ddx work integration.
  - BLOCK threshold policy tuning.
  - GraphQL exposure of OutcomeReason.

PARENT
  ddx-e1a576a7.

DEPS
  - ddx-d1dae2dd: pre-dispatch lint loop policy must exist first.
  - ddx-68706ef9: runner-backed lint hook must exist first.
  - ddx-4375292b: runner-backed post-attempt triage hook must exist first.
  - ddx-e1a576a7: parent decomposition edge requested by the parent attempt instructions.

description:
PROBLEM
No GraphQL resolver exists for Artifact.typeDefinitions. The ArtifactTypePanel (ddx-4fd71cf3) needs this resolver to show matched type definitions for an artifact. Without it, the frontend cannot retrieve template/prompt/example content for any artifact.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql (or equivalent): Artifact type does not have a typeDefinitions field.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go: no typeDefinitions resolver function exists for the Artifact type.
- The ddx library registers ArtifactTypeDefinitions under the library plugin structure, but no resolver maps from a library artifact path to its type definition prefix.
- File content for template/prompt/example must be loaded on-demand (not at graph build time) with a size cap to avoid loading large files into memory.
- ddx-1d52a30c (artifact type definition data model, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add typeDefinitions: [ArtifactTypeDefinition!]! field to the Artifact type in schema.graphql.
- Add resolver in graphql_resolver.go that:
  1. Takes artifact's prefix (via prefixOf helper).
  2. Looks up all matching ArtifactTypeDefinition entries for that prefix (multi-match supported).
  3. On-demand loads template/prompt/example file content with size cap (e.g., 64KB); truncates with a 'truncated' flag if exceeded.
- Return array (not single) — prefix collision returns all matches.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend rendering (ddx-4fd71cf3 handles that).
- Caching (may be added later with ddx-4a7eed8c cache infrastructure).

description:
PROBLEM
The web UI encodes old lifecycle vocabulary in filters, badges, and queue summaries. It includes a derived ready status as if it were a stored status and lacks proposed/cancelled as first-class lifecycle values.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/beads/+layout.svelte:77 defines STATUS_OPTIONS as open, ready, in-progress, closed, blocked.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/beads/+layout.svelte:269-286 styles status badges without proposed/cancelled lifecycle states.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md:664-669 still describes a needs_human operator-attention filter.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:287-313 feeds queue summary counts from old Ready/BlockedAll semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the frontend to present the six stored lifecycle statuses and derived queue buckets from the updated GraphQL/API contract. Stored status filters should include proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled. Execution-ready and dependency-waiting are derived views, not stored status options. The old needs review/needsHuman filter should become proposed/operator-attention and use status=proposed. External blocked should be visually distinct from dependency-waiting open work.

NON-SCOPE
Do not redesign the entire bead UI. Keep layout changes scoped to status filters, badges, counts, queue summaries, and tests required by the new lifecycle contract.

DEPS
Depends on the server GraphQL/API contract.

description:
PROBLEM
Raw Vale findings should not leak implementation details to users. DDx needs normalized rule IDs, rationales, suggested edits, and line-merge behavior that match the product contract.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:97-130 says Vale-specific names stay behind DDx output, DDx owns rationale/suggested edits, and multiple same-line word hits should merge when one sentence has one underlying problem.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Finding normalization is high-judgment work. It determines whether the checker is actionable and sparse, or noisy Vale output that agents will either ignore or over-apply.

PROPOSED FIX
- Map Vale check IDs to DDx rule IDs.
- Add DDx rationale and suggested edit metadata.
- Merge multiple same-line word hits when one sentence has one underlying issue.
- Preserve paths, commands, headings, tables, frontmatter, IDs, and technical density.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new rule families beyond the initial pack.
- Do not wire `ddx try`/`ddx work` hooks.
- Do not run full-doc cleanup.

DEPS
Depends on initial Vale rules and the Vale JSON adapter.

description:
CONTEXT
The prose checker needs fixture coverage before rule and CLI work so agents can distinguish useful findings from noisy false positives. This bead adds test inputs and expected outputs only; it should not implement checker logic unless a tiny schema helper is already required by existing test conventions.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/docprose/testdata/ or the fixture path selected by TD-036
- cli/internal/docprose/*_test.go only if needed to lock fixture loading/golden comparison conventions
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md only if the selected fixture path needs a minor correction

FIXTURE CASES
- Generic AI-like prose that should be flagged: unsupported robust/comprehensive/seamless claims, vague importance language, filler transitions, repeated openings.
- Technical DDx prose that should not be over-flagged: headings, lists, ADR/spec terminology, precise implementation references.
- Vocabulary behavior: accept DDx, bead, Helix, Fizeau-like project terms when configured; reject known filler when unsupported.
- Mode differences: technical, planning, and public prose.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- CLI command implementation.
- Default skill text.
- Review integration.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx needs a labeled evaluation corpus before prose rules can be trusted. Without positive and negative fixtures, rule changes can claim success while producing structural false positives or missing AI-slop constructions.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:132-191 defines corpus groups, metrics, and gates, but no corpus harness or golden normalized findings exist.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Corpus design is high-judgment work. It decides which prose findings count as useful, which technical density must stay quiet, and how DDx avoids optimizing for detector-like noise instead of actionable documentation quality.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add `cli/internal/docprose/testdata/corpus/` with positive, negative, and real DDx excerpt groups.
- Add a corpus loader and expected normalized-finding JSON schema.
- Add tests that compare normalized DDx findings, not raw Vale output.
- Include negative samples for paths, commands, tables, frontmatter, IDs, API names, and acceptance criteria.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not tune every Vale rule in this bead.
- Do not switch the public command to Vale.
- Do not run a full-doc cleanup.

DEPS
Depends on the style-pack skeleton so the corpus harness can target packaged DDx rules.

acceptance:
1. Add `TestBeadCreateMutationSpec_ParityAcrossCLIRESTMCP` or equivalent focused tests proving parent and custom fields survive all kept create paths.
2. Add `TestBeadUpdateMutationSpec_ParityAcrossCLIRESTMCP` or equivalent focused tests proving title, priority, labels, acceptance, assignee, parent, description, notes, set, and unset behavior is either supported or rejected explicitly.
3. Dedicated claim/unclaim behavior remains covered by existing endpoints or explicit tests documenting why it is not part of the generic update spec.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd ./internal/server/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Bead create/update has multiple mutation surfaces with different field sets. The Cobra commands support parent and custom `--set` fields on create, and title/status/priority/labels/acceptance/assignee/parent/description/notes/claim/unclaim/set/unset on update. REST and MCP expose smaller ad hoc request shapes, so fields can be silently unavailable or dropped depending on caller.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/bead.go:326-332` registers create flags including `parent` and `set`; REST create at `cli/internal/server/server.go:1638-1646` omits `set`, and MCP create at `cli/internal/server/server.go:4366-4383` omits both `parent` and `set`. `cli/cmd/bead.go:604-616` registers update flags including title, assignee, parent, set, unset, claim, and unclaim; REST update at `cli/internal/server/server.go:1686-1693` only accepts status/labels/description/acceptance/priority/notes, and MCP update at `cli/internal/server/server.go:4394-4414` only accepts status/labels/description/acceptance.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce canonical bead create/update mutation request structs and shared apply helpers used by Cobra, REST, and MCP. Unsupported control-plane operations such as claim/unclaim may remain dedicated endpoints, but they must be represented intentionally and documented rather than omitted by drift.

NON-SCOPE

description:
PROBLEM
A refactor this broad can regress silently if old lifecycle labels survive in tests, docs, prompts, command surfaces, startup paths, or live tracker rows. The final work needs a conformance pass that proves all implementation edges use the pure state machine, the queue has been one-way migrated, and old labels are migration-only.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `rg -n "needs_human|needs-human|needs investigation|needs-investigation|triage:needs-investigation|ambiguous_needs_human|SkippedNeedsInvestigation|BlockerKindNeedsHuman|BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation" cli docs .agents/skills/ddx .claude/skills/ddx` currently returns many active control-plane references across store, agent, GraphQL, CLI, docs, and skills.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server/graphql, cli/cmd, processmetrics, frontend files, and startup command assembly each own partial lifecycle conditionals today.
- The live `.ddx/beads.jsonl` contains legacy lifecycle labels, so final acceptance must include running the migration and committing the tracker result.

PROPOSED FIX
Run the final conformance sweep after all integration beads land. Replace remaining active lifecycle conditionals with calls to the pure lifecycle module or transition API. Apply the one-way lifecycle migration to the live tracker through `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply`, commit the tracker change, and verify startup no longer refuses the repository. Add grep/static guards where practical so needs_human/needs-investigation cannot re-enter as active queue labels.

NON-SCOPE
Do not introduce new lifecycle semantics beyond the approved spec. Do not manually edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; live migration must go through the implemented migration command.

DEPS
Depends on all spec, core, transition write, store, migration, startup gate, agent, routing, worker-output, metrics, CLI, API, frontend, and skill beads in this epic.

The original blocker was periodic claim liveness dirtying `.ddx/beads.jsonl` while `ddx work` was running. Current code appears to have the intended implementation, but the bead prompt is stale and still points at the old tracker-writing heartbeat path. Leaving the stale prompt open risks duplicate or regressive implementation work.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:884-897 now calls `w.Store.TouchClaimHeartbeat(beadID)` from the worker heartbeat goroutine instead of the old tracker-writing `Store.Heartbeat` path.
- cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:13-25 stores claim liveness under `$TMPDIR/ddx-claim-heartbeats/<project-hash>/<bead>.json`, outside `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:64-101 writes, removes, and reads the external lease file.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:660-685 keeps `Store.Heartbeat` only as a deprecated compatibility wrapper that validates status and then calls `TouchClaimHeartbeat`; it no longer writes heartbeat fields to the tracker.
- cli/internal/bead/store_test.go:775-791 already contains `TestStoreHeartbeat_RemovedOrNoTrackerWrite`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_liveness_test.go:64-96 already contains `TestWorkLoop_ClaimLivenessDoesNotMutateBeadsJSONL` and `TestWorkLoop_LivenessRecordedOutsideTracker`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Treat this bead as verification and closeout, not greenfield implementation.
- Confirm the work loop has no production periodic liveness call that writes `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- Run the focused liveness/heartbeat tests and then the package gate used by worker beads.
- If a focused test fails, fix only the regression needed to keep liveness out of the tracker; do not introduce a second liveness store.
- If the focused tests and package gate pass, close the bead with the verifying commit/result.

NON-SCOPE

- Do not move durable semantic transitions such as claim, unclaim, close, cooldown, or evidence out of `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- Do not implement Axon or another tracker backend in this bead.

3. Replay runs in one-shot mode: emits a "replay" event with the source attempt-id, does NOT count toward the bead's drain-attempt history.
4. A new evidence bundle is created at .ddx/executions/<new-run-id>/ with manifest noting replay_of=<original>.
5. A metrics row is appended to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl with replay_of=<original-attempt-id> for grouping.
6. Replay prints a side-by-side comparison of original vs replay metrics.
7. `ddx bead replay-bench <bead-id> --variants <list>` runs multiple replays in parallel and outputs an outcome matrix.
8. TestReplay_LoadsOriginalManifest, TestReplay_OverridesAppliedToDispatch, TestReplay_DoesNotCountTowardBeadAttemptHistory, TestReplay_AppendsMetricsRowWithReplayOf, TestReplay_PrintsComparison, TestReplayBench_MultipleVariantsInParallel all pass.

description:
PROBLEM
  The metrics file (.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, ddx-054b2449)
  and analyze CLI (ddx-09ceb430) reveal which models / prompts
  / harnesses succeed at what rate. But they cannot answer
  "would this specific attempt have succeeded with a different
  configuration?" That question matters for prompt iteration
  and model comparison — A/B testing on real beads, not
  synthetic fixtures.

  Currently the only way to re-run a bead is to reopen it and
  let the drain claim it again with whatever default config
  is in effect. There's no controlled replay against alternate
  config.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/cmd/agent_run.go runs a fresh execution with the
    configured harness/model.
  - .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ contains the prompt.md and
    manifest.json that defined the original execution.
  - There is no `ddx bead replay` or equivalent.
  - For prompt-template iteration, operators currently must
    manually copy prompts and run them ad-hoc — no integration
    with bead state, no metrics row, no comparison view.

PROPOSED FIX
  Implement `ddx bead replay <attempt-id> [flags]`:

  1. Load the original attempt's manifest.json and prompt.md
     from .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ (or the mirror, per
     ddx-b2c6ae22).

  2. Construct a new dispatch request with overrides from
     flags:
       --harness <name>          override the original harness
       --model <name>            override the original model
       --profile <name>          override the original profile
       --min-power <n>           override the routing floor
       --prompt-from <path>      override the prompt content
                                 (substitute prompt-template
                                 iteration for the original)
       --rev <ref>               override the base revision

  3. Run the dispatch in a one-shot mode (does NOT count toward
     the bead's drain-attempt history; emits a "replay" event
     with the original attempt_id and the new run-id).

  4. Capture a fresh evidence bundle under
     .ddx/executions/<new-run-id>/ tagged with
     manifest.json containing both the new and the
     original-source attempt-id for comparison.

  5. Append a row to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl with a new
     attempt_id, marked replay_of=<original-attempt-id> so the
     analyze CLI can group replays.

  6. Print a side-by-side comparison:
     - original: outcome, cost, duration, tool_call_count
     - replay:   outcome, cost, duration, tool_call_count
     - delta:    relative metrics

  CLI sub-mode: `ddx bead replay-bench <bead-id>
  --variants <var1>,<var2>,...` runs multiple replays in
  parallel against the same source attempt with different
  configurations. Output is a matrix of outcomes for A/B
  analysis.

  Tests:
    - TestReplay_LoadsOriginalManifest
    - TestReplay_OverridesAppliedToDispatch
    - TestReplay_DoesNotCountTowardBeadAttemptHistory
    - TestReplay_AppendsMetricsRowWithReplayOf
    - TestReplay_PrintsComparison
    - TestReplayBench_MultipleVariantsInParallel

NON-SCOPE
  - UI / dashboard for replay comparison.
  - Auto-selecting replay configurations based on metrics
    patterns (would be valuable; out of scope here).
  - Cross-machine replay (requires the warm mirror to be
    configured).

INTERSECTIONS
  - DEPENDS ON: ddx-054b2449 (metrics extraction) — replay rows
    extend the same schema.
  - Depends loosely on ddx-b2c6ae22 (mirror) — for replaying
    attempts whose local evidence has been pruned.
  - Sister bead ddx-09ceb430 (analyze CLI) — gains awareness
    of replay rows via replay_of grouping.

PROPOSED FIX

4. TestInvestigationRetry_RequestsSmartRoute verifies an autonomous investigation retry raises route constraints on the next attempt.
5. TestInvestigationRetry_NoSmartRouteDoesNotAddLegacyLabels verifies unavailable smart routing does not recreate needs_human/needs-investigation labels.
6. Existing routing lint tests still prove execution paths do not call ResolveRoute directly.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestInvestigationRetry|TestRoutinglint|TestZeroConfigEscalation' -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The new lifecycle semantics say ambiguous investigation should keep moving when a smart agent can make progress, but no bead currently enforces that reruns of investigation-class work are routed with sufficient power. Without this, open investigation work can churn through the same weak route and look blocked again.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:203-215 currently maps needs-investigation evidence to a needs-human action instead of a smart rerun policy.
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:186-228 passes profile/min_power from runtime config into Fizeau Execute, but the lifecycle plan does not yet define when investigation retries should raise those bounds.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation_test.go covers tier escalation mechanics, but the lifecycle refactor has no test tying investigation outcomes to a smart/frontier rerun.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a lifecycle retry policy for investigation-class outcomes that remain status=open. When the state machine/migrator/agent outcome says autonomous investigation is still possible, the next ddx work attempt must request a smart-capable route through existing power/profile controls without hard-pinning a model. If no viable smart route exists, treat that as system/routing unready or status=proposed only when operator action is truly required by the spec.

NON-SCOPE
Do not reintroduce DDx-owned provider/model selection. Fizeau still owns concrete routing; DDx only passes abstract power/profile constraints for the next attempt.

DEPS
Depends on no-changes, triage, and agent parking changes.
title: work: route autonomous investigation retries through smart power
