description:
PROBLEM
In the coordinator→client mutation path (Story 14/15), the originating peer identity must be forwarded from coordinator to spoke unchanged, and the spoke must authorize on both the origin AND the coordinator identity. Today cli/internal/federation/fanout.go supports only query fan-out with no identity forwarding; the mutation envelope from S15-7b carries headers but no production caller enforces dual authorization on the spoke side.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: query fan-out exists but no requireTrustedForwarded middleware or augmentation of requireTrusted handles forwarded mutations.
- The spoke's requireTrusted (cli/internal/server/server.go) checks only the immediate transport peer — it does not parse X-DDx-Origin-Identity or X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers.
- No dual-authorization path exists: a forwarded mutation that passes coordinator trust but carries an untrusted origin is not rejected.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add requireTrustedForwarded middleware (or augment requireTrusted) in cli/internal/server/server.go:
  - Parse X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity from incoming request.
  - Validate origin identity against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy.
  - Validate coordinator identity against registered federation peers.
  - Return 403 if either check fails or either header is missing.
- Coordinator forwarding path (built in S15-7b): when forwarding a prompt-driven mutation, set X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified incoming peer identity (from WhoIs at coordinator edge); set X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Identity MUST NOT be rewritten in transit.

NON-SCOPE
- Identity signing/attestation structure (that's ddx-e2c217d1).
- Mutation routing envelope (that's S15-7b / ddx-eb75a32d, a dep).
- Non-mutation endpoints.
title: S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization (origin + coordinator)

acceptance:
1. `TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema` or equivalent asserts the generated prompt contains the same field names and shape as the bead-lifecycle readiness schema, including `tractability`, `score`, `rationale`, and `readiness_checks` as an array.
2. `TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_ForbidsScalarReadinessChecks` or equivalent asserts the generated prompt explicitly says `readiness_checks` MUST be a JSON array and must not be an object/string.
3. `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md`, `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` remain synchronized for the readiness/intake JSON contract.
4. `ddx skills check .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle` passes, or the repo-equivalent skill validation command passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_.*|TestPreClaimReadiness_.*|TestReadiness.*" -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The pre-claim readiness/intake hook is producing malformed `readiness_checks` shapes during live `ddx work` runs, including object-shaped and string-shaped values. Decoder hardening is tracked separately, but there is also a prompt/skill contract mismatch that encourages model drift: the bead-lifecycle skill documents a readiness schema with `readiness_checks` as an array, while the runtime prompt asks for a partially different field set.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:30-31`, `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:30-31`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md:30-31` say `MODE: intake` is compatibility readiness mode unless the caller gives a narrower schema.
- The same skill copies at lines 71-125 document the readiness JSON schema with `tractability`, `score`, `rationale`, `readiness_checks` as an array of objects, `suggested_fixes`, `rewrite`, `suggested_child_beads`, and `waivers_applied`.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:242-248` builds the live pre-claim prompt with `MODE: intake` but asks for `classification, confidence, reasoning, readiness_checks, suggested_fixes, suggested_child_beads, and waivers_applied`; it omits `tractability`, `score`, and `rationale`, uses legacy `confidence/reasoning`, and also says not to include rewrite fields.
- This split contract leaves the model deciding which schema to follow, so malformed `readiness_checks` values can appear even when the skill itself shows an array example.

PROPOSED FIX
Align the live pre-claim prompt and all shipped bead-lifecycle skill copies around one explicit intake/readiness contract. Either make the runtime prompt quote the exact readiness-mode JSON shape DDx decodes, or make the skill define a deliberately narrower intake schema that exactly matches the runtime prompt. In either case, explicitly state that `readiness_checks` MUST be a JSON array, may be empty, and every entry MUST be an object with `reason`, `verdict`, `evidence`, and `checkable_before_attempt`. Keep the operator-required threshold strict: prompt-quality nits go in `suggested_fixes`; only true blockers become `operator_required`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not replace decoder hardening in ddx-c81c3cad; DDx must still tolerate malformed model output. Do not change readiness classifications or queue state-machine semantics. Do not make readiness failures block work by default.
title: readiness: align intake prompt with bead-lifecycle schema

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 12 query methods (`List`, `ListWithArchive`, `Ready`, `ReadyExecution`, `ReadyExecutionBreakdown`, `ProposedOperatorAttention`, `NeedsHuman`, `Blocked`, `ExternalBlocked`, `DependencyWaiting`, `BlockedAll`, `Status`) that compute derived views over the bead corpus. Per TD-027 §6.1 these compose into `BeadQueries` interface; per AC #1 every interface method takes `ctx context.Context`. Several callers (`cli/cmd/work_focus.go`, `cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go`, `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go`) are clean ISP narrowing candidates — they use queries only.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` (and related files for derivation logic) defines these 12 methods without ctx. The Foundation bead declared `BeadQueries` interface (ctx-aware) but the `*Store` methods don't yet match.

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update all 12 query methods on `*Store` to take `ctx context.Context` as first parameter.
2. Add compile-time assertion: `var _ BeadQueries = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through every caller of these methods. Many callers can also narrow their `*bead.Store` field type to `bead.BeadQueries` (ISP win) — opportunistic narrowing in this bead; full narrowing pass in BL.
4. No method removal — these methods stay on `*Store`; they ARE the storage primitives for derived views (no Operation pattern needed; pure reads).

# NON-SCOPE
- Queue derivation logic (TD-027 §2.1) — not modified.
- Pagination / cursor support for List — deferred (TD-027 §25 open question).
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. All 12 query methods on `*Store` take `ctx context.Context` as first parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ BeadQueries = (*Store)(nil)` compiles.
2. Caller files updated to pass ctx. Test: `TestBeadQueries_*_AcceptsContext` (parameterized over each method).
3. At least 3 caller files narrowed from `*bead.Store` to `bead.BeadQueries` field type. Concrete:
   - `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` (uses `BlockedAll`, `List`, `ProposedOperatorAttention`, `ReadyExecution` — pure queries).
   - `cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go` (uses `ReadyExecution`).
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go` (uses `DependencyWaiting`, `ExternalBlocked`, `Ready`, `Status` — pure read-side resolver).
   - `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` (uses `ReadAll` — strong reader narrowing candidate; BeadReader, not Queries, but related).
4. Existing query tests in `cli/internal/bead/` continue to pass.
5. Ctx cancellation propagates: if ctx is cancelled mid-query, the method returns `ctx.Err()`. Test: `TestBeadQueries_RespectsContextCancellation`.
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:queries, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (Foundation — BeadQueries interface declared).
title: Q: BeadQueries — add ctx to 12 query methods on *Store; opportunistic caller narrowing (TD-027 §6.1)

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 lifecycle-transition methods (`TransitionLifecycle`, `SetLifecycleStatus`, `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `CloseWithEvidence`, `AppendNotes`, `Reopen`, `ParkToProposed`) that encode HELIX-flavored state-machine policy directly in the storage layer. Callers in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, and `cli/internal/server/graphql/` hold `*Store` concretely and invoke these methods. Per TD-027's storage-vs-workflow separation, the state-machine validation belongs in a workflow helper package, with the storage primitive being `Apply(ctx, id, op Operation)` plus typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes lifecycle methods that mutate status with HELIX-specific transition validation embedded. `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go`, and `cli/cmd/bead.go` call these methods directly. The new `Operation` pattern (added in Foundation bead `ddx-c6317784`) provides the primitive but the workflow helpers don't exist yet.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §14 helper package `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/`:

1. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`: `SetStatus{Status}`, `SetLifecycleStatus{Status, Options}`, `SetCloseEvidence{SessionID, CommitSHA}`, `ReopenOp{Reason, Notes}`, `ParkToProposedOp{Reason}`, `AppendNotes{Notes}`.
2. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/transition.go` with helpers:
   - `Transition(ctx, l BeadLifecycle, r BeadReader, id, newStatus string) error` — validates against TD-027 §2 transition matrix; calls `l.Apply(ctx, id, SetLifecycleStatus{Status: newStatus})`.
   - `CloseWithEvidence(ctx, l, id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error`.
   - `Reopen(ctx, l, id, reason, notes string) error`.
   - `ParkToProposed(ctx, l, id, reason string) error`.
   - `AppendNotes(ctx, l, id, notes string) error`.
   - `SetStatus(ctx, l, id, status string) error` — for callers that don't need state-machine validation.
3. Migrate callers:
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go` — `store.Reopen(...)`, `store.CloseWithEvidence(...)`, `store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(...)` → `lifecycle.Reopen(ctx, store, ...)` etc.
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go` — `store.TransitionLifecycle`, `store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `store.Reopen` → helper calls.
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — lifecycle transition usages → helpers.
   - All test files that use these methods (where they assert workflow behavior) → helpers; tests that assert storage primitive behavior stay using `Apply` directly.
4. Remove `*Store.TransitionLifecycle`, `SetLifecycleStatus`, `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `CloseWithEvidence`, `AppendNotes`, `Reopen`, `ParkToProposed` (7 methods).

# NON-SCOPE
- Other concerns (claim, cancel, cooldown, queries, dependency, archive) — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — bead `ddx-74452926`.
- Changes to the state machine itself (statuses, transitions) — defined in TD-027 §1–§2; not modified here.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares the 6 lifecycle Operation types listed in PROPOSED FIX. Each implements `Apply(b *Bead) error`. Test: `TestOperation_LifecycleOps_AllImplementApply` (reflection-enumerated).
2. `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/` package exists with the helper functions. Each helper has at least one test against `*Store`: `TestOpsLifecycle_Transition_HappyPath`, `TestOpsLifecycle_Transition_RejectsInvalidTransition`, `TestOpsLifecycle_CloseWithEvidence_RecordsProvenance`, `TestOpsLifecycle_Reopen_FromClosedFails` (closed is terminal), `TestOpsLifecycle_ParkToProposed_AppendsTriageEvent`.
3. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 lifecycle methods. Compile-time check: `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (TransitionLifecycle|SetLifecycleStatus|UpdateWithLifecycleStatus|CloseWithEvidence|AppendNotes|Reopen|ParkToProposed)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returns nothing.
4. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store`. Verified by `grep -rln 'store\.\(TransitionLifecycle\|SetLifecycleStatus\|UpdateWithLifecycleStatus\|CloseWithEvidence\|AppendNotes\|Reopen\|ParkToProposed\)' cli/` outside the bead package and test fixtures, which returns zero matches.
5. Existing test behavior preserved: `cli/internal/bead/store_test.go`, `chaos_test.go`, `backend_conformance_test.go` continue to pass (they test storage primitives, not the workflow helpers).
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
No chaos or integration test suite validates federation behavior under failure conditions. Unit tests cover individual components but no test exercises multi-node failure scenarios (spoke crash, hub crash, network partition, version skew, duplicate node_id). Shipping federation without chaos coverage risks silent data loss or incorrect routing under common failure modes.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/: unit tests cover fanout and registry separately but no test spins up multiple in-process httptest.Server instances to simulate a real federation topology.
- The test patterns from ddx-13e19750 / ddx-e59bff83 (closed tests) established the two-node httptest setup; this bead extends to chaos scenarios.
- ddx-0793ac75 (federated GraphQL resolvers, a dep) must land first as the chaos tests exercise the full resolver + routing stack.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/federation/chaos_test.go (or federation_integration_test.go):
  - Test fixture: spin up N in-process httptest.Server instances (hub + N spokes).
  - Chaos scenarios (each as a t.Run):
    1. Spoke crash: spoke goes offline mid-operation; hub fan-out returns partial results.
    2. Hub crash: spoke loses hub connection; spoke enters degraded mode.
    3. Network partition: simulated by closing httptest.Server; requests timeout gracefully.
    4. Version skew: spoke reports different SpecVersion; hub logs warning and continues.
    5. Duplicate node_id: second spoke with same node_id is rejected at registration.

NON-SCOPE
- Load testing or performance benchmarks.
- External network; httptest only.
title: federation: backend chaos/integration suite

acceptance:

2. The test uses deterministic latency (channels or synctest), not wall-clock sleeps.
3. The test uses a per-test sandbox via testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir().
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerLock_' green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  After the sister implementation bead removes network I/O from Land(),
  Land() is local-only: worktree-merge + update-ref + evidence commit
  under a brief local lock, with no fetch or push. A worker that crashes
  mid-Land (between UpdateRefTo and the next bead's claim) can leave the
  tracker lock held by a dead process. The next worker that starts must
  break the stale lock and recover cleanly. There is no test for this
  local-only recovery path today.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:196-218 breakStaleTrackerLock removes
    the lock if the owner PID is dead.
  - No test exercises the scenario where the dead owner had completed
    UpdateRefTo (advancing local main) but did not have a chance to
    perform any follow-up tracker writes before exiting.
  - With network I/O removed from Land(), the only stale-lock-recovery
    scenario worth covering is the local-only one: worker died after
    advancing local ref; next worker breaks lock, sees local-ahead state,
    proceeds.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add one chaos test in cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go (or a new
  file cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go):

  TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand:
    - Simulate a worker that acquired withMainGitLock, called
      UpdateRefTo successfully (local main advanced), then was killed
      without releasing the lock.
    - Next worker arrives, calls breakStaleTrackerLock at
      tracker_lock.go:196-218, observes the lock is held by a dead PID,
      breaks it.
    - Next worker proceeds with its own Land() and asserts it sees the
      local-ahead state (does not re-merge the previous worker's work,
      does not roll back).

  Use a per-test sandbox (testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir()) and
  channel-based barriers / synctest for deterministic timing — no
  wall-clock sleeps in CI.

NON-SCOPE
  - The sister implementation bead handles the network-I/O removal.
  - Push-race or fetch-race scenarios are obsolete after the sister
    implementation bead lands; they no longer exist in single-node mode.
  - Multi-machine coordination chaos coverage is a future federation
    concern.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --rate-limit-max-wait` is exposed as a CLI flag, but the ExecuteLoopSpec parser never reads it after the execute-loop spec unification. Operators can pass a retry budget and get the default behavior instead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/work.go:86` registers `--rate-limit-max-wait`, but `parseExecuteLoopSpec` in `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1394-1452` reads and persists the neighboring execute-loop flags without reading that duration. `singleTierAttempt` in `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1653-1675` constructs `agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime` without setting `RateLimitMaxWait`, even though the runtime field exists at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:174-189`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a canonical rate-limit retry budget field to `executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec`, parse the Cobra flag into it, persist/round-trip it across REST and GraphQL worker specs, and pass it into `agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime.RateLimitMaxWait` for each attempt. Preserve the documented semantics: zero uses the default budget; negative disables retry.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau model catalog, routing candidate selection, or provider release/module files.

PARENT
ddx-e34994e2

DEPS
ddx-fb290074 audit report.
title: work: wire rate-limit retry budget through ExecuteLoopSpec

2. TestReady_StaleClaimedInProgressIsReady in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go asserts a stale-claimed in_progress bead with satisfied deps appears in Ready() output.
3. No other test in cli/internal/bead/... regresses (existing Ready/Blocked/queue tests stay green).

description:
PROBLEM
  Two places answer "is this bead ready?" and they disagree. The lifecycle
  module (lifecycle.go) classifies a bead's queue bucket from facts; the
  store.go Ready() helper then re-filters by raw status, producing a different
  answer than the lifecycle classifier when an in_progress bead has a stale
  claim.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:155-208 EvaluateLifecycleQueue routes
    StatusInProgress at line 190-194 through evaluateOpenLifecycleQueue when
    !ClaimFresh, which can produce LifecycleBucketReady at line 232.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1754-1766 readyFiltered then iterates the
    classifier output and applies a second filter at line 1763:
        if entry.Status == StatusOpen { ready = append(...) }
    This filter is gated by !executionOnly, so external readers
    (ddx bead ready non-executionOnly path) see a different result than
    queue-execution callers (executionOnly path).
  - Net effect: a stale-claimed in_progress bead with deps satisfied is
    "ready" per EvaluateLifecycleQueue, "not ready" per store.Ready() in
    operator-facing mode, and "ready" per store.Ready() in execution mode.
    Three answers; one question.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Decide the canonical answer: per TD-031 §6, claim metadata is not status,
    and a stale-claimed in_progress bead is recoverable through the heartbeat
    TTL mechanism. The lifecycle module's classifier already encodes this.
    Therefore the second filter at store.go:1763 is redundant and incorrect.
  - Edit cli/internal/bead/store.go:1754-1766: remove the
    "if entry.Status == StatusOpen" branch; both executionOnly and
    operator-facing paths consume LifecycleBucketReady directly.
  - Add regression test TestReady_StaleClaimedInProgressIsReady in
    cli/internal/bead/store_test.go (or extend an existing nearby test) that:
      a. Creates a bead, claims it (open -> in_progress).
      b. Manually expires the heartbeat (set claimed-at + heartbeat TTL).
      c. Asserts Ready() returns the bead in operator-facing mode (not just
         the executionOnly path).
  - Verify no other call sites of readyFiltered or Ready depend on the
    removed filter; grep for s.Ready, store.Ready, .Ready( across cli/.

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing EvaluateLifecycleQueue or the lifecycle bucket enumeration.
  - Adding new claim-recovery semantics.
  - Migrating callers between executionOnly and non-executionOnly paths.

INTERSECTIONS
  - ddx-ca1c66ee (epic parent).
  - No deps on B1/B2; can run in parallel with the TD-031 amendment.
title: bead/store: unify Ready definition - drop redundant Status==Open filter

4. Existing TestExecuteBeadWorkerEpicIsNotOrdinaryWork remains green for epics with open children.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` currently reports dependency-ready epics only as structural skips. That is correct for epics with open child work, but stale for epics whose direct children are all closed. Operators then have to manually notice and close completed epics, and the no-ready-work diagnostic keeps showing noise.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1416 appends every ordinary epic container to ReadyExecutionBreakdown.SkippedEpics.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1482 filters ordinary epic containers out of ReadyExecution entirely.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1696 defines isOrdinaryEpicContainer as true for any issue_type `epic`, without considering whether the epic has open children or only closed children.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1940-1942 renders all skipped epics with the same "epics are structural containers; decompose into tasks" message, so completed epics are not surfaced as closure candidates.

PROPOSED FIX
Teach the execution queue to distinguish epics with open children from epics whose direct children are all closed. Resolve the prior product fork conservatively: `ddx work` must not auto-close epics. It should surface completed epics as explicit closure candidates in human and JSON output so an operator can verify and close them intentionally with `ddx bead close`.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and/or cli/cmd/work_plan_test.go for operator-facing output

NON-SCOPE
- Do not execute epics through the normal bead executor. Epics remain structural containers.
- Do not auto-close epics from `ddx work`; closure remains an explicit operator action.
- Do not close epics that still have any direct child whose status is not closed.
- Do not implement recursive grandchild graph closure beyond the existing direct-child/dependency model unless a failing test proves it is required.
- Do not change task queue priority ordering for ordinary executable tasks.

description:
PROBLEM
The May 7 execute-bead incidents left the operator checkout with staged deletions of tracked execution evidence and mixed tracker/code state. More worker retries before evidence is preserved can erase the forensic trail for ddx-c2a66f45, ddx-377ebf92, and ddx-b1cf1f6b or fold unrelated dirty files into later landing/evidence commits.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- .gitignore:437-444 explicitly un-ignores .ddx/executions/*/{prompt.md,manifest.json,result.json,checks.json,usage.json}, so those files are durable execution evidence and must not be deleted as cleanup.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:619-633 stages and commits execution evidence during land, but the current operator index already contained unrelated staged deletions for .ddx/executions/20260507T183240-4c35f5a3/manifest.json and prompt.md.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:491-500 commits whatever is staged, so a dirty index can contaminate a later evidence commit.

PROPOSED FIX
- Restore/unstage the staged evidence deletions for .ddx/executions/20260507T183240-4c35f5a3/manifest.json and prompt.md.
- Preserve known incident refs in the operator notes or tracker evidence: ddx-377ebf92 result_rev be4fdf4dc1228e1bc25506952c35f41c9fb4dadc and ddx-c2a66f45 preserved iteration refs if present.
- Commit tracker/evidence-only cleanup separately from implementation changes so follow-up workers start from a clean index.
- Verify tracked execution evidence remains discoverable with git ls-files.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement landing, cleanup, run-state, or checkpoint code changes in this bead.
- Do not retry ddx work or ddx try.
- Do not edit .ddx/beads.jsonl by hand; use ddx bead commands only.

DEPS
No deps. This is the containment gate for the rest of the incident remediation plan.
title: containment: preserve incident evidence before more workers

description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup manager can delete a registered execute-bead worktree that is still active when singleton run-state no longer points at it. This matches the May 7 vanished-worktree failures where the agent ran for many minutes and spent model budget, but post-execution git rev-parse failed because /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-... no longer existed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:109-132 defaultExecutionCleanupLivenessProbe only preserves metadata matched by one runState or a local metadata liveness marker.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:253-269 preserves live candidates only after that one-probe result says live.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:271-313 removes registered worktrees when they are registered and not considered live.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:776-783 writes cleanup metadata without an active refreshed liveness marker, and :787-789 only defers removal for the owning process.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make cleanup enumerate all per-attempt live records from the dependency bead and pass the full live set to liveness decisions.
- Preserve registered worktrees when any live attempt record, live pid/session, or fresh heartbeat matches attempt_id or worktree_path.
- Reclaim registered worktrees only when the attempt is terminal/stale and heartbeat expiry/age policy says it is safe.
- Record cleanup observations that distinguish preserved_live_attempt from removed_stale_registered_worktree.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not broaden scratch-prefix cleanup; existing ddx-2df8e946 owns stale DDx scratch roots.
- Do not alter landing/evidence behavior.
- Do not delete published .ddx/executions or .ddx/runs evidence.

DEPS
Depends on per-attempt run-state/liveness and on ddx-2df8e946 for the broader DDx-owned scratch cleanup scope.
title: cleanup: preserve concurrent live execute-bead worktrees

description:
PROBLEM
Execute-bead pre-dispatch checkpointing still mutates the parent checkout before the isolated worktree exists. On May 7 this surfaced as failures before execution: one checkpoint ran lefthook go-test with go test -race while cgo was unavailable, and another tripped the large-files hook on a DDx-generated .ddx/backups/*.jsonl artifact. These are parent-checkout hook/index failures, not bead implementation failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:740-768 holds the tracker lock across commitTrackerLocked, gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(projectRoot, ...), resolveBase, and WorktreeAdd.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:749-755 intentionally checkpoints caller dirt as a real commit before creating the isolated worktree.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:420-489 RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes through the normal checkout/index path, which invokes normal git commit hooks.
- .gitignore:424 ignores .ddx/backups/, but staged or force-added DDx backup artifacts can still be present in the parent index and trip hooks.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace parent-checkout hook-running checkpoint commits with an internal temp-index/commit-tree checkpoint path or an equivalent hook-free internal commit path.
- Preserve the intended checkpoint semantics: caller dirt that should be part of the worker base remains reachable as a normal commit/ref without running project hooks.
- Explicitly exclude DDx runtime, evidence, backup, run-state, lock, and cleanup metadata paths from pre-dispatch checkpoint staging.
- Keep commitTrackerLocked behavior for legitimate tracker mutations.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not disable hooks for normal human or implementation commits.
- Do not change worker worktree commit behavior after the agent runs.
- Do not change cleanup/liveness behavior.

DEPS
Depends on incident containment so existing staged evidence deletions and dirty evidence artifacts are resolved before checkpoint behavior is changed.
title: agent: isolate pre-dispatch checkpoint from hooks

description:
PROBLEM
Concurrent execute-bead attempts publish liveness through a singleton .ddx/run-state.json file. One active attempt can overwrite another attempt's run-state, and cleanup only sees the last writer as live. Worktree isolation cannot protect active worktrees if live-state tracking is global and single-valued.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:12-14 defines one RunStateFileName, run-state.json.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:37-73 WriteRunState atomically overwrites that single file for every attempt.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:93-100 ClearRunState removes that single file on any completing attempt.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:791-805 writes and clears the singleton state around each attempt.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:116-123 preserves candidates only when their metadata matches the one runState passed to the liveness probe.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add per-attempt run-state records, for example .ddx/run-state/<attempt-id>.json, as the source of truth for live attempts.
- Include bead_id, attempt_id, harness/model, started_at, worktree_path, pid, session_id when available, refreshed_at, and expires_at.
- Refresh attempt liveness while the agent runs and clear only that attempt's record on completion.
- Preserve .ddx/run-state.json as a compatibility aggregate or summary view until existing operator/HELIX consumers migrate.
- Update readers/tests so cleanup and status code can enumerate all live attempts.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement cleanup reclamation policy in this bead beyond making all live attempts visible.
- Do not change queue claim semantics.
- Do not migrate historical execution records.

DEPS
Depends on pre-dispatch checkpoint isolation so new liveness files are not accidentally committed by the parent checkpoint path.
title: agent: replace singleton run-state with per-attempt liveness

description:

Successful execute-bead work can still collide with the operator checkout during land. The May 7 ddx-377ebf92 attempt completed successfully at result_rev be4fdf4dc1228e1bc25506952c35f41c9fb4dadc, but landing/push recovery tried to merge through a dirty projectRoot and failed because local tracked and untracked files would be overwritten. - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1262-1269 BuildLandRequestFromResult always sets WorktreeDir to projectRoot because the worker worktree has already been removed.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:619-633 landEvidence stages and commits the evidence directory in the same checkout used for landing.

description:
PROBLEM
Unit tests can pass while the full DDx worker lifecycle still collides across cleanup, concurrent workers, landing, evidence, and dirty operator checkout state. The May 7 incident crossed all of those boundaries: one active worktree disappeared before result capture, another successful result could not land because projectRoot was dirty, and parent pre-dispatch checkpointing failed before isolation.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:728-805 creates attempt worktrees, cleanup metadata, and run-state.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:253-313 decides whether to preserve or remove registered worktrees.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:619-633, :700-725, :1093-1133 own evidence commit, land, push, and push recovery.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:451-556 owns cleanup/resource passes before claims, but existing e2e coverage under ddx-d3aca2b7 does not exercise two simultaneous active workers plus dirty landing.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an end-to-end regression that starts two fake/sleeping execute-bead attempts with distinct worktrees and per-attempt liveness, runs cleanup mid-flight, and verifies both worktrees survive.
- Add an end-to-end dirty-checkout landing scenario where a successful result lands/preserves evidence without overwriting or committing operator dirt.
- Add a worktree_lost scenario proving the bead remains retryable and evidence/diagnostics survive.
- Use temp dirs and fakes/injectable hooks; do not fill real disks or call live model providers.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not consume real disk/inodes to simulate ENOSPC.
- Do not run live external harness/model calls.
- Do not test unrelated candidate-cycle review behavior.

DEPS
Depends on landing sync guard, cleanup live-worktree preservation, and worktree_lost classification.

description:
PROBLEM
After a successful land, DDx attempts to refresh the operator checkout so the files match the new target branch tip. That sync currently mutates the main index and force-checks out changed paths. When the operator checkout has local edits in those same paths, syncing can overwrite or destabilize those edits even though the agent work happened in an isolated worktree.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:338-405 SyncWorkTreeToHead runs git read-tree HEAD and checkout-index -f for files changed between fromRev and HEAD.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:710, :775, :811, :1129, and :1193 call SyncWorkTreeToHead as best-effort after ref updates, merge, rollback, push auto-recovery, or preserved iteration recovery.
- The May 7 ddx-377ebf92 push-conflict event showed dirty projectRoot paths overlapped the landed branch paths, including .ddx/beads.jsonl, AGENTS.md, execute_bead_loop.go, and FEAT docs.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an overlap check before syncing the operator checkout: compare files changed by the landed/recovered commit range with dirty tracked and untracked projectRoot paths.
- If there is no dirty overlap, keep the existing sync behavior.
- If there is dirty overlap, skip checkout sync, leave refs/result/evidence intact, and record an observable checkout_sync_deferred detail/event rather than failing the bead.
- Ensure push auto-recovery and preserved-iteration recovery also use the guard.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change how clean temp landing worktrees merge/push; the dependency bead owns that.
- Do not auto-stash or discard operator changes.
- Do not change retry cooldown policy for real merge conflicts.

DEPS
Depends on clean landing/evidence isolation so sync is the only remaining projectRoot mutation after successful land.
title: land: defer operator checkout sync on dirty overlap

description:
PROBLEM
When an attempt worktree vanishes before DDx can read HEAD, the result is reported as generic execution_failed/transport with ResultRev equal to BaseRev. That hides the root cause and makes the work look like an agent/task failure even when the agent ran to completion and infrastructure lost the worktree.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:940-978 reads gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath) after agent execution; on failure it writes a task_failed result with ResultRev set to baseRev and Reason set to the raw rev-parse error.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:25-44 defines failure modes but has no worktree_lost class.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1901-1909 derives outcome reason from status/error text and can collapse missing-worktree infrastructure into generic transport/unknown handling.
- The May 7 ddx-c2a66f45 and ddx-b1cf1f6b attempts both failed with git rev-parse HEAD: chdir ... no such file or directory after non-trivial agent runtime/cost.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a stable FailureModeWorktreeLost and classify missing worktree/chdir rev-parse failures into it.
- Write result.json diagnostics that include attempted worktree_path, attempt_id, bead_id, cleanup metadata presence, run-state/liveness state, and whether the path was registered with git worktree.
- Ensure worktree_lost is infrastructure/transport, does not increment no-progress/no_changes budget, and leaves the bead retryable or parked according to existing transport policy.
- Preserve prompt/manifest/result evidence even when the worktree is gone.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not recover lost uncommitted implementation changes.
- Do not change cleanup deletion policy; sibling cleanup bead prevents the loss.
- Do not change model/provider routing.

DEPS
Depends on per-attempt liveness so diagnostics can include the live-state record that should have protected the worktree.
title: agent: classify vanished attempt worktrees as worktree_lost

acceptance:

2. The installed `ddx` binary on PATH carries the v0.13.1 routing symbol `ProviderUnreachable`. Verified by `strings $(which ddx) 2>/dev/null | grep -c "ProviderUnreachable"` returning a value >= 1.
3. The v0.13.0 cooldown→hard-gate bug is no longer reproducible: after restarting `ddx work --watch` against a deliberately-off provider, no more than one `WARN provider error, retrying attempt=4` line per provider appears in `~/Projects/ddx/.ddx/logs/ddx-server.log` over a 5-minute observation window. Verified by `bash -c 'awk -F"\"" "/WARN provider error, retrying attempt=4/{n[\$4]++} END{for(p in n) if (n[p] > 1) exit 1; exit 0}" ~/Projects/ddx/.ddx/logs/ddx-server.log' && echo OK` printing OK after a 5-minute soak.
4. ddx test suite is green at integration HEAD: `cd ~/Projects/ddx && go test ./cli/... -count=1 -timeout 5m` exits 0.
description:
fizeau v0.13.1 is released and contains the autorouting fix the ddx workers were stalling on. Bump the ddx-side fizeau pin to v0.13.1 and restart the ddx work / ddx server processes so they pick up the new binary.

Context: with v0.13.0, every first dispatch attempt after a host (e.g. bragi) went off-network burned the full ~165s retry budget (5 attempts × ~30s dial timeout + exponential backoff) before the routing layer learned the host was unreachable. v0.13.0's hard-gate (`Inputs.ProviderUnreachable`) only populated from snapshot discovery errors, which lagged the first dispatch failure.

v0.13.1 closes two gaps:
- Route-attempt failures with dial-class errors (dial tcp / connection refused / no route to host / i/o timeout / no such host / 502 Bad Gateway / 503 / 504) now populate `Inputs.ProviderUnreachable` immediately. One failed dispatch hard-gates the provider for the cooldown TTL on the very next call. Non-dial failures (context canceled, 4xx, wall-clock timeouts) stay as soft demotion so sticky-lease keeps working through single transient hiccups.
- New `RefreshIfStale` snapshot refresh mode + `Cache.MaybeRefreshSync` primitive. `buildRoutingInputs` now uses it so a cold-start route call re-probes stale providers in-line (bounded by 5s discovery timeout per source) before scoring. The first call after a host dies is now at worst the 5s probe rather than the full retry budget.

Release: https://github.com/easel/fizeau/releases/tag/v0.13.1
Commit: 52c436e8 (fix routing) + the v0.13.1 tag.

Integration steps:
1. Update whatever pins the ddx workers carry on the fizeau SDK / agent / binary to v0.13.1 (go.mod replace block, install.sh hash, agent_binary_url, whatever the actual mechanism is).
2. Rebuild / reinstall ddx workers.
3. Restart any long-running `ddx work --watch`, `ddx server`, or similar processes so they load the new binary.
4. Verify by killing bragi (or pointing at an off host) and watching `ddx work` — instead of ~165s of `WARN provider error, retrying attempt=N` against bragi, the router should pick an alternative on the second-or-later request without burning the retry budget.
title: integrate fizeau v0.13.1 (routing hard-gate fix)

description:
PROBLEM
Operators and agents asking "is the worker still working beads?" or "how is the queue progressing?" can receive the status of a DDx worker from the wrong repository. In the observed incident, the user was asking about the Niflheim queue, but the agent ran a global `ps aux | grep -E "(ddx work|ddx try)"` and reported a DDx repo worker on `ddx-bbdbc903`. This is actively harmful: it makes project A look busy because project B has a worker, and it hides whether project A has stalled.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:22-31` tells agents how to check queue state with `ddx bead ready`, `ddx bead blocked`, `ddx bead status`, and `ddx bead list`, but it gives no required project-root confirmation or project-scoped live-worker check.
- `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:82-99` warns not to mix health/status commands, but does not warn against global process scans or require that worker reports match the requested project root.
- `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:143-159` documents `ddx work` and `ddx try`, but does not document a safe way to ask "which live workers belong to this project?".
- `cli/cmd/work.go:76` exposes `--project`, and `cli/cmd/work.go:144-165` includes `project_root` in terminal/JSON loop output, but DDx has no corresponding `ddx work status` / `ddx worker status` local CLI that lists live worker processes for the current project and filters out other repositories.
- `cli/cmd/server_workers.go:13-175` provides server-backed worker listing/logging, but that only helps when the local server worker registry is running; it does not solve ordinary inline `ddx work` shells that operators check from a terminal.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a project-scoped live-worker status surface and update skill guidance so agents cannot accidentally answer with cross-project workers.

Recommended implementation shape:
- Add `ddx work status` (or an equivalent `ddx worker status`) that defaults to the current git/project root, accepts `--project`, and lists only live `ddx work` / `ddx try` processes whose process cwd, `--project`, execution worktree, or durable worker record maps to that root.
- Include `--all-projects` for explicit global inspection. The default must never report workers from other repositories.
- Output should include project root, pid, age, command, active bead ID when inferable, execution worktree or bundle path when inferable, and a clear `no live workers for <project>` result when none match.
- Update `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md` and `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md` to require: first resolve the requested project root; then use the project-scoped status command; never use raw global `ps | grep ddx work` as evidence without filtering by project root; always name the project whose worker status is being reported.
- If a live worker is found for another project while answering a scoped question, mention it only as an explicitly out-of-scope process, not as proof that the requested project is progressing.

NON-SCOPE
Do not build a full multi-project scheduler. Do not require the DDx server to be running. Do not change bead claiming semantics. Do not kill or restart existing workers. Do not remove the server-backed worker APIs.
title: work: report live workers only for the requested project

description:
PROBLEM
  Local plugin installs need to make DDx skills live-editable by symlinking package-owned skill directories, but the current local path uses its own discovery rules and can choose the wrong skill source when pointed at the DDx repo root.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/cmd/install.go:254-372 implements local install separately from the registry package installer. It loads the manifest but then calls `installLocalSkillSymlinks(absPath, absProject, force)` without passing `pkg.Install.Skills`. cli/cmd/install.go:510-563 chooses `<pluginRoot>/.agents/skills` before `<pluginRoot>/skills`, so `ddx plugin install ddx --local .` can target destination copies in the repo root instead of the intended package source. The plan requires local overlays to honor manifest mappings and either resolve the repo root safely to `library/` or fail before modifying skill outputs.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/cmd/install.go: replace hard-coded local skill discovery with mapping-driven symlink creation from `pkg.Install.Skills`.
  - Symlink each child skill directory from the mapping source to each mapping target, e.g. `library/skills/ddx -> .agents/skills/ddx` and `.claude/skills/ddx`.
  - Add repo-root safety: if a local path is not a package root but contains `library/package.yaml` for the requested package, resolve to that package root, or fail clearly before writing outputs.
  - Preserve local overlay invariants: do not mutate plugin version pins and do not auto-commit.

NON-SCOPE
  - Remote registry real-file installs; sibling bead owns package mapping copy behavior.
  - Moving the actual skill content to `library/skills/ddx`.
  - Changing public plugin command names.

PARENT + DEPS
  Parent: ddx-6862f2ac. Depends on the registry mapping bead so local and remote installers share mapping semantics.
title: plugin install: symlink local package skill mappings safely

description:
# PROBLEM
The conformance suite (`ddx-958b8fc3`) parameterizes tests across `*Store` and `AxonStore`, but `AxonStore` is tested against an httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint — a mock. Production AxonStore deployment requires verifying behavior against an actual Postgres-backed Axon service. Without real-wire tests, semantic differences (timestamp precision, transaction isolation, NULL handling, etc.) can silently diverge between the mock and prod.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/axon/` and the conformance suite use mocked transports. No CI job runs AxonStore against real Postgres. Per `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`'s audit, "Zero tests run against Postgres" was a gap. The current state remains gap-ful for production verification.

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a real-wire integration test package under `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/`. Each test:
   - Provisions a Postgres instance (via Docker testcontainer, `dockertest`, or similar in-CI Postgres).
   - Starts an Axon service against that Postgres (or uses Axon's standalone test mode).
   - Constructs `AxonStore` via the real GraphQL transport pointing at the Axon service.
   - Runs the existing conformance suite + Axon-specific tests against the real backend.

2. Test cases (in addition to the parameterized conformance):
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_ClaimCAS_AtomicUnderConcurrency` — 10 concurrent claims; assert exactly one succeeds, others get `ErrAlreadyClaimed`.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_HeartbeatNativeUpdate` — assert `SetClaimHeartbeat` produces a single `UPDATE` SQL statement (no full-row read-modify-write); verify via Postgres query logs.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_EventAppend_Monotonic` — concurrent event appends preserve TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8 (monotonic per bead).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_TransactionIsolation` — `Apply` operations are atomic under SERIALIZABLE / READ COMMITTED (whatever Lakebase default is).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_NullPreservation` — `omitempty` JSONL fields map correctly to Postgres NULL/JSONB representation.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_TimestampPrecision` — RFC3339Nano roundtrips with Postgres `TIMESTAMPTZ` (nanosecond truncation behavior documented).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_SchemaVersionDefault` — beads with `schema_version` absent default to v1 on read (TD-027 §11.4).

3. CI job: `.github/workflows/integration-axon.yml` runs the real-wire tests on a Postgres service container. Gated to manual trigger initially (Postgres setup is slow); converted to nightly once stable. Not on every PR.

4. Documentation: `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/README.md` explains how to run locally (`docker-compose up postgres-test` + `go test -tags=integration ./internal/bead/integration/axon/...`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Production Lakebase setup — separate ops/infra concern.
- Performance benchmarks — separate work; this bead is correctness.
- Migrating the existing conformance suite to use real Postgres — would slow every PR. The httptest-served suite stays as the fast inner-loop test; real-wire is a gated outer-loop verification.

# Acceptance criteria

1. New package `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/` with at least 7 real-wire tests listed above.
2. Tests run against a real Postgres instance (testcontainer or service container in CI). Test infrastructure documented in package README.
3. Build tag `//go:build integration` separates these tests from the default test run. `go test ./...` (no tag) doesn't run them; `go test -tags=integration ./internal/bead/integration/axon/...` does.
4. CI workflow `.github/workflows/integration-axon.yml` provisioned. Initial trigger: `workflow_dispatch` (manual). Validation: at least one successful run end-to-end before merge.
5. The full parameterized conformance suite from `ddx-958b8fc3` runs against the real Postgres backend (re-using the same test cases). Mock vs. real-wire produces identical pass/fail signal for every test.
6. Real-wire-specific findings documented as new tests (concurrency CAS, native heartbeat update via Postgres logs, transaction isolation, NULL/JSONB handling, timestamp precision, schema_version defaults).
7. Docs link added to TD-027 §28.4 (downstream acceptance) once landed.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:axon, area:testing, kind:test, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-9c5bca8f (AxonStore must exist), IMP bead (importer used in test setup to populate Postgres with fixtures), ddx-958b8fc3 (conformance suite to re-use).
title: WIRE: Real-wire Axon integration tests against actual Postgres (CI nightly)

description:
PROBLEM
Fizeau has published v0.12.2 after DDx's current pinned dependency. DDx is still pinned to github.com/easel/fizeau v0.12.1 in cli/go.mod:7, so `ddx work` cannot rely on the upstream auto-routing fixes needed for normal unpinned execution. The desired runtime behavior is: default implementation work uses the best available subscription agent/quota through Fizeau auto-routing; review can use the alternate configured harness/model; explicit DDx flags remain opaque constraints; configured local providers remain available only when explicitly selected or validated, not as default route choices.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/go.mod:7 pins github.com/easel/fizeau v0.12.1 while upstream tag v0.12.2 is available.
- cli/cmd/work.go:28 documents that `ddx work` forwards --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque passthrough constraints and that Fizeau owns routing within power bounds.
- cli/cmd/work.go:90 and cli/cmd/work.go:91 expose separate review harness/model pins, so the integration must preserve implementation/review separation instead of collapsing both roles into the same hard route.
- cli/cmd/try.go:228 through cli/cmd/try.go:294 normalizes profile/power settings and forwards them through CLI overrides; after the upgrade this path must keep zero-config execution unpinned while preserving explicit pins.
- docs/agent-execute.md:14 through docs/agent-execute.md:19 define `ddx run` as one upstream Execute call with MinPower/MaxPower and optional passthrough constraints, with upstream owning harness/provider/model routing.
- docs/agent-execute.md:91 through docs/agent-execute.md:93 says ResolveRoute is debug/status-only and must not feed route choices back into execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Upgrade `github.com/easel/fizeau` to v0.12.2 in `cli/go.mod`/`cli/go.sum`, resolve any API changes in the DDx agent-service boundary, and add focused regression tests around DDx's routing contract. The implementation should prove that unpinned `ddx work`/`ddx try` requests leave harness/provider/model empty for Fizeau to auto-route, explicit --harness/--provider/--model/--profile flags still pass through unchanged, review routing remains separately configurable, and local provider configuration is not treated as a default implementation route before validation work is complete. Keep routinglint green: no DDx-side model catalog, profile ladder, fuzzy model matching, provider fallback, or execution-time ResolveRoute selection should be introduced.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement local provider validation in this bead.
- Do not redesign DDx routing configuration or reintroduce retired model_overrides/profile_ladders/default_harness behavior.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` without flags produces `lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate for pins min_power=7 max_power=8: N candidates rejected` because the pre-dispatch lint hook inherits `rcfg.Profile()="default"`, and fizeau's "default" profile pins `min_power=7,max_power=8`. Operators whose providers fall outside that band — local providers (power<=6) or frontier cloud providers via openrouter (power=9) — see every candidate rejected and the agent exits in <1s with no_evidence_produced. The post-attempt triage hook has the same latent bug. Pre-claim intake already sidesteps this with `ClearProfile: true`. Repro: in this repo with no flags, `ddx work --once` will emit the dispatch-failure event on the next claimed bead — see `.ddx/executions/20260508T173611-c6069449/manifest.json` events for a captured instance.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/routing_profile.go:11` normalizes empty profile to `"default"`.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:186-189` flows `rcfg.Profile()` into `ServiceExecuteRequest.Profile` when no `ProfileOverride` is set and `ClearProfile` is false.
- `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132` constructs the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime` without `ClearProfile` or `ProfileOverride`, so the lint hook's request carries `Profile="default"`.
- `cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118` has the same omission.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:96-106` demonstrates the fix shape: `ClearProfile: true`.
- Downstream: fizeau's `internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml` pins the `default` profile to `min_power: 7, max_power: 8`; `service_routing.go` `routePowerPolicyForRequest` overlays that on every request whose `Profile` field is set.

PROPOSED FIX
- In `NewPreDispatchLintHook` (`cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132`), set `ClearProfile: true` on the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime`. Lint is a mechanical scoring task; no profile constraint is needed.
- In `NewPostAttemptTriageHook` (`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118`), set `ClearProfile: true` for the same reason.
- Do not change `executeOnService`, `NormalizeRoutingProfile`, or any caller that legitimately uses Profile="default" with explicit `--harness/--model` overrides (those bypass the profile bound via fizeau's `routePowerBoundsForRequest`).
- Do not touch `NewPreClaimIntakeHook`; it already does the right thing.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change `NormalizeRoutingProfile` or the meaning of "default" — execute-bead and `ddx run` callers still rely on it as the default request shape and supply their own `--harness`/`--model`/`--profile` to bypass the bound.
- Do not modify fizeau's catalog.
- Do not add a new global setting; the fix is local to the two hook constructors.

PARENT + DEPS
No parent. No deps.

description:
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel errors.
4. `cli/internal/bead/context.go` — typed accessors `WithIdentity`/`IdentityFromContext`/`WithTrace`/`TraceFromContext`. Package docstring per TD-027 §9 enumerating allowed ctx values.
5. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` (expanded) — declare `BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle` (Create + Apply only at this stage), `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter` interfaces. All ctx-first. Event interfaces are declarations only in this bead; no `*Store` event compile-time assertions until E. Composite `Backend` is not added yet (other sub-interfaces missing until per-concern beads land). `OperationApplier` optional interface declared. `RawBackend` retained with new docstring warning new backends off whole-corpus shape.

## Modify *Store

6. Add `*Store.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` method per TD-027 §7.2: type-asserts wrapped `RawBackend` to `OperationApplier` and delegates; otherwise generic `WithLock + ReadAll + op.Apply + WriteAll`.
7. Add ctx as first parameter to `*Store.Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, plus `GenID`. Do not change `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind` here; E owns event ctx threading.

## Modify JSONLBackend

8. Add `JSONLBackend.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` — satisfies `OperationApplier`. Empty type-switch + generic fallback (the per-concern Claim bead adds the `SetClaimHeartbeat`/`ClearClaimHeartbeat` sidecar-optimization cases later).

## Caller migration (ctx threading only — scoped to foundational non-event methods)

9. Update every caller in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that calls the foundational non-event methods (Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID) to pass `ctx` through. Use `cmd.Context()` for cobra commands; existing request ctx for HTTP/GraphQL handlers; `context.Background()` only where no ctx is naturally available.

# NON-SCOPE
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`) — E bead `ddx-f39b41b3`.
- Helper packages (`ops/<concern>/`) — per-concern beads handle these.
- Removing any `*Store` workflow methods (Claim, Heartbeat, TransitionLifecycle, RequestCancel, SetExecutionCooldown, etc.) — per-concern beads handle removal as they migrate callers.
- AxonStore implementation — separate bead `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- Caller narrowing from `*bead.Store` field types to `bead.Backend` interface — happens in BL bead `ddx-74452926` after per-concern beads complete.
- Module boundary lockdown (relocation to `internal/storage/`) — BL bead.
- Schema-versioning migration framework — deferred until first breaking change.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:feature, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027, reliability

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: none — this bead is the gate.

# Cross-references
- TD-027 §5 (storage vs. workflow separation)
- TD-027 §6 (sub-interface taxonomy)
- TD-027 §7 (Operation pattern)
- TD-027 §7.2 (Apply routing through OperationApplier)
- TD-027 §8 (ID generation)
- TD-027 §9 (context discipline)
- TD-027 §10 (sentinel errors)
- TD-027 §11 (bead data model)
- TD-027 §13 (event vocabulary — controlled list)
- TD-027 §23.1 (gating bead acceptance criteria)

description:
PROBLEM
Phase events (queueing, running, terminal, bead-result JSONL) are emitted from 4 separate sites in execute_bead_loop.go. Adding a new phase or changing event shape requires coordinated edits across all 4 sites, introducing subtle divergence. Today the JSONL-on-disk shape is only verified by manual inspection against a baseline fixture.

ROOT CAUSE
The 4 emission sites in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:
- :577 — queueing phase event
- :596 — running phase event
- :1076-1091 — terminal phase event (with Disposition/ParkReason)
- :1093 — bead.result JSONL write

Each site constructs and emits events independently with no shared Phase enum or emitPhase abstraction. The C0 fixture diff approach (byte-identical JSONL baseline) is the only correctness gate, but it is not automated.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/phases.go defining Phase enum: Queueing | Running | Terminal.
- Add emitPhase(ctx, store, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error called by state transitions.
- Replace the 4 current emission sites with calls to emitPhase.
- Verify JSONL on-disk shape is byte-identical to the C0 baseline (automated fixture diff in test).

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the JSONL event format or adding new event types.
- Renaming execute_bead_loop.go (separate bead ddx-1d867ec1).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: terminal phase emission should fail open rather than silently diverge across call sites.
- P3: the emitted phase stream must stay observable and structured.
- P4: a phase-event bug on one bead must not affect other beads.
- P5: the phase stream supports operator-visible state.
title: C12: single phase-event emission (replaces 4 emit sites with state-machine-driven emitPhase)

acceptance: 1. 429 errors from Fizeau trigger per-bead wait + retry per TD-031 RateLimitRetryContract (per ddx-673833f4). 2. **Per RateLimitRetryContract: NO persisted status change before budget exhaustion; provider stays in rotation. bd/br compatibility (ADR-004) preserved.** 3. Retry-After honored when present; exponential backoff fallback when absent. 4. --rate-limit-max-wait flag bounds per-bead total wait (default 5 min). 5. Beyond budget: bead transitions per TD's outcome→state mapping for 'rate-limited beyond budget' — status stays in canonical 6, additional context conveyed via labels/events. 6. RecordRouteAttempt invoked for transparency per RateLimitRetryContract. 7. Tests cover Retry-After-respected and exponential-backoff paths. 8. Quota-exhaustion path is explicitly NOT this bead's concern (per QuotaPauseContract separation in TD). 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**
description:
Distinct from quota exhaustion (see ddx-aede917d). Rate-limiting (HTTP 429 with Retry-After header — per-second/per-minute throttle) means 'slow down', not 'I'm out for the period'. Provider stays in rotation; current bead retries after waiting.

Per-bead retry policy:
1. On 429 / rate-limit error from Fizeau (NOT NoViableProviderForNow which is quota): wait the Retry-After interval (capped at 60s default, configurable via --rate-limit-max-wait), retry the SAME bead with the SAME provider
2. If no Retry-After header: exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 15s, 30s, 60s) up to the cap
3. Per-bead total rate-limit wait budget (default 5 min); past that, mark execution_failed with reason 'rate-limited beyond budget'
4. Provider availability state unchanged (stays in rotation; this is per-request throttle, not provider-out)

Should use Fizeau's existing RecordRouteAttempt feedback channel for transparency (records the rate-limit event so the routing engine has signal even though it doesn't change provider availability).

description:
PROBLEM
  Three observed cases on 2026-05-10 of fake-work: implementer
  models write aspirational commit messages but the actual diff
  is minimal/absent. The reviewer rubber-stamps the attempt and
  the bead closes with AC not satisfied.

  Cases:
    - ddx-1f774edf "remove network I/O from Land()" — claimed
      PreClaimGitOps interface + LandingGitOps cleanup; diff
      was 4 files / 30 lines.
    - ddx-94a368b6 "conformance transition-matrix coverage" —
      preserved on iteration ref, never landed.
    - ddx-d22293ba "wire RetainDays prune" — claimed prune loop,
      counters, 5 tests, agent-logs/workers scope; diff was 5
      lines setting a default value.

  The reviewer ALREADY emits per_ac[].grade + per_ac[].evidence
  (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:204-234). The
  defect is that the evidence string is UNVERIFIED TEXT. A
  rubber-stamping reviewer can write "evidence": "types.go:42"
  for an AC it never checked. Strengthening reviewer prose
  without a mechanical forcing function would create
  false-positive BLOCKs on legitimate work (renamed tests,
  negative AC, mechanical refactors, etc.) and still fail to
  catch fake-work convincingly.

  This bead is the FIRST of two: it adds a mechanical
  AC-verification tool that both implementer (pre-commit
  self-check) and reviewer (gate ratification) invoke with the
  SAME inputs. Sister bead changes the reviewer behavior to
  ratify this tool's output rather than re-grep the diff
  independently.

CURRENT STATE
  - No CLI subcommand performs per-AC verification mechanically.
  - The reviewer prompt asks for per-AC grading but provides no
    structured input — reviewer must grep a possibly-30k-line
    diff to find evidence, which scales poorly and invites
    rubber-stamping.
  - Implementer has no way to self-check whether its work would
    pass review before committing — pure information
    asymmetry.

PROPOSED FIX
  Implement `ddx bead ac-check <id>` (or `ddx bead check <id>`)
  with the following behavior:

  1. Inputs: bead ID; the working tree (or a specific rev
     range, default: merge-base..HEAD).

  2. Read b.Acceptance from the bead; parse into numbered AC
     items.

  3. Per-AC inspection pipeline. Classify each AC by
     lightweight regex/heuristic, then mechanically resolve:

     a. test-name AC (matches /Test[A-Z]\\w+/):
        - Run `go test -run '^<name>$'` on relevant packages.
        - Record pass/fail/missing.

     b. build/gate AC (matches "passes", "green", "lefthook",
        "pre-commit"):
        - Invoke `lefthook run pre-commit` or `go test ./...`
          per AC text.
        - Record exit code.

     c. negative AC (matches "no calls to", "does not",
        "remove", "no longer"):
        - `git grep` the named symbol on POST-IMAGE.
        - Pass if 0 hits (or fewer than baseline).

     d. symbol AC (matches `` `Foo.Bar()` `` or similar
        backtick-quoted identifier):
        - `git grep` the symbol; require ≥1 hit AND at least
          one diff hunk touching it.

     e. mechanical AC (matches "rename", "doc", "comment"):
        - File existence / rename detection via `git
          diff --name-status`.

     f. prose AC (everything else):
        - Emit `needs-judgment=true`. NEVER auto-pass, NEVER
          auto-fail.

  4. ACAliases support. The bead body may include an optional
     `ACAliases:` map (e.g., `AC #3: TestFooBar -> TestFoo/bar`)
     so a legitimate refactor can declare its substitutions.
     The tool reads it; the reviewer sees it.

  5. Output. Both human-readable and machine-readable:
     - Stdout: human-readable table per AC.
     - File: `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json` with
       structured per-AC results.

     Example structured output:
       {
         "schema_version": 1,
         "attempt_id": "...",
         "bead_id": "ddx-...",
         "summary": {"pass": 3, "fail": 1, "needs_judgment": 1},
         "items": [
           {"ac": 1, "kind": "test-name", "name": "TestFoo",
            "result": "pass", "evidence": "package x; test ran 0.04s"},
           {"ac": 4, "kind": "symbol", "name": "RemovedExecutionDirs",
            "result": "fail",
            "evidence": "grep found 0 hits; AC requires symbol present"},
           {"ac": 3, "kind": "prose",
            "result": "needs_judgment",
            "evidence": "no mechanical anchor; defer to reviewer"}
         ]
       }

  6. CLI: `ddx bead ac-check <id> [--rev-range <base>..<head>]
     [--package <go-package>] [--json]`. Idempotent. No
     side effects on the bead store.

  7. Tests:
     - TestACCheck_TestNameAC_Passes
     - TestACCheck_TestNameAC_Renamed_WithAlias
     - TestACCheck_TestNameAC_Missing_Fails
     - TestACCheck_NegativeAC_FlagsResidue
     - TestACCheck_NegativeAC_AbsenceConfirmed_Passes
     - TestACCheck_SymbolAC_PresentInDiff_Passes
     - TestACCheck_SymbolAC_NotInDiff_Fails
     - TestACCheck_MechanicalAC_RenameDetection
     - TestACCheck_ProseAC_NeedsJudgment_NeverAutoFail
     - TestACCheck_ProseAC_NeedsJudgment_NeverAutoPass
     - TestACCheck_JSONOutput_SchemaVersion1
     - TestACCheck_RegressionFakeRetainDays: replay the
       917cfbfa7 fake-work diff; assert ac-check yields
       multiple `fail` results for the missing prune logic
       and tests.

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the reviewer prompt or gate behavior (sister bead).
  - Wiring ac-check into execute-bead workflow (sister bead).
  - Strictness modes by bead label (sister bead).
  - New verdict types like REQUEST_CLARIFICATION (sister bead).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "fix(review): reviewer ratifies ac-check" —
    that bead consumes ac-check.json as the reviewer's primary
    input and changes the gate semantics. THIS bead is purely
    additive and can land independently.
  - Existing reviewer per_ac[] structure (execute_bead_review.go:204-234)
    is preserved; this tool produces structured input for it
    rather than replacing it.
title: feat(bead): ddx bead ac-check — mechanical per-AC verification tool (additive)

acceptance:
1. `test -f docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md` passes.
2. `rg -n "Prose Quality Support|not an AI detector|detector bypass|advisory|technical|planning|public|ddx doc prose --changed" docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md` returns matches for each required concept.
3. The FEAT acceptance criteria include structural, command-verifiable outcomes and avoid subjective-only phrases such as "sounds human" as acceptance.
4. If docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md is touched, it only adds a concise reference and does not re-specify the feature.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
CONTEXT
This bead creates the governing feature spec for DDx Prose Quality Support. The desired user outcome is not AI-detection evasion; it is clear, specific, voice-preserving prose in DDx governing artifacts and user-facing docs. The feature must define how DDx targets common AI-writing tropes with deterministic, explainable findings.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md
- docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md only if needed to add a short feature reference

REQUIRED SPEC CONTENT
- Problem: vague/generic prose weakens document-driven development.
- Primary users: DDx maintainers, project maintainers, and agents writing/reviewing docs.
- Content modes: technical, planning, and public prose.
- Product surfaces: default skill, deterministic checker/rules, ddx doc prose --changed, later review integration.
- Non-goals: no AI detector, no detector bypass, no blocking by default, no rewriting authorial voice away.
- Measurable acceptance criteria using structural findings: file, line, rule id, severity, rationale, suggested edit; project vocabulary; advisory default.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Implementing the checker.
- Choosing the final tool boundary beyond naming deterministic prose checks.
- Adding CLI flags or plugin assets.
title: Define FEAT-027 Prose Quality Support

3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerStdout_ReadinessMalformedSchemaIsActionable` exists and proves worker output contains a concise message naming `readiness_checks` without printing a raw Go struct type for either object- or string-shaped malformed payloads.
4. Existing readiness tests including `TestReadinessHookEmptyOutput_DeduplicatesPrefix`, `TestReadinessUnavailableOutputIsActionable`, and `TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesCanonicalReadinessJSON` remain green.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreClaimReadiness_.*|TestExecuteBeadWorkerStdout_Readiness.*|TestReadiness.*" -count=1` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
During plain `ddx work` runs on 2026-05-13, the pre-claim readiness hook repeatedly printed raw schema errors such as `readiness check unavailable: decode result: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field .readiness_checks of type []struct { Reason string ... } (continuing)` and, from a concurrent Codex worker, `cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field .readiness_checks ...`. DDx continued, but the operator-visible message is a raw Go JSON decode error and the malformed readiness output is treated as hook-unavailable instead of being normalized into actionable evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:56-67` defines readiness classification payloads with `readiness_checks []preClaimReadinessCheck`. `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:307-323` probes `readiness_checks` as an array before deciding which decoder to use, so model/hook responses with `readiness_checks` as an object or string fail the entire probe decode. Existing tests cover empty output and valid array payloads, but not malformed singleton object or scalar-shaped `readiness_checks`.

PROPOSED FIX
Make readiness decoding resilient to `readiness_checks` being a singleton object or scalar string. Normalize a singleton object to a one-element slice. For scalar/string values, return a structured malformed-readiness result with a concise actionable message naming `readiness_checks`, preserving the value as evidence if useful, and preserving forward progress. The worker log should not expose raw Go struct types.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the readiness classification vocabulary. Do not make malformed readiness output a bead blocker unless the configured quality mode already blocks system-unready hook failures. Do not change implementation dispatch routing.
title: readiness: normalize malformed readiness_checks payloads

acceptance:

2. TestPreCloseReview_DefaultRunsTwoNoToolReviewers verifies two reviewer invocations receive the same evidence bundle and correlation fields role=reviewer, review_group_id, reviewer_index=0/1, bead_id, attempt_id, result_rev, and implementer route facts.
3. TestPreCloseReview_UnanimousApproveCloses verifies close occurs only when both reviewers APPROVE and each approval includes per-AC evidence.
4. TestPreCloseReview_ApproveWithoutEvidenceIsReviewError verifies an evidence-free APPROVE is classified as review_error: unparseable and does not close the bead.
5. TestPreCloseReview_RequestChangesBlocksCloseAndSchedulesRepair verifies REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK with fixable implementation findings records review-fixable-gap, leaves the bead unclosed, and starts the next implementation cycle with repair_context_from_review_group.
6. TestPreCloseReview_TerminalClassificationsBlockOperator verifies review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error block with needs_human and do not schedule unbounded retries.
7. TestPreCloseReview_ReviewErrorRetryBudgetIsPerReviewerSlot verifies review errors retry by result_rev + reviewer_index up to review_max_retries, then emit review-manual-required and block without close.
8. TestPreCloseReview_NoClosedBeadReopen verifies REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK never closes and reopens a bead; the failed review prevents close in the first place.

description:
PROBLEM
ADR-024 and FEAT-010 now require adversarial review to be the default pre-close gate, but current loop/review code still models single post-merge review and reopen behavior. A successful implementation can be closed even though the review path is not modeled as a two-reviewer unanimous pre-close gate with repair-cycle traceability.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 documents ReviewVerdict as post-merge review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 documents Reopen as the REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK path after a bead was closed.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:474 builds one reviewer request, without review_group_id or reviewer_index correlation metadata.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:528 runs a single DefaultBeadReviewer.ReviewBead invocation and parses one verdict.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:242 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:279 assert REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK reopen behavior instead of pre-close blocking behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a pre-close review coordinator used by execute-loop after a close-eligible implementation result and before CloseWithEvidence.
- Default behavior runs two no-tool reviewer invocations against the same bounded evidence bundle. Each invocation carries role=reviewer plus bead_id, attempt_id, result_rev, review_group_id, reviewer_index, and implementer route facts.
- Close only when both reviewers return APPROVE with per-AC evidence. Treat APPROVE without required evidence as review_error: unparseable.
- Any evidenced REQUEST_CHANGES or BLOCK prevents close and classifies the result into review_fixable_gap, review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, or review_error.
- review_fixable_gap schedules a bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. Terminal classifications block the bead with needs_human or decomposition metadata as specified by TD-031.
- Review transport/provider/parse errors retry per result_rev + reviewer_index up to review_max_retries; exhaustion emits review-manual-required and blocks without closing.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL review-session UI mutations.
- Manual human conversation review sessions.
- Changing the evidence assembler beyond adding the review-group fields required by this gate.
