description:
PROBLEM
execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036 handles no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, and push-conflict inline in the loop body. These are distinct outcome behaviors that belong in try.Attempt so the loop only sees Disposition + Cooldown + ParkReason + RecordEvents.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036: the block that follows a bead attempt contains outcome-specific branches (no_changes, decomposition, push_failed, push_conflict) as raw loop logic rather than Disposition values from try.Attempt.
- ddx-b24e9630 (no_changes verification): adjudicateNoChangesContract function exists in the codebase — confirm it is called from try.Attempt's no_changes branch (not merely defined). If wired: preserve. If unwired: this child must wire it.
- ddx-c6e3db02 (rate-limit retry): EvaluateRetryPolicy (or similar) must be called from try.Attempt's rate-limit detection path — confirm actual wiring before this refactor moves it.
- try/attempt.go:67 is the candidate location for the relocated no_changes adjudication logic.
- The dead-code audit (ddx-09d2990c) must close before this child to establish which functions are genuinely wired vs. only defined.

PROPOSED FIX
- Remove lines 875-1036 from execute_bead_loop.go; replace with: read Disposition + Cooldown + ParkReason + RecordEvents from try.Attempt result.
- Move no_changes adjudication (verification_command runner + triage label logic) into try.Attempt's no_changes branch near try/attempt.go:67.
- Move rate-limit retry (Retry-After honor + exponential backoff + budget cap) into try.Attempt's rate-limit detection.
- Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation on no_changes; stays in canonical state 6; uses triage:no-changes-unverified label per ADR-004.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not modify the Drain loop contract beyond replacing lines 875-1036 with Disposition/Cooldown reads.
- ddx-09d2990c wire-or-delete must close first (hard dep).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: no_changes, decomposition, push-failed, and push-conflict must fail open instead of wedging the loop.
- P2: try.Attempt owns the outcome classification; the drain loop should not own per-outcome policy.
- P3: emit structured events so operators can see the exact degradation instead of a silent retry loop.
- P4: the handling must stay per-bead.
- P6: auto-retry should remain limited to transient classes only.

description:
PROBLEM
  A 2026-05-09 alignment review of the bead lifecycle (spec TD-031, types.go,
  lifecycle.go, store.go, reconcile.go, plus all CLI/server/agent mutation
  call sites) found the state machine is well-specified but has six concrete
  alignment, duplication, and test-coverage gaps. The implementation funnels
  ~all status writes through transitionLifecycleInPlace + ValidateLifecycleTransition
  (good), but: reconcile-close bypasses ClosureGate without the spec saying so;
  Ready() filters status twice with conflicting answers; the backend conformance
  suite never exercises the transition matrix; "park to proposed" is duplicated
  6x in agent/*.go; claim-Extra key cleanup is duplicated in Reopen + Unclaim.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:97 ValidateLifecycleTransition is the single
    transition gate.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:619 transitionLifecycleInPlace is the single
    chokepoint for status writes.
  - cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:236-242 closes beads via UpdateWithLifecycleStatus
    directly, bypassing the ClosureGate enforced by store.go:1245.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1763 readyFiltered re-filters Status==StatusOpen
    after the lifecycle module already classified the bucket.
  - cli/internal/bead/backend_conformance_test.go:53-166 covers create/update/
    claim/deps/events but no status transitions.
  - 6 "park to proposed" call sites in cli/internal/agent/{execute_bead_loop.go,
    execute_bead_post_review.go, execute_bead_review_terminal.go,
    execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go} duplicate the same boilerplate.
  - Reopen (store.go:1306-1317) and Unclaim (store.go:823-831) maintain
    parallel lists of claim-Extra keys to clear.

PROPOSED FIX
  Decompose into 6 children, executed in dependency order:
  - B1 (doc-only): amend TD-031 to declare reconcile-close vs evidence-close.
  - B2 (code): align reconcile.go with amended TD-031 and add comment at
    store.go:471 noting Create-time status default is the lone exception.
  - B3 (code): drop redundant Status==Open filter at store.go:1763; let
    EvaluateLifecycleQueue be the single source of truth for "ready".
  - B4 (test): extend backend_conformance_test.go with the TD-031 transition
    matrix against both backend_jsonl and axon_backend.
  - B5 (refactor): extract Store.ParkToProposed helper covering the 6 agent
    duplicates.
  - B6 (refactor): consolidate claim-Extra key list so Reopen + Unclaim share
    one definition.

NON-SCOPE
  - File-level reorganization of store.go (carving into claim.go/closure.go/
    queue_view.go). Pure mechanical and not in scope here; can be filed later.
  - Adding new persisted statuses or changing the canonical-status enum.
  - Changes to bd/br interchange (ADR-004 contract is fixed).

INTERSECTIONS
  - TD-031: B1 amends it; B2-B6 implement against amended spec.
  - ADR-004: out of scope (interchange contract is fixed).
  - ADR-023 (lifecycle quality policy): adjacent; not changed by this epic.
title: bead-lifecycle alignment review: spec, dedupe, and conformance

description:
PROBLEM
  The PostLadderExhaustionHook infrastructure (parent child ddx-e012af90) routes
  SpecGap and PersistentExecutionFailed failure classes to a REFRAME path, but no
  reframer prompt template and no reframer dispatch logic exist. When the hook
  fires for these classes, it must dispatch a strong-tier agent to rewrite the
  bead's description and AC, then apply those edits via the existing
  actionable_but_rewritten path in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has no reframer dispatch code.
  library/prompts/agents/reframer.md does not exist.
  The intake actionable_but_rewritten apply path exists but is only called
  from pre-claim intake (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1068-1088);
  it is not wired into the post-exhaustion recovery hook.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Add library/prompts/agents/reframer.md defining:
     - Input contract: original bead body (description + AC) and failure history
       (last N attempt summaries, stop conditions, review verdicts).
     - Output contract: JSON with fields description (string|null) and acceptance
       (string|null); null means "leave unchanged".
     - Role guidance: preserve governing refs, named tests, file:line refs, deps.
  2. Add reframer dispatch in cli/internal/agent/recovery_reframe.go:
     - runReframer(ctx, store, executor, beadID, failureHistory) → ReframeResult
     - Dispatches a smart+ tier agent using harness determined by ExecutorConfig.
     - Parses structured JSON output; validates it contains at least one non-null field.
     - Calls applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite() with the parsed edits.
     - On success: sets status=open, clears consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey,
       emits reframe-applied event with cost.
  3. Failure modes explicitly handled (each returns ReframeResult{failed:true}):
     - Timeout exceeded.
     - Empty output from agent.
     - Structurally-invalid JSON (cannot parse).
     - No-op edits: both description and acceptance unchanged after normalization.
  4. Hook (from ddx-e012af90) invokes runReframer for SpecGap and
     PersistentExecutionFailed classes; on success returns PostLadderExhaustionResult
     {attempted:true, succeeded:true, path:Reframe, costUSD:...}.

NON-SCOPE
  Decompose path (child bead 3). Circuit breaker (child bead 4).
  The fallback from reframe-failure to decompose is wired in child bead 4.
title: feat(escalation): implement REFRAME path with reframer.md template and agent dispatch

1. Child beads under this epic define the spec policy, implementation, observability, and regression coverage for the downgrade-loop fix.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx readiness can currently turn operator-accepted open work back into status=proposed, which interrupts continuous forward progress and can create a downgrade loop after an operator promotes a bead back to open. The queue should keep moving unless DDx has a hard, evidenced operator-decision blocker.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:219-224 and :485-493 allow readiness to move ambiguous or underspecified work to status=proposed, while docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:85-99 says lifecycle quality defaults to WARN-ONLY. The policy boundary is underspecified.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 parks terminal readiness outcomes to proposed without checking whether an operator already promoted the same bead back to open.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 appends intake.blocked with outcome/detail only; it has no stable rule fingerprint or operator-override signal.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate child beads that define and implement a forward-progress readiness policy: open is the execution lane; proposed is the hard operator-decision escape; proposed -> open is a durable operator acceptance signal; readiness warnings are observable but do not block by default.

NON-SCOPE
- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.
- Rewriting execute-bead branch merge policy.
- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Root epic. Child beads under this epic carry the execution details and dependencies.
title: bead-lifecycle: coordinate forward-progress readiness policy

description:
PROBLEM
  ddx-d22293ba was filed and closed (commit 917cfbfa7) but the
  implementation is fake. The commit message claims:
    - Add RetainDays int field to ExecutionCleanupManager
    - Implement pruneEvidenceDirs deletion loop
    - Add RemovedEvidenceDirs counter
    - Surface counter in telemetry
    - Add 5 named tests
    - ApplyDefaults sets RetainDays=7

  The actual diff is 5 lines in cli/internal/config/types.go
  setting a default value in NewConfig.ApplyDefaults. None of
  the claimed implementation exists. The reviewer approved the
  attempt; the closure gate did not catch the discrepancy.

  Sister bead at queue-rank=36 strengthens the reviewer to
  catch this pattern going forward. THIS bead does the actual
  work that ddx-d22293ba was supposed to do, against the
  amended scope (90-day default, executions + agent-logs +
  workers).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go ExecutionCleanupManager.Cleanup
    still has no prune loop. scanCompleteEvidenceDirs (line 453)
    only emits observations.
  - cli/internal/config/types.go now has a default RetainDays=7
    (set by 917cfbfa7), but the value is never read.
  - .ddx/agent-logs/ and .ddx/workers/ retention is entirely
    absent.

PROPOSED FIX
  Implement what ddx-d22293ba's commit message claimed, against
  the amended (correct) scope:

  1. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go: implement
     pruneOlderThan helper. For each of:
       a. .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/
       b. .ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl (and svc-*.jsonl)
       c. .ddx/workers/worker-<id>/
     delete if mtime is older than ExecutionsConfig.RetainDays
     days.

  2. Skip active items across all three paths:
       a. executions: skip if attempt_id is referenced by an
          active run-state file.
       b. agent-logs: skip if session_id is referenced by a
          live worker (PID check or run-state cross-ref).
       c. workers: skip if worker PID is still alive.

  3. cli/internal/config/types.go ApplyDefaults:
       - CHANGE default RetainDays from 7 to 90 (per ADR-024
         P2 — the user explicitly chose 90 to balance reconstruct
         capability vs disk usage).
       - RetainDays=0 disables pruning.

  4. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go ExecutionCleanupSummary
     gains counters: RemovedExecutionDirs, RemovedAgentLogs,
     RemovedWorkerDirs. Each surfaced in cleanup log.

  5. Use git rm -rf for tracked file deletions (preserves git
     state); fall back to os.RemoveAll for untracked.

  Tests (the SAME tests ddx-d22293ba's commit message claimed
  but didn't write):
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldExecutionDirs
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldAgentLogs
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldWorkerDirs
     - TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveItems (all three paths)
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DefaultRetainDays90
     - TestExecutionCleanup_RetainDaysZero_DisablesRetention
     - TestExecutionCleanup_RecordsCounts (all three counters)

NON-SCOPE
  - The mirror backend (sister bead ddx-b2c6ae22).
  - Gitignore flip (sister bead ddx-15617e59, already done).
  - Metrics extraction (sister bead ddx-054b2449).
  - One-shot bulk cleanup of existing dirs (operator can do
    this manually before this bead lands).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Supersedes the partially-shipped ddx-d22293ba. That bead
    set the default value; this bead delivers the actual prune
    behavior.
  - Sister bead at rank 36 (reviewer-strengthening) will catch
    similar fake-work patterns going forward.
  - Sister bead ddx-054b2449 (metrics extraction) SHOULD land
    BEFORE this bead so metrics are captured before evidence
    is pruned.

acceptance:

3. The first executable surface is advisory and can be run against changed Markdown files without blocking unrelated DDx workflows.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx is document-driven: PRDs, FEAT specs, technical designs, ADRs, bead descriptions, review prompts, and close notes are governing artifacts. When those documents drift into generic AI-like prose, vague importance language, or terminology inconsistency, agents receive weaker execution prompts and reviewers lose checkable design intent.

GOAL
Add DDx Prose Quality Support: DDx should help users and agents write clear, specific, voice-preserving project prose and flag common AI-writing tropes through advisory guidance plus deterministic checks. This is not an AI detector and must not be framed as detector bypass.

PROPOSED SLICES
1. Define the FEAT product contract for prose quality support.
2. Define the technical design for a deterministic prose quality pipeline.
3. Add fixtures/golden expected findings to keep false positives under control.
4. Ship a default-plugin human-writing-support skill.
5. Add default rules and DDx vocabulary.
6. Add ddx doc prose --changed as the first advisory CLI surface.
7. Document configuration and non-goals.
8. Later, integrate prose findings into bead review after noise is known.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement AI-authorship detection. Do not make prose findings blocking by default. Do not wire this into ddx work or bead review until the advisory doc command has landed and has fixture coverage.
title: EPIC: Add DDx Prose Quality Support

description:
PROBLEM
Recent `ddx work` attempts failed repeatedly, but the causes are mixed together in tracker events and execution bundles. Operators cannot tell which failures are bead-readiness defects versus provider, host resource, git, review, close-policy, or unknown failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` stores execute-bead, bead-quality, routing, review, and cost events in the same per-bead event streams; recent events include provider exhaustion, review transport, ENOSPC, git index lock, no_changes, and tests_red-like checkpoint failures without a durable cross-attempt report.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:670` logs pre-claim intake failures inline and continues claiming, leaving the analysis spread across terminal output and bead events.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-751` logs pre-dispatch lint failures separately from intake, so one operator-visible failure taxonomy is not available.

PROPOSED FIX
Create a durable report under `docs/helix/06-iterate/` from recent `.ddx/beads.jsonl` events and `.ddx/executions/*` bundles. The report must group failed beads by estimated cause, include examples, state whether each cause is checkable by bead readiness before claim, and identify the owning follow-up surface.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change specs, skills, Go code, tracker state beyond this bead, or live worker behavior.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx maintains a parallel model catalog: an on-disk override at `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml`, a built-in seed catalog (`DefaultModelCatalogYAML`), tier→surface→model mappings (`smart→opus`, `standard→sonnet`, `cheap→haiku`), and HTTP/MCP endpoints that serve it. Fizeau already owns the authoritative model catalog (`~/.config/fizeau/models.yaml` + embedded yaml), exposes models via `svc.ListModels()`, and resolves tier→model via `ModelRef` ("smart"/"standard"/"cheap"). Every tier→model lookup DDx does is duplicate state that drifts from fizeau's truth, violates the routing-boundary contract (CONTRACT-003 / ADR-024 — "DDx forwards opaque pins/power bounds; fizeau owns model selection"), and is the same class of violation already retired by ddx-3bd7396a (`ResolveTierModelRef`). The catalog file at `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` is shadow state that this bead deletes outright; operators with the file see a `ddx doctor` warning pointing at fizeau's location.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go` (entire file, 143 lines) — defines `ModelCatalogYAML` schema, `DefaultModelCatalogPath() = ~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml`, `LoadModelCatalogYAML`, `ApplyModelCatalogYAML`, and the hardcoded seed `DefaultModelCatalogYAML()` with smart/standard/cheap tier→surface→model mappings.
- `cli/internal/agent/catalog.go` (entire file, 207 lines) — defines `Catalog`, `CatalogEntry`, `BuiltinCatalog`, `NewCatalog`, `Resolve(ref, surface)`. Pure DDx routing intelligence.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_catalog_shared.go` (entire file, 60 lines) — `ApplyCatalogFromService` overlays fizeau's catalog onto DDx's; only exists because DDx insists on holding its own copy.
- `cli/internal/agent/models.go:7-26` — `harnessToSurface` map and `ResolveModelTier(harness, tier)` that wraps `BuiltinCatalog.Resolve`. Tier→model resolution that fizeau owns.
- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:32` — `Runner.Catalog *Catalog` field.
- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:54-66` — `NewRunner` overlays built-in seed + fizeau service catalog + `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` on every Runner construction.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:806` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:53` — `ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))` resolves a concrete reviewer model client-side instead of asking fizeau.
- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1230-1236` — reads `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` for an `agent` subcommand.
- `cli/internal/server/agent_catalog.go` (entire file, 387 lines) — `handleAgentCatalog` serves the on-disk catalog over HTTP; tier/surface response shapes; same loader paths.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go:853,856,3376,3748-3749` — `GET /api/agent/catalog`, `GET /api/projects/{project}/agent/catalog`, MCP tool `ddx_agent_catalog`.
- `cli/internal/agent/models.go:36-41` `BenchmarkArm.ResolveArm` resolves Model from Tier via `ResolveModelTier`; benchmarks must instead require an explicit Model or pass `ModelRef` through to fizeau.
- Lint anchor: `cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go:75` already forbids `ResolveTierModelRef` per ddx-3bd7396a; that rule needs siblings for `BuiltinCatalog`, `ResolveModelTier`, and the catalog loader symbols so future code cannot reintroduce the violation.

PROPOSED FIX
Aggressive deletion. Do not refactor or reshape; remove.

1. Delete `cli/internal/agent/model_catalog_yaml.go` entirely.
2. Delete `cli/internal/agent/catalog.go` entirely.
3. Delete `cli/internal/agent/agent_catalog_shared.go` entirely.
4. Delete `cli/internal/server/agent_catalog.go` entirely. Remove `GET /api/agent/catalog`, `GET /api/projects/{project}/agent/catalog`, and the MCP `ddx_agent_catalog` tool registration from `cli/internal/server/server.go:853,856,3376,3748-3749`. Frontend/operator consumers that need a catalog view should call fizeau directly (or a thin proxy added in a follow-up if a real consumer surfaces).
5. In `cli/internal/agent/models.go`, delete `harnessToSurface` and `ResolveModelTier`. Keep `BenchmarkArm` struct but change `BenchmarkArm.Model` to be required: `ResolveArm()` becomes a no-op or is deleted; benchmark callers must supply an explicit Model or `ModelRef`. (Audit benchmark callers — likely under `cli/cmd/`, possibly `cli/internal/agent/compare_*.go`.)
6. In `cli/internal/agent/runner.go`, drop the `Catalog *Catalog` field (line 32) and the catalog-construction block (lines 54-66) from `NewRunner`. Drop the `r.catalog()` accessor and any consumers; `BuiltinCatalog` is gone, so leftover references will fail to compile and force callsites to be cleaned in this bead.
7. In `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:806` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:53`, replace `ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))` with passing `ModelRefOverride: "smart"` to `dispatchViaResolvedConfig` and leaving `Model` empty. Fizeau resolves smart→concrete model. Update the surrounding routing-fact emission to drop the locally-resolved model name; record only what fizeau returns in `final.RoutingActual`.
8. In `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1230-1236`, delete the catalog-loading block and any subcommand it gated. If the subcommand is purely a catalog viewer, delete the subcommand outright.
9. Extend `cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go` forbidden-identifier list with: `BuiltinCatalog`, `ResolveModelTier`, `LoadModelCatalogYAML`, `DefaultModelCatalogYAML`, `DefaultModelCatalogPath`, `ApplyModelCatalogYAML`, `ApplyCatalogFromService`, `CatalogEntry`, `ModelCatalogYAML`. Each entry cites this bead and the routing-boundary rationale.
10. Add a `ddx doctor` check that flags an existing `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` with: "DDx no longer reads ~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml; configure models via fizeau at ~/.config/fizeau/models.yaml. Safe to delete this file."

NON-SCOPE
- Do not write a migration tool that converts `~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml` to fizeau format. Operators delete the file or copy entries into `~/.config/fizeau/models.yaml` themselves; the doctor check tells them where.
- Do not change fizeau's catalog or any fizeau-side surface.
- Do not retire the `harness/provider/model/model-ref/min-power/max-power` passthrough flags themselves; only the DDx-side model resolution that they bypass.
- Do not delete `escalation.ModelTier` — the type is still used as a request-time hint passed via ModelRef.

PARENT + DEPS
No parent. No deps. Sibling/successor to ddx-3bd7396a (which retired `ResolveTierModelRef`). Independent of ddx-c54b817c and ddx-ea91688b.

description:
PROBLEM
After an operator reviews a proposed bead and promotes it back to open, pre-claim readiness can evaluate the same finding again and park the bead back to proposed. That creates a downgrade loop and contradicts the operator intent to keep work moving.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md:70-75 already defines proposed -> open as operator-driven triaged, but cli/internal/bead/store.go:587-628 status transitions mutate lifecycle metadata without necessarily appending a durable triaged event that readiness can query.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 unconditionally parks terminal readiness findings without checking for a later operator triage/acceptance.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 stores intake.blocked without a stable fingerprint, so even if an operator override exists the loop cannot tell whether the current readiness finding is the same one.

PROPOSED FIX
- Reuse the existing TD-027 triaged semantics for proposed -> open. Ensure operator promotion records a durable signal: either a triaged event or an explicit operator-accepted marker attached to the status transition event body.
- Add a stable readiness finding fingerprint for parkable intake findings. Include at least the decision class/rule, normalized reason, and a hash of prompt-relevant bead fields used to determine whether the bead changed since operator acceptance.
- Before parking an open bead for pre-claim readiness, check whether a later proposed -> open operator acceptance covers the same fingerprint. If yes, append/emit override-honored evidence and proceed instead of parking.
- If prompt-relevant fields changed after the operator acceptance, allow readiness to re-evaluate and park with a new fingerprint.

NON-SCOPE
- No broad WARN/BLOCK policy changes; those are in ddx-91898f19.
- No new event vocabulary unless the spec/observability bead authorizes it.
- No changes to closed/reopen semantics for closed -> open.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on ddx-ef9e8aec.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go reviewerDispatchProfile bumps MinPower when CountPriorReviewErrorsForSlot > 0 for the same result_rev.
2. Each subsequent review-error retry adds another ladder step until top-of-ladder.
3. A "reviewer-escalated" event is emitted on each bump.
4. TestReviewerEscalation_OnSecondTransportError, TestReviewerEscalation_OnThirdError_TopOfLadder, TestReviewerEscalation_PairingDegraded_TriggersEscalation all pass.

description:
PROBLEM
  When a reviewer invocation fails with OutcomeReviewTransport,
  OutcomeReviewContextOverflow, or returns an empty/unparseable
  body, the bead retries with the SAME reviewer model (and same
  MinPower). After DefaultReviewMaxRetries (3) the bead parks to
  status=proposed.

  This violates ADR-024 P3 (reviewer always stronger than
  implementer) under fault conditions. Repeated transport errors
  on a same-tier reviewer should bump reviewer MinPower to escape
  whatever pool/instance is failing, not retry into the same
  failing context.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go reviewerDispatchProfile
    (line ~697-736) reads MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 once,
    then dispatches; review-error retry reuses the same MinPower.
  - There is no "bump reviewer MinPower on consecutive errors"
    logic in the review dispatch path.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go reviewerDispatchProfile:
    when CountPriorReviewErrorsForSlot > 0 for the same result_rev,
    add another ladder step to MinPowerOverride.
  - On second review-error, reviewer dispatches at impl.ActualPower
    + 2 (or top-of-ladder, whichever is lower).
  - On third review-error, reviewer dispatches at top-of-ladder.
  - On fourth review-error: park to status=proposed (current
    behavior).
  - Emit "reviewer-escalated" event when a bump occurs, with the
    new MinPower and the reviewer-error count.

  Tests:
     - TestReviewerEscalation_OnSecondTransportError: assert
       MinPower bump after first transport error.
     - TestReviewerEscalation_OnThirdError_TopOfLadder: assert
       reviewer reaches top tier after two errors.
     - TestReviewerEscalation_PairingDegraded_TriggersEscalation:
       when ReviewPairingDegraded fires (impl and reviewer at same
       tier), the next reviewer dispatch bumps a tier.

NON-SCOPE
  - Reviewer dispatch concurrency (existing two-slot pattern stays).
  - Adjusting DefaultReviewMaxRetries (out of scope).
  - Implementer escalation on review_block (sister bead R1).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR-024)" P3.
  - The existing ReviewPairingDegraded event (telemetry) gains a
    causal link to escalation.
title: fix(escalation): reviewer transport/empty errors escalate reviewer tier

description:
PROBLEM
GraphQL worker dispatch has the same parallel-struct smell as REST, so fixing only the REST handler would leave another transport able to drop future execute-loop fields.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 defines a GraphQL-local request struct and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127-141 manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. It also parses worker-loop mode and idle interval separately from REST, bypassing a shared spec lifecycle and risking a stale public alias.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode rawArgs directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Preserve workers.default_spec behavior from cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:83-104 by applying defaults onto the unified spec before server-side ApplyDefaults/Validate.
- Route GraphQL through the same server-side helper used by REST so ApplyDefaults, Validate, SpecVersion handling, ProjectRoot assignment, Mode/IdleInterval semantics, and StartExecuteLoop are not reimplemented.
- Delete the GraphQL-local request struct and the parallel StartExecuteLoop literal.
- Do not accept or emit the removed polling flag alias on the GraphQL worker-dispatch path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change GraphQL schema or worker kind names beyond the rawArgs decoded into the shared spec.
- Do not change workers.max_count semantics.
- Do not change REST handler behavior; that belongs to the REST child.
notes: Removed the stale GraphQL poll field requirement. GraphQL now follows the same Mode plus IdleInterval contract as REST/server worker dispatch.
title: server: route GraphQL worker dispatch through ExecuteLoopSpec

acceptance:
1. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md explicitly lists the DDx-owned scratch prefixes and `cleanup.json` metadata rule named in this bead.
2. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines the default minimum age rule: expired/terminal metadata-backed paths may be removed, and metadata-less recognized-prefix scratch roots require mtime age >= 6 hours plus no live PID/session evidence.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines the soft cleanup trigger as below 512 MiB free bytes or below 8192 free inodes before claim, and preserves the current hard stop floor of 64 MiB and 1024 inodes.
4. docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md mirrors the same prefix, age, soft-trigger, and hard-floor values in its roots/ownership and resource-preflight sections.
5. docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md P8 references the explicit FEAT-010/SD-025 cleanup policy so future worker beads do not treat leaked DDx test/e2e scratch roots as out of scope.
6. The bead no longer carries a `needs_human` label after those values are recorded.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource spec bead was blocked because the governing docs named cleanup scope in broad terms but did not give implementers exact policy values for soft resource pressure, minimum scratch age, and recognized DDx-owned scratch prefixes. Without those choices, cleanup implementation beads can either under-clean leaked DDx roots or overreach into non-DDx temp directories.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:585-650 defines execution resource cleanup, but lines 605-608 still say `soft high-water threshold` and `hard floor` without concrete values.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:618-623 includes known DDx prefixes and minimum age/mtime rules, but it does not list the prefixes or the default age.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:224-229 and 277-288 repeat the same policy without values.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:13-16 currently defines only a last-resort hard floor of 64 MiB free bytes and 1024 free inodes.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:16-18 defines `cleanup.json` as the ownership metadata file, and cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:212-220 currently scans only entries whose names start with `ExecuteBeadWtPrefix`.

PROPOSED FIX
Update FEAT-010, SD-025, and P8 with explicit policy constants and anchors:
- Recognized DDx-owned scratch prefixes: `ddx-exec-wt`, `ddx-claim-heartbeats`, `ddx-metric-keepalive`, `ddx-test-`, `ddx-e2e-`, and any temp root containing DDx `cleanup.json` metadata with matching project ownership.
- Minimum age: metadata-backed paths may be removed when liveness is expired or the owning attempt is terminal; metadata-less recognized-prefix scratch roots require mtime age >= 6 hours and no live PID/session evidence.
- Hard floor: preserve the current hard stop floor of 64 MiB free bytes and 1024 free inodes until a config override bead changes it.
- Soft high-water cleanup trigger: run cleanup before claim when any checked temp/evidence root is below 512 MiB free bytes or below 8192 free inodes, then re-check before claiming.
- Insertion anchors: FEAT-010 `### Execution Resource Cleanup` after lines 600-608 and 618-623; SD-025 `### Roots and ownership` lines 224-229 and `### Resource preflight and loop-fatal handling` lines 285-288; reliability P8 lines 47-56.
- Remove the `needs_human` blocker once those explicit policy choices are documented.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement cleanup code changes in this spec bead.
- Do not permit deletion of arbitrary non-DDx temp directories.
- Do not change provider quota, routing, review policy, or candidate-cycle behavior.
- Do not delete complete `.ddx/runs/<id>`, complete `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>`, preserved worktrees, active registered worktrees, or `refs/ddx/iterations/...`.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-f3dfde0d. No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
  cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:61-76
  investigationRetryInitialMinPower jumps DIRECTLY to the highest
  viable MinPower when applyNoChangesSmartRetry sets
  Extra["triage.tier_hint"]==TierSmart. This is a binary jump
  (current power → top of ladder), violating ADR-024 P2 ("escalate
  on failure" — implies stepwise climb).

  Net effect: a no-changes outcome on a cheap-tier model leaps
  immediately to top tier on the next retry. If standard tier would
  have succeeded, the user pays top-tier cost unnecessarily.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2712-2735
    applyNoChangesSmartRetry: writes Extra["triage.tier_hint"]=TierSmart.
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:61-76
    investigationRetryInitialMinPower: when hint==TierSmart, returns
    the highest viable MinPower from the ladder.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go applyNoChangesSmartRetry:
    write the NEXT-tier MinPower floor (current actualPower + 1
    ladder step) into Extra["triage.tier_hint"], NOT TierSmart as a
    name.
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go
    investigationRetryInitialMinPower: read the numeric floor from
    Extra and return that, not the top of ladder.
  - When the bead has been through cheap → standard already and
    that still produced no_changes, write the standard+1 step
    (smart). When standard → smart still no_changes, status=proposed
    (genuine spec gap; operator must clarify).

  Tests:
     - TestNoChangesSmartRetry_StepwiseClimb: assert each no_changes
       failure bumps the next-attempt MinPower by exactly one ladder
       step.
     - TestNoChangesSmartRetry_TopTierExhausted_GoesToProposed:
       assert when no_changes happens at the top tier, the bead is
       parked to status=proposed (genuine spec gap, not retry).

NON-SCOPE
  - General ladder mechanics (cli/internal/agent/escalation/).
  - Refactoring the smart-retry hint format.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR-024)" P2 principle.
title: fix(escalation): no_changes smart retry climbs ladder one step at a time

description:
PROBLEM
The DDx prose checker needs Vale-backed rules for unsupported claims, AI-slop polish, filler, missing actor/action, repeated openings, token cost, and vocabulary. Without tuned rules, `ddx doc prose` cannot improve documentation clarity or token cost reliably.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:85-107 defines the initial rule pack and implementation notes, but the Vale style files and corpus golden cases are not implemented.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Rule semantics are high-judgment work. The bead must balance catching common model-generated prose against preserving precise technical text, commands, IDs, tables, and legitimate density.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add Vale style files for the initial DDx rule families.
- Add corpus golden cases for unsupported claims, AI slop, filler transitions, missing actor/action, repeated openings, token-cost candidates, and vocabulary substitution.
- Use Vale scopes/token ignores for Markdown structure.
- Keep raw Vale messages short and leave DDx rationale/suggested edits to normalization.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not switch `ddx doc prose` to Vale yet.
- Do not wire workflow hooks.
- Do not make findings blocking.

DEPS
Depends on the corpus harness so rule behavior is measured against positives and negatives.
title: docprose: port initial rules to Vale styles

description:
PROBLEM
The core queue queries still implement lifecycle policy with local conditionals: ready filtering excludes lifecycle labels, blocked means unsatisfied dependencies, status counts only cover a subset of statuses, and needs-human has a dedicated query lane. That directly conflicts with the new status-owned lifecycle contract.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1346-1355 exposes Ready and ReadyExecution separately from the new lifecycle contract.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1358-1377 implements NeedsHuman as open plus LabelNeedsHuman.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1379-1452 has ReadyExecutionBreakdown fields for SkippedNeedsInvestigation and filters LabelNeedsInvestigation/LabelNeedsHuman/no-changes triage labels.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1548-1573 defines Blocked as open beads with unclosed deps.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1575-1719 classifies dependency waits, status=blocked, needs_human, needs_investigation, cooldowns, ineligible, superseded, and epics in one store-specific function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1804-1867 counts Open/Closed/Ready/Blocked/NeedsHuman but not the six lifecycle statuses.

PROPOSED FIX
Refactor store ready, execution-ready, dependency-waiting, externally-blocked, operator-attention, and status-summary queries to call the pure lifecycle state machine. Ready/work eligibility must be status=open with satisfied dependencies, no active claim, and no cooldown/ineligible/superseded/epic-exclusion facts. Dependency waiting must remain status=open and appear as a derived waiting bucket, not status=blocked. status=blocked must represent external recheckable blockers only. status=proposed replaces NeedsHuman for operator-attention queries. closed satisfies dependents; cancelled does not.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change agent parking paths or API/UI names in this bead except where store tests require updated helper names. Agent/API/UI integration beads consume the new store behavior.

DEPS
Depends on the pure lifecycle core and storage status preservation.
title: bead: route ready and blocked queries through lifecycle core

description:
PROBLEM
The escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) was added in S10_2 but has no production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. Each bead currently gets exactly one singleTierAttempt without any rung escalation on substantive failure. The existing tier→profile mutation in the loop is an ad-hoc workaround that should be replaced by the ladder.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: singleTierAttempt is called exactly once per bead iteration; there is no loop over ladder rungs.
- cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30: Ladder.Next is defined but has no call site in execute_bead_loop.go or any other production file (confirmed by dead-code audit ddx-09d2990c).
- The existing tier→profile mutation (a manual MinPower bump) in the executor closure is the only escalation behavior; it is not driven by the ladder contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the single singleTierAttempt call in execute_bead_loop.go with a real loop that:
  1. Calls singleTierAttempt with current rung's MinPower.
  2. On substantive failure (non-park, non-budget-exceeded), calls ladder.Next to get next rung.
  3. Terminates when ladder.Next returns no further rungs or a stop condition triggers.
- Remove the existing tier→profile mutation; ladder.Next drives all MinPower escalation.
- Pass Role='implementer' and CorrelationID='<bead_id>:<attempt_id>' on every ExecuteRequest.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the ladder definition or rung configuration (S10_2 owns that).
- Cost-cap relocation (belongs to C9 / ddx-9228a484).

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/bead/store.go SetExecutionCooldown writes execute-loop-cooldown-base-rev alongside execute-loop-retry-after.
2. cli/internal/bead/store.go activeRetryCooldown checks the base-rev: if origin/main HEAD has advanced past the recorded base-rev, returns active=false even if wall-clock has not expired.
3. The current origin HEAD is read once per ready-queue evaluation pass (cached, not per-bead).
4. TestActiveRetryCooldown_RevBoundAutoClears asserts cooldown clears when HEAD advances.
5. TestActiveRetryCooldown_SameRevStillActive asserts cooldown stays active when HEAD has not advanced.
6. TestActiveRetryCooldown_NoBaseRevFallsBackToWallClock asserts legacy cooldowns without base-rev still work.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Cooldowns survive their cause. When a code fix lands on origin
  that resolves the cause of a cooldown (e.g., a data race fix that
  was blocking pre-push tests), affected cooldowns remain active
  until wall-clock expiry — typically hours after the fix is
  available. Drain idles even though the world has moved on.

  Sister bead R1 removes push_failed cooldowns entirely (the most
  egregious case). This bead handles the GENERAL pattern: when
  origin/main advances past the rev at which a cooldown was set,
  the cooldown's "world-state" is stale and should auto-clear.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1524-1533 activeRetryCooldown is
    purely wall-clock: parses execute-loop-retry-after, returns
    retryAfter.After(now).
  - SetExecutionCooldown does not record the base-rev at which the
    cooldown was set.
  - There is no auto-invalidation hook anywhere.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Extend SetExecutionCooldown to record the base-rev:
     - cli/internal/bead/store.go: SetExecutionCooldown gains a
       baseRev parameter; writes execute-loop-cooldown-base-rev to
       Extra alongside execute-loop-retry-after.
     - All callers updated to pass current main HEAD or attempt
       base-rev.

  2. Extend activeRetryCooldown to check rev advance:
     - If execute-loop-cooldown-base-rev is set AND current
       origin/main HEAD has advanced past that rev, return
       active=false (cooldown auto-cleared).
     - "Advanced past" means: current HEAD is a descendant of the
       cooldown base-rev OR current HEAD != cooldown base-rev (use
       git merge-base or simple rev equality, depending on
       implementation cost).
     - Cache the current origin HEAD per ready-queue evaluation pass
       to avoid one git read per bead.

  3. Document the contract:
     - Add comment at activeRetryCooldown explaining the rev-bound
       semantics.
     - Reference TD-031 §5 invariant.

  Tests:
     - TestActiveRetryCooldown_RevBoundAutoClears: set cooldown at
       rev A, advance HEAD to rev B (descendant), assert
       activeRetryCooldown returns false.
     - TestActiveRetryCooldown_SameRevStillActive: set cooldown at
       rev A, leave HEAD at rev A, assert active=true.
     - TestActiveRetryCooldown_NoBaseRevFallsBackToWallClock:
       legacy cooldowns without base-rev still work via wall-clock
       only (backward-compat).

NON-SCOPE
  - Hooks for arbitrary "cause resolved" detection beyond rev-advance
    (e.g., specific test now passing). Out of scope; rev-advance is
    the simple proxy.
  - Migrating existing cooldowns to record base-rev (they expire on
    wall-clock; new ones get the new field).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead R1 (push_failed no cooldown) reduces the impact of
    this bead but does not eliminate the need: other cooldowns
    (no_changes, no_viable_provider, etc.) still benefit from
    rev-bound auto-clear when applicable.
  - Sister bead R2 (bulk clear-cooldowns) is the manual escape
    valve; this is the automatic version.
title: feat(bead): rev-bound auto-invalidation for push_failed cooldowns

description:
Decomposed from REACH-BACKFILL (ddx-83440482). The Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability) flagged 1 symbol(s) in package `internal/registry` as unreachable from cli/ entry roots (deadcode RTA).

Symbols:
- internal/registry/manifest.go:126 — MarshalPackage

For each: WIRE (add to production call graph) or DELETE (genuinely obsolete). If neither is clear within ~15 min, annotate `// wiring:pending <follow-up-bead-id>` and file a follow-up bead.

description:
PROBLEM
  ddx try must invoke bead-quality lint after selecting a candidate and before claiming it, but the execute-bead loop currently has no lint hook invocation or warn/block policy at that point.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:640-714 runs PreClaimHook and RoutePreflight before Store.Claim. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:668-710 is the current pre-claim RoutePreflight gate; cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:714 calls Store.Claim. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:29 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreDispatchLintHook from closed contract bead ddx-c8dc1146, but no loop path invokes it and no path emits bead-quality.lint events.

PROPOSED FIX
  - In cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go, invoke runtime.PreDispatchLintHook(ctx, candidate.ID) after RoutePreflight succeeds and before Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee).
  - Default behavior is WARN-ONLY: append a bead event kind bead-quality.lint containing score, rationale, suggested_fixes, waivers_applied, and any warning details, then continue to claim regardless of score.
  - Add opt-in BLOCK behavior from resolved config key bead-quality.lint.block_threshold_score. Only a valid hook response with Score below threshold refuses dispatch; infrastructure errors fail open.
  - On block, emit operator-readable log/loop event text citing bead-lifecycle SKILL.md guidance and skip claiming that bead. The skipped bead must be marked attempted for this Run so the loop moves on instead of spinning on the same candidate.
  - Treat hook errors, missing skill/harness, bad JSON surfaced through the hook, and timeouts as warning events and proceed with claim.

NON-SCOPE
  - Defining hook/result types; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.
  - Implementing the runner-backed lint hook body; covered by ddx-68706ef9.
  - Post-attempt triage and OutcomeReason handling; covered by ddx-4375292b.
  - ddx work integration.

PARENT
  ddx-e1a576a7.

DEPS
  No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and its hook contract is available in cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.
title: agent: invoke pre-dispatch lint hook before claim

description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/config/types.go:245 defines a RetainDays field on
  ExecutionsConfig, but no code reads or acts on it. The cleanup
  manager scans .ddx/executions/ via scanCompleteEvidenceDirs
  (line 453) but never deletes. Additionally, .ddx/agent-logs/
  (~860 MB local) and .ddx/workers/ (~34 MB) accumulate without
  bound — they're per-machine local state with no retention.

  After sister bead "docs(ADR): formalize 3-tier evidence
  storage" defines the 3-tier architecture, this bead implements
  the local-tier retention policy: prune all three local-tier
  paths (executions/, agent-logs/, workers/) together on the
  same retention window.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/config/types.go:245 ExecutionsConfig.RetainDays
    declared, never read.
  - .ddx/executions/: 3,427 dirs, ~1.4 GB.
  - .ddx/agent-logs/: 2,365 files, ~860 MB. Gitignored
    (.gitignore:421). Local-only.
  - .ddx/workers/: 311 dirs, ~34 MB.
  - ExecutionCleanupManager.Cleanup runs every ~10 minutes
    during drain (cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go:13)
    and on drain startup. Today it scans but never prunes.

PROPOSED FIX
  Extend ExecutionCleanupManager.Cleanup to prune all three
  local-tier paths on retention expiry:

  1. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go: implement
     pruneOlderThan helper. For each of
     .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/, .ddx/agent-logs/<file>.jsonl,
     .ddx/workers/worker-<id>/ — delete if mtime is older than
     ExecutionsConfig.RetainDays days.

  2. Skip active items:
     - executions: skip if attempt_id is referenced by an
       active run-state file (cli/internal/agent/run_state*.go).
     - agent-logs: skip if the session_id is referenced by an
       active worker.
     - workers: skip if worker is still alive (PID check).

  3. Default RetainDays in cli/internal/config/loader.go (or
     similar): 90 days. Per ADR principle P2. Operators can
     override via .ddx.yml.

  4. RetainDays=0 disables pruning (existing legacy behavior).

  5. ExecutionCleanupSummary gains counters:
     RemovedExecutionDirs, RemovedAgentLogs, RemovedWorkerDirs.
     Emit each in the cleanup log line.

  6. CRITICAL ORDERING: this bead DEPENDS on sister bead
     "feat(metrics): extract per-attempt rows" landing first.
     Pruning local evidence is only safe after metrics rows
     have been extracted into .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.

  Tests:
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldDirs (executions)
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldAgentLogs (agent-logs)
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldWorkerDirs (workers)
     - TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveItems (all three)
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DefaultRetainDays90
     - TestExecutionCleanup_RetainDaysZero_DisablesRetention
     - TestExecutionCleanup_RecordsCounts (all three counters)

NON-SCOPE
  - The mirror backend (sister bead).
  - Gitignore flip (sister bead B30).
  - Metrics extraction (sister bead).
  - One-shot bulk cleanup of existing dirs (operator can run
    this manually before this bead lands, or rely on the
    automated pass to gradually catch up).

INTERSECTIONS
  - DEPENDS ON: sister bead "feat(metrics): extract per-attempt
    rows" — metrics must be captured before evidence is pruned.
  - Sister bead B30 (gitignore flip): after this bead bounds
    in-tree dir count, B30 completes the cleanup by un-tracking.
  - Sister bead B31 (mirror): mirror runs BEFORE pruning so
    archived copies exist before delete.
title: feat(executions): wire RetainDays config to ExecutionCleanupManager prune

description:
PROBLEM
ADR-024 requires default adversarial pre-close review to use two independent reviewer invocations, but the candidate-cycle refactor intentionally starts with transitional single-slot review to stabilize the pipeline. Once single-slot review/repair is correct, default two-slot candidate review must replace the transitional path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:283-305 wraps a plain BeadReviewer into a one-slot ReviewGroupResult when the reviewer does not implement reviewGroupReviewer.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:307-352 reduces review groups conservatively, but default production dispatch does not yet guarantee two independent slots.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:109-145 requires two independent reviewer invocations and unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE to permit close.

PROPOSED FIX
- Enable two reviewer slots by default in the candidate-cycle reviewer.
- Stamp shared review_group_id and distinct reviewer_index correlation facts.
- Aggregate conservatively: unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE required; any evidenced non-approve enters classification; malformed/empty/transport failures consume per-candidate, per-slot review retry budget.
- Remove the transitional single-slot exception from docs if this bead completes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the same-worktree repair loop semantics except to feed aggregate review results into the existing classification step.
- Do not force different concrete providers; same-provider review emits pairing-degraded evidence only.

DEPS
Depends on stable single-slot pre-land review/repair and routing power floors.
title: review: enable default two-slot candidate review group

acceptance:
1. Add a focused test such as `TestExecuteBeadWorker_ProviderTimeoutRetriesWithRouteExclusion` proving a service attempt that fails with a provider connectivity timeout records structured route failure evidence and retries without selecting the same provider/model tuple when an alternate eligible route exists.
2. Add or update routing-intent evidence tests so `fallback_chain` or an equivalent field records the failed provider/model and the subsequent route choice.
3. The retry preserves operator intent: no `--harness`, `--provider`, or `--model` pins are invented, and explicit operator pins still win and do not silently route elsewhere.
4. The implementation does not hardcode Fizeau policy names or provider names; it consumes route metadata/constraints exposed by Fizeau or local structured failure evidence.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorker_ProviderTimeoutRetriesWithRouteExclusion|Test.*RoutingIntent|Test.*RouteFailure" -count=1` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A no-flag `ddx work` run now reaches implementation, but Fizeau repeatedly selected `fiz/bragi/qwen3.5-27b` under `policy=cheap` even though actual chat calls to `http://bragi:1234/v1/chat/completions` timed out. The worker spent about 2m45s retrying the same unreachable endpoint and ended `ddx-b4c5151a` with `execution_failed`, while the route event recorded `fallback_chain:[]`. This preserves forward progress compared with lifecycle parking, but it still wastes attempts and does not adapt away from a provider that just failed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:241-258 sends the policy/profile request to Fizeau and correctly leaves concrete provider selection to Fizeau, but DDx does not add any negative constraint or route-failure feedback after the selected provider fails.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2140-2209 records the failed attempt and moves on/cools down, but it does not trigger an immediate route retry that excludes the just-failed provider or escalates within the same policy after provider connectivity failure.
- cli/internal/agent/profile_select.go:451-470 chooses the policy/profile only; it has no path for using recent route-failure evidence when asking Fizeau for the next route.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a route-failure feedback path for provider connectivity failures. When a service-backed attempt fails before producing agent output because the selected provider endpoint timed out or was unreachable, DDx should record structured route failure evidence and retry the same bead with the same operator intent while avoiding the exact failed route when Fizeau supports that constraint, or by raising the fallback floor/policy only after the cheap/free route has actually failed. The retry must not hardcode provider names or policy names.

NON-SCOPE
Do not bypass Fizeau for normal provider/model selection. Do not hardcode `bragi`, `cheap`, `default`, or local provider tier names. Do not mark local providers unusable globally based on a single attempt without an expiry. Do not route directly to paid providers when another free/local eligible candidate can be tried.

OBSERVED EVIDENCE
- Worker route: `do route fiz/qwen3.5-27b provider=bragi reason=policy=cheap; score=105.0`.
- Provider failures: repeated `dial tcp 100.127.38.115:1234: i/o timeout`.
- Bead event for `ddx-b4c5151a`: `routing ... provider=bragi model=qwen3.5-27b fallback_chain:[]` followed by `execute-bead ... outcome_reason=timeout`.
title: routing: avoid repeating a failing auto-routed provider

description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource contract needs an end-to-end regression harness, not only isolated unit tests. The May 6 failure crossed command, git worktree, tracker claim, resource classification, and operator-output boundaries: a unit-only suite could still miss the exact behavior where ddx work claims the next bead after ENOSPC.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:686-718 owns real git worktree setup/cleanup, while cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 owns queue drain loop state; existing tests cover many individual branches but not the full resource-exhausted incident path.
- cli/cmd/try.go:306-342 and cli/cmd/work.go:101 route command contexts into workers, but there is no command-level e2e test proving cleanup/resource failures produce correct operator output and tracker state.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:95-145 tests orphan recovery shapes, but not full DDx temp root cleanup plus registered worktree pruning plus claim safety.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:449-498 requires cleanup to be inline, loop-level, background, observable, and loop-fatal on resource exhaustion.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add end-to-end tests that use a temp project, temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, real git worktree commands where feasible, and injectable resource-check failures where filling a filesystem would be unsafe.
- Verify tracker state after failure: no extra bead claimed, failed bead open/re-claimable, no retry-after cooldown for host resource exhaustion.
- Verify cleanup state after failure: stale unregistered dirs and stale registered worktrees are removed; active/preserved evidence survives.
- Verify operator output and loop events contain cleanup summary and resource_exhausted stop reason.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not create a real ENOSPC by filling /tmp or exhausting inodes in CI.
- Do not require live harness/model/provider calls.
- Do not test unrelated production-reachability bead content.

4. Invalid label (unknown tier name) falls back to default with a warning event.

description:
PROBLEM
  Today every bead starts at the configured global MinPower (or
  operator override). A 3-line typo fix and a 500-line refactor
  both start at the cheap tier, even when the bead title or
  description make it obvious the cheap tier will fail.

  ADR-024 P1 (cheapest first) is correct as a default, but cost
  optimization includes "use the right cheap" — when the operator
  knows a bead is hard, starting one ladder step up avoids a
  guaranteed-failed cheap attempt.

  Per ADR-024 Operator escape hatches subsection (sister bead
  ddx-451cdead), operators should be able to hint difficulty per
  bead via a label.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go investigationRetryInitialMinPower
    handles tier-hint reads but only for no_changes smart retry.
  - There's no general-purpose "start at this floor" hint
    mechanism for fresh beads.
  - The existing budget:<USD> label (sister bead ddx-8645c5d4)
    is the structural template; this bead adds a parallel
    tier:hint=<NAME> label.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add label parsing for tier:hint=<name> where <name> is one of:
    cheap, standard, smart, premium (or whatever ladder tier
    names are configured).
  - When a fresh bead is claimed, parse the label; if present,
    set the initial MinPower floor to that tier's MinPower.
  - If no label, use the configured default (current behavior).
  - If label is present but invalid (unknown tier name), log a
    warning and fall back to the default.
  - Compose with --min-power flag: the label sets a floor; the
    flag can raise it further but cannot lower below the label.
  - Document in CLAUDE.md (or operator docs): tier:hint=<name>
    label semantics.

  Tests:
     - TestTierHint_StartsAtLabelFloor
     - TestTierHint_NoLabel_UsesDefault
     - TestTierHint_InvalidLabel_FallsBackToDefault
     - TestTierHint_FlagRaisesFloorAboveLabel

NON-SCOPE
  - Auto-detection of bead difficulty from content (separate
    concern; this is operator-hint-only).
  - Tier-name validation against the configured ladder (operator
    is responsible for matching configured tier names).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-451cdead (ADR-024 amendment) Operator escape
    hatches subsection — this bead implements the tier-hint label.
  - Sister bead ddx-cf79f10c (no_changes stepwise) — uses the
    same hint mechanism for retries; this bead generalizes it
    to fresh claims.
title: feat(routing): bead-difficulty hint label sets initial ladder floor

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Context is king · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go changes live under cli/ · use focused unit tests with real store/in-memory stubs · run targeted go test plus lefthook · preserve tracker mutations through ddx bead commands</practices>
<governing>FEAT-010 and TD-031 require rejected readiness rewrites to park with needs_human instead of returning to the execution-ready lane. Rejected rewrite evidence must explain why preservation could not be proven.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
When readiness returns actionable_but_rewritten but the rewrite validator rejects the proposed mutation, ddx work logs a blocked outcome and unclaims the bead without parking it. In continuous mode, the same bead can be selected again immediately, producing a loop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:767-783 handles `PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten` validation errors by emitting `pre_claim_intake.blocked`, calling `Unclaim`, and continuing. It does not call `parkBeadPostIntakeRejection`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:798-821 correctly parks other terminal non-actionable intake outcomes with `needs_human`; the unsafe rewrite rejection branch diverged from that policy.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go:383-433 verifies unsafe rewrites do not execute, but does not assert `needs_human`, `intake.blocked` bead evidence, or removal from `ReadyExecution`.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1508-1511 already excludes `needs_human` beads from `ReadyExecution`, so parking is sufficient to stop the loop.

PROPOSED FIX
- In the unsafe rewrite rejection branch, call `parkBeadPostIntakeRejection` with outcome `ambiguous_needs_human` and the validator detail before unclaiming.
- Change operator wording from `releasing` to `parking for human review` or equivalent.
- Preserve the validator failure detail in the event stream and bead evidence.
- Strengthen tests so rejected rewrites are proven to be open/unclaimed, labeled `needs_human`, recorded with `intake.blocked`, and absent from `ReadyExecution`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the validated replacement rewrite policy; that is ddx-a41e6fbb.
- Do not change readiness routing or model selection.
- Do not change generic ambiguous_needs_human parking semantics outside this branch.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cd555c0. Depends on ddx-5cd555c0 so the spec-evolution bead lands first.

description:
PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.
title: agent: wrap artifact bodies with untrusted-data delimiters

| ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt: - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)" - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)" - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5" - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate - "CHANGELOG entry merged" Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed, and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses. acceptance:
1. Import and backend code preserve proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled status values without defaulting valid values to open.
2. Unknown legacy pseudo-status values such as needs_human and needs_investigation are rejected in normal import/update paths with a clear error that points to the one-way lifecycle migrator; only the lifecycle migrator may convert them.
3. TestImportPreservesAllLifecycleStatuses covers all six statuses.
4. TestImportRejectsLegacyPseudoStatusesOutsideLifecycleMigrator covers needs_human and needs_investigation pseudo-status inputs.
5. TestChaosAllowedStatusesIncludesSixLifecycleValues covers chaos/property fixtures with proposed, blocked, and cancelled included.
6. TestAxonBackend_ListReadyBlocked is updated only as needed to preserve statuses while leaving query semantic changes to the store bead.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestImport.*Lifecycle|TestImportRejectsLegacy|TestChaosAllowedStatuses|TestAxonBackend_ListReadyBlocked|TestSchema|TestSync' -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Storage, import, backend, and schema-adjacent paths do not consistently preserve or validate the six upstream lifecycle statuses. Some paths only recognize open, in_progress, and closed, so proposed/blocked/cancelled can be lost or treated as open.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/import.go:262-263 preserves only open, in_progress, and closed and otherwise defaults to open.
- cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go uses allowed statuses that exclude proposed, blocked, and cancelled.
- cli/internal/bead/types.go:59-75 has the six constants but comments and validation are not governed by the new state-machine contract.
- cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go:TestAxonBackend_ListReadyBlocked currently treats ready/blocked through old store semantics.
- cli/internal/bead/sync_test.go:238 still says non-status needs_investigation should be encoded as label/event, which must be reframed as legacy migration metadata.

PROPOSED FIX
Wire storage-facing validation and import/export/backend preservation to the pure lifecycle status set. Proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled must round-trip without being silently rewritten. Unknown historical pseudo-status values such as needs_investigation must route through explicit compatibility/migration handling rather than becoming active lifecycle values. Update schema/sync tests that assert the status vocabulary.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change readiness/blocked queue derivation in this bead except where backend tests need status preservation fixtures. Store query behavior is owned by the store wiring bead.

DEPS
Depends on the pure lifecycle core.
title: storage: preserve six lifecycle statuses across import and backends

At minimum: TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn, TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding, TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead, TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop, TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2 (matches ddx-c8f79963 / try.Attempt disposition contract).

Each row of the audit names the test function and the disposition.

This bead's full retirement step inherits those lifts as prerequisites.
description:
PROBLEM
All 21 ddx agent subcommands remain live in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85 — none are Hidden, none are Deprecated. The migration to ddx run/try/work was supposed to happen but only ddx work was added. Operators still use ddx agent surface, and skill manifests + docs still reference ddx agent.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:67-85: 21 cobra command registrations for ddx agent subcommands (run, execute-bead, execute-loop, benchmark, condense, capabilities, replay, list, doctor, log, etc.) are all live. None set Hidden=true or Deprecated.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: execute-loop and execute-bead are aliases; after C13 (ddx-387a0178), ddx work and ddx try replace these.
- .agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, skills/: skill manifests reference 'ddx agent run' and 'ddx agent execute-bead'.
- docs/: README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website/content/docs/cli/_index.md reference ddx agent.
- Depends on C13 (ddx-387a0178) which adds ddx try CLI — cannot retire ddx agent until ddx try exists.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Delete cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and all cli/cmd/agent_*.go sibling files.
2. Verify: ddx run + ddx try + ddx work cover every prior ddx agent operation:
   - ddx agent run → ddx run
   - ddx agent execute-loop → ddx work
   - ddx agent execute-bead → ddx try
   - ddx agent list → ddx agent list removed (or migrated to ddx harness list)
   - ddx agent doctor → ddx doctor
   - ddx agent log → ddx log (or removed)
3. Update skill manifests to use new commands.
4. Update docs (README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, website CLI docs).
5. Update lefthook hooks if any reference ddx agent.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding new flags or subcommands to ddx run/try/work (out of scope).
- Deprecation period (user directive: 'completely gone').
notes:
Re-sequenced under ddx-f088a3fd after operator decision: remove, do not deprecate. Previous deps on alias-preserving ddx-387a0178/ddx-3ee4ddcf were removed because those beads conflict with the no-alias plan. This bead now follows the spec ratification bead ddx-424fdac4.

Closed-bead audit 2026-05-12: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go still registers the ddx agent namespace and subcommands, and tests still exercise agent execute-bead. The bead acceptance requiring removal of cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go and cli/cmd/agent_*.go is not satisfied.

description:
CONTEXT
The first executable surface for Prose Quality Support is an advisory command over changed Markdown files. This should give users value without blocking ddx work, ddx bead review, or ddx doc validate by default.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/cmd/doc*.go or existing doc command files
- cli/internal/docprose/ checker integration code
- config schema/types for prose settings if not already landed
- command tests under cli/cmd and checker tests under cli/internal/docprose

COMMAND BEHAVIOR
- Add `ddx doc prose --changed`.
- Optionally support explicit paths if TD-036 includes them, but changed Markdown is required.
- Findings include file, line, rule id, severity, rationale, and suggested edit.
- Exit behavior is advisory by default; do not break unrelated workflows on findings.
- Respect project config for mode, vocabulary, includes/excludes, and severity threshold.
- If an external checker is selected by TD-036 and missing, emit a clear advisory infrastructure message per the TD rather than panicking.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Bead review integration.
- CI blocking policy.
- AI-authorship scoring.

description:
PROBLEM
The downgrade-loop failure needs an end-to-end regression guard. Unit tests for individual helpers are not enough; a future change could still reintroduce an auto-downgrade across two ddx work/watch passes after operator promotion.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1037-1041 documents that terminal non-actionable readiness outcomes unclaim and park so the bead does not reappear in ReadyExecution until operator review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 implements that park path. Without a full loop test, a promoted open bead can be parked again by the same readiness result.
- cli/cmd/agent_run_passthrough_test.go and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop*_test.go contain readiness/proposed assertions today, but no test proves proposed -> open operator acceptance survives repeated worker passes for the same finding while hard manual-required cases remain parked.

PROPOSED FIX
Add regression coverage that exercises the real loop shape:
- Create or seed an open bead.
- Simulate/readiness-return a parkable finding and assert first pass parks to proposed with evidence.
- Promote proposed -> open through the supported bead lifecycle path.
- Run at least two additional worker/readiness passes with the same finding and assert the bead does not re-enter proposed.
- Run a separate hard manual-required scenario that must still park to proposed, so continuous-forward-progress does not become agent guessing.

NON-SCOPE
- No new production policy beyond what ddx-91898f19, ddx-cd69c24, and ddx-78ab5d61 implement.
- No live Fizeau or external model calls; use existing test runner/hook stubs.
- No changes to unrelated post-land review tests except updating expected event bodies if needed.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on ddx-91898f19, ddx-cd69c24, and ddx-78ab5d61.
title: agent: add downgrade-loop regression coverage

acceptance:
1. TD-031 contains the authoritative status-owned state machine, including allowed transitions, derived queue buckets, dependency-satisfaction rules, and the rule that labels/events/Extra explain state but do not control lifecycle.
2. FEAT-004, SD-004, FEAT-010, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, docs/triage/decomposition.md, FEAT-008, FEAT-006, and TD-024 no longer describe needs_human or triage:needs-investigation as active queue states.
3. The docs explicitly say dependency waiting stays status=open and is derived from unsatisfied deps; status=blocked is reserved for rare external recheckable blockers.
4. The docs explicitly say proposed is the operator-intervention lifecycle state and replaces needs_human for active routing.
5. The docs explicitly say cancelled is materially different from closed because cancelled does not satisfy dependents unless another explicit dependency policy later says otherwise.
6. `rg -n "needs_human|needs-human|needs investigation|needs-investigation|triage:needs-investigation" docs/helix docs/triage` returns only legacy/migration/backcompat mentions that are locally labeled as such.
7. `rg -n "blocked.*depend|depend.*blocked" docs/helix docs/triage` shows dependency waiting described as derived waiting, not status=blocked.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/graphql/...` passes as a regression gate.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The governing docs do not define a single authoritative bead lifecycle. They describe upstream status values, derived ready/blocked queues, needs_human labels, needs-investigation labels, cooldowns, and no-changes outcomes as overlapping lifecycle controls. Downstream code and operators cannot know which field owns the bead's current state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:102 names the upstream status enum, but docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:173,226,253,272,289,367,420 still route lifecycle through no_changes_needs_investigation and needs_human labels.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:39-41 says ready excludes needs_human and status includes a needs_human subcount, while docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:80 calls needs_human an open-label lane.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-004-beads-tracker.md:187-224 defines Blocked as open beads with unsatisfied deps instead of external blockers only.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:190,408,430,463,489 park execution failures as needs_human.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:22-23,73,110 defines needs-human escalation as a queue state.

PROPOSED FIX
Edit the governing specs so status is the sole lifecycle field owned by DDx. Define the six statuses exactly: proposed = operator decision required and not autonomous-work eligible; open = accepted active work, including dependency-waiting work; in_progress = claimed/running; blocked = accepted work paused by an external, recheckable blocker; closed = terminal satisfied and satisfies dependents; cancelled = terminal not-doing and does not satisfy dependents. Dependency waits are derived from dependencies, not status=blocked. Replace active needs_human and triage:needs-investigation lifecycle language with status=proposed, keeping old labels only as legacy metadata/migration terms where explicitly marked. Update TD-031 first, then FEAT-004, SD-004, FEAT-010, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, docs/triage/decomposition.md, FEAT-008, FEAT-006, and TD-024 where their wording depends on the old labels.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Go, GraphQL, Svelte, or skill files in this bead. Do not add a new TD; this is a contract correction across existing governing artifacts.

DEPS
No implementation deps. This bead is the contract input for all code changes in this epic.

description:
PROBLEM
The REST worker start endpoint drops execution fields because it decodes into a handler-local request struct that is narrower than the CLI/runtime spec.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 defines a private request struct missing opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power, from_rev, and spec_version. cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 then manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, creating another silent omission point.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Resolve ProjectRoot using existing resolveRequestedProject behavior, write it back to the spec, then call ApplyDefaults and Validate before StartExecuteLoop.
- Add a shared server-side start helper if needed so REST and GraphQL use the same ApplyDefaults -> Validate -> StartExecuteLoop lifecycle.
- Return HTTP 400 for unsupported future SpecVersion with a JSON capability list; keep unknown JSON fields tolerated for newer-server/older-client compatibility.
- Preserve existing 403 trust check and 422 unknown project behavior.

NON-SCOPE

- Do not modify GraphQL dispatch; a separate child owns that parallel path.
