acceptance:
1. Audit report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/parallel-struct-audit.md exists, covers at minimum: ddx agent run (CLI + REST + MCP variants), ddx work passthrough behavior, GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (and any other GraphQL adapters), server worker variants, ddx bead create.
2. For each command: programmatically-generated field tables for each layer (cobra / REST request struct / GraphQL adapter / spec / consumer); diff between layers; explicit drop count per command.
3. Drop-risk score (count of fields dropped between any two layers) explicit per command.
4. Summary table at top of report.
5. For each high-drop-risk command: a follow-up bead filed with ExecuteLoopSpec refactor as template; bead ID listed in the report.
6. For commands judged clean: explicit rationale ("only 1 layer" or "fields collapse via X" — not just "looks fine").
7. ddx try and ddx run, if not shipped, recorded as N/A with one-line rationale; not chased.
8. Report committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ and bead closed once review confirms coverage.
description:
The ExecuteLoopSpec unification (ddx-29058e2a) addresses one instance of the parallel-struct smell. The same anti-pattern likely exists in other ddx commands where flags travel cobra → function param → loose map → HTTP request struct → spec struct → consumer. Find them and either:
- File unification beads following the ExecuteLoopSpec pattern, OR
- Document them as already-clean (and why).

COMMANDS TO AUDIT (per codex review — expanded)

1. ddx agent run (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go ~395-414 cobra flags) PLUS its server endpoint /api/agent/run (cli/internal/server/server.go:2150-2188) AND its MCP equivalent (cli/internal/server/server.go:4537-4553). The server endpoint accepts only Harness/Prompt/Model/Effort while cobra has many more flags — same drop risk as ExecuteLoop, narrower fields.
2. ddx work passthrough (alias for ddx agent execute-loop with implicit OpaquePassthrough — covered by ddx-29058e2a but verify the implicit behavior is documented in the spec)
3. GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137) — covered by ddx-29058e2a explicitly, but audit any OTHER GraphQL adapters with similar patterns
4. Server-side worker variants in cli/internal/server/workers.go — anything else that takes a Spec struct
5. ddx bead create / update — flag set is small but check whether server endpoint mirrors
6. ddx server commands (ddx server workers list/show/log) — read-only, less risk but verify
7. ddx try (cli/cmd/try.go) — SHIPPED. INCLUDE in audit. The runTry function constructs config.CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime in parallel to runWork's runAgentExecuteLoopImpl (see comment at try.go:155 acknowledging the duplication). Audit whether the same parallel-struct smell that plagues ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec also exists at the cobra-→-spec boundary for try, and if so file a follow-up unification bead. (Stale 2026-05-04 note "ddx try NOT shipped" was corrected 2026-05-07.)
8. ddx run (cli/cmd/run.go) — SHIPPED. INCLUDE in audit. Layer-1 invocation primitive; flags forward to Fizeau Execute. Lower drop-risk than try/work but verify cobra → service request shape parity.

AUDIT METHOD (per codex — generated diff, not gut-feel)

For each command:
1. Enumerate cobra flag list (programmatic: parse cobra command struct or grep for `cmd.Flags().*Var`).
2. Enumerate corresponding server JSON request struct fields (programmatic: AST parse the request struct in handler).
3. Enumerate the spec/persisted struct fields (programmatic).
4. Enumerate the final consumer fields (programmatic).
5. Diff each layer's field set; classify each field as: execution / control-plane / output-only / unsupported / dropped.
6. Drop-risk score: count of fields that exist in cobra but NOT in subsequent layers.

OUTPUT

Report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/parallel-struct-audit.md:
- One section per command audited
- For each: layer count, list of fields per layer, classification table, drop-risk score, recommendation (unify / clean / N/A)
- Summary table: command | layer count | drop-risk count | recommendation

If unification candidates found, file follow-up beads using the ExecuteLoopSpec refactor (ddx-29058e2a) as the template.

DEP CHANGE (per codex)
- DEP on ddx-29058e2a is RELAXED to "may begin read-only command discovery + diff generation in parallel; only the implementation-pattern recommendations need ddx-29058e2a as reference." Update dep semantics so this can start now.
title: audit: parallel-struct smell — find other ddx commands with multi-layer flag wiring

acceptance:
1. Execute-loop calls the review-group pre-close gate before CloseWithEvidence for close-eligible implementation results.
2. TestPreCloseReview_UnanimousApproveCloses verifies both evidence-backed APPROVE results are required to close.
3. TestPreCloseReview_ApproveWithoutEvidenceIsReviewError verifies evidence-free APPROVE does not close and records review_error: unparseable.
4. TestPreCloseReview_RequestChangesPreventsClose verifies REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK leave the bead unclosed and do not invoke Reopen.
5. Stale reopen assertions in execute_bead_review_test.go are updated to pre-close blocking semantics.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreCloseReview" -count=1 passes.
7. TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded either passes or is confirmed pre-existing via git-log evidence documented in the commit message.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After review-group dispatch exists, execute-loop must use it as a pre-close gate. Today review REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK semantics are still expressed as reopen behavior rather than preventing close.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:294 exposes CloseWithEvidence without a required pre-close review decision boundary.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 documents Reopen for post-close REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:242 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:279 assert reopen behavior for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK (line numbers may have shifted after prior attempts; verify before editing).

PRIOR ATTEMPT CONTEXT
- result_rev 3b03e5a: implementation present and review-gate tests written; review step itself was malformed (review_error: unparseable), merge never landed.
- result_rev aca87ab: TestPreCloseReview suite passes; commit blocked by TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded (4 of 5 beads closed, missing HEAD:bead-0005.txt). Prior agent confirmed this failure is not introduced by the review-gate change.

FIRST ACTION
Before writing any code, run `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreCloseReview' -count=1`. If all three TestPreCloseReview_* cases pass, skip re-implementation and proceed directly to the integration-test verification step below.

PROPOSED FIX
- Invoke the review-group coordinator before CloseWithEvidence for every close-eligible implementation result.
- Close only when both reviewers APPROVE and each approval includes per-AC evidence.
- Treat APPROVE without evidence as review_error: unparseable.
- REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK prevent close and record the appropriate review event without closing/reopening the bead.

INTEGRATION TEST GATE
After the review-gate implementation is verified, run `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded' -count=1 -v`. If it fails, determine via `git log --oneline` and targeted blame whether the failure predates this bead's changes. If pre-existing: document the git-log evidence in the commit message and proceed. If introduced by this bead: fix it before committing.

NON-SCOPE
- Repair-cycle escalation and review-error retry budget.
- Root-cause fix of the integration test if failure is confirmed pre-existing.

title: review: enforce adversarial pre-close approval before CloseWithEvidence

acceptance:
1. TestWorkResourcePreflight_RunsCleanupBelowSoftFloor proves ddx work checks resource pressure before claim and invokes cleanup when temp/evidence roots are below the soft threshold even if they are above the hard floor.
2. TestWorkResourcePreflight_ContinuesAfterCleanupRestoresBudget proves the loop claims work only after cleanup brings roots back above the configured budget.
3. TestWorkResourcePreflight_StopsBelowHardFloorAfterCleanup proves the loop emits resource_exhausted and claims no bead when cleanup cannot restore the hard floor.
4. TestWorkResourcePreflight_ReportsBeforeAfterCapacity verifies loop events and command output include before/after bytes/inodes plus cleanup reclaimed counts.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestWorkResourcePreflight_RunsCleanupBelowSoftFloor|TestWorkResourcePreflight_ContinuesAfterCleanupRestoresBudget|TestWorkResourcePreflight_StopsBelowHardFloorAfterCleanup|TestWorkResourcePreflight_ReportsBeforeAfterCapacity" -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Even with periodic cleanup implemented, ddx work can still continue until the temp filesystem is nearly exhausted because resource preflight only fails below a very small hard floor and cleanup scope may not be invoked as a pressure-relief action early enough. The work cycle should keep temp usage bounded before claiming more beads, not wait for ENOSPC symptoms.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:13-16 defines executionResourceMinFreeBytes as 64 MiB and executionResourceMinFreeInodes as 1024, which is a last-ditch hard floor rather than an operational safety margin for agent worktrees and tests.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:106-131 runs cleanup only after a root check is already unhealthy, so cleanup is not used as a soft-pressure response while the host is degraded but still above the hard floor.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:550-551 runs a cleanup pass before each claim, but it does not inspect resource pressure after that pass and decide whether to claim, retry cleanup, or stop.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:501-503 starts the periodic worker, but the startup/pre-claim path does not assert an operator-visible resource budget such as minimum free GiB or minimum free percentage.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add configurable resource budgets: soft cleanup threshold and hard stop floor for bytes and inodes, with conservative defaults suitable for isolated worktrees and e2e tests.
- Run cleanup before claim whenever the temp/evidence roots are below the soft threshold; re-check after cleanup.
- If roots remain below the hard floor, stop the work loop with resource_exhausted before claiming another bead.
- Emit loop events and command output that report before/after free bytes/inodes, cleanup reclaimed bytes/inodes, and the reason for continuing or stopping.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement broad OS package cache cleanup or non-DDx directory deletion.
- Do not change routing, model retry, quota retry, or review retry behavior.
- Do not remove the operator ddx cleanup command; this bead makes the work cycle use the same manager automatically.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-cf37253d for the spec contract and ddx-2df8e946 for cleanup scope broad enough to reclaim leaked DDx scratch roots.
title: work: gate claims on temp resource budget after cleanup

description:
PROBLEM
Both execute-bead prompt strings (Claude: 1078 words, Agent: 1020 words) contain prose paragraphs that consume more tokens than equivalent bullet checklists. 19 load-bearing guardrails are embedded in prose without explicit enumeration, making omission hard to detect. No invariant tests verify that selectors and the missing-governing path work correctly.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or prompts package): the shared blocks (extracted by ddx-e2171a2c, a dep) are prose paragraphs averaging ~25 words each. Equivalent checklists would cut to ~10 words per guardrail.
- No FEAT-022-referencing comment block exists that enumerates all 19 guardrails inline.
- No invariant tests exist for prompt selectors (which harness-specific block is chosen) or missing-governing path (behavior when governing doc is absent).
- ddx-e2171a2c (shared block extraction, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Tighten shared blocks: convert prose paragraphs to bullet checklists targeting >= 30% word reduction on both Claude and Agent variants (~400 words saved on Claude, ~450 on Agent).
- Verify all 19 guardrails preserved: enumerate each guardrail in a comment or test.
- Add FEAT-022-referencing comment block listing all 19 guardrails inline (with FEAT-022 cross-reference).
- Add invariant tests:
  - TestPromptSelector_CorrectBlock: verifies each harness gets its specific block.
  - TestPromptSelector_MissingGoverning: verifies missing-governing path uses fallback or errors predictably.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing prompt semantics or dropping any of the 19 guardrails.
- Adding new guardrails (those go in the rubric update).
